## 2026-06-17 â€” LANE L4 "Gowers-norm inverse theorem / nilsequences / quadratic Fourier (Greenâ€“Taoâ€“Ziegler) on the SUP M(n)" REDUCES-TO-FENCE F1/F7 (energy) + F0 (conservation) + F11 (fold synonym) (probe_wfH_L4_gowers{,_fold}.py, _wfL4_gowers_quadratic_fold.lean)

LANE (cluster: alien/cross â€” additive-combinatorics frontier). Ask: does the inverse theorem for the
Gowers `U^k` norms (Greenâ€“Taoâ€“Ziegler, Ann. of Math. 176 (2012) 1231â€“1372, arXiv:1009.3998;
finite-field case Taoâ€“Ziegler / Bergelsonâ€“Taoâ€“Ziegler, Tao 254B Notes 5) â€” which detects correlation
with degree-`(kâˆ’1)` polynomial phases / `(kâˆ’1)`-step nilsequences BEYOND the linear Fourier spectrum
â€” give a genuinely non-second-order, non-linear handle on the SUP `M(n)=max_{bâ‰ 0}|خ·_b|`,
`خ·_b=خ£_{xâˆˆخ¼_n}e_p(bx)`, that dodges the dead energy route (F1/F12)? RESEARCHED (cite, verified):

(i) **Uآ² IS the energy / the خ·_b themselves.** `â€–fâ€–_{Uآ²}^4 = خ£_خ¾|fج‚(خ¾)|^4` (Tao 254B Notes 5; Green
*Additive Combinatorics* ch. 5). For `f=1_{خ¼_n}`, `fج‚(b)=خ·_b/p`, so `â€–1_{خ¼_n}â€–_{Uآ²}^4 = Eâ‚‚(خ¼_n)/pآ³`
= additive energy; the Uآ² inverse theorem returns the linear phase `e_p(bx)`, i.e. the `خ·_b`. The
4th absolute moment of these Gaussian periods is computed via Fermat-curve point counts
(Garciaâ€“Lorenzâ€“Todd, arXiv:2112.13886) and explicitly controls the moment, NOT the sup. = fence F1/F7.

(ii) **Monotonicity runs the WRONG way.** `Uآ²â‰¤Uآ³â‰¤Uâپ´â‰¤â€¦` (Tao 254B Notes 3 Ex.19) and `â€–fج‚â€–_âˆ‍â‰¤â€–fâ€–_{Uآ²}`,
with `M=pآ·â€–fج‚â€–_âˆ‍`. So `M â‰¤ pآ·â€–fâ€–_{Uآ²} â‰¤ pآ·â€–fâ€–_{U^k}` âˆ€kâ‰¥2: each higher norm is LARGER â‡’ a LOOSER
ceiling. No `U^k` (k>2) sharpens the energy bound on a single coefficient. = fence F0 (the âˆڑlog tail
is invisible to fixed moments). The inverse theorem is moreover an EXISTENCE/lower-bound tool, not
an upper bound â€” it cannot cap `M` at all (Tao 254B Notes 5 Prop. 6: a fn can have `U^{d+1}=1` with
negligible correlation to all low-degree classical polynomials).

(iii) **THE decisive mechanism (this file): the quadratic obstruction FOLDS to the same wall.** The
one non-reducing hope was `1_{خ¼_n}` correlating with a genuine quadratic phase `e_p(cxآ²)` MORE than
any linear phase. EXACT-INTEGER probe (خ²=4 where feasible, 4â‰¤nâ‰¤64): the quadratic correlation
`Q(n)=max_{câ‰ 0}|خ£_{xâˆˆخ¼_n}e_p(cxآ²)|` DOES exceed `M(n)` and the gap GROWS (`Q/M = 1.06,1.36,1.43,1.62`
at `n=8,16,32,64`) â€” so the terse prior "it's just the wall" (N17) needed a mechanism. The mechanism:
`xâ†¦xآ²` is a 2-to-1 hom of `خ¼_n` onto the index-2 subgroup `خ¼_{n/2}` (probe: `خ¼_nآ² = 2آ·خ¼_{n/2}` EXACTLY),
so `خ£_{xâˆˆخ¼_n}e_p(cxآ²) = 2آ·خ£_{yâˆˆخ¼_{n/2}}e_p(cy) = 2آ·خ·^{(خ¼_{n/2})}_c`. The probe confirms the EXACT
identity `Q(n)=2آ·M(خ¼_{n/2})` to machine precision (`|Qâˆ’2M_half|=0`) for `n=8,16,32,64`. So the Uآ³
"non-linear" obstruction IS twice the SAME BGK/Paley wall over the half-size 2-power subgroup `خ¼_{n/2}`;
Uâپ´,Uâپµ,â€¦ iterate the fold to `خ¼_{n/4}`,`خ¼_{n/8}`,â€¦, never leaving the family. = fence F11 (object-change
synonym). The inverse theorem fed `1_{خ¼_n}` returns a Gauss period over a smaller 2-power subgroup.

VERDICT = REDUCES-TO-FENCE F1/F7 + F0 + F11. Lean (`_wfL4_gowers_quadratic_fold.lean`, axiom-clean
`[propext,Classical.choice,Quot.sound]`, real `lake build` 8313 jobs): `quadratic_phase_folds_to_half`
(the exact 2-to-1 fold engine: a phase pulled through any exactly-2-to-1 hom sums to `2أ—` its image
sum), `quadratic_correlation_le_two_mul_half_sup` (`Q â‰¤ 2آ·M_half` magnitude form), and
`gowers_higher_norm_no_improvement` (`Supâ‰¤Uâ‚‚â‰¤Uâ‚ƒ â‡’ Supâ‰¤Uâ‚ƒ`, the looser-ceiling no-go). Probes
`scripts/probes/probe_wfH_L4_gowers{,_fold}.py` (exact integer fold-set equality + numpy argmax).
Confirms & sharpens the terse in-tree verdicts N17 (GTZ inverse Gowers REDUCES), E8 (خ¼_n phase
collapses to linear), and DISPROOF_LOG "Uآ² Gowers = Eâ‚‚ = Johnson".

## 2026-06-17 â€” LANE L1 "Hardyâ€“Littlewood circle method / Weyl differencing / Vinogradov minor-arc on the SUP M(n)" REDUCES-TO-FENCE F1/F12 (+F0) (probe_wfHL1_circle_method_supform.rs, _wfHL1_circle_method_supform.lean)

LANE (cluster: alien/cross â€” analytic number theory). Ask: does a major/minor-arc decomposition,
or a Weyl-differencing / Vinogradov minor-arc estimate, bound the prize SUP
`M(n)=max_{bâ‰ 0}|خ·_b|`, `خ·_b=خ£_{xâˆˆخ¼_n}e_p(bx)`, in a SUP form that is NOT a moment of `خ·_b` (i.e.
that dodges the dead additive-energy route F12)? RESEARCHED (cite): Tao 2013 "Bounding short
exponential sums on smooth moduli via Weyl differencing" â€” Weyl differencing requires INTERVAL
summation + rational/polynomial phase + smooth modulus, gaining by reducing the modulus PERIOD;
inapplicable to a multiplicative subgroup with a LINEAR phase. Bourgainâ€“Glibichukâ€“Konyagin (J. LMS
2006) get the SOTA `Mâ‰¤n^{1âˆ’o(1)}` by ABANDONING the circle method for additive combinatorics
(sum-product / Balogâ€“Szemerأ©diâ€“Gowers) â€” precisely because the multiplicativeâ†”additive structure
clash (the sum-product phenomenon) is invisible to arc/Weyl machinery. Shakan's exposition: the SOTA
route uses the large SPECTRUM `Spec_خ±(H)` + additive energy and does NOT optimize a moment bound.
Vinogradov's mean-value theorem (Wooley; Bourgainâ€“Demeterâ€“Guth) is intrinsically a MEAN-VALUE
(moment) method `âˆ«|f|^{2k}` â€” the fence-F12 object by construction; it has no individual/sup form.

VERDICT = REDUCES-TO-FENCE F1/F12 (+F0). The circle method offers exactly TWO Fourier-dual handles on
`خ·_b`, plus a saving MECHANISM that needs structure `خ¼_n` lacks; all three pinned by exact-integer
probe (p prime, pâ‰،1 mod n, خ¼_n proper, خ²â‰ˆ4, p~nâپ´, NEVER n=pâˆ’1; n=16,32,64):

  (i) WEYL DIFFERENCING / van der Corput on the degree-1 phase âں¶ the additive AUTOCORRELATION =
      the 2nd moment. `|خ·_b|آ²=خ£_h A_n(h)e_p(bh)`, `A_n(h)=#{xâˆ’y=h}`. Differencing a LINEAR phase does
      not lower its degree (none to lower); it returns `A_n`, a nonnegative count: diagonal `A_n(0)=n`,
      off-diagonal total `n(nâˆ’1)=(nâˆ’1)آ·diag` (probe T1: off/diag ratio EXACTLY nâˆ’1 = 15/31/63 at
      n=16/32/64) â€” GROWS with n, positive-definite, phase-blind to the argmax. Fence F0 (conservation)
      + F1 (energy). [Same object as in-tree `period_autocovariance_eq` Wienerâ€“Khinchin tautology and
      the positive-definite geometric side of H2 Kuznetsov/RTF.]

  (ii) MAJOR/MINOR-ARC POWER-SUM is the ONLY nontrivial arc split, and it IS the moment. `خ£_b خ·_b^{2r}
      = qآ·Nâ‚€(خ¼_n,2r) = qآ·E_r` (`subgroup_gaussSum_rawMoment`); major arc `b=0` gives `خ·_0^{2r}=n^{2r}`,
      minor arcs `bâ‰ 0` carry the deviation. Positivity âں¹ induced sup bound `M^{2r}â‰¤qآ·E_r`, i.e.
      `(qآ·E_r)^{1/2r}`, which by the moment no-go is `â‰¥n` for every r. Probe T2 (exact-int E_r at خ²=4):
      the moment optimum `min_r (qآ·E_r)^{1/2r}` OVERSHOOTS the floor `âˆڑ(n log m)` by `1.54 / 1.82 /
      2.48` at n=16/32/64 (ratio rising with n) â€” fence F12 (bounded-K Wick DEAD past depth onset).

  (iii) the Weyl/Vinogradov SAVING MECHANISM (smooth-modulus period reduction / interval+polynomial
      phase) is INAPPLICABLE: `خ¼_n` is a multiplicative subgroup, NOT an interval â€” probe T3: `خ¼_n`
      meets every additive window of length p/n in O(1) points (max = 3/3/5 at n=16/32/64, ~equidistr.,
      not interval-filling) â€” and the phase `bx` is linear. Nothing for differencing to localize or
      reduce. This is exactly why BGK abandons the circle method.

Distinct from prior circle-method bricks: `CircleMethodFreeSetSupport` ran it on the bad-scalar count
`a` (REMOVES the char sum â†’ binomial threshold); `CrossCellCircleMethodSplit` ran it on the

RESEARCHED (cite): resonance method (Soundararajan; Boberâ€“Goldmakher arXiv:1109.1786 large character
sums; Bondarenkoâ€“Seip arXiv:1507.05840 خ¶; de la Bretأ¨cheâ€“Tenenbaum) produces **LOWER** bounds on

(arXiv:1602.08875) CUE-max `log Nâˆ’(3/4)loglog N` is SHARP and sub-Gaussian but available **only for
a LOG-CORRELATED field** (`Cov(log|P(خ¸)|,log|P(خ¸')|)âˆ¼âˆ’log|خ¸âˆ’خ¸'|`). One-/n-level density (Katzâ€“Sarnak,
Iwaniecâ€“Luoâ€“Sarnak) controls LOW-LYING ZEROS near the central point, NOT the max; and is a `qâ†’âˆ‍`
averaged limit (HORN-EFF, same as C13/C14/C31).

VERDICT = REDUCES-TO-FENCE **F0** (the only one-sided handle is the iid-Gaussian union-bound upper
tail; transferring it to the deterministic arithmetic family is the 2nd-order-blind Johnson wall â€”
the `âˆڑlog` excess is the rare-event tail) **/ F11** (the extreme-value prediction `âˆڑ(2n log(p/n))`
*equals* the open BGK object; object-change synonym). Prize-faithful, exact-integer multi-prime probe
(`probe_wfHK2_logcorr_field.rs`; `p` PRIME, `pâ‰،1 mod n`, `خ¼_n` proper, `خ²â‰ˆ4`, `p~nâپ´`, NEVER `n=pâˆ’1`):

HORN-WHITE (NEW, the load-bearing measurement). The field `bâ†¦log|خ·_b|` is **WHITE NOISE, not
log-correlated**. `Cov(log|خ·_b|,log|خ·_{b'}|)/Var` at archimedean lags `d=1..256` AND 2-adic lags
`vâ‚‚(bâˆ’b')=0..7` are ALL `â‰ˆ0` (`|cov/var|<0.04`, no `âˆ’ln d` slope) for every generic prime at
n=32,64,128. (Sole exception: `p=2^16+1` Fermat prime â€” non-generic, spurious; excluded by 2nd
prime.) âں¹ the FHK/ABB log-correlated-field machinery â€” the ONLY route to a max **sharper** than the
iid-Gaussian one â€” **does NOT apply**. There is no covariance structure to exploit, archimedean or

HORN-GAUSSIAN (confirms C31). Excess kurtosis of `Re خ·_b â†’ 0` (`âˆ’0.10` n=32 â†’ `âˆ’0.035` n=128): the
Satoâ€“Tate / symmetry-type law is the **complex Gaussian**, support all of `â„‚`, **NO compact edge**.
"Symmetry-group edge bounds the max" is a category error â€” the law is unbounded; its "edge" is its
extreme value = the open object.

HORN-TARGET (the reduction). A white-noise Gaussian field over `m=(pâˆ’1)/n` cosets has iid extreme
value `âˆڑnآ·âˆڑ(2 log m)=âˆڑ(2nآ·log(p/n))`. Probe: empirical max tracks `âˆڑ(2n ln m)` (ratio `0.78â†’0.89`,

floor. The EVT heuristic REPRODUCES the open statement; it does not prove it. The one-sided upper

to the specific arithmetic family requires exactly the `âˆڑq`-cancellation in short subgroup character

Sibling to REFUTED C13 (vertical Satoâ€“Tate sup-control), C14 (Kloosterman purity), C31 (Satoâ€“Tate
support edge), C29 (Kowalskiâ€“Sawin path CLT) â€” same effectivity + Gaussian-unbounded-support family.

`probe_wfH_K3_berry_tabor_evt.rs`, de-tied/unfolded level spacing):
- **T1 â€” Poisson confirmed.** Gap-ratio `âں¨râں©` of the SORTED DISTINCT magnitudes = **0.387, 0.392,
  0.390, 0.421** at (n=64,m=2.5e5),(64,4.1e6),(128,2.0e6),(256,1.6e7) â€” dead-on Poisson `0.386`, NOT
  GUE `0.603`. The spectrum is genuinely integrable / level-repulsion-free (Berryâ€“Tabor). `M/âˆڑ(n log
  m)` plateaus at 1.24â€“1.30.
- **T2 â€” DECISIVE: spacing is BLIND to the max.** Multiplying ONLY the single largest value by `K`
  (one "bad coset" resonance) leaves `âں¨râں© = 0.3869` **identically unchanged** for K=1,2,5,20,100,
  while the **max scales exactly أ—K**. A family can be Poisson-spaced with an arbitrarily inflated
  max: the bulk spacing statistic carries ZERO information about the single tail event that IS the
  prize sup.
- **T3 â€” the rigorous-EVT upgrade is F12.** The EVT threshold `u_n=âˆڑ(2n log m)` sits at tail-depth
  `âˆڑ(2 log m) â‰ˆ 5` s.d. (`#{|خ·_b|â‰¥u_n}=0` at testable scales; max comes in below the iid threshold).
  Rigorous Poisson EVT (Leadbetter: maxâ‡’Gumbel iff long-range mixing `D(u_n)` AND local
  anti-clustering `D'(u_n)`, extremal index خ¸=1, hold AT u_n) requires controlling the law to
  moment-order `r â‰ˆ (âˆڑ(2 log m))آ²/2 = log m` â€” the effective CLT at depth `log m` = `E_râ‰¤(2r-1)â€¼n^r`
  at `râ‰ˆlog m` = BCHKS 1.12 = **F12** (DEAD at خ²=4, exact-arithmetic refuted).

RESEARCHED (cite): Berryâ€“Tabor conjecture (Marklof, ICMP2000 survey; Poisson spacing for integrable
systems). Leadbetter `D(u_n)/D'(u_n)` mixing + anti-clustering conditions for stationary-sequence EVT
(Leadbetter 1974 PTRF, 1983 "Extremes and local dependence"); extremal index خ¸ â€” خ¸=1 â‡” isolated
exceedances (needs tail/threshold control, NOT bulk spacing). Rudnickâ€“Sarnak pair-correlation; the
lattice-points-on-circles Poisson-spacing theorem (Bourgainâ€“Rudnickâ€“Sarnak / arXiv:2112.08522,
Invent. 2025) is explicitly a LOCAL BULK nearest-neighbour result, says nothing about a maximum. No
proven mixing/independence of `خ·_b` across cosets at the threshold level exists â€” only the
QUALITATIVE joint Gauss-sum equidistribution (Rojas-Leأ³n 2207.12439), a qâ†’âˆ‍ averaged limit, not the
effective uniform tail-depth-`log m` control rigour needs.

VERDICT = REDUCES-TO-FENCE **F0** (the Poisson/Berryâ€“Tabor spacing is a BULK statistic, structurally
blind to the rare-event tail sup â€” `âں¨râں©` is literally invariant under a single-coordinate tail warp
that sends the max â†’ âˆ‍; this is the conservation law in spacing-statistics dialect) **+ F12** (the
only path from spacing to a rigorous max â€” Leadbetter `D'(u_n)` anti-clustering at the threshold â€”
needs the dead threshold-depth `râ‰ˆlog m` energy/CLT input). No non-energy EVT input survives: the
spacing law is real but tail-blind. **Floor stays OPEN** â€” method-boundary verdict, not a closure or
refutation. Lean: `_wfHK3_berry_tabor_poisson_evt.lean` (`spacing_blind_to_sup` = the warp
obstruction; `evt_threshold_depth_is_logm` = the F12 reduction; 5 theorems axiom-clean
`{propext,Classical.choice,Quot.sound}`, no sorryAx). Distinct from K2 (log-correlation/FHK gate):
K3 is the spacing-vs-tail (bulk-vs-max) distinction.

## 2026-06-17 â€” LANE H2 "Kuznetsov / Petersson / relative-trace-formula amplification for the Gauss-period sup" REDUCES-TO-FENCE F5/F1 (probe_wfH2_kuznetsov_rtf_geometric_side.py, _wfH2_kuznetsov_rtf_geometric_side.lean)

LANE (cluster: automorphic / amplification â€” flagged the LEAST-conservation-blind by the manifesto,
since Iwaniecâ€“Sarnak amplification is *designed* to beat the 2nd moment for sup-norms). Ask: is
`M(n) = max_{bâ‰ 0}|خ·_b|`, `خ·_b=خ£_{xâˆˆخ¼_n}e_p(bx)`, an automorphic/Hecke sup-norm whose
`خ£_b f(b) خ·_b^k` admits a (relative) trace-formula expansion whose spectral/geometric side gives
cancellation in the SUP invisible to the moment? RESEARCHED: Kuznetsov/Petersson = early relative
trace formula (Jacquet); geometric side = weighted **Kloosterman** sums (GL(2)), spectral side =
GL(2) Maass/holomorphic Fourier coeffs; Deshouillersâ€“Iwaniec spectral large sieve exploits it.
Iwaniecâ€“Sarnak amplification (sup-norm) WINS **iff the geometric side has off-diagonal cancellation**
(literature: "for amplification to work, the geometric side is expected to have non-trivial
cancellation", arXiv:2503.06224 orbit-method survey + the level-aspect filtration papers).

VERDICT = REDUCES-TO-FENCE **F5** (abelian torus âں¹ zero gap) **/ F1** (positive-definite geometric
side = no spectral saving = the energy). Two structural failures, prize-faithful (`p` prime,
`pâ‰،1 mod n`, `خ¼_n` proper, `خ²â‰ˆ4`, `p~nâپ´`, NEVER `n=pâˆ’1`):

1. **WRONG GROUP â€” `خ·_b` is GL(1)/abelian (Gauss sum), not Kloosterman (GL(2)).** By the abelian-
   Cayley-graph spectral theorem (eigenvalues `خ£_{gâˆˆS}د‡(g)`, eigenvectors = the fixed additive
   characters; Podestأ،â€“Videla 1911.08549) `خ·_b` = the eigenvalue of `Cay(F_p^+,خ¼_n)`; the dual
   expansion writes it as a linear combination of **Gauss sums** `g(دˆ)`, NOT a Kloosterman sum
   `خ£e_p(x+a/x)`. The dilation-torus Hecke algebra is commutative & 1-dim per frequency âں¹ **no Hecke
   multiplicities** for an amplifier (F5). Sibling of the REFUTED C29 (Kowalskiâ€“Sawin Kloosterman-
   PATH CLT, same object-mismatch); this is the *amplification / trace-formula* sibling.

2. **POSITIVE-DEFINITE GEOMETRIC SIDE â€” nothing to cancel (exact-integer probe).** The only
   amplifiers a trace formula produces are class functions of the dilation index. For the additive
   amplifier `a_b=e_p(âˆ’h b)` the amplified 2nd moment expands (Parseval) to the geometric side
   `A(h)=خ£_b e_p(âˆ’h b)|خ·_b|آ² = pآ·#{(x,y)âˆˆخ¼_nآ²:xâˆ’y=h}` â€” the **additive autocorrelation of `خ¼_n`**.
   Every term `A(h)â‰¥0` (a count): `A` is **positive-definite**, diagonal `A(0)=pآ·n`, off-diagonal
   total `خ£_{hâ‰ 0}A(h)=pآ·(nآ²âˆ’n)=(nâˆ’1)آ·A(0)` â€” it **GROWS** with `n`, never `o(diagonal)`. The probe
   confirms EXACTLY (`n=16,32,64`, `خ²â‰ˆ4`): `E_2=3n(nâˆ’1)` = the char-0 Wick value (2nd moment blind to
   the worst `b`), off/diag ratio `= nâˆ’1`, and even the ORACLE amplifier average `â‰ھ Mآ²`. So the
   amplified average collapses to the flat energy `pآ·E_r` â€” fence F1/F12 (`(qآ·E_r)^{1/2r} â‰¥ n` âˆ€r,
   `_MomentMethodNoGo`). The amplification method needs an OSCILLATORY (Kloosterman) geometric side;
   the abelian period supplies a non-oscillatory positive count instead.

Lean (`_wfH2_kuznetsov_rtf_geometric_side.lean`, axiom-clean `âٹ†{propext,Classical.choice,Quot.sound}`,
no `sorry`): `offdiag_is_growing_multiple` (off-diag `=(nâˆ’1)آ·diag`), `geometric_side_no_cancellation`
(positive-definite âں¹ off-diag `â‰¥0`, nothing to cancel), `amplified_average_le_max` (amplifier can't
exceed the max it doesn't already know), `kuznetsov_route_bound_ge_card` / `kuznetsov_route_dead`
(re-export of the moment-method no-go: the route's quantitative output is `â‰¥n`). Method-boundary
verdict, NOT a floor refutation; the floor `Mâ‰¤Câˆڑ(n log(p/n))` stays OPEN on the BGK/Paley wall. This
rigorizes the long-standing flag (`deltastar-Bmun-IS-generalized-Paley-spectral-gap` آ§5: automorphic
sup-norm papers are methodological analogs, not a ready bound).

## 2026-06-17 â€” ANGLE T16/G4-1 "Frobenius-refined Chang cover of the H-invariant large spectrum" REDUCES-TO-WALL (probe_wfT16_chang_frobenius_dim.rs, probe_wfT16_balance_arith.rs, _wfT16_chang_frobenius_cover.lean)

ANGLE (cluster G4, post-2020 additive combinatorics, STRUCTURE/COVERING not norms): cover the large
spectrum `Spec_خ±={b:|خ·_b|â‰¥خ±n}` of `1_{خ¼_n}` by `D` dilation-cosets via Chang's lemma, then claim a
NEW factor `1/خ¼` (`خ¼=logâ‚‚n`) reduction of the dissociated dimension from 2-power Frobenius orbits of
the `{-1,0,1}`-relations, getting `D=Câ‚€خ±âپ»آ²(log(p/n)/خ¼)` (vs Chang's wall `Dâ‚€=Câ‚€خ±âپ»آ²log(p/n)`); then
"counting + Parseval-balance over â‰¤nآ·D large cosets at خ±=M/n forces Mâ‰¤Câˆڑ(n log(p/n))" â€” the floor.

VERDICT = REDUCES-TO-WALL (F1 primary via Rudin/Khintchine; F0 the balancing reversal). Three
independent failures, all prize-faithful (p prime, pâ‰،1 mod n, خ¼_n proper, خ²=4, p~nâپ´, NEVER n=p-1):

1. **F1 â€” Chang's dimension IS a second moment (literature).** Chang's lemma `dim(Spec_خ±)â‰¤Câ‚€خ±âپ»آ²
   log(1/density)` is proved via **Rudin's inequality**, itself a corollary of **Khintchine** (the
   dissociated characters behave as independent آ±1 variables; the dimension bound is the even-moment
   estimate). So `Dâ‚€` is conjugate to the energy ladder F1. The candidate ADMITS the base `Dâ‚€` form
   recovers the BGK WALL â€” the entire prize content rests on the `1/خ¼` factor.

2. **The `1/خ¼` Frobenius collapse is empirically FALSE** (`probe_wfT16_chang_frobenius_dim.rs`). The
   mechanism needs the large spectrum organized into 2-power squaring orbits whose relations collapse
   the dimension. Measured: the squaring map `bâ†¦bآ²` (only 2-power orbit map, since خ¼_n is 2-power)
   does NOT preserve |خ·| â€” `|خ·_{b*آ²}|/M` at the argmax b* drops to â‰ˆ0.05 immediately (n=64:1.00â†’0.063;
   n=128:1.00â†’0.047; n=256:1.00â†’0.35). No Galois invariance of the spectrum to collapse along. And the
   measured dissociated dimension of Spec_خ± tracks Dâ‚€ as a CONSTANT FRACTION (d/Dâ‚€â‰ˆ0.18â€“0.36 at خ±=0.5,
   n=8..64), NOT Dâ‚€/خ¼ (which would force d/Dâ‚€â†’1/خ¼, the sequence 0.33,0.25,0.20,0.17,... â€” not seen).

3. **F0 â€” the balancing step is REVERSED** (`probe_wfT16_balance_arith.rs`). Even granting a cover
   `|Spec_خ±|â‰¤nآ·D`, combining it with Parseval `خ£_{bâ‰ 0}|خ·_b|آ²=n(p-n)` gives a LOWER bound on M, never
   an upper one: `massâ‰¤|Spec|Mآ²` âں¹ `Mآ²â‰¥mass/|Spec|=n(p-n)/(nآ·D)=(p-n)/Dâ‰¥n` (Johnson RMS), and this
   INCREASES as the cover D shrinks. So a smaller dissociated dimension (the candidate's whole point)
   pushes the only forced M-bound UP. Measured: RMS coset period = âˆڑn to full precision (Johnson),
   خ£_{bâ‰ 0}|خ·_b|آ²=n(p-n) exact, forced M-lower â‰¥ Johnson at every n=8..256. Cardinality+Parseval cannot
   upper-bound the sup; it pins the Johnson floor â€” the conservation law F0 verbatim. A constant-cover
   (the `1/خ¼` form makes D=O(1) in n at خ²=4) would force the ABSURD Mâ‰³n^{3/2}, far above true âˆڑ(n log).

DISTINCT from A7/A12 (which killed the PRIMAL Crootâ€“Sisask almost-period object via the Lآ²/L^q norm
threshold q*=خ²/(خ²-1)<2): T16 attacks the DUAL (Chang dissociated-dimension / covering) object, but
lands on the same wall â€” Chang's dimension is the Rudin/Khintchine 2nd moment (F1) and the covering
cardinality enters Parseval on the lower-bound side (F0). The `1/خ¼` Galois refinement, the only new
lever, has no empirical support (squaring breaks the peak). NOVEL vs literature (no paper applies a
Frobenius-orbit collapse to Chang's dissociated dimension for 2-power subgroups â€” confirmed: Shkredov
QJM2012/Cochraneâ€“Pinner use خ“-ENERGY not orbit-collapse), ABSENT from codebase (grep: only DISPROOF_LOG
false-positives), but REDUCES.

LEAN (axiom-clean [propext,Classical.choice,Quot.sound], all six theorems, no sorryAx):
`_wfT16_chang_frobenius_cover.lean` â€” `balance_is_lower_bound` (mass/Sâ‰¤Mآ², the only Parseval+cardinality
relation, lower-bound direction), `forced_lower_antitone_in_D` (the forced lower bound grows as D
shrinks), `forced_lower_at_least_johnson` (Mآ²â‰¥(p-n)/Dâ‰¥n at خ²=4), `refined_dim_is_constant_in_n` (the
1/خ¼ form is O(1) in n at خ²=4), `constant_cover_forces_absurd_lower` (an O(1) cover forces a false huge
M-lower), `conservation_law_T16` (F0: the unique forced relation is the monotone-wrong-way Johnson

## 2026-06-17 â€” ANGLE A14 "Terwilliger-algebra operator-norm bound on M" OBSTRUCTION (probe_wfA14_terwilliger_dim.rs, probe_wfA14_terw_fast.rs, _wfA14_terwilliger_module_dim.lean)

ANGLE (manifesto route 5b): bound `M(n)=خ»â‚‚(Cay(F_p,خ¼_n))=max_i|خ·_i|` by the Terwilliger
(subconstituent) algebra `T(xâ‚€)` of the cyclotomic scheme on `Z_p` â€” generated by the
Boseâ€“Mesner algebra (the `m=(pâˆ’1)/n` coset adjacency matrices) plus the dual idempotents
`E*_i` (diagonal shell projectors). The operator-norm method (Terwilliger 1992; tridiagonal
pairs) confines an eigenvalue of `A` to an irreducible `T`-module and, IF every module is THIN
(dim â‰¤ diameter+1), pins it by a Krawtchouk three-term recurrence â€” exactly how eigenvalues are
bounded on the **Hamming scheme** `H(d,2)` (`dim T = (d+1)(d+2)(d+3)/6 = خک((logâ‚‚V)آ³)`,
polylog in the vertex count `V`).

The route's bet: the prize graph sits in a scheme whose Terwilliger algebra is low-dimensional,
so `M` is confined to a small block with a sub-`âˆڑq` norm.

**EXACT computation kills it (modular-rank closure over `F_l`, `l=2آ·10âپ¹+11`, prize-faithful
proper `خ¼_n`, `n=2^خ¼`, `p` prime, `n|pâˆ’1`, `pâ‰«n`):** the cyclotomic scheme on a PRIME number of
vertices is the documented worst case for Terwilliger dimension. Measured closed form

  `dim T(xâ‚€) = mآ·(p+1) + 1`,  `m=(pâˆ’1)/n`  â†’  **`خک(pآ²/n)`, LINEAR in the vertex count `p`.**

| n | p  | m  | measured `dim T` | `m(p+1)+1` |
|---|----|----|------------------|------------|
| 2 | 13 | 6  | 85               | 85         |
| 2 | 29 | 14 | 421              | 421        |
| 4 | 29 | 7  | 211              | 211        |
| 4 | 37 | 9  | 343              | 343        |
| 4 | 53 | 13 | 703              | 703        |
| 8 | 41 | 5  | 211              | 211        |
| 8 | 73 | 9  | 667              | 667        |

(Confirms Malekiâ€“Razafimahatratra 2025, arXiv:2510.17118: "the gap in dimension between
Terwilliger algebras can be arbitrarily large, as in the case of Paley graphs on a prime number
of vertices.") The average irreducible-module dimension is `dim T / #modules â‰ˆ خک(m)=خک(p/n)â†’âˆ‍`:
the modules are NOT thin, there is NO bounded block to confine `M`, and the operator-norm
certificate degenerates to the trivial `âˆڑp` â€” vacuous at `خ²=4` where the target is `âˆڑ(n log p)`.

**Constraint lemma (why the locus does not move):** the Terwilliger algebra of a *Schurian
translation* scheme on `Z_p` is `خک(pآ²/n)`-dimensional precisely because the scheme is NOT
`P`-polynomial (distance-regular) for `n>2` â€” the very property (thin modules / tridiagonal
action) that powers the Krawtchouk operator-norm bound on the Hamming scheme is absent on the
prime-order cyclotomic scheme. This is the Terwilliger-framed instance of the project's
conservation-law meta-theorem: a bound whose only input is bounded-dimensional scheme structure
caps at the trivial scale. The genuine cancellation in `M` is the Gauss-period spread (the open
BGK core), not a thin-module output.

DISTINCT from A6 (Schurâ€“Siegelâ€“Smyth, reduces-to-johnson via averaging): A14 is the *operator/
spectral* framing and dies on a different mechanism â€” the module-dimension blow-up â€” not the
averaging/`L^âˆ‍`-blindness. (Route 6a SSS was already disposed as A6; route 5b Terwilliger had
0 in-tree.)

Lean (axiom-clean, real `lake build` 8313 jobs OK, autoImplicit=false): `terwDim n p = m(p+1)+1`,
`terwDim_ge_card` (`â‰¥ p` for `mâ‰¥1`, so not polylog), `terwDim_strictMono_in_p` (linear, never
saturates), `dimGap_witness` (`terwDim 4 53 = 703 > 8آ·hammingTerwDim 6 = 672`), `terwDim_matches_probe`
(ties to exact computation). Axioms: `[]` / `[propext, Quot.sound]` â€” no `sorryAx`.
File `Frontier/_wfA14_terwilliger_module_dim.lean`; probes `probe_wfA14_terwilliger_dim.rs`,
`probe_wfA14_terw_fast.rs`.

## 2026-06-17 â€” ANGLE A12 "L^q Croot-Sisask almost-periodicity (GROWING q) -> sup control of M" OBSTRUCTION (probe_wfA12_lp_croot_sisask.rs, _wfA12_lp_croot_sisask_threshold.lean)

ANGLE (manifesto A12, Croot-Sisask/Kelley-Meka-PFR of the bad locus): the prior A7 refutation tested
ONLY the L^2 reading of Croot-Sisask (empty L^2 almost-period set). A12 attacks the FRESH seam: the
*strong* L^q form of CS for GROWING q -- the form Sanders' Bogolyubov-Ruzsa and the Kelley-Meka 3AP
argument actually use -- since as q->inf the L^q norm -> sup, this is the only CS form that could
reach the rare-event peak M (the prize gap) that the L^2 form is structurally blind to.

NEW QUANTITY (closed form, prize-faithful): the CS L^q guarantee |T|/p >= exp(-C q eps^{-2} ln(1/alpha))
yields >=1 almost-period iff q <= ln p / ln(1/alpha). At the prize regime p=n^beta, alpha=n/p=n^{1-beta},
so ln(1/alpha)=(beta-1)ln n, ln p=beta ln n, giving the THRESHOLD (favorable eps=C=1)
    q* = beta/(beta-1).
At beta=4 this is q* = 4/3 < 2: CS is vacuous ALREADY BELOW L^2, never reaching the q->inf sup regime.

PROBE (prize-faithful: p PRIME, p=1 mod n, mu_n PROPER, beta=4, NEVER n=p-1), n in {16,32,64}:
  - p = 100049 / 1048609 / 16777601 (beta = 4.15 / 4.00 / 4.00), alpha = 2^{-12.6/-15/-18}.
  - qmax_guarantee = ln p / ln(1/alpha) = 1.32, 1.33, 1.33 (FLAT, prime-independent) -- matches q*=beta/(beta-1).
  - CS guarantee log-count ln|T| at q=2,4,8,16,32,64 = (-6, -23, -58, -128, -268, -548) at n=16, down to
    (-8, -33, -83, -183, -383, -782) at n=64 -- the guaranteed almost-period count is EXPONENTIALLY < 1
    at every q >= 2. The MEASURED L^q almost-period set is empty (|T_0.5|/p = 0 at every q >= 2; the few
    high-q survivors are sampling artifacts).
  - ||f||_q/M climbs (0.125 -> 0.771 at n=64, q=2..64): you WOULD need q->inf to see the peak, but the CS
    guarantee dies at q ~ 1.33, so the regime that sees M is exactly the regime where CS is empty.
  - BAD-LOCUS coset count (PFR/KM "<=n cosets past Johnson" target): #cosets |f|>=M/2 = 278,120,8 for
    n=16,32,64 (SHRINKS in n, hits <=n only at n=64) and #cosets |f|>=0.9M = 0 EVERY n. So the near-peak
    locus is a THIN, isolated, dilation-fixed set -- the peak is achieved on few cosets, which is the WRONG
    direction for the prize (rigid isolation, not smallness of M).

VERDICT = OBSTRUCTION (axiom-clean). A12 is STRICTLY STRONGER than A7: A7 ruled out one norm (L^2);
A12 rules out the ENTIRE L^q family at once via the closed-form threshold q*=beta/(beta-1), and pins the
exact crossover: q* >= 2 <=> beta <= 2 (cs_beta_for_L2), i.e. CS reaches L^2 only when the field is
SUB-PRIZE dense (p <= n^2), NEVER at beta=4. The q->inf sup regime that would force M down is unreachable
for every beta > 1. WHY (conservation law, refined): Croot-Sisask's almost-period count is governed by
the density alpha through ln(1/alpha); at alpha = 2^{-128} (prize) the count budget exp(-q ln(1/alpha)) is
exhausted before q reaches even 2, so NO L^q norm with q>=2 -- hence no approximation to the sup -- carries
any CS guarantee. The post-2020 strong L^q upgrade does not change the verdict: it is vacuous one notch
earlier than the L^2 form A7 killed.

LEAN (axiom-clean [propext,Classical.choice,Quot.sound], real lake build):
_wfA12_lp_croot_sisask_threshold.lean -- cs_threshold_eq (q*=beta/(beta-1)), cs_guarantee_empty
(q>q* => guarantee < 1 period), cs_threshold_lt_two (q*=4/3<2 at beta=4), cs_beta_for_L2
(q*>=2 <=> beta<=2), cs_sup_unreachable (every q>=2 empty at prize numbers). Probe: probe_wfA12_lp_croot_sisask.rs.

IDEA [DSAR-BMT] (#444, route: Bernsteinâ€“Markovâ€“Turأ،n on خ·). `خ·_b = خ£_{xâˆˆخ¼_n} e_p(bx)` is a SPARSE
trig polynomial: as `P(z)=خ£_{xâˆˆخ¼_n} z^x` it has exactly `n` nonzero Fourier coefficients (the indicator
of `خ¼_n âٹ‚ â„¤/pâ„¤`), all unit, spread over `[0,p)`. PROPOSED: the sparsity (`n` coeffs) + multiplicative-
subgroup support structure gives a Turأ،n/Bernsteinâ€“Markov-type ceiling `M(n)=max_{bâ‰ 0}â€–خ·_bâ€– â‰¤ f(n,sparsity)`
better than trivial `n` â€” WITHOUT the BGK coefficient/support arithmetic.

VERDICT: **REDUCES TO THE BGK WICK BOUND AT LOG DEPTH (no sparsity escape).** Two faces:
(i) The sparsity COUNT alone gives *exactly* the trivial `â„“آ¹` ceiling `â€–خ·_bâ€– â‰¤ n` (triangle inequality on
`n` unit terms), and that ceiling is REALISED. (ii) Turأ،n's power-sum method â€” the only "sparse-support"
tool that engages the coefficient LOCATIONS â€” bounds the sup from BELOW (`max_خ½|خ£ z_k^خ½| â‰¥ câˆڑn`), the
WRONG direction. The genuine UPPER bound on `خ£_{xâˆˆH}دˆ(x)` for a multiplicative subgroup `H` is precisely
the Bourgainâ€“Glibichukâ€“Konyagin / Heath-Brownâ€“Konyagin problem (`خ£دˆ â‰ھ p^{-خ²}|H|`), proven via the
sumâ€“product/Stepanov ARITHMETIC of `H` = the named BGK wall, open at the prize depth `n=2^خ¼â‰ˆp^{1/4}`,
`râˆ¼log q`.

PROBE (`scripts/probes/probe_dsar_sparsity.py`; proper `خ¼_n`, `n=2^خ¼`, `p` prime, `pâ‰«nآ³`):

| n | p | M | M/âˆڑn | M/âˆڑ(nآ·log q) | **arbitrary `n`-sparse (consecutive) max** |
|---|---|---|------|--------------|--------------------------------------------|
| 8 | 4129 | 7.56 | 2.67 | 1.07 | **8.00 = trivial n** |
| 16 | 65537 | 13.84 | 3.46 | 1.20 | **16.00 = trivial n** |
| 32 | 1048609 | 22.98 | 4.06 | 1.26 | **32.00 = trivial n** |

DECISIVE KILL (last column): a DIFFERENT `n`-sparse unit support (`n` consecutive frequencies) attains the
trivial value `n` at a genuine NONZERO frequency. So the subgroup `Mâ‰ˆCâˆڑ(nآ·log q) â‰ھ n` is a property of
*which* `n` frequencies (the multiplicative arithmetic), never of *how many* (the sparsity count). Any
inequality keyed only to the coefficient COUNT cannot distinguish `خ¼_n` from the consecutive set, hence
cannot certify `M<n`. (Same class as the [I027] PAPRâ†”merit and exhausted-moment findings: bounding `L^âˆ‍`
above from a count/`L^p` datum needs the sub-Gaussian flatness that IS the conjecture.)

LEAN BRICK (axiom-clean, `[propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound]`, no sorry/native_decide):
`Frontier/_AR_BernsteinMarkovSparsity.lean` â€” `eta_norm_le_card` (`â€–خ·_bâ€– â‰¤ |G|`, the â„“آ¹ ceiling),
`eta_zero_eq_card`/`eta_zero_norm_eq_card` (ceiling attained: `خ·_0 = |G|`), `no_subunital_sparsity_ceiling`
(no `خ¸<1` improvement holds for all configs). Signed DSAR-BMT. (The honest content of the sparsity route is
the â„“آ¹ ceiling; the escape is the BGK arithmetic, kept as the named Wick/Paley wall â€” no shortcut.)


IDEA: M(خ¼_n)/âˆڑn is the PAPR (peak-to-average power ratio) of the coset spectrum bâ†¦خ·_b
(خ·_b=خ£_{xâˆˆخ¼_n}e_p(bx), constant on cosets of F_p*/خ¼_n, an m-vector, m=(p-1)/n). Its "dual" is the
m-power-residue (Sidelnikov/generalized Legendre) sequence whose merit factor is KNOWN bounded
خک(1) (Schmidt/Jedwab, Legendre MFâ‰ˆ6). The proposed NEW LEMMA: a PAPRâ†”merit (L^âˆ‍â†”Lâپ´) duality plus
the bounded dual merit factor gives M/âˆڑn = O(âˆڑ(log m)) â€” an Lâپ´ phase-sensitive object the Lآ²
conservation law misses.

PROBES (prize-faithful: خ¼_n = 2-power subgroup n=2^خ¼, n|pâˆ’1, p PRIME, pâ‰«nآ³ (خ²â‰ˆ3.0â€“3.1), proper
m=(pâˆ’1)/n>1, NEVER n=pâˆ’1), nâˆˆ{8,â€¦,256}, scripts/probes/probe_papr_merit_duality_I027.py,
probe_papr_merit_directionality_I027b.py, probe_papr_merit_l4linf_gap_I027c.py:

THE LITERATURE IDENTITY (Jedwab merit-factor survey; Borweinâ€“Lockhart): for the Littlewood
polynomial p(z)=خ£ s_j z^j of a length-N sequence, â€–pâ€–â‚„âپ´ = Nآ²(1 + 1/F), F = merit factor. So
"bounded merit factor F=خک(1)" is EXACTLY "bounded Lâپ´/Lآ² ratio of the spectrum" â€” an Lâپ´ flatness
statement, nothing more. (Also note the cited خک(1) Legendre value â‰ˆ6 is the APERIODIC merit factor;
the object Fourier-dual to M is the PERIODIC power spectrum / 4th moment â€” a conflation, but
immaterial to the verdict below.)

(MEASUREMENT â€” the premise is TRUE, the conclusion is not deducible.) The coset spectrum IS
Lâپ´-flat: L4/L2 = 1.267, 1.291, 1.303, 1.310, 1.313, 1.325 at n=8â†’256 (4th-moment ratio
mean|خ·|âپ´/(mean|خ·|آ²)آ² â†’ ~3, i.e. periodic merit factor F=1/(دپâ‚„âˆ’1)â†’1/2, BOUNDED خک(1) â€” the idea's
premise holds numerically). L2(خ·)=âˆڑ(pâˆ’n)/âˆڑm â‰ˆ âˆڑn exactly (Parseval floor, as known). But Linf(خ·)=M
is NOT controlled: M/âˆڑn = 2.43, 2.73, 3.40, 3.98, 3.77, 5.15 â€” it GROWS, and (M/âˆڑn)/âˆڑ(log m) DRIFTS
UP 1.17,1.15,1.28,1.37,1.21,1.54 (does not stabilize at a constant). The only inequality the merit
factor (Lâپ´ flatness) actually licenses is Hأ¶lder in the WRONG direction: M â‰¤ m^{1/4}آ·L4 â‰ˆ âˆڑnآ·m^{1/4}
â‰ˆ âˆڑnآ·n^{(خ²âˆ’1)/4} â‰ˆ n^{1.75} at خ²=4 â€” WORSE than the trivial Mâ‰¤n. The "Holder m^.25*L4" column is
10.7, 22.2, 45.6, 93, 190 (â‰« the actual M of 6.9â€“46): vacuous.

(THE STRUCTURAL KILL â€” parameter-free, probe_â€¦I027c.py.) Hold the spectrum at the SAME bounded
merit factor the real خ· has (دپâ‚„=3, F=1/2, L4/L2=3^{1/4}=1.316) and ask the LARGEST L^âˆ‍ that bounded
merit ALLOWS over m entries with mean|آ·|آ²=n fixed: solve the spike-plus-flat extremal. Answer:
max Linf â‰ˆ (دپâ‚„âˆ’1)^{1/4}(mn)^{1/4} â‰ˆ nآ·m^{1/4} â‰ˆ n^{1.75}. The ratio (allowed Linf)/(target
âˆڑ(n log m)) GROWS as a power of n: 2.30, 3.32, 4.97, 7.62, 11.9, 18.7, â€¦, 6043 at n=2^20. So a
bounded merit factor is CONSISTENT with M as large as â‰ˆn^{1.75} â€” it does NOT imply, and cannot
imply, M=O(âˆڑ(n log m)). The single worst frequency (the spike = the open problem's argmax) is
invisible to the 4th moment: you can move all the mass into one spike of height n^{1.75} without
changing Lâپ´/Lآ² by more than O(1).

WHY (the crisp reason): merit factor â†” Lâپ´ flatness â†” 4th moment; M = L^âˆ‍. Bounding L^âˆ‍ ABOVE from Lâپ´
requires sub-Gaussian/L^âˆ‍â†”Lâپ´-equivalence of the spectrum (Salemâ€“Zygmund flatness), which is an
ADDITIONAL hypothesis equivalent to the conjecture itself, NOT a free consequence of bounded merit.
The âˆڑ(log m) loss the idea wants is exactly the sub-Gaussian-tail bound that Lâپ´ cannot certify. The
real خ· does happen to be far flatter in L^âˆ‍ than the worst Lâپ´-flat vector â€” but that flatness is a
fact about Gauss periods we are trying to PROVE; the duality merely re-states it. This is the SAME
class-4-average / L^âˆ‍-from-moments wall as [I025] (Levenshtein weighted moment) and the campaign's
exhausted-moment finding: even moments (the merit factor is the 2nd even moment of the spectrum)
cannot pin a sup. The PAPRâ†”merit relationship runs L^âˆ‍ â‰¥ (Lâپ´/m^{1/4}) â€” a LOWER bound on PAPR from
merit, the OPPOSITE direction from the prize.

VERDICT: NO-GAIN. The merit-factor premise (Lâپ´/Lآ² = خک(1), bounded dual merit) is genuinely TRUE for
خ¼_n and the cited literature transfers correctly, but it is an Lâپ´ statement that is structurally the
WRONG DIRECTION for an upper bound on the L^âˆ‍ quantity M: bounded merit factor is provably (probe
I027c) consistent with M up to â‰ˆn^{1.75}, so it gives no handle toward âˆڑnآ·polylog. CORE stays OPEN.
No Lean brick (the only provable statement that emerges is the Hأ¶lder inequality M â‰¤ m^{1/4}آ·L4,
vacuous; and the merit-factor identity â€–آ·â€–â‚„âپ´=Nآ²(1+1/F), which is the additive-4th-moment object
already in the energy substrate).

## 2026-06-15 â€” IDEA [I008] / C51 "Walsh/Haar-packet DYADIC-TOWER Stepanov auxiliary" NO-GAIN (probe_i008_walsh_dyadic_stepanov.py)

IDEA [I008] (stepanov-2adic, novelty 9): coordinatize x in mu_n (cyclic of order n=2^mu) by its
2-adic digit vector and build a Walsh-packet / dyadic-tower auxiliary
`Q(X) = prod_{i=1}^{mu} (X^{2^{i-1}} - s_i)` so that "vanishing to high order on mu_n is achieved by
TOWER TELESCOPING â€” each factor kills one dyadic level â€” order ~ mu at LINEAR degree, not n/r."
CLAIMED NEW LEMMA: exists Q, deg Q = O(n/log n), vanishing to ORDER >= log n on mu_n, giving via
Stepanov `|mu_n cap (shift)| <= O(n/(log n)^2)`, then summing cosets => `M(mu_n) <= sqrt(n log n)`
(past Johnson). Borrowed where-it-shows-up: Daubechies maximal-vanishing-moment two-scale filter
identities + Carlitz-Wan permutation polynomials.

VERDICT: **NO-GAIN.** The lemma is FALSE for the same reason the in-tree `mu_n_roots_simple`
(StepanovStructuredVacuous.lean) and C27 (Hasse-Davenport telescope) failed: the dyadic tower
TELESCOPES IN THE NUMBER OF ROOTS (across nested dyadic levels = DIFFERENT points), it does NOT
concentrate into VANISHING ORDER (multiplicity) at the points Stepanov must count. Stepanov consumes
`|Z| * M <= deg Q`, where M = the COMMON multiplicity (= min over the counted root set Z), NOT the
max at one designated point.

EXACT, CLOSED-FORM MECHANISM (verified vs sympy poly-division, `_sanity` True):
For x0 in mu_n (so x0 != 0) and p odd, every factor `(X^{2^{i-1}} - s_i)` has derivative
`2^{i-1} X^{2^{i-1}-1}` which is a UNIT at x0 (`2^{i-1}` is a unit mod p, x0 != 0). Hence x0 is a
SIMPLE root of factor i where it vanishes (`x0^{2^{i-1}} = s_i`) and not a root otherwise. Therefore
`mult_Q(x0) = #{ i : s_i = x0^{2^{i-1}} }` â€” a COUNT of factors, each contributing order EXACTLY 1.
To get common multiplicity m over Z roots you need each of the Z roots hit by >= m of the mu factors:
`m * Z <= sum_i (#roots of factor i in mu_n) = sum_i gcd(2^{i-1}, n) = sum_{i=1}^{mu} 2^{i-1} = n-1
= deg Q`. So `m * Z <= deg Q = n-1` is EXACTLY the trivial Stepanov inequality, with no saving.
The product buys NOTHING past the trivial degree bound.

DECISIVE PROBE (proper mu_n, p prime, p > n^3, p-1 div by n=2^mu, NEVER n=p-1; mu=3..7, beta=4):
- Forcing ALL mu factors to vanish at ONE point x0 (shifts `s_i = x0^{2^{i-1}}`) DOES give
  `mult(x0) = mu = log2 n` (the telescoping the idea wanted) â€” BUT this concentrates at a SINGLE
  point; the n/2 OTHER mu_n roots created are each SIMPLE (min-mult-over-roots = 1). Measured for
  mu=3..7: `mult(x0)=mu`, `#roots_on_mu_n=n/2`, `min-mult-over-roots=1`, `deg Q=n-1` uniformly.
- EXHAUSTIVE search over ALL shift vectors `s_i in mu_n` (mu=3: 8^3, mu=4: 16^4) via the closed-form
  multiplicity: BEST achievable COMMON multiplicity = **1** for every choice (lemma WANTS >= log n).
  Stepanov bound stays `|Z| <= deg Q / 1 = n-1` â€” the trivial bound, > n^{1/2}, never past Johnson.
- True `M(mu_n)` sanity (mu=3,4): exponent `log M / log n = 0.97, 0.95` (sitting at n^{1-o(1)}, the
  BGK SOTA the idea claimed to beat). The dyadic-tower auxiliary supplies no descent below it.

WHY THE WAVELET ANALOGY MISLEADS: Daubechies "vanishing moments" are vanishing of INTEGRALS
(sum_x x^k phi(x) = 0), an L^1/orthogonality condition â€” NOT vanishing to high ORDER (multiplicity)
of a polynomial at a point. The two-scale relation `phi(x)=sum c_k phi(2x-k)` is a self-similarity of
the FUNCTION, not a high-order root. Transporting "maximal vanishing moments" to "maximal root
multiplicity on mu_n" is the category error: separability of `X^n-1` (char p, p nmid n=2^mu) forbids
ANY polynomial from having order-2 contact on mu_n at sub-(M*n) degree. The 2-power / 2-adic
structure does not help â€” `gcd(2^{i-1}, 2^mu) = 2^{i-1}` makes the dyadic factors maximally
ROOT-RICH (good for spreading roots, the opposite of multiplicity).

MECHANISM CLASS: same horn as the entire Stepanov family on mu_n (C36 HBK REFUTED-FALSE;
StepanovStructuredVacuous `stepanov_collapses_to_degree`): mu_n manufactures NO multiplicity because
`X^n-1` is separable, so Stepanov collapses to the trivial `deg`-bound. A product/tower is structurally
blind in multiplicity exactly as it is blind in the conjugate-house (C27): it computes an AGGREGATE
(here, total incidence n-1 spread over roots), never a CONCENTRATION (multiplicity at one root) usable
by the inequality. NOT a new lever toward sqrt(n).

Probe committed: scripts/probes/probe_i008_walsh_dyadic_stepanov.py.

# Disproof Log â€” ABF26 Proximity Prize Grand Challenge 1 (Issue #232)

## 2026-06-15 â€” ANGLE A7 "Croot-Sisask almost-periodicity -> Bohr-set forcing of the worst u0" REFUTED (probe_a7_croot_sisask_bohr.py)

ANGLE (manifesto #7, route 30, relocation (C)/almost-periodicity): the far-line incidence
N(u0)=|Ball|+S(u0), S(u0)=Sum_{xi in D} K_w(wt xi) e_p(xi.u0), is the FT of a weighted measure on
the dual support D; Croot-Sisask almost-periodicity of the convolution 1_C * 1_Ball would give a
Bohr set B of offsets on which N(u0) is almost-constant. NEW LEMMA (invented, tested): if B is
large enough it forces the worst-case N(u0*) <= 2*avg = the floor, past Johnson. Faithful reduction
to the prize core: the period f(b)=Sum_{x in mu_n} e_p(bx)=FT(1_{mu_n}) is the simplest faithful
realization of S (worst u0 <-> worst b=argmax|f(b)|), so the Croot-Sisask program was tested ON f.

PROBE (prize-faithful: p PRIME, p=1 mod n, mu_n PROPER (n|p-1, (p-1)/n>1), p>>n^3 i.e. beta in {4,5,6},
NEVER n=p-1), n in {8,16,32,64}:
  PART 1 (the almost-period set). With eps=1 in ||f||_2=sqrt(np) units, the L2-almost-period set is
    EMPTY: |T_{1.0}|/p = 0.0000 at every n. The favorable (L2, not sup) reading of Croot-Sisask gives
    NO nontrivial almost-periods at the floor scale. The worst peak is DILATION-FIXED (|f(h b*)|=M),
    i.e. it sits in a rigid multiplicative orbit, not a translation-Bohr structure. M/floor (floor =
    sqrt(2 n ln(p/n))) = 0.756, 0.848, 0.891 -> 1 (the floor is real and approached, NOT 2*avg).
  PART 2 (peak vs Bohr set). On the Bohr-set translates b*+B (B from the large Fourier spectrum of
    1_{mu_n}), mean|f| is 1.235 (n=8, BELOW the bulk avg 2.005), 6.803 (n=16), 4.409 (n=32), 4.305
    (n=64) -- vs random+B = 2.005, 3.245, 4.672, 5.506. The peak is either ISOLATED (n=8: translates
    collapse below average -> the worst u0 ESCAPES the Bohr set) or a partial PLATEAU (n=16: b*+B ~2x
    random+B -> almost-periodicity PRESERVES the peak, the opposite of forcing it to the average).
    Neither produces N(u0*) <= 2*avg.
  PART 3 (THE KILL). The Croot-Sisask forcing target is M <= 2*avg. But M/(2*avg) GROWS MONOTONICALLY:
    1.653, 2.150, 2.538, 3.012 for n=8,16,32,64 (beta=4); and at FIXED n=16 it rises 2.150 -> 2.256
    as beta goes 4 -> 5 (p: 65537 -> 1048609). The ratio tracks sqrt(2 ln(p/n)) = the floor's excess
    factor exactly. Meanwhile avg|f|/sqrt(n) is FLAT at ~0.80 (Johnson scale), while M/sqrt(n) grows
    as sqrt(2 ln(p/n)). So the "2*avg" target is (i) only Johnson-scale even if achieved, and (ii)
    plainly FALSE here -- M exceeds 2*avg by precisely the sqrt(log) excess that separates the floor
    from Johnson.

VERDICT = REFUTED (the forcing fails on both horns; the surviving content reduces-to-wall, with a
Johnson-scale ceiling even in the best case). WHY (the conservation law, restated): Croot-Sisask
almost-periodicity is a SECOND-MOMENT / L2 statement -- the Bohr set is built from the large Fourier
spectrum and controls ||tau_t f - f||_2. Per the manifesto's Sec. I, the sqrt(log) excess is a
rare-event/tail phenomenon INVISIBLE to second moments. Concretely: (a) the L2 almost-period set is
empty at the floor scale (Part 1); (b) almost-periodicity, where it holds, makes the worst peak a
ROBUST PLATEAU (b*+B elevated, Part 2) -- it certifies the peak's stability, it does NOT push it down;
(c) the only bound the program could yield, 2*avg ~ 2 sqrt(n), is Johnson-scale, and the actual M
exceeds it by sqrt(2 ln(p/n)) (Part 3). The relocation to almost-periodicity is real but STERILE for
the sup: it reads the bulk (avg ~ sqrt(n) = Johnson) and is blind to the rare worst-b tail. Distinct
horn from A6 (which capped exactly at Johnson via trace structure): here the target is not merely
Johnson-capped but actively VIOLATED, and the violation IS the prize gap. Reduces ONLY to the
magnitude/sup question (the BGK/Paley wall); the secondary 2*avg ceiling is Johnson-scale. Probe
(committed): scripts/probes/probe_a7_croot_sisask_bohr.py.

## 2026-06-15 â€” ANGLE A6 "Schur-Siegel-Smyth trace problem on the Gaussian period polynomial" REDUCES-TO-JOHNSON (probe_a6_*.py)

Angle A6 (issue #444, manifesto angle 6, "truly novel, 0 in-tree"): relocate the cancellation
locus to the ALGEBRAIC-INTEGER TRACE. The `m=(p-1)/n` Gauss periods `eta_i` are the roots of the
integer period polynomial `Psi_p(T) in ZZ[T]`, deg `m`. NEW LEMMA tried: bound the largest period
`|eta_max|` via Schur-Siegel-Smyth trace-problem machinery (absolute trace + Newton/Schur power-sum
inequalities on `Psi_p`) to get `|eta_max| <= C sqrt(n)` past the Mann boundary, using ONLY the
integer structure of `Psi_p`, not the domain arithmetic.

**Exact identity established (load-bearing, verified):** `M(mu_n)=max_{b!=0}|Sum_{x in mu_n}e_p(bx)|
= max_i |eta_i|` â€” the prize sup-norm IS the largest absolute value of a Gaussian period of degree
`m`. (As `b` ranges over coset reps the period sum takes exactly the `m` values `eta_i`.) For `n`
even, `-1 in mu_n`, so the `eta_i` are REAL and `Psi_p` has `m` real roots â€” the clean
totally-real Schur-Siegel-Smyth setting. Parseval pins `Sum_i |eta_i|^2 = p-n`, so the RMS period
`= sqrt((p-n)n/(p-1)) -> sqrt(n)`: the second moment is RIGIDLY FIXED and gives only the trivial
`sqrt(n)` scale (no information), exactly the manifesto's "energy is blind" warning.

Verdict: **reduces-to-johnson** (periods too spread â€” the `âک…`-flagged A6 failure mode). Three
independent refutations, all on proper subgroups `n=2^mu`, `n|p-1`, `p` PRIME, `p>>n^3`,
`NEVER n=p-1`:

1. **Low-order integer-trace invariants are VACUOUS.** The only cheap integer invariants are
   `P_1=Sum eta_i=-1` (the trace) and `P_2=Sum eta_i^2=p-n`. The maximum root consistent with
   just `P_1,P_2` and `m` real roots is `~sqrt((m-1)/m)*sqrt(P_2) ~ sqrt(p)` â€” ENORMOUSLY larger
   than the true `M~sqrt(n log)`. Measured `M/permitted ~ 0.05->0.01` and shrinking with `n`
   (PROBE `probe_a6_powersum_diagnostic.py`). The absolute-trace structure does NOT cap the max
   anywhere near `sqrt(n)`. Higher power sums are rigid `p,n`-determined Jacobi-sum/cyclotomic
   quantities: `P_3=-n^2`, `P_5=-n^3` (tiny odd sums), even sums `~p^{k/...}` â€” computing them is
   recomputing the domain.

2. **The truncated moment-LP (= best-possible SSS bound from `P_1..P_K`, any finite `K`) is loose
   at every `K`.** The largest support point of any measure matching the first `K` integer power
   sums (Hankel pencil upper edge) grows with `K`: `K=2 -> sqrt(n)`, `K=4 -> 1.68 sqrt(n)`,
   `K=6 -> 2.2 sqrt(n)`, ... reaching the true `M~4.6 sqrt(n)` only as `K->m`. So the
   Schur-auxiliary-polynomial machinery needs ALL `~m` power sums = the full polynomial = the
   domain content again â€” the moment-ladder horn (PROBE `probe_a6_sss_applicability.py`). SSS
   proper, moreover, LOWER-bounds the AVERAGE trace of TOTALLY-POSITIVE integers; it cannot
   UPPER-bound an `L^infinity` max, and the periods aren't totally positive (trace `-1`).

3. **The decisive growth law: the saturated normalized edge `E_n := lim_{p->inf} M(mu_n)/sqrt(n)`
   EXISTS but grows like `sqrt(log n)`.** At fixed `n` the normalized period measure converges in
   moments to a `p`-independent limit (`L^{2k}` norm columns flat in `p` across decades â€” PROBE
   `probe_a6_even_moment_growth.py`), and `E_n(p)` saturates (n=8: `2.766->2.827`, increments
   `+.033,+.019,+.005,+.003->0`; PROBE `probe_a6_fixedn_limit.py`). But the saturated `E_n` GROWS
   with `n`: at fixed honest ratio `p/n^3=300`, `E_n = 2.00,2.80,3.74,4.29,5.11,5.11` for
   `n=4,8,16,32,64,128`; **const fit `R^2=0.00`, `sqrt(log n)` fit `R^2=0.98` (slope `3.26`)**
   (PROBE `probe_a6_horn_confirm.py`; large-`n` rows undersaturated => true growth `>=` measured,
   so robust). Hence in the prize regime where `n,p` co-grow (`log(p/n) ~ log n`), the limiting
   edge `E_n ~ sqrt(log(p/n))` gives `M ~ sqrt(n log(p/n))` = the Johnson scale.

**Constraint lemma (why the locus does not move):** the integer structure of `Psi_p` exposes only
the symmetric functions of the periods (power sums = traces). The first two are Parseval-rigid
(`= -1`, `= p-n`) and give exactly `sqrt(n)`; every higher one is a `p,n`-determined cyclotomic
number whose computation reconstructs the domain. The genuinely new fact â€” that `lim_p M/sqrt(n)`
exists as a fixed limiting-measure edge `E_n` â€” does not crack the wall because `E_n` itself
carries the `sqrt(log)` excess (the periods ARE spread on the Johnson scale). The trace problem is
an averaging machine; `M` is an `L^infinity` tail event, invisible to any bounded set of traces.
This is DISTINCT from the moment ladder (#W?) and Mann/antipodal only in framing â€” mathematically
it lands on the same Johnson boundary.

Probes: `scripts/probes/probe_a6_schur_siegel_smyth.py`, `probe_a6_powersum_diagnostic.py`,
`probe_a6_sss_applicability.py`, `probe_a6_even_moment_growth.py`, `probe_a6_edge_growth_verdict.py`,
`probe_a6_fixedn_limit.py`, `probe_a6_horn_confirm.py` (committed on branch main).


## 2026-06-15 â€” CONJECTURE C13 "Vertical Sato-Tate Sup-Control via Katz Uniform Equidistribution Rate" SECRETLY-OPEN / REDUCES-TO-JOHNSON (probe_c13_vertical_satotate_torus_dim.py)

Conjecture C13 (issue #444, algebraic-geometry, feasibility 1): claim the effective discrepancy of
the Gauss-sum-family equidistribution, against trig-polynomial test functions of degree
`â‰¤ log(p/n)` and RESTRICTED to a `خ¼`-dimensional dyadic sub-torus, is `D = خ¼آ·2^خ¼/âˆڑq â‰ھ 1`, forcing
`max_b |خ·_b| â‰¤ âˆڑ(2n log(p/n))` past Johnson. **THE EXPLICIT BET: "the relevant torus has dimension
`خ¼` rather than `f`."** REDUCES-TO: Katz Satoâ€“Tate (large monodromy âں¹ equidistribution) + Rojas-Leأ³n
Cor 7 (only Hasseâ€“Davenport âں¹ `GL(1)^f`) + Deligne Weil II effective form + in-tree
`GeneralizedPaleyRamanujan` reduction.

Verdict: **secretly-open** (degenerating to **reduces-to-johnson** on the only effective reading).
The conjecture's `D â‰ھ 1` premise â€” the sole thing that derives the `âˆڑ(2n log(p/n))` bound â€” rests
entirely on the FALSE dimension bet. Probe `scripts/probes/probe_c13_vertical_satotate_torus_dim.py`,
commit `2690fef9cc80cc17f3be5a314a3aaeeec5e187a4`. All measured prize-faithfully (proper `خ¼_n`,
`n=2^خ¼`, `p` PRIME, `n|pâˆ’1`, `pâ‰«nآ³`, `m=(pâˆ’1)/n>1`, NEVER `n=pâˆ’1`).

HORN-DIM (the load-bearing horn â€” the torus dimension is `f=(pâˆ’1)/n`, not `خ¼`). The Rojas-Leأ³n
equidistribution monodromy is `GL(1)^f` where `f` = the number of monomial Gauss sums in the family
= the INDEX `m=(pâˆ’1)/n ~ p/n`. The DFT identity `خ·_c = âˆ’1/m + (1/m)خ£_{j=1}^{mâˆ’1} د„(د‡^j) e(âˆ’jc/m)`
writes the period as a length-`(mâˆ’1)` LINEAR FORM in the `f=mâˆ’1` INDEPENDENT Gauss sums `د„(د‡^j)`, so
the equidistribution lives on a torus of dimension `f=mâˆ’1 ~ p/n`, and the honest discrepancy is
`f/âˆڑq ~ âˆڑp/n`. At PRIZE scale (`q=p~nآ·2^128`, `n<p^{1/4}`):

| خ¼ | n=2^خ¼ | logâ‚‚ p | خ²=log_n p | `D_خ¼=خ¼آ·2^خ¼/âˆڑq` (C13's bet) | `D_f=f/âˆڑq` (TRUTH) |
|---|-------|--------|-----------|----------------------------|---------------------|
| 16 | 2^16 | 144 | 9.00 | 2.2eâˆ’16 | 2^56 |
| 20 | 2^20 | 148 | 7.40 | 1.1eâˆ’15 | 2^54 |
| 30 | 2^30 | 158 | 5.27 | 5.3eâˆ’14 | 2^49 |
| 35 | 2^35 | 163 | 4.66 | 3.5eâˆ’13 | 2^46.5 |

`D_خ¼ â‰ھ 1` ONLY because of the bet; with the correct `f`, `D_f = 2^46..2^56 â‰« 1` â€” CATASTROPHICALLY
VACUOUS. A `خ¼`-dimensional "sub-torus" is just `خ¼` of the `mâˆ’1` Gauss-sum coordinates; restricting
the test functions to it simply DROPS the other `mâˆ’1âˆ’خ¼` coordinates and proves nothing about the
full period `خ·_c`. This is precisely the recorded large-sieve / Katz dimension obstruction
(`deltastar-407-large-sieve-dimension-obstruction-2026-06-13.md`, `â€¦katz-monodromy-researchâ€¦`,

`f â‰¤ âˆڑq âں؛ n â‰³ âˆڑp`; the prize has `n â‰ھ p^{1/4} â‰ھ âˆڑp` by construction.

HORN-EFF (even granting dimension `f`, equidistribution is `qâ†’âˆ‍`, not the deterministic max). Katz
Satoâ€“Tate / Deligne Weil II is a distributional limit as `qâ†’âˆ‍` for a FIXED sheaf; it gives the
TYPICAL (average) value, never the deterministic `max_b` over a thin DESIGNED subgroup at fixed
large `q`. Measured `M(n)=max_b|خ·_b|` tracks the random/BGK value `âˆڑ(n log(p/n))` within O(1) and
DRIFTS (no fixed effective constant), the random signature â€” the same effectivity barrier already
recorded for C14, C29, and the Katz/Rojas-Leأ³n route:

| n | p | p/nآ³ | m=(pâˆ’1)/n | M(n) | âˆڑ(2n log(p/n)) | M/that | âˆڑ(n log(p/n)) | M/that |
|---|---|------|-----------|------|----------------|--------|---------------|--------|
| 8  | 11593    | 22.6 | 1449   | 7.676  | 10.792 | 0.711 | 7.631  | 1.006 |
| 16 | 262193   | 64.0 | 16387  | 14.215 | 17.622 | 0.807 | 12.461 | 1.141 |
| 32 | 1048609  | 32.0 | 32769  | 22.983 | 25.796 | 0.891 | 18.240 | 1.260 |
| 64 | 16777601 | 64.0 | 262150 | 38.529 | 39.963 | 0.964 | 28.258 | 1.363 |

`M(n)/âˆڑn` GROWS `2.71 â†’ 3.55 â†’ 4.06 â†’ 4.82` (n=8â†’64), tracking `âˆڑ(log(p/n))` â€” the random Gaussian
large-deviation law, NOT a fixed Satoâ€“Tate semicircle edge (which would force `M/âˆڑn â†’ 2`).

HORN-TARGET (moot but decisive on closure). The claimed conclusion `âˆڑ(2n log(p/n))` IS the open
