## 2026-06-15 â€” CONJECTURE C48 "Effective Sauermann-Wigderson Polynomial-Method Rank Bound for the Agreement System" REDUCES-TO-JOHNSON (probe_c48_sauermann_wigderson_rank.py)

Conjecture C48 (issue #444, algebraic-geometry, feasibility 2): claim the Sauermannâ€“Wigderson
polynomial-method rank bound, applied to the simultaneous far-line agreement system over `F_p`,
gives a list bound below budget `n` past Johnson, exploiting the bounded degree of the agreement
variety and the proven rigidity transfer (in-tree `RigidityReductionPrizeScale`). "A modern
polynomial-method lever distinct from CLP." REDUCES-TO: Sauermannâ€“Wigderson polynomial-method rank
bounds + in-tree `RigidityReductionPrizeScale` + bounded-degree agreement variety.

**Verdict: reduces-to-johnson** (with a dimensional category error in even importing the SW
formula). Probe `scripts/probes/probe_c48_sauermann_wigderson_rank.py`. Probed over proper `خ¼_n`,
`p` prime `pâ‰ˆn^4` (`pâ‰«nآ³`, never `n=pâˆ’1`), `nâˆˆ{16,32,64}` and the prize-band cap arithmetic out to
`n=1024`. Three horns, each independently fatal.

**What Sauermannâ€“Wigderson actually is (arXiv:2010.00077, JLMS 2022).** For fixed `kâ‰¥2` and `n`
large, the *minimum possible degree* of a polynomial `Pâˆˆâ„‌[x_1,â€¦,x_n]` with `P(0)â‰ 0` vanishing to
multiplicity `â‰¥k` at every point of the **Boolean hypercube** `{0,1}^n \ {0}` equals `n+2k-3`
(`k=1` is Alonâ€“Fأ¼redi: degree `n`). It is an **extremal degree LOWER bound** on multiplicity-`k`
vanishing over the **`n`-dimensional Boolean grid** `{0,1}^n`, over `â„‌` (char 0). It is *not* a
list-size / incidence upper bound and *not* an algebraic-variety rank statement.

**(H1) Dimensional category error (the C39 H2 mechanism again).** SW lives in `{0,1}^n`, an
`n`-fold product; its content `n+2k-3` is a phenomenon of the GROWING number of grid coordinates
(`nâ†’âˆ‍`). The prize agreement "variety" `B = {خ±âˆˆF_p : x^a+خ±x^b is خ´-close to RS[k]}` is a
**1-parameter family in a cyclic group** â€” the eval domain `خ¼_n` is a single-coordinate point set
on a line (dimension 1), with NO `n`-fold Boolean product to host the SW formula. This is exactly
the category error already recorded for C39 ("cap-bound-in-`F_q^N` vs cap-bound-in-`â„¤/p`"): the
polynomial-method dimensional saving lives in the ambient product dimension and is vacuous in a
1-dimensional cyclic ambient. Importing `n+2k-3` here is meaningless.

**(H2) Wrong direction / wrong quantity; it only re-derives the `kâˆ’1` ceiling.** SW is a degree
LOWER bound ("to vanish to order `k` off the origin you NEED degree `â‰¥n+2k-3`"). To convert a
vanishing-degree fact into a list-SIZE UPPER bound you run it through Alonâ€“Fأ¼redi /
Schwartzâ€“Zippel / Vandermonde dimension counting. Probed on explicit `RS[F_p,خ¼_n,k]`
(`nâˆˆ{16,32}`, `kâˆˆ{n/8,n/4}`, 780 polynomial pairs each): the maximum pairwise agreement of
distinct deg-`<k` codewords on `خ¼_n` is `â‰¤ kâˆ’1` in EVERY case (measured `maxAgree=0` for random
pairs; the structural ceiling is `kâˆ’1`). That ceiling is *exactly* the in-tree Vandermonde rank
obstruction `rank_collapse_on_kset` / `poly_method_subset_incidence_bound` (`ListIncidencePolyMethod.lean`)
and the multiplicity double-count `card_le_choose_div_choose_of_pairwise_inter` (`FisherPastJohnsonCap.lean`).
SW's multiplicity refinement RE-DERIVES, it does not lower, `agree(c,c')â‰¤kâˆ’1`.

**(H3) The optimal poly-method cap is field-blind and super-polynomial past Johnson; rigidity
transfer does not help.** The in-tree axiom-clean cap `|L|آ·C(a,k) â‰¤ C(n,k)` IS the best output of
any polynomial method that uses only the `kâˆ’1` agreement ceiling (it is precisely the
Sauermannâ€“Wigderson-style multiplicity double count). Probed at prize-band rates `دپâˆˆ{1/4,1/8}`
just past the Johnson agreement radius `aâ‰ˆâŒˆâˆڑ(nk)âŒ‰+1`: the cap `C(n,k)/C(a,k)` is `432` at `n=32`,
`4.0آ·10آ²آ³` at `n=256`, `3.7آ·10âپ¹âپµ` at `n=1024` â€” astronomically above budget `n` at every interior
point. By `abstract_incidence_bound` the cap is FIELD-BLIND (identical for a generic injective
domain), so it cannot exploit `خ¼_n`'s multiplicative smoothness. And `RigidityReductionPrizeScale`
only makes `خ´*` **char-INDEPENDENT** (`I_p = I_0`, char-`p` incidence equals char-`0` incidence at
prize scale) â€” it does NOT shrink the char-`0` cap. So feeding SW the proven rigidity transfer
buys nothing past Johnson: SW + rigidity still lands exactly on the field-blind `kâˆ’1` Johnson wall.

**Conclusion.** C48 is the Sauermannâ€“Wigderson sibling of C39 (CLP/EG). SW is a Boolean-hypercube
minimum-degree theorem with no cyclic analogue (H1), it produces a degree lower bound that only
re-derives the proven Vandermonde `kâˆ’1` ceiling (H2), and the resulting cap is the in-tree
field-blind super-polynomial incidence bound that is the converged Johnson wall (H3). The
past-Johnson lever is the open BGK / smooth-domain incidence (multiplicity) count, which SW does
not supply. The "bounded degree of the agreement variety" is real but is precisely what gives the
`kâˆ’1` ceiling and nothing past it. Reduces-to-johnson; confidence high.

## 2026-06-15 â€” CONJECTURE C46 "Mahler-Measure / Lehmer Structure-Aware Norm on the Period Polynomial" SECRETLY-OPEN (probe_conjecture_refute_c46.py, commit ad99a21774)

Conjecture C46 (issue #444, gauss-period-exact, feasibility 2): a "structure-aware Mahler measure"
of the period polynomial `خ¨_p(T)=âˆڈ_{cosets}(Tâˆ’خ·_c)` (weighting by the antipodal cyclotomic
structure) gives a SHARPER house bound than Cauchy, reaching `house=M(خ¼_n) â‰¤ âˆڑ(2n log p)` PAST
Johnson via the proven Myerson coefficient structure + in-tree antipodal pair `z+(âˆ’z)=0`.
REDUCES-TO: Mahler measure / Lehmer house inequality + Myerson coefficients + antipodal structure.
**Verdict: secretly-open** â€” it hides the open BGK/Paley sup-norm. The fourth distinct probe to
hit the **houseâ‰ symmetric-functional archimedean gap** (same wall as C08-Myerson, C26-Stickelberger,
C27-HasseDavenport, C9). Already pre-flagged as a wall-risk in `docs/kb/deltastar-100-attacks/README.md`
lines 281/529-533 ("Mahler-measure 'house' framing is elegant but the m periods are NOT equimodular
... real content = |conjugate| equidistribution = the Sato-Tate/Paley wall; reduces to the open core").

LOAD-BEARING FACT (in-tree): `house(خ·)=max_c|خ·_c|=max_{aâ‰ 0}|خ£_{xâˆˆخ¼_n}e_p(ax)|=M(خ¼_n)` = THE OPEN
OBJECT (single-conjugate Lâˆ‍). Mahler measure `Mah(خ¨)=âˆڈ_{|خ·_c|>1}|خ·_c|` is a SYMMETRIC PRODUCT over
the `m=(pâˆ’1)/n` conjugates. The ONLY honest Mahler/Lehmer house inequalities are (a) `house â‰¤ Mah(خ¨)`
(trivial, = product, exponentially-in-m too weak) and (b) `house â‰¥ Mah^{1/#big}` (geometric mean of
the big roots, a LOWER bound). C46 needs a sharp UPPER bound = the wrong direction for a product.

DECISIVE PROBE (proper خ¼_n, n=2^خ¼ خ¼=3..5, p prime, p~nâپ´â‰«nآ³, NEVER n=pâˆ’1, 4 primes/n):
- **T1 target shape REAL:** house=M(خ¼_n)=7.56, 13.84, 22.98 (n=8,16,32) â‰ˆ âˆڑ(n log p) below trivial n.
- **T2/T5 Mahler is exponentially-in-m too weak as an upper bound:** `log10(Mah)=158, 1710, 17683`
  (grows LINEARLY in m=516, 4096, 32769) â†’ `log10(Mah/house)=157, 1708, 17682`. "house â‰¤ Mah(خ¨)" is
  astronomically weaker than the target `âˆڑ(2n log p)=11.5, 18.8, 29.8`. The Myerson/Cauchy coefficient
  route is the SAME symmetric functions, already shown 10آ²â€“10آ³ too weak in C08 (rerun confirmed).
- **T3 antipodal weighting halves the exponent AT MOST:** خ·_{âˆ’c}=conj(خ·_c) (âˆ’1âˆˆخ¼_n) gives equal-
  modulus pairs, so a fully antipodal-aware Mahler is `Mah^{1/2}` = a product over m/2 roots, still
  `log10â‰ˆ79, 855, 8841` â‰« target. Collapsing productâ†’max is NOT what the antipodal relation does.
- **T4 DECISIVE â€” Mahler does NOT determine house:** at FIXED n, house spread = 0.10, 0.64, 1.14 is
  DECOUPLED from log10(Mah) spread = 2.65, 3.60, 91.55. A symmetric product cannot pin the single
  largest conjugate. `Mah^{1/#big}=2.6, 3.3, 4.2 â‰ˆ âˆڑn` (the geometric mean) = the Lآ²/JOHNSON scale.

The `âˆڑ(log p)` prize factor IS the conjugate-argument SPREAD `{arg خ·_c}` = the open BGK/Paley sup-
norm (best PROVEN `n^{1âˆ’o(1)}` BGK ineffective; di Benedetto `n^{0.989}` only `n>p^{1/4}`, outside the
prize). The in-tree `L2MahlerNormBound.lean` already proves the strongest unconditional Mahler/AM-GM
norm bound, and its `l2_dominates_house` theorem makes the direction explicit: Lآ²/Mahler is a LOWER
bound on the house. No coefficient weighting converts the symmetric product into the Lâˆ‍ house.
Probe: scripts/probes/probe_conjecture_refute_c46.py, commit ad99a21774. Same wall as C08/C26/C27/C9.

## 2026-06-15 â€” CONJECTURE C47 "Cocycle Phase-Alignment Multi-Level for the Gauss-Sum Arguments" SECRETLY-OPEN / REDUCES-TO-JOHNSON (probe_conjecture_refute_C47_cocycle_phase.py, commit abec15e6e)

Conjecture C47 (issue #444, gauss-period-exact, feasibility 2): a multi-level cocycle/coboundary
decomposition of the Gauss-sum ARGUMENTS `arg د„(د‡_j)` across the dyadic Hasseâ€“Davenport tower forces
PHASE NON-ALIGNMENT, bounding the period sup-norm `M(خ¼_n) = max_{bâ‰ 0}|خ£_{xâˆˆخ¼_n} e_p(bx)|` by
`âˆڑ(n log p)` PAST Johnson; it "attacks the archimedean phase distribution directly." REDUCES-TO:
cocycle/coboundary phase decomposition + Hasseâ€“Davenport tower + in-tree antipodal structure.

**Verdict: secretly-open** (the only past-Johnson lever is the open archimedean argument
distribution), **degenerating to reduces-to-johnson** (the cocycle/coboundary algebra is exact but
pins only the symmetric/Lآ² = geometric-mean = Johnson `âˆڑn` scale). C47 is the ADDITIVE/argument-side
twin of C27 (multiplicative HD/Jacobi telescope, refuted above) and C28 (Grossâ€“Koblitz period);
all three are governed by the same in-tree no-go bricks `_DyadicJacobiCocycleNonContraction.lean`
(unimodular cocycle âں¹ no cancellation), `_GrossKoblitzPhaseNoGo.lean` (the `خ“_p` unit part / any
reflection relation transmits at most a discrete sign, pinning a phase only at index 2), and
`_PhaseAlignmentReality.lean` (the only "alignment" the dyadic structure forces is reality of `خ·_b`,
which holds for ALL `b` in thick AND thin regimes â€” not thinness-essential). Probe over PROPER `خ¼_n`
(`n=2^خ¼`), `p` PRIME, `p>nآ³`, NEVER `n=pâˆ’1`, with EXACT Gauss sums for the cocycle test.

**Horn 1 â€” the cocycle "decomposition" is EXACTLY the Jacobi relation taking arguments; it holds
trivially and is vacuous (existence â‰  bound).** The named object is precise: taking arguments in the
Jacobi relation `د„(د‡_j)د„(د‡_k) = J(د‡_j,د‡_k)آ·د„(د‡_{j+k})` gives, with `a_j := arg د„(د‡_j)`,
`c_{j,k} := arg J(د‡_j,د‡_k)`, the identity `a_j + a_k âˆ’ a_{j+k} = c_{j,k}` (mod 2د€) â€” i.e. the
argument family `(a_j)` is a 1-cochain on the cyclic dual `â„¤/m` (`m=(pâˆ’1)/n`) whose coboundary
`خ´a` equals the Jacobi-argument 2-cochain `c`. The probe verifies this **EXACTLY**: `cocycle_err =
0.00e+00` at `خ¼=2` (`p=73,m=18`) and `خ¼=3` (`p=521,m=65`). So the "multi-level cocycle/coboundary
decomposition" is just the Jacobi relation and always exists. It is a tautology, not a bound. (This
is the in-tree `_DyadicJacobiCocycleNonContraction` fact: the cocycle factor `c_{j,k}=J_{j,k}/âˆڑp` is
**unimodular** `|c|=1`, so it imposes NO magnitude constraint beyond unit modulus and is consistent
with a fully-aligned `â€–Pâ€–=m` saturating configuration â€” `exists_unimodular_cocycle_saturating_triangle`.)

**Horn 2 â€” DECISIVE: the residual phase is SPREAD, not LINEAR. C47 needs "non-alignment âں¹ a single
spike"; the data gives the opposite â€” full equidistribution = the random âˆڑ(n log p) value.** C47's
hope is that `c=arg J` being a coboundary on cyclic `â„¤/m` makes `arg د„(د‡_j) =` (linear in `j`) +
(coboundary), a RIGID phase law whose DFT is one spike (height `m` at one frequency, near-zero
elsewhere âں¹ sup-cancellation past Johnson). The probe measures the DFT of the unit-phase family
`e^{i a_j}` directly (= the period itself): the off-spike-to-spike ratio is `dft_spread = 0.7735`
(`خ¼=2`) and `0.9507` (`خ¼=3`) â€” the phases are spread across essentially ALL frequencies, NOT a single
linear spike. The د‡آ² uniformity statistic (`0.22 < 9.49` crit; `16.48 < 24.0` crit) confirms the
`arg د„` family is equidistributed on the circle. So the cocycle/coboundary solve recovers the
EQUIDISTRIBUTED argument distribution, whose sup over `b` is the random extreme-value `âˆڑ(n log p)` â€”
which is the OPEN bound itself, not a proof of it. (This matches Heath-Brownâ€“Patterson cubic/Kummer
Gauss-sum argument equidistribution and the in-tree `_GrossKoblitzPhaseNoGo` finding that for `mâ‰¥3`
the phase is "the wild Kummer quantity, not a residue formula" and equidistributes.)

**Horn 3 â€” the cocycle transfers NO cancellation: the floor sits at/above the random-phase value.**
The reconstructed sup `M=house` tracks `âˆڑ(n log p)` to within 7%: `M/âˆڑ(n log p) âˆˆ [0.93, 0.99]` over
12 prize-thin primes (`p/nآ³âˆˆ[1.00,1.16]`), with one row at `1.301` (n=32, p=32993) where the REAL
floor EXCEEDS the random value (100th percentile of the null). A coboundary is a per-frequency phase
shift; phase shifts cannot lower an L^âˆ‍ sup. The cocycle is consistent with zero cancellation
(`flat_target_below_cocycle_saturation`): the only bound the unimodular cocycle forces is the trivial
triangle bound `â‰¤ m`, and the flat target `âˆڑ(n log p) â‰ھ m` is strictly stronger and NOT implied.

**Why secretly-open (the hidden open step).** Solving the coboundary equation `خ´a = arg J` for the
1-cochain `a` does NOT pin `a`: the solution is unique only up to a HOMOMORPHISM `â„¤/m â†’ â„‌/2د€â„¤` (the
linear phase `خ»j` = the cohomology/homomorphism freedom). That freedom â€” the genuine archimedean
argument of `د„` modulo the magnitude â€” IS the open object: its distribution is the Kummer/cubic-and-up
Gauss-sum argument equidistribution, and the sup over `b` of the resulting period is the BGK/Paley
sup-norm `M(خ¼_n) â‰¤ Câˆڑ(n log(p/n))`, the recognized ~25-year-open analytic-NT core. The cocycle fixes
the SYMMETRIC part (`a_j + a_k` data â†” the magnitude `|د„|=âˆڑq` â†” the norm `N=âˆڈخ·_c` â†” the Lآ²
geometric-mean `â‰ˆâˆڑn` = the JOHNSON scale, exactly C27's P1) and leaves the antisymmetric/homomorphism
phase (the conjugate spread = the `âˆڑ(log p)` prize factor) UNDETERMINED.

**Why not refuted-false:** the cocycle identity is genuinely true (it is the Jacobi relation), and the
true `M(خ¼_n) â‰ˆ âˆڑ(n log p)` IS the conjectured bound â€” C47 states the right value but the MECHANISM
(cocycle algebra) cannot prove it; it is exact-but-empty for the sup. Hence secretly-open, not false.

**Why not survives:** the past-Johnson content is entirely in the homomorphism-freedom/argument
distribution, which is the open BGK core under the route-elimination meta-theorem (no closed/
p-independent/cocycle object reaches past Johnson; the cocycle is p-independent algebra and caps at
the symmetric/Lآ²/Johnson scale).

**Mechanism class.** Archimedean-argument sibling of the C27 multiplicative-telescope horn: a product
(C27) computes a symmetric function (norm) blind to which conjugate is largest; a cocycle/coboundary
(C47) computes the symmetric argument relations blind to the homomorphism phase = which frequency
aligns. Both reach the `âˆڑn` geometric-mean/Johnson scale; both leave the archimedean conjugate-spread
`âˆڑ(log p)` = BGK open. Probe: `scripts/probes/probe_conjecture_refute_C47_cocycle_phase.py`, commit
`abec15e6e36a47da95a4125863bd5c14f6def9ac`. In-tree no-go bricks (axiom-clean): `_DyadicJacobiCocycleNonContraction.lean`, `_GrossKoblitzPhaseNoGo.lean`, `_PhaseAlignmentReality.lean`. Confidence high.

## 2026-06-15 â€” CONJECTURE C40 "BCH/RS Weight-Distribution List Bound via MacWilliams Duality" REDUCES-TO-JOHNSON (probe_c40_macwilliams_bch_weightdist.py)

Conjecture C40 (issue #444, gmmds-homds, feasibility 2): the dual (BCH) weight distribution of
RS[k] over خ¼_n, computed via MacWilliams + the proven antipodal/cyclotomic-coset structure, bounds
the number of low-weight dual words and hence the far-line list size below budget PAST Johnson.
REDUCES-TO: MacWilliams identity + BCH weight-distribution moments + in-tree cyclotomic-coset
structure + L3 orbit-count.

**Verdict: reduces-to-johnson** (with a secretly-open undertone on the only past-Johnson lever).
The MacWilliams / weight-distribution object is exactly the second-moment functional already in the
tree â€” domain-INDEPENDENT, its list bound is the Johnson/annulus bound, and it is provably blind to
the structure خ´* depends on. Probe `scripts/probes/probe_c40_macwilliams_bch_weightdist.py` (exact
integer arithmetic mod prime p, proper subgroup خ¼_n, p > nآ³, NEVER the full group): all four
predictions pass.

**Horn 1 â€” the weight distribution is a DOMAIN-INDEPENDENT functional (it forgets خ¼_n).** A weight
distribution `A_w = #{c : wt(c)=w}` is a permutation-invariant functional of the code; MacWilliams is
the exact Krawtchouk transform `A^âٹ¥ = (1/|C|)آ·Kآ·A` between `C` and `C^âٹ¥`. RS[k] over ANY n-subset of
F_p is MDS (in-tree `reedSolomonFrame_isMDS`; O109 CENSUS 1 confirms the empirical weight dist matches
the classical MDS closed form `A_w = C(n,w)آ·خ£_j(âˆ’1)^j C(w,j)(q^{wâˆ’d+1âˆ’j}âˆ’1)` at every tested instance).
- **P1 (probe, empirical at small q):** `A_w(خ¼_n) == A_w(random subset) == A_w(arithmetic-progression
  domain) == MDS formula` â€” IDENTICAL at every tested (n,k,q) (n=4,6; q=13,17; proper subgroups).
- **P2 (probe):** the MacWilliams transform of A[RS[k]] equals A[RS[nâˆ’k]] (the dual GRS is again MDS),
  i.e. the "dual BCH weight distribution" is itself the SAME fixed MDS form â€” domain-blind. The
  antipodal/cyclotomic-coset structure lives in the DOMAIN, which the weight distribution has already
  forgotten; nothing about خ¼_n survives the transform that was not already in (n,k,q). Verified
  nâˆˆ{8,12,16}, k=4, p>nآ³.

âں¹ The entire MacWilliams/BCH-weight-distribution object depends only on (n,k,q). But خ´* is provably
DOMAIN-DEPENDENT past Johnson (خ¼_n is the worst case; BKR10 / BCHKS Thm 1.13). A functional that is
identical for خ¼_n and for a random domain cannot pin the worst-case-over-خ¼_n threshold.

**Horn 2 â€” its list bound IS the second-moment / Johnson bound, vacuous past Johnson.** The only list
bound a symmetric weight functional yields is the "many low-weight codewords in a ball" / annulus
count = the second moment. This is exactly the in-tree object: `AgreementMomentTwo.sum_agreement_
spectrum_sq` proves M2 "enters through the weight enumerator alone, which MDS pins" â€” the variance is
weight-distribution-determined and domain-independent. And `CandidateBridgeLoop16` proves its wall:
`second_moment_fails_in_band` / `sq_gt_iff_large_gap` â€” the second-moment denominator `aآ²âˆ’nآ·b`
(`a=(1âˆ’خ´)n`, `b=دپn`) is `> 0` iff `(1âˆ’خ´)آ²>دپ` iff `خ´ < 1âˆ’âˆڑدپ` = Johnson, and `â‰¤ 0` (no bound) strictly
past Johnson.
- **P3 (probe):** for n=16, kâˆˆ{2,4,8}, the denominator is `>0` at `خ´_Jâˆ’0.01` and `â‰¤0` at `خ´_J+0.01`
  â€” the bound is silent over the ENTIRE window interior `(1âˆ’âˆڑدپ, 1âˆ’دپ)` it is supposed to control.

**Horn 3 â€” the real far-line list is domain-DEPENDENT, invisible to the functional (SECRETLY-OPEN
undertone).** The far-line incidence `I(خ´)` (the prize object) is NOT a permutation-invariant
functional of the weight distribution: a far line `x^a+خ± x^b` close to RS[k] is a low-degree-pencil
object whose خ±-count obeys the orbit-count law L3 (`I = Nآ·n/gcd(bâˆ’a,n)`), depending on WHICH points the
domain uses.
- **P4 (probe, genuinely past Johnson):** over the actual subgroup خ¼_n the structured bad-scalar set
  (the KK/O11â€³ mechanism, `bad خ± = âˆ’eâ‚پ(S)` over r-subsets S) DIFFERS from the same combinatorial count
  over a random domain (m=8,r=3: 40 vs 56; m=16,r=4: 1185 vs 1507; m=32,r=5: 32672 vs 32707; all at
  radii خ´ strictly past the effective Johnson radius). The far-line list distinguishes خ¼_n from a
  random domain; the weight distribution (P1/P2: identical for both) provably cannot.

So to reach past Johnson C40 must inject a domain-DEPENDENT input â€” which is precisely the open
p-dependent BGK sup-norm `M(خ¼_n) â‰¤ Câˆڑ(nآ·log(p/n))` (faces 3â†”4 of the open core). MacWilliams duality,
being a domain-blind symmetric transform, cannot supply it.

**Why not refuted-false:** MacWilliams duality and the MDS weight distribution are genuinely true and
formalized (in-tree O109/M2). C40 is not false; it is a TRUE-but-Johnson-only object whose past-Johnson
rider ("bounds the far-line list past Johnson") is unsubstantiated and would require the open BGK core.
Same horn-family as C41 (GS/MDS, reduces-to-johnson), C32/C21 (the past-Johnson lever is the open BGK
bound renamed).

**Probe:** `scripts/probes/probe_c40_macwilliams_bch_weightdist.py`, commit pinned below. Python-only
(exact arithmetic mod prime); no Lean changed.

## 2026-06-15 â€” CONJECTURE C41 "Guruswami-Sudan / Johnson is Tight for Explicit خ¼_n + potential-function خک(1/log n) strip" REDUCES-TO-JOHNSON (degenerates to secretly-open on the past-Johnson lever)

Conjecture C41 (issue #444, gmmds-homds, feasibility 2): the GS list-decoding analysis combined
with proven MDS structure (`reedSolomonFrame_isMDS`) bounds the worst-case list at Johnson, and a
"refined potential-function argument" extends the bound a خک(1/log n) strip PAST Johnson for explicit
خ¼_n. REDUCES-TO: Guruswami-Sudan + Johnson bound + `reedSolomonFrame_isMDS` + potential-function
refinement.

**Verdict: reduces-to-johnson** (the GS/MDS half is closed and TRUE but caps exactly at Johnson),
**degenerating to secretly-open** on the only past-Johnson lever (the unspecified
"potential-function refinement" IS the open BGK/line-ball core renamed). No probe needed â€” the
refutation is a logical reduction to ALREADY-FORMALIZED in-tree no-go theorems.

**Horn 1 â€” the GS half is machine-checked to cap at Johnson (CLOSED but Johnson-only).** ArkLib
already contains the EXACT two-sided characterization of the Guruswami-Sudan certificate system:
- `GSJohnson.gs_johnson_wall` (`GSJohnsonWall.lean:241`): GS feasibility forces `n(kâˆ’1) < tآ²`, i.e.
  the certified agreement threshold `t > âˆڑ(n(kâˆ’1))` = the Johnson radius, **for EVERY multiplicity m
  and EVERY weighted degree D**. No GS parameter choice certifies agreement at or below Johnson.
- `GSExactWall.exact_wall` (`GSExactCountWall.lean:139`): even the EXACT lattice-point monomial count
  (the sharpest possible "refined" interpolation-space dimension â€” precisely what a "refined GS
  analysis" would use) keeps the same `âˆڑ(cآ·n)` wall: it reaches `t=60` vs Johnson's `50` at the
  witnessed instance, still STRICTLY above Johnson (`wall_consistency`, line 202), never below.
- `ArkLib.CodingTheory.GSReachesJohnson.t_close_to_johnson` (`GSReachesJohnson.lean`): the matching
  "reaches" direction â€” GS IS feasible at `t â‰¤ âˆڑ(cآ·n(1+1/m)) + 2`, converging to Johnson FROM ABOVE
  as `mâ†’âˆ‍`. So GS is `âˆڑ(cآ·n) < t_GS(m) â‰¤ âˆڑ(cآ·n(1+1/m))+2`: the machine is *CHARACTERIZED*, not merely
  bounded. The file's own docstring states it verbatim: "any certificate past Johnson for the prize
  must be a genuinely different system." `reedSolomonFrame_isMDS` enters only via the pairwise-agreement
  â‰¤ nâˆ’d input to the second-moment Johnson list bound (`JohnsonList.johnson_list_bound`,
  `JohnsonListBound.lean:38`) â€” which is the canonical Johnson-capped object (`Lآ·(aآ²âˆ’nb) â‰¤ nآ²`). MDS
  sharpens the constant `b=nâˆ’d`, not the EXPONENT; it gives Johnson, not past-Johnson.

âں¹ The "GS + Johnson bound + MDS" component pins خ´* at exactly `1âˆ’âˆڑدپ` (Johnson), not past it. This is
the standard list-decoding analysis, and it is closed â€” but it is the boundary, not the interior.

**Horn 2 â€” the only past-Johnson lever is the open core under a new name (SECRETLY-OPEN).** The
entire past-Johnson content of C41 is the single phrase "a refined potential-function argument
extends the bound a خک(1/log n) strip past Johnson." This object:
- Does NOT exist in-tree. `grep -rn "potential" ArkLib/Data/CodingTheory/ProximityGap/*.lean`
  returns ZERO potential-function GS machinery; the only hit (`KKH26DimGeneralSharpPin.lean:28`)
  explicitly flags the `r â‰² âˆڑ(nآ·ln n)` asymptotic potential of the sharp count as "a heuristic
  estimate, *not*" a proof. There is no `reducesTo` named brick C41 can cite.
- Is exactly the open object. The خک(1/log n) strip past Johnson is the window interior
  `(1âˆ’âˆڑدپ, 1âˆ’دپâˆ’خک(1/log n))` â€” the literal prize. Extending the worst-case far-line list bound from
  Johnson into that strip is the statement "the RS[k+1] candidate list concentrates to its first
  moment" (`MonomialLineListBridge.lean:37-38`, `LineListBound.lean:28-29`), which the route-elimination
  meta-theorem (L8) and the in-tree characterization both certify requires the **open p-dependent BGK
  sup-norm moment** `M(خ¼_n) â‰¤ Câˆڑ(n log(p/n))`. A "potential function" that yields a خک(1/log n) strip
  is just the open BGK moment renamed â€” same wall as C32 (BSG-antipodal "sharpening" = the original
  open BGK bound under a new name) and C21 (the genpos lever is vacuous on the affinely-DEPENDENT
  worst-case far-line list, which IS the open BGK/line-ball core).

**Why not refuted-false:** the GS/MDS/Johnson half is genuinely true and formalized; C41 is not a
false claim, it is a TRUE-but-Johnson-only claim with an unsubstantiated past-Johnson rider. The
rider is not refuted (it is the open prize), it is simply not closed â€” so the honest horn is
reduces-to-johnson (closed core reaches only Johnson) with the past-Johnson lever secretly-open.
This is the single most common horn (matches C21's "secretly-open masquerading as closed via the
beyond-Johnson lever being vacuous/open on the worst-case object").

**Mechanism class.** Same family as every GS/algebraic-certificate attempt: the GS parameter
optimization has a HARD `âˆڑ((kâˆ’1)n)` barrier proven in-tree at every multiplicity and even at the
exact-count refinement (`gs_johnson_wall`, `exact_wall`, `GSReachesJohnson`); the "potential-function
refinement" is the unproven past-Johnson lever = the open BGK line-ball core. No probe required â€” the
no-go is already machine-checked and axiom-clean (`#print axioms GSJohnson.gs_johnson_wall`,

## 2026-06-15 â€” CONJECTURE C39 "Tao-Croot-Lev-Pach for the Bad-Scalar Set as a Cap Configuration in â„¤/p" REFUTED-FALSE / REDUCES-TO-JOHNSON (probe_c39_clp_cap_badset.py, commit c3b9cd438)

Conjecture C39 (issue #444, additive-combinatorics, feasibility 2): claim the bad-scalar set
`B_w` is 3-AP-free / a cap configuration in `(â„¤/p,+)`, so the polynomial-method cap bound
(Crootâ€“Levâ€“Pach / Ellenbergâ€“Gijswijt) forces `|B_w| â‰¤ p^{1âˆ’c}` for fixed `c>0`, which with the
gcd-coset law L3 caps the per-direction list below budget `n` past Johnson. REDUCES-TO: CLP/EG
cap bound + in-tree orbit-count law L3 + FactorizationRigidity.

**Verdict: refuted-false** (the cap bound is a category error / dimensionally vacuous in cyclic
`â„¤/p`), and the salvageable core is **reduces-to-johnson/circular** (it pins only the already-known
coset size `S`, never the open multiplicity `N`). Probe over proper `خ¼_n`, `p` prime `pâ‰ˆn^4` (`pâ‰«nآ³`,
never `n=pâˆ’1`), `nâˆˆ{16,32,64,128}`. Three independent horns:

**(H1) The cap hypothesis is actually TRUE for the L3 object â€” and that is the trap, not the win.**
The single-codeword bad-خ± set is, by orbit-count law L3, `B = {âˆ’z^{j(aâˆ’b)} : j} âٹ‚ F_p`, a GEOMETRIC
progression (multiplicative coset of `âں¨z^{aâˆ’b}âں©` scaled by `âˆ’1`). The probe counts 3-APs `x+z=2y`
inside `B` and finds **zero nontrivial 3-APs in every tested direction** (all `(a,b)`, all `n`): a
geometric progression of roots of unity is generically 3-AP-free. So C39's hypothesis holds â€” but
the object it holds for is exactly the L3 coset, whose size is already known.

**(H2) The cap BOUND does not exist in cyclic `â„¤/p`.** CLP (`â„¤/4^N`) and Ellenbergâ€“Gijswijt (`F_q^N`)
give an EXPONENTIAL-IN-AMBIENT-DIMENSION saving: a 3-AP-free subset of `F_q^N` has size `â‰¤ c_q^N`
with `c_q<q`, the saving living in `Nâ†’âˆ‍`. `â„¤/p` is the `N=1` (one-dimensional) cyclic group â€” there
the polynomial/slice-rank method gives NOTHING beyond trivial. The only proven 3-AP-free bound in
`â„¤/p` is Behrend/Bloomâ€“Sisask `r_3(p)=p/exp(c(log p)^t)=p^{1âˆ’o(1)}`, which is **NOT** `p^{1âˆ’c}` for any
FIXED `c>0`. Importing the EG saving exponent into a cyclic group is the same category error as C05
(additive vs multiplicative): there it was "AP-in-exponent vs AP-in-`F_p`"; here it is "cap-bound-in-
`F_q^N` vs cap-bound-in-`â„¤/p`".

**(H3) Even granting the FALSE `p^{1âˆ’c}`, it is strictly WEAKER than the trivial in-tree L3 bound, and
bounds the wrong quantity.** With `nâ‰ˆp^{1/4}` the budget is `O(n)=O(p^{1/4})`; `p^{1âˆ’c}â‰«n` for every
`c<3/4`. But L3 already gives `|B| = n/gcd(bâˆ’a,n) â‰¤ n` EXACTLY, for free. A cap bound of size `~p^{1âˆ’c}`
(â‰« `n`) therefore caps nothing L3 does not already cap better. More decisively, a SIZE bound on the
cap `B` controls only the coset size `S`; the orbit-count law is `I_pencil = N_pencil آ· S` and
`_B2DoorAOrbitCount.lean` flags `S` as **circular** ("#bad=O(n) is the prize floor itself"). The open
quantity is the MULTIPLICITY `N_pencil` (how many `deg<k` codewords realize the same bad خ± / the full
gate over all codewords), which a cap/size bound on `B` cannot reach. So even a perfect cap bound
pins `S` (known) and leaves `N` (open BGK character-sum count) untouched â€” same horn as C05.

**Conclusion.** C39 is the cap-bound sibling of C05's Freiman-doubling claim. C05 conflated additive
vs multiplicative structure; C39 conflates the high-dimensional `F_q^N` polynomial method with cyclic
`â„¤/p` (no fixed-exponent cap bound exists there) AND, even granting the bound, only pins the already-
known coset size `S`, never the open multiplicity `N`. Refuted-false on the cap-bound step;
reduces-to-johnson/circular on whatever survives. Confidence high.

## 2026-06-15 â€” CONJECTURE C14 "Kloosterman-Sheaf Purity on the Antipodal-Paired Dyadic Period (Katz Kl_{n/2}, Sp/SL)" REFUTED-FALSE (probe_c14_kloosterman_purity.py)

Conjecture C14 (issue #444, algebraic-geometry, feasibility 2): Mann's antipodal decomposition
`خ·_b = 2 خ£ cos(2د€ b x / p)` is a real cosine sum; claim it is the Frobenius trace of a
hyper-Kloosterman sheaf `Kl_{n/2}` with monodromy `Sp/SL`, so Deligne purity gives
`|خ·_b| â‰¤ 2âˆڑ(nâˆ’1)` â€” stronger than the prize target, pinning `خ´*` past Johnson. REDUCES-TO:
Katz GKM Ch. 11 (Kl_m monodromy Sp_m/SL_m) + Deligne Weil II + Mann/Conwayâ€“Jones antipodal
(in-tree) + in-tree `AntipodalEvenOddDescent`.

Verdict: **refuted-false** â€” an explicit, reproducible counterexample at EVERY prize `n`. Probe
`scripts/probes/probe_c14_kloosterman_purity.py`, commit `1af1e83a64ee408ac16368ff4e33fded4492c952`.
All rows measured prize-faithfully: PROPER `خ¼_n` (`n = 2^خ¼`), `p` PRIME, `n | pâˆ’1`, `p â‰« nآ³`,
`m = (pâˆ’1)/n â‰« 1`, NEVER `n = pâˆ’1`.

| n | p | p/nآ³ | m=(pâˆ’1)/n | M=max\|خ·_b\| | C14 bound 2âˆڑ(nâˆ’1) | M/bound |
|---|---|------|-----------|-------------|-------------------|---------|
| 8   | 4129     | 8.1  | 516    | 7.558  | 5.292  | 1.43أ— |
| 16  | 65537    | 16.0 | 4096   | 13.838 | 7.746  | 1.79أ— |
| 32  | 1048609  | 32.0 | 32769  | 22.983 | 11.136 | 2.06أ— |
| 64  | 16777601 | 64.0 | 262150 | 38.529 | 15.875 | 2.43أ— |
| 128 | 14605697 | 7.0  | 114107 | 49.461 | 22.539 | 2.19أ— |

Cleanest witness: `n=16, p=65537` (Fermat prime, proper `خ¼_16`, `m=4096`): `max|خ·_b| = 13.84`
(real, `Im = âˆ’2.2eâˆ’15`, so Mann's antipodal decomposition is CORRECT) at `b*=16`, versus the C14
bound `2âˆڑ15 = 7.75` â€” a **1.79أ— violation**. The Mann/Conwayâ€“Jones real-valuedness premise holds;
the Kloosterman-purity *inference* from it is false.

HORN â€” OBJECT MISMATCH (the wrong sheaf; `خ·_b` is NOT a hyper-Kloosterman trace).
- The hyper-Kloosterman sum is `Kl_m(a;p) = خ£_{x_1آ·آ·آ·x_m = a, x_i âˆˆ F_p^*} e_p(x_1+آ·آ·آ·+x_m)`. It is
  the trace of the Kloosterman sheaf `Kl_m`, **rank `m`**, pure of weight `mâˆ’1`, monodromy `Sp_m`
  (m even)/`SL_m` (m odd). Deligne purity gives `|Kl_m| â‰¤ m آ· p^{(mâˆ’1)/2}` â€” a **p-DEPENDENT** bound.
  For `m=2` (classical, Sp_2) that is `2âˆڑp`, NOT `2âˆڑ(nâˆ’1)`. There is no instantiation of Kloosterman
  purity that yields a `p-independent` edge `2âˆڑ(nâˆ’1)`: the `âˆڑ` in Deligne purity is `âˆڑp`, the field
  size, not `âˆڑ(rank)` or `âˆڑ(# terms)`.
- `خ·_b = خ£_{yâˆˆخ¼_n} دˆ(by)` is a **cyclotomic Gauss period** (a partial additive-character sum over a
  *multiplicative subgroup*), a fundamentally different object. Its correct sheaf-theoretic home is the
  Kummer/hypergeometric Gauss-sum FAMILY whose geometric monodromy is **`GL(1)^f`** (Rojas-Leأ³n
  arXiv:2207.12439, Katz [Kat88]; in-tree `KatzEffectiveGaussSum.lean`, `MonodromyConductorScaffold.lean`,
  `docs/kb/deltastar-407-katz-monodromy-research-2026-06-13.md`), giving an **asymptotically-independent
  GAUSSIAN (UNBOUNDED) law** â€” `M(n) â‰ˆ âˆڑ(n log(p/n))` with NO hard `âˆڑ`-edge. The conjecture's premise
  that there is a single Sp/SL Kloosterman sheaf with a bounded edge contradicts the established
  GL(1)^f monodromy of the actual family.

HORN â€” THE MEASURED LAW HAS NO EDGE. `M/âˆڑn` GROWS monotonically `2.67 â†’ 3.46 â†’ 4.06 â†’ 4.82` (n=8â†’64),
the signature of a `âˆڑlog`-type Gaussian large deviation (Kluyver/random-walk; in-tree
`deltastar-407-new-ideas-katz-monodromy-framing-2026-06-13.md` line 9â€“11), NOT a fixed semicircle/
Satoâ€“Tate edge `2âˆڑn` (which would force `M/âˆڑn â†’ 2`). A bounded-`2âˆڑ(nâˆ’1)` edge is empirically absent.

Even granting (counterfactually) the bound, `2âˆڑ(nâˆ’1) = خک(âˆڑn)` would sit at the additive-energy/Johnson
scale `âˆڑS` (meta-theorem L8: 2nd-order/âˆڑS objects cap at Johnson) â€” so on its own arithmetic it could
not pin `خ´*` PAST Johnson regardless; the route-elimination meta-theorem already excludes any closed
`p-independent âˆڑn` edge from reaching past Johnson. But that is moot: the bound is simply FALSE.

**Probe committed:** `scripts/probes/probe_c14_kloosterman_purity.py` (self-contained, sympy GF(p);
coset-reduced exact sup-norm sweep). Commit `1af1e83a64ee408ac16368ff4e33fded4492c952`. Sibling to the
C29 Kowalskiâ€“Sawin Kloosterman-path entry below (same object-mismatch family: the prize period is a
multiplicative-subgroup Gauss period, not any Kloosterman-sheaf object).

## 2026-06-15 â€” CONJECTURE C29 "Kowalskiâ€“Sawin Kloosterman-Path Functional CLT Sup-Bound" SECRETLY-OPEN / REDUCES-TO-JOHNSON (probe_c29_kowalski_sawin_path_clt.py)

Conjecture C29 (issue #444, algebraic-geometry, feasibility 2): apply the Kowalskiâ€“Sawin
functional CLT for partial-sum *paths* to the period family and claim the sub-Gaussian sup of the
limiting path process bounds `max_c |خ·_c| â‰¤ âˆڑ(2 n log p)` past Johnson, via the proven
large-monodromy support theorem (Deligne Weil II + functional CLT). REDUCES-TO: Kowalskiâ€“Sawin
Kloosterman-paths + Deligne Weil II + large-monodromy support theorem.

Verdict: **secretly-open** (degenerating to **reduces-to-johnson** on the only effective reading).
The import fails on three independent, mutually reinforcing horns, all measured prize-faithfully
(PROPER `خ¼_n`, `n = 2^خ¼`, `p` PRIME, `p â‰« nآ³`, NEVER `n = pâˆ’1`). Probe
`scripts/probes/probe_c29_kowalski_sawin_path_clt.py`, commit `702858d5d35cddf2447a0283c9e886efb082f070`.
Sibling to the REFUTED Brick-Ledger #1 ("Conjecture (G) periods jointly sub-Gaussian var n") and the
Salemâ€“Zygmund / Lamzouri-CLT route (`deltastar-salem-zygmund-gausssum-chaining-2026-06-13.md`).

HORN A â€” OBJECT MISMATCH (the named theorem governs a *different* random object). The
Kowalskiâ€“Sawin path process is `K_p(t) = (1/âˆڑp) خ£_{0â‰¤aâ‰¤tp} Kl(a;1,1;p) âˆˆ C([0,1])` â€” the
**partial-sum path of ONE Kloosterman sum indexed by the summation variable `a`**, normalized by
`âˆڑp`. Its functional CLT (Deligne equidistribution as `pâ†’âˆ‍` for the FIXED Kloosterman sheaf) gives a
sub-Gaussian sup `sup_t |K_p(t)|`, which is the maximal *partial sum*, an O(1)-scale fluctuation of a
length-`p` walk. The prize object is `max_{câ‰ 0} |خ·_c|` where `خ·_c = خ£_{xâˆˆخ¼_n} e_p(g^c x)` is the
**COMPLETE (full) period sum** and the max runs over the **dual frequency index `c âˆˆ â„¤/m`**. Measured:
path-sup = 0.74â€“0.88 (Weil O(1) scale) versus period-sup = 7.4â€“15.8 (the n-term object) at the SAME
`(p,n)`. They are governed by different processes; the path CLT does not bound `max_c|خ·_c|`.

| p | n | m | period max\|خ·_c\| | âˆڑ(n log p) | ratio | KS path-sup \|K_p\| |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 193 | 16 | 12 | 7.432 | 9.176 | 0.810 | 0.876 |
| 769 | 16 | 48 | 9.100 | 10.311 | 0.883 | 0.741 |
| 3329 | 16 | 208 | 11.085 | 11.392 | 0.973 | 0.828 |

HORN B â€” EFFECTIVITY GAP (the decisive horn; the CLT is asymptotic-in-`p`, not effective at fixed
prize `q`). The Kowalskiâ€“Sawin CLT is a distributional limit as `pâ†’âˆ‍` at FIXED conductor/sheaf; the
sub-Gaussian sup of the LIMIT process bounds the LIMIT, not the fixed-`q` tail with an explicit
constant. The sub-Gaussian variance proxy `c(p,n) = 2 ln MGF(t*) / t*آ²` does NOT lock to `n`: over a
خ²-sweep at `n=8` it sits at proxy/n = 0.77 â†’ 0.76 â†’ 0.72 and **drifts** as خ² = log_n p grows. A CLT
that had landed effectively would force proxy/n â†’ 1 with a `p`-independent constant; the observed drift
is exactly the signature that the limit is approached only as `pâ†’âˆ‍`, i.e. **ineffective at the prize's
fixed (large) `q`** â€” the same effectivity barrier already recorded for the Katz/Rojas-Leأ³n Gauss-sum
equidistribution route (`deltastar-407-katz-monodromy-research-2026-06-13.md`).

| p | n | خ² | m | max\|خ·\| | âˆڑ(n ln p) | ratio | proxy/n |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 2081 | 8 | 3.67 | 260 | 7.277 | 7.818 | 0.931 | 0.766 |
| 4129 | 8 | 4.00 | 516 | 7.558 | 8.161 | 0.926 | 0.755 |
| 32801 | 8 | 5.00 | 4100 | 7.843 | 9.121 | 0.860 | 0.717 |

HORN C â€” GROWING-DIMENSION OBSTRUCTION (single fixed sheaf vs growing family). Via the DFT identity
`خ·_c = âˆ’1/m + (1/m) خ£_{j=1}^{mâˆ’1} د„(د‡_j) e(âˆ’jc/m)`, the period is a sum of `mâˆ’1 â‰ˆ p/n` Gauss sums â€”
a family whose **dimension / conductor grows with `p`** (the same large-sieve dimension obstruction
noted for the FKM/Bombieri large-values route, `deltastar-407-large-sieve-dimension-obstruction-2026-06-13.md`).
The large-monodromy support theorem is invoked for ONE fixed sheaf; it does not deliver a uniform
sub-Gaussian sup over a `(p/n)`-growing family. Measured `max_c|خ·_c|/âˆڑ(n log p) âˆˆ [0.86, 0.99]` â€” the
period-sup tracks the **random / Salemâ€“Zygmund value** `âˆڑ(n log p)`. That scale IS the open BGK core:
the target the CLT wants to deliver is precisely the random-model derandomization that is open
everywhere (`deltastar-gate-is-sqrt-cancellation-BGK-fullpower-short-2026-06-13.md`).

The deeper structural point. C29 names real theorems (Kowalskiâ€“Sawin Kloosterman-paths, Adv. Math.
2016; Deligne Weil II) but applies them off-object and off-regime. The genuine probabilistic content
the prize needs â€” a sub-Gaussian tail with proxy `O(n)` for the GROWING `(mâˆ’1)`-term Gauss-sum DFT,
uniform and EFFECTIVE at fixed `n â‰ˆ p^{1/8}` â€” is exactly the residual already isolated as
"Lamzouri's CLT one regime further" (extend the 2-D Gaussian value-distribution CLT from length
`p^{o(1)}` to length `p^{خ²}` at fixed `خ²`), which is the Bourgain barrier = the open BGK âˆڑ-cancellation.
The Kloosterman-path CLT supplies neither the right object (HORN A), the effectivity (HORN B), nor the
growing-family uniformity (HORN C). Sub-Gaussian sup-control of a single-sheaf path process caps at the
random-model `âˆڑ(n log p)` value asymptotically â€” and that asymptotic value, made worst-case at fixed
`q`, is the open core that itself sits at the Johnsonâ†”capacity boundary the meta-theorem reserves for
the open p-dependent regime. No new non-BGK lever is delivered.

Probe committed: `scripts/probes/probe_c29_kowalski_sawin_path_clt.py` (self-contained, sympy/numpy
GF(p); three horns: object mismatch, خ²-sweep proxy drift, random-scale tracking; proper `خ¼_n`,
`p` prime, `p â‰« nآ³`, never `n=pâˆ’1`).

## 2026-06-15 â€” CONJECTURE C24 "Relaxed-rMDS additive-inflation" REDUCES-TO-JOHNSON (probe_c24_relaxed_rmds_additive.py)

Conjecture C24 (issue #444, gmmds-homds): use BGM's relaxed `MDS(L+1) âں¹ list â‰¤ L` and claim the
in-tree corank deficiency `خک(a)` only ADDITIVELY inflates the list (`L_relaxed = L_generic + خک(a)`),
with `خک(aآ²)` slack `<` budget at prize scale, pinning `خ´* â‰¥ 1âˆ’دپâˆ’c'/log n` past Johnson.
Verdict: **reduces-to-johnson** (with a secretly-open undertone). Two independent defects, both
measured prize-faithfully (PROPER خ¼_n, p PRIME, p â‰ˆ 8nآ³ â‰« nآ³, n < pâˆ’1). Probe
`scripts/probes/probe_c24_relaxed_rmds_additive.py`, commit `9daa05149`. Sibling to C23/C43/C44.

DEFECT 1 â€” "additive inflation" MIS-READS BGM's relaxed theorem (the fatal semantics error).
BGM's relaxed bound is `list â‰¤ (relaxation order) âˆ’ 1`: the list size IS the order at which the code
is still MDS, minus one. The CORANK is precisely the deficiency in the order at which MDS HOLDS â€”
`MDS(â„“)` holds âں؛ every order-â‰¤â„“ generalized-Vandermonde minor is full rank âں؛ corank 0 at all
orders â‰¤ â„“. A corank-`d` deficiency at order `a` means `MDS(a)` FAILS, so the smallest `L` for which
the relaxed hypothesis `MDS(L+1)` holds forces `L+1 â‰¥ a`, giving `list â‰¤ aâˆ’1 = خک(a) = خک(n)`. The
corank does NOT add on top of a generic `O(1/خ·)` list; it SETS the relaxation order, collapsing the
bound to the TRIVIAL `خک(n)` counting list (= the whole far-coset orbit; orbit-count L3: `I â‰¤ n`).
`L_relaxed = L_generic + خک(a)` is a category error.

DEFECT 2 â€” the corank IS `خک(a) = خک(n)`, confirmed exactly (so even C24's own premise gives `خک(n)`).
The in-tree law `corank_Fp(خ¼_a;E) = a âˆ’ #distinct(e mod a)` reproduced **5400/5400 EXACT, char-faithful**
over proper خ¼_n at p â‰ˆ 8nآ³. On the binding interior coset `a = n/2` the max list-relevant corank is
`d = n/4 = خک(n)` (measured: 2, 4, 8, 16 for n = 8, 16, 32, 64). So the relaxation order needed is
`خک(n)` âں¹ `list â‰¤ خک(n)`. `خک(aآ²) ~ nآ²` "slack" is moot: the OBJECT being bounded is already `خک(n)`
= the whole orbit = the trivial counting bound = the open BGK/counting regime, NOT a poly,
below-orbit, between-Johnson-and-capacity pin.

NET. The only regime where relaxed-MDS yields a USEFUL (poly, sub-orbit) list is order â‰¤ the generic
MDS order = Johnson. Past Johnson the structural corank of خ¼_n is `خک(a)` (the antipodal/HOMDS horn of
the route-elimination meta-theorem), so the honest relaxed-MDS bound is the trivial `خک(n)`. Beating it
needs the open p-dependent count of the actual far-coset list (= the BGK/Paley wall). C24 relocates,
does not remove, the wall. DO NOT re-claim "relaxed rMDS_d additively inflates the خ¼_n list by `خک(a)`
with `خک(aآ²)` slack âں¹ خ´* past Johnson" â€” the corank sets the order, not the slack, and the bound is
trivial. Same wall as P7 (DISPROOF_LOG line ~99), C23, C43, C44.

## 2026-06-15 â€” CONJECTURE C26 "Stickelberger-House" SECRETLY-OPEN (probe_c26_stickelberger_house.py)

Conjecture C26 (issue #444): the dyadic Gauss-period house (= max archimedean conjugate
modulus = M(خ¼_n), the open object) is â‰¤ Câˆڑ(n log p) via the EXACT Stickelberger ideal
factorization of د„(د‡), which "forces the conjugates to be BALANCED without any moment/
equidistribution input" (Dukeâ€“Garcia house framework). Verdict: **secretly-open** â€” it
hides the open BGK/Paley sup-norm step. Reconfirms two prior 2026-06-14 entries below
(Galois/house + p-adic/Stickelberger REFUTED; HANDLE A3 REFUTED) with a fresh prize-shaped probe.

DECISIVE OBSTRUCTION (two independent, both measured at p~n^4 â‰« n^3, n=2^خ¼, NEVER n=p-1):
- **Stickelberger is Galois-INVARIANT on the conjugate orbit.** The periods {خ·_c} are the full
  Galois orbit of one algebraic integer; Stickelberger gives each conjugate the SAME prime-ideal
  valuation profile (they are Galois-conjugate ideals; val = base-p digit-sum, Grossâ€“Koblitz exact).
  So the p-adic data carries ZERO information about WHICH conjugate is largest:
  corr(|خ·_c|, p-adic valuation) = 0.020, 0.022, 0.019, âˆ’0.004 (n=8,16,32,64). The archimedean
  size is the UNIT/root-of-unity (phase) factor of د„(د‡) â€” explicitly NOT pinned by Stickelberger
  (sign/unit-root ambiguity open even for index-2,4 Gauss sums; Yangâ€“Xia, Aoki).
- **The conjugates are NOT balanced** (the claim's load-bearing step is false). house/geomMean =
  5.0, 6.4, 6.2, 7.2 (GROWING with n); house/min â‰ˆ 2785 .. 14498. Balance would give house/geomâ‰ˆ1.
  The norm |N|^{1/m} (= geomMean, what Dukeâ€“Garcia/Habegger compute) UNDER-estimates the house by
  the growing factor 5â€“7أ—. Norm â†› house unless conjugates are flat = the open equidistribution.
- **Object is real but bound undelivered.** house â‰ˆ 3.4â€“3.8آ·âˆڑn â‰ˆ âˆڑ(n log p) (below trivial n) â€” so
  the TARGET shape is correct, but Stickelberger delivers only the p-adic valuation, not the âˆڑn
  archimedean cancellation. Pinning house from norm = forcing balance = the open BGK/Paley sup-norm
  (best PROVEN n^{1âˆ’o(1)} BGK; di Benedetto n^{0.989} only n>p^{1/4}, outside the prize).
Probe: scripts/probes/probe_c26_stickelberger_house.py, commit 16a4ab116. Same wall as A3/Galois.

## 2026-06-15 â€” CONJECTURE C27 "Hasseâ€“Davenport Dyadic-Lifting Telescope" REDUCES-TO-JOHNSON / SECRETLY-OPEN (probe_c27_hassedavenport_telescope.py)

Conjecture C27 (issue #444, gauss-period-exact): the 2-power Gauss-sum tower `خ¼_2âٹ‚â‹¯âٹ‚خ¼_{2^خ¼}`
linked by Hasseâ€“Davenport lifting telescopes the period sup-norm `M(خ¼_n)` into a PRODUCT of
Jacobi sums (each `|J|=âˆڑp`), the `2^خ¼` levels contributing `âˆڑ(log p)`, giving
`M(خ¼_n)â‰¤âˆڑ(n log p)` PAST Johnson via a purely MULTIPLICATIVE recursion (claimed non-analytic,
hence outside the moment-wall impossibility map). Reduces-to: HD product/lifting (exact) +
`د„(د‡)د„(د‡')=J(د‡,د‡')د„(د‡د‡')`, `|J|=âˆڑp` (proven). Verdict: **reduces-to-johnson (= secretly-open)**.

This is the MULTIPLICATIVE-telescope twin of C26 (Stickelberger, above) and was already
promoted-then-refuted as **C9** by the algebraic-route loop
(`docs/kb/deltastar-conjecture-loop-algebraic-route-2026-06-13.md`, lines 80â€“89). Same fatal
fact: `M(H)=max_{aâ‰ 0}|خ·_a|=HOUSE(خ·)` (max conjugate modulus), and any product/telescope (HD,
Jacobi, norm) computes only a SYMMETRIC function of the `m=(pâˆ’1)/n` conjugates `{خ·_c}` â€”
chiefly the NORM `N=âˆڈ_c خ·_c`. A product is structurally BLIND to which conjugate is largest. By
AMâ€“GM the norm gives only `|N|^{1/m}â‰ˆâˆڑn` = the geometric mean = the Lآ²-average = the JOHNSON
scale, i.e. a LOWER bound `M(H)â‰¥âˆڑn`, useless as the claimed `âˆڑ(n log p)` UPPER bound. The
`âˆڑ(log p)` prize factor IS the conjugate spread = the archimedean argument distribution
`{arg د„(د‡_j)}` = the BGK/Paley open core, which no multiplicative recursion supplies.

DECISIVE PROBE (all distinct conjugate periods, proper `خ¼_n`, `n=2^خ¼`, خ¼=3..6, `p` prime,
`p>nآ³`, NEVER `n=pâˆ’1`, 5 primes/n):
- **P1 geometric mean = âˆڑn (Johnson scale):** `gm/âˆڑn â‰ˆ 0.50â€“0.59` uniformly for all n â€” the norm
  (everything a telescope yields) sits at the âˆڑn Lآ²-average.
- **P2 prize factor = conjugate spread, invisible to products:** `house/gm` GROWS 4.2â†’5.3â†’6.3â†’7.5
  (n=8â†’16â†’32â†’64), tracking `âˆڑ(log(p/n))`. The âˆڑ(log p) gap is real and the product misses it.
- **P3 DECISIVE â€” norm does NOT determine house:** at FIXED n, `gm_spread â‰¤ 0.22` across primes
  while `house_spread = 0.34, 0.70, 6.78, 7.24` (n=8,16,32,64). Two fields with essentially equal
  `|N|` have `M(H)` differing by 6â€“7. A product/telescope CANNOT pin the sup.

So the HD/Jacobi telescope is exact and proven but reaches only the âˆڑn geometric-mean = Johnson
scale (reduces-to-johnson); the past-Johnson upper bound `âˆڑ(n log p)` (the TRUE value) needs the
archimedean conjugate-spread = BGK sup-norm open core (secretly-open). "Non-analytic âں¹ off the
moment wall" is correct but irrelevant: the bound never leaves the Lآ²/Johnson scale â€” it lands on
a DIFFERENT wall (the house-vs-norm archimedean gap). NOT refuted-false (the true M(خ¼_n)â‰ˆâˆڑ(n log p));
the MECHANISM cannot prove it. Probe: scripts/probes/probe_c27_hassedavenport_telescope.py,
commit aa781ec66. Same wall as C26/C9/A3 (the houseâ‰ norm archimedean gap).

Goal: keep trying to **disprove** the ABF26 Grand-Challenge-1 conjecture, then
**disprove the disproof**. Record every attempt so we don't repeat ourselves and
so we zero in. Keep lemmas that *constrain* even if they don't fully disprove.
Default assumption: my disproof is wrong â€” find the precise reason it fails and
make that reason a sorry-free Lean lemma.
