## 2026-06-12 â€” UDR-edge band amendment: RADIUS coverage closed by composition (the no-go stands for the dichotomy budget)

The fifth no-go above is intrinsic to the universal DICHOTOMY's two branches, not to the
radius band: the subset-ownership law (`march_badScalars_card_mul_le`, glueing constant)
is radius-free, so the band `n âˆˆ [2w+k+1, 2w+2k)` moves at the subset budget
`C(n,k+1)/((k+1)آ·p)` â€” `UDREdgeClosure.lean` (`udrEdgeBand_closure`,
`le_mcaDeltaStar_subset_law_w`).  Below-UDR radius coverage is gapless at every rate.
What remains open on the band is only the BUDGET gap: the dichotomy's
`n^{k+1}/(nâˆ’2wâˆ’2k+1)^k` shape is unavailable there; recovering it needs the خ³-line
mechanism (explainers of bad scalars move polynomially `p_خ³ = P(خ³)` with codeword
coefficients; the column dichotomy â€” â‰¤ aâˆ’1 identically-zero columns else joint â€” gives
`#bad â‰¤ nآ·deg P`; the missing piece is SLOPE COLLAPSE: `deg P â‰¤ 1` on the band, probe
`_scratch_probe_slope_collapse.py`).

**Generic-domain addendum (same day):** the band is also closed for EVERY injective
evaluation domain (no smoothness) by a second independent mechanism â€” the خ³-preserving
puncture descent (`BelowUDRPuncture.lean`): a direction vanishing at a witness point
`xâ‚€` forces every explainer through `(xâ‚€, uâ‚€ xâ‚€)` at every خ³, and dividing the instance
by `(X âˆ’ xâ‚€)` maps `(n, k, w) â†’ (nâˆ’1, kâˆ’1, w)` at the same خ³ with `n âˆ’ k` (hence the
UDR slack دƒ = nâˆ’2wâˆ’k) invariant; induction on k bottoms at the k = 1 universal law.
Result: `#badآ·(nâˆ’2wâˆ’k) â‰¤ n^{k+1}` on ALL of `2w+k+1 â‰¤ n` â€” one statement, the whole
below-UDR range, generic domain (`belowUDR_badScalars_card_mul_le`,
`le_mcaDeltaStar_belowUDR`, `udrEdgeBand_closure_generic`; probe
`probe_edgeband_puncture.py`, 633/633 descent checks).  Budget comparison stands: on
smooth domains in the band the subset budget `C(n,k+1)/((k+1)p)` is sharper, and on
`2w+2k â‰¤ n` the dichotomy budget is sharper â€” the descent adds generality (any domain),
uniformity (one statement), and a reusable خ³-preserving transfer mechanism (it is
radius-agnostic: above UDR it descends every bad scalar whose witness meets the
direction's zero set; the complementary stratum has witnesses concentrated on the
support).  (Slope collapse was refuted the same day â€” see the entry above; the
descent bound here is unaffected, and the surviving two-regime pencil law plus the
moment-fiber question remain the named budget targets on smooth domains.)

## 2026-06-12 â€” SLOPE COLLAPSE REFUTED (directed t=2 construction); the surviving two-regime pencil law

The slope-collapse conjecture (UDR-edge amendment above: every band bad-family's
explainer map is affine, `deg P â‰¤ 1`) is FALSE as stated: the explicit quadratic-pencil
construction (`_scratch_probe_nonaffine_directed.py`, wt371) produces genuine nonaffine
bad families wherever the scalar field gives room.  Witness at the band instance
`(p, n, k, w) = (73, 9, 2, 3)`, `a = 6`:
`uâ‚€ = (64, 48, 33, 62, 37, 56, 46, 22, 36)`, `uâ‚پ = (5, 29, 30, 32, 65, 5, 29, 61, 55)`,
bad scalars `{12, 16, 36}`, no affine selection (verified over all explainer pairs).
The first random probe's zero-nonaffine reading at `p = 17, 19` was a small-field
artifact.  Design: column polys `âˆ’mâ‚‚(i)(خ³âˆ’râ‚پلµ¢)(خ³âˆ’râ‚‚لµ¢)` with `mâ‚‚` a nonzero codeword and
the `خ³آ¹`/`خ³âپ°` coefficients absorbed into the FREE `خµ`/`uâ‚€` â€” t = 2 needs no rigidity
beyond the top coefficient.

**What survives (verified by the same construction): the two-regime pencil law.**
Identically-zero columns of a degree-`t` pencil:
* `t = 1`: capped at `a âˆ’ 1` by joint-exclusion only âں¹ `#bad_affine â‰¤ (nâˆ’z)/(aâˆ’z)`,
  maximal `w + 1` at `z = aâˆ’1`;
* `t â‰¥ 2`: the TOP Newton coefficient `m_t` is a nonzero codeword (divided differences
  of codewords), so `z â‰¤ k âˆ’ 1` âں¹ `#bad â‰¤ tآ·(nâˆ’k+1)/(aâˆ’k+1)` â€” at the band â‰ˆ `2t`.
  The probe's max nonaffine family (3) meets the `t = 2` cap `âŒٹ2(nâˆ’k+1)/(aâˆ’k+1)âŒ‹ = 3`
  exactly; it CANNOT beat the affine cap at `t = 2`.

**The open t-control, reduced:** beating `w + 1` needs `t â‰ˆ w/2`, and a degree-`t`
pencil constrains `t âˆ’ 2` symmetric functions of every column's root set to be
codeword-ratios â€” for non-constant ratios, â‰¤ `kâˆ’1` columns per value; for constant
ratios, the root `t`-sets lie in a fiber of `t âˆ’ 2` prescribed power sums of the scalar
set `خ“`.  So the band adversary beyond affine is a SCALAR-SIDE deep moment-fiber
extremal problem (the census programme's object, transposed from the domain to the
scalar field): large nonaffine families need `|خ“| â‰ˆ w` scalar sets that are
moment-degenerate to depth `â‰ˆ w/2` with large fibers.  Conjectured cap (next probe):
additive moment fibers at depth `tâˆ’2` collapse the supply below the affine cap for all
`t â‰¥ 2` âں¹ `#bad â‰¤ w + O(1)` on the band.
## 2026-06-12 â€” `WindowPencilAnchored` is NOT universal: class V (vanishing-denominator rows)

The window pencil law (`WBPencilWindowLaw.lean`) counts â‰¤ `(w+1)+n(w+1)+1` bad
scalars for stacks with an ANCHORED representation pair (some adjugate entry of
some square row-selection of the coefficient pencil â‰¢ 0, âں؛ corank â‰¤ 1 over
`F(خ³)`).  The hope that every doubly-WB-solvable stack admits an anchored
representation is **FALSE**:

* **Class V** â€” rows that are *rational-with-exceptions* (`u = R/â„“` off the
  domain zero set of `â„“`, free at the zeros; the WB relation `â„“(x_i)u_i = R(x_i)`
  forces `R` to vanish with `â„“`, so these are honest WB rows) â€” generically has
  pencil corank â‰¥ 2 for EVERY representation pair: 103/130 fresh class-V stacks
  at `(13,6,1,2)` have NO anchored pair under FULL enumeration of both rows' WB
  solution spaces (`probe_wb_window_anchor_existential.py`); coranks up to 4
  occur (`probe_wb_window_corank2.py`).
* **The common-factor reduction is insufficient**: dividing the forced shared
  factor (the `(xâˆ’a)`'s where `â„“` and `R` jointly vanish) out of all four data
  polynomials preserves corank verbatim under the uniform caps, and with the
  correctly shrunken cofactor cap (`m âˆ’ z`) still leaves a residue class
  degenerate â€” the survivors are recognizable: shared reduced denominators,
  post-reduction polynomial rows (`probe_wb_window_reduced_anchor.py`).
* **The saving observation**: every unanchored stack found has â‰¤ 2 mca-bad
  scalars.  Mechanism (the proof route for the corrected residual): for
  `gcd(â„“â‚€â„“â‚پ, Z_D) = 1` data below UDR, two `F(خ³)`-independent kernel solutions
  force `Qâ‚پZâ‚‚ = Qâ‚‚Zâ‚پ` (degree < n) and `Z_D âˆ£ (A+خ³B)g âˆ’ Lدپ` for the gcd-split
  `Z_j = gخ¶_j` â€” every kernel solution is a polynomial multiple of ONE primitive
  triple `(g, دپ, c)` with slack in all three caps (the kernel is `K[x]`-cyclic);
  a bad scalar needs `g(خ³,آ·)` to split over the domain, i.e. the anchored
  incidence count re-runs on the primitive curve.

**Surviving form** (`WBPencilWindowCapstone.lean`): `UnanchoredLinear` â€” stacks
with no anchored pair have â‰¤ `n` bad scalars (budget aligned with the repaired
`WindowRationalLinear`; strictly weaker than it via
`unanchoredLinear_of_windowRationalLinear`).  The below-UDR law
`خµ_mca â‰¤ ((n+1)(w+1)+1)/q` holds under this residual alone
(`epsMCA_le_below_udr_of_unanchoredLinear`), with the anchored bulk carried by
the proven pencil law.

## 2026-06-12 â€” The deep-band supply: production closure via agreement caps is VACUOUS (analysis, not a refutation of the reduction)

The agreement-capped supply instance (`explainable_cores_card_of_agreement_le`:
`#explCoresآ·C(k+m+1,k) â‰¤ C(n,k)آ·C(Aâˆ’k,m+1)`) combined with the near-line
dichotomy (`near_scalar_unique`: at most one scalar's line exceeds agreement
`(n+k)/2`) does NOT close the production deep-band count: at `A â‰ˆ (n+k)/2`,
`k â‰ˆ n/2`, the bound `C(n,k)آ·C(Aâˆ’k,m+1)/C(k+m+1,k) â‰ˆ C(n,k)آ·2^{âˆ’(m+1)}` is of
the same order as the witness mass `C(n,k+m+1)/q^mآ·q^m`, leaving
`#badSet â‰³ 2^{m+1}/q^m` â€” vacuous for `m â‰¥ 1` at production `q`.  The loss is
the `C(n,k)` factor from `k`-subset determination (only `q^k` codewords exist,
but `C(n,k)` `k`-subsets are counted).  Sharpening requires bounding the number
of codewords at agreement `â‰¥ k+m+1` â€” below Johnson agreement that is
quantitatively the list-decoding wall (the known `خ´*`â†”LD coupling).  The
supply-side mathematics above the wall is proven; the wall itself remains the
open core, now in its sharpest isolated form.
## 2026-06-12 â€” round 9: the k-simplex packing collapses; the packing cap at `n` is FALSE (the overlap law)

Two round-8 census conjectures resolved in opposite directions
(`probe_packing_envelope.py`, `PackingEnvelope.lean`):

* **"k â‰¥ 3 disjoint (e+1)-simplex packings extend the envelope" â€” REFUTED.**
  The k-packing stack (`uâ‚پ = qâ‚€|_{Sâ‚پ}`, `uâ‚€ = (Xآ·qâ‚€)|_{Sâ‚پ}`, `qâ‚€` vanishing on the
  complement `Z` of the union) only depends on `(sâ‚پ, z) = (|Sâ‚پ|, |Z|)`: the `kâˆ’1`
  other blocks are an undifferentiated reservoir whose every point contributes one
  `(Xâˆ’x)qâ‚€`-alignment scalar.  Count `n âˆ’ z` at threshold `min(nâˆ’sâ‚پ+1, sâ‚پ+z+1)`;
  the per-radius optimum is the bisimplex value, and any `k â‰¥ 2` packing's count
  `k(e+1)` is `â‰¤ 2E+2` at its own radius with the unified window implied
  (`kPacking_subsumed`, pure arithmetic).  Probe T2: at `(97,16,d=5), t=9` the
  `k=3,e=3` stack carries exactly its `12` while the `(sâ‚پ,z)=(8,0)` optimum carries
  `16` at the same threshold.  `k â‰¥ 3` never appears on the envelope.

* **"the packing count caps at `min(n, 2E+2)`" â€” REFUTED: the OVERLAP law.**
  The round-8 `t=7` hint (`20` at `(97,16,2)`) is a genuine char-0 family â€”
  hill-climbs at `p = 97` AND `p = 257` independently converge to `2(nâˆ’t)+2 = 20`
  with identical structure (two `(E+1)`-point simplex blocks overlapping in
  `nâˆ’2t+2 = 4` points, zero leftovers).  Reverse-engineered mechanism: stack
  `(X, 1)` on `Sâˆ–Y`, tuned `(a_x, b_x)` on the overlap `Y`, zero off `S`; each
  overlap point carries TWO bad scalars (its kill `âˆ’a_x/b_x` and its align
  `(xâˆ’a_x)/(b_xâˆ’1)`), so the count is `n + |Y|`, exceeding `n`.  Proven general
  (`overlap_packing_epsMCA_lower_bound`) and instantiated word-level exact at
  `(12289, 16, d=2)`: `20` bad scalars at radius `9/16` and `18` at `1/2`
  (probe-verified exact counts).  Grand packing law: `2(nâˆ’t)+2` on the whole
  window `d+2 â‰¤ t â‰¤ (n+d+1)/2`.

Side observation (probe T1): at `t = d+3` cells, e.g. `(97,16,4), t=7`, sub-optimal
`(sâ‚پ,z)` stacks pick up large SMALL-FIELD surpluses (`34` observed vs `14` designed;
expected-count heuristic `C(10,5)آ·C(6,2)/97 â‰ˆ 39`) â€” the same codim-0 coincidence
species as the censused mod-17 extras; vanishes for `p â‰« poly(n)`.

### The any-domain sub-Johnson supply laws â€” REFUTED by the Frobenius secant family (Fable, 2026-06-13)

Context: the crossing/Cauchyâ€“Schwarz campaign proved the mean-degree law `خ£|A| â‰¤ 2n`
for pairwise-`â‰¤1` families down to the Johnson agreement `tآ² â‰¥ 2n`, and noted that
below it set systems blow up (projective planes), so "any proof must couple the word."
This entry sharpens the wall: **coupling the word is not enough â€” the blowup is
realized by genuine RS agreement families** (`FrobeniusSubfieldBlowup.lean`, 8 theorems,
axiom-clean; probe `probe_frobenius_blowup.py`, exact at `(p,e) = (3,2),(3,3),(5,2)`).

Mechanism (JH01/BSKR06 in the #389 charter objects): char `p`, `k = 2`, the Frobenius
word `w(z) = z^p` over any `ً‌”½_p`-affine-closed domain (e.g. all of `ً‌”½_{p^e}`, `n = p^e`).
Freshman's dream: the affine codeword `zâ‚€^p + u^{pâˆ’1}(zâˆ’zâ‚€)` agrees with `w` on the
ENTIRE affine line `zâ‚€ + ً‌”½_p u` â€” every secant of the Frobenius graph is `p`-rich.
At level `t = p` (sub-Johnson: `tآ² = n^{2/e} â‰¤ n`):

* **"`خ£|A| â‰¤ 2n` (or any `Cآ·n`) below Johnson for RS agreement families" â€” REFUTED.**
  The family is pairwise-`â‰¤1`, every member exactly `p = t` points
  (`frobenius_explainable_inter_le_one`), and the mass SATURATES the universal pair
  bound exactly: `خ£|A| = n(nâˆ’1)/(pâˆ’1)` (`frobenius_mass_floor`,
  `frobenius_mass_gt_two_n`).  At `e = 2` this is `â‰ˆ n^{3/2}` at `tآ² = n` â€” failure
  one notch below the Johnson threshold `2n`, matching the projective-plane envelope
  (the family IS the affine plane `AG(2,p)`, sheared into a function graph).
* **"the capped optimum / explainable-core supply is linear in `n` at fixed `(k,m)`"
  (the growth-law census conjecture, measured at prime `q`) â€” REFUTED in any-domain
  form.**  At fixed `(k, m) = (2, pâˆ’3)`: explainable-`p`-core count
  `= n(nâˆ’1)/(p(pâˆ’1)) = خک(nآ²)` (`frobenius_supply_floor`); against the named Prop:
  any `B` for `ExplainableCoreSupply dom 2 (pâˆ’3) B` has `n(nâˆ’1) â‰¤ p(pâˆ’1)آ·B`
  (`frobenius_charter_floor`).  The prime-`q` census probes were structurally blind
  to this: the mechanism needs a proper subfield.

What is NOT refuted: `ExplainableCoreSupply` with `B` subexponential in the witness
mass (the Frobenius supply is only quadratic â€” the self-cap `deg(X^p âˆ’ aX âˆ’ b) = p`
bounds every agreement at `p`); and the production setting `خ¼_n âٹ‚ ً‌”½_q` with `q` prime,
where the only `ً‌”½_p`-lines have size `q â‰« n`.  Consequence for the programme: the
sub-Johnson supply statement is irreducibly DOMAIN-coupled â€” any proof must use the
absence of `ً‌”½_p`-affine structure in the evaluation domain (no-large-subfield /
additive-unstructuredness of `خ¼_n`), not just `w âˆ‰ C` and pairwise distance.

#### Addendum (same day, second lane): COMPOSITE subfields obstruct too â€” at every band (`SubplaneSupplyFloor.lean`)

The Frobenius-secant entry above leaves two gaps, both now closed
(`SubplaneSupplyFloor.lean`, axiom-clean; probe `probe_subplane_supply.py`, exact at
`Fâ‚‰/K=Fâ‚ƒ`, `Fâ‚‚â‚‡/K=Fâ‚ƒ`, `Fâ‚پâ‚†/K=Fâ‚„`):

* **Any prime-power subfield `K`, `r = |K|` â€” not just the prime one.**  Shear
  `AG(2, K)` into `F` by `(a,b) â†¦ a + خ»b`, `خ» âˆ‰ K`: domain `n = rآ²` points, word
  `w(a+خ»b) = b`.  The `rآ²+r` lines of the plane become honest degree-`<2` codewords
  (`y = (sx+c)/(1+خ»s)`, `y = (xâˆ’aâ‚€)/خ»`) agreeing with `w` on exactly `r = âˆڑn` points
  each, pairwise `â‰¤ 1` (probe: agreement histogram is `{0,1,r}` ONLY).  In char 2 the
  Frobenius mechanism is vacuous (`t = 2` fills no core) â€” here `K = ً‌”½_{2^j}` bites at
  every `j`: **binary-tower (char-2 production) fields carry the explosion whenever
  the evaluation domain contains a sheared `K`-plane; only PRIME fields are immune to
  the subfield mechanism.**
* **Every band at once**: `subplane_supply_floor` â€” any `B` for
  `ExplainableCoreSupply dom 2 m B` has `(rآ²+r)آ·C(r, 2+m+1) â‰¤ B`, for ALL `m` with
  `2+m+1 â‰¤ r` simultaneously (`â‰ˆ n^{(m+3)/2}`, superpolynomial at every fixed band
  depth) â€” vs the single tuned band `2+m+1 = p` of the secant floor.

Unchanged verdict, sharpened scope: the sub-Johnson supply statement is irreducibly
domain-coupled, the coupling needed is *absence of sheared-subfield-plane structure*,
and the surviving positive targets are prime fields and (conjecturally) smooth `خ¼_n`
domains â€” for binary-tower deployments the supply route is closed unconditionally at
subfield-aligned domains.

## 2026-06-12 â€” خ¼_n-immunity is q-CONDITIONAL: prime-field, multiplicative-subgroup linear supply REFUTED at `n = خک(q)` (the generic-density phase)

Claim refuted: "`SubJohnsonSupplyResidual dom k m B` holds with `B = O(n)` (or any
`B = o(C(n,t)/q^{m+1})`, `t = k+m+1`) for multiplicative subgroups `خ¼_n âٹ‚ ً‌”½_q`,
`q` prime" â€” the surviving hope of the Frobenius entry above ("the production setting
`خ¼_n âٹ‚ ً‌”½_q` with `q` prime") and the convergence comments' "خ¼_n tracks the random set
`~O(n)`".  Both were measured at `q â‰« n` only.

Witness (`MonomialSupplyWitness.lean` + the pair-hash probe): `w = x^t`, `t = k+m+1`.
Capped for free (`monomial_word_agreement_le`: agreements `â‰¤ t â‰¤ 2k+m+1`); a `t`-set
`T` is an explainable core iff the remainder window of `x^t mod âˆڈ_{iâˆˆT}(Xâˆ’x_i)`
vanishes (`explainableOn_of_remainder_degree_lt` â€” the remainder IS the codeword;
`monomial_supply_ge`).  The window is codimension `m+1`: density `C(n,t)/q^{m+1}`.
Exact counts at `(k,m) = (2,1)`, `t = 4`, cap `4 â‰¤ 6`:

* full units of `ً‌”½â‚پâ‚‚â‚‡` (`n = 126`): **630 = 5.0آ·n** cores (agreement profile
  `{1:5418, 2:3969, 3:42, 4:630}` â€” cap verified);
* full units of `ً‌”½â‚‚â‚…â‚‡` (`n = 256`): **2,624 = 10.25آ·n**;
* **`خ¼â‚„â‚€â‚‰â‚† âٹ‚ ً‌”½â‚پâ‚‚â‚‚â‚ˆâ‚‰` â€” THE standard 2-smooth NTT domain: 103,424 = 25.25آ·n**,
  33% ABOVE the generic `C(n,4)/qآ² â‰ˆ 77,546` â€” subgroup vanishing-sum arithmetic
  ENHANCES the density; the opposite of immunity;
* full units of `ً‌”½â‚ƒâ‚پ` (the census scale): 30 = 1.0آ·n â€” invisible; the measured
  "linear growth law" (30/46/67/86 at `q = 31`) was a scale artifact.

Consequences: (i) the unconditional pair-count ceiling `B = C(n,k)` is asymptotically
TIGHT at `n ~ q` (within the `t!`-constant) â€” at fixed rate and `n = خک(q)` the supply
question is closed at `خک(n^k)`; (ii) the open charter is now `q`-conditional:
`q^{m+1} â‰³ n^{tâˆ’1}/t!` (production `q â‰¥ 2^128` is safely inside); (iii) the corrected
exact-solution target is the **two-phase law** `E_max(خ¼_n) = خک_{k,m}(n + C(n,t)/q^{m+1})`
â€” arithmetic/fibre families rule `q â‰« n`, generic density rules `n = خک(q)`, and any
positive proof must use BOTH the additive-unstructuredness of `خ¼_n` (Frobenius entry)
AND the field-size hypothesis (this entry).
## 2026-06-12 â€” the sub-Johnson supply curve IS small-set Szemerأ©diâ€“Trotter (Fable; `GridSupplyRefutation.lean`)

A reframing entry, complementary to the two-branch/Frobenius/subplane/monomial
witnesses above.  All of those exhibit blowup; this one *names the curve* and points
at a formalizable unconditional improvement.

Observation: for `k = 2` an explainable `t`-core (`t = k+m+1 â‰¥ 3`) is exactly a
`t`-subset of the word's graph `P = {(dom i, w i)} âٹ‚ ً‌”½_qآ²` that is collinear, and (for
`t â‰¥ 3`) it lies on a *unique* affine codeword.  Hence **explainable-core supply =
`خ£_{lines â„“} C(a_â„“, t)` = the rich-line incidence count of the graph point set** â€”
the Szemerأ©diâ€“Trotter rich-line problem.  The capped residual restricts to `a_â„“ â‰¤ t+2`.

Witness (`GridSupplyRefutation.lean`, axiom-clean, `n = 36`, `ZMod 41`, fixed band
`(k,m) = (2,1)`, `t = 4`, cap `6`): the `6أ—6` sheared grid `(i,j) â†¦ (i+7j, j)` (abscissae
= base-7 digits âں¹ word graph; shears preserve lines).  `grid_word_cap` proves the cap
*structurally* (no root budget: a non-constant line meets each constant row `â‰¤ 1أ—`; six
rows) âں¹ the word is in the `SubJohnsonSupplyResidual gridDom 2 1` class.  Yet
`subJohnsonSupplyResidual_floor_grid`: every valid `B â‰¥ 234`, strictly above the
partition target `90` and the (now-dead) mean-degree-law target `180`.  Asymptotically
(`probe_grid_supply_refutation.py`) the family-capped mass is the ST extremal
`خ£ a_â„“ = خک(nآ²/tآ²)`, violation `أ—11.2` at `n = 400` â€” and the construction is over â„¤,
so it transfers to `ً‌”½_p` for every prime `p > 2Nآ³` (subfield-free production primes
included), the prime-field counterpart of the subplane mechanism.

* **REFUTED**: "the mean-degree law `خ£ a_â„“ â‰¤ 2n` holds on the residual's hypothesis
  class at a fixed band" (the `n â‰¤ 20` census stopped below the ST onset `n ~ tآ³`).
* **NOT refuted, and the corrected shape target**: for additive/`خ¼_n` domains the
  rich-line count obeys the small-set finite-field ST conjecture
  `L_{â‰¥t} = O(nآ²/tآ³ + n/t)`.  Concrete **unconditional partial**: Stevensâ€“de Zeeuw
  (via Rudnev's point-plane bound) gives `I(P,L) â‰² |P|^{11/15}|L|^{11/15} + |P| + |L|`
  in the production range `p â‰« poly(n)`, hence `L_{â‰¥t} â‰² n^{11/4}/t^{15/4}` â€” which
  **beats the packing bound `nآ²/tآ²` for every `t > n^{3/7}`**, i.e. on the band
  `(n^{3/7}, âˆڑ(2n))` strictly below Johnson `âˆڑ(2n)`.  This is the first unconditional
  strictly-sub-Johnson supply improvement; formalized as a named-residual import +
  consumer chain in `STSupplyReduction.lean` (the heavy Rudnev bound is the import
  surface; the reduction `RichLineBound â†’ capped supply â†’ bad-set count` is proven).

## 2026-06-13 â€” THE UNIVERSAL MEAN-DEGREE LAW IS FALSE (two-branch parabola countermodel); the supply growth law is two-regime

The conjectured universal form of the mean-degree law ("`خ£_c a_c â‰¤ 2n` over the
capped large-agreement family of any word", probe census `717da6067` / the #389
thread's "final measured form") is **REFUTED** in the open sub-Johnson range
`tآ² < 2(kâˆ’1)n` (`TwoBranchSupplyCountermodel.lean`, axiom-clean):

* **The countermodel family**: split `D = A âٹ” B`, set `w = xآ²` on `A`,
  `xآ² + c` on `B`.  Every degree-`<2` polynomial agrees with each branch on
  `â‰¤ 2` points (root budget), so every codeword agreement is `â‰¤ 4 â‰¤ 6 =
  2k+m+1`: **agreement-capped unconditionally**.  The line through
  `(xâ‚پ,xâ‚پآ²), (xâ‚‚,xâ‚‚آ²)` meets branch `B` at the roots of
  `zآ² âˆ’ (xâ‚پ+xâ‚‚)z + (xâ‚پxâ‚‚+c)`; when the discriminant `(xâ‚پâˆ’xâ‚‚)آ² âˆ’ 4c` is a
  nonzero square with both roots in `B`, the line is 4-rich.  About `1/8` of
  the `A`-pairs qualify: `خ£ a_c ~ nآ²/16`, **quadratic**.
* **Machine-checked instance** `(q,n) = (101, 80)`: interval domain
  `{0..79}`, `A = {0..39}`, `c = 29`: `107` four-rich lines (census-exact:
  these are ALL the `â‰¥4`-rich lines; max agreement `4`), `خ£ a_c = 428 > 160 =
  2n`.  `universalMeanDegreeLaw_REFUTED`.
* **Why the censuses missed it**: at `q = 31, n â‰¤ 24` the two-branch count
  sits below `2n` â€” the crossover `nآ³ â‰ˆ 64qآ²` is exactly where the growth
  census stopped.  `probe_two_branch_subjohnson_supply.py`: 21/25 instances
  violate (first at `p = n = 41`, exact counting); the linear-B form
  (`â‰ˆ 3.625n`) falls from `p = 251` (957 cores vs 910).
* **What survives**: `mean_degree_law_deep` and the crossing/CS route are
  hypothesis-guarded (`tآ² â‰¥ ~2(kâˆ’1)n`) and unaffected â€” and now provably
  SHARP: below the threshold the truth is a different growth law, not a
  missing word-coupling refinement of the linear one.
* **The corrected named target** (`CappedSupplyTwoRegimeLaw`, PRIME fields):
  `S_max(capped) = خک(n + C(n,k+m+1)/q^{m+1})` â€” partition floor + random
  mean.  The two-branch family realizes the mean term constructively (and
  `r`-branch words `x^k + c_j` extend this to every band `m` with
  `rآ·k â‰¤ 2k+m+1`).  The general-field form is FALSE by subfield transport:
  over `q = pآ²` with `D = F_p âٹ‚ F_{pآ²}` the same construction carries
  `~ nآ²/64` cores while the mean term collapses to `O(1)` â€” char-2/extension
  production settings need a no-large-subfield-structure hypothesis.
  Consumer arithmetic: any `B = O(n + C(n,t)/q^{m+1})` still delivers
  prize-grade bad-scalar counts through `deep_band_badSet_card_of_residual`
  (`#badSet â‰³ min(C(n,t)/(qلµگآ·n), q/const)`) â€” the wall is recalibrated, not
  destroyed.
## 2026-06-12 â€” "Linear/subexponential supply on smooth domains" is FALSE for DYADIC domains (multiplicative subspace polynomials)

The rounds 78â€“84 census conjecture â€” capped supply `خ£_c C(a_c,t) = O(n)` (`B = O(n)`),
"empirically true with subexponential `B`" â€” is **refuted for the explicit smooth 2-adic
(FFT) domains the prize targets**. The linear value is the *random-RS / Singleton-optimal*
list size; structured smooth domains beat it exponentially. The census hill-climbs missed
the structured extremizer, exactly as hill-climbing misses BKR subspace polynomials.

**Construction** (`EsymmFiber.lean`, axiom-clean; `probe_coset_union.py`, full-enum
verified خ¼â‚پâ‚†/خ¼â‚ƒâ‚‚). Domain `خ¼_n`, `n = 2^خ¼`. Reformulation: a degree-`t` word's explainable
`t`-cores are the `t`-subsets `A` with prescribed `e_1..e_{m+1}` (Vieta;
`explainable_iff_forcedPoly_degree`). For `w = wtآ·X^t + (deg<k)` the prescribed values are
`0`. A union `A` of `s` cosets of `خ¼_d` (`d = 2^j â‰¥ m+2`, `sآ·d = t`) has
`âˆڈ_{aâˆˆA}(Xâˆ’a) = expand_d(Q)` âˆˆ `F[X^d]`, so `e_1(A)=â€¦=e_{dâˆ’1}(A)=0` âٹ‡ the constraints, and
the forced explainer has degree `â‰¤ tâˆ’d = kâˆ’1 < k`. Hence every such union is an explainable
core (`explainable_of_expand`), and there are `C(n/d, t/d)` of them
(`smooth_dyadic_supply_lower_bound`):

  `#explainable t-cores â‰¥ C(n/d, t/d) = 2^{H(دپ)آ·n/dآ·(1+o(1))}`  â€” EXPONENTIAL.

Regime: agreement `خ± = t/n = دپ + d/n`, just above capacity, strictly sub-Johnson
(`خ± < âˆڑدپ`). `خ¼_d âٹ‚ خ¼_n` = the multiplicative analogue of an additive BKR subspace
polynomial; needs only the multiplicative 2-adic subgroup tower (no subfield â€” works over
prime fields). Consistent with: only random RS reaches capacity (GG25 2025/2054); the
up-to-capacity proximity-gap conjecture is false (BCIS-2025 2025/2055, CS25 2025/2046); the
barrier is the list-decoding radius (PGâ‡’LD, BCIS-2025 Thm 1.9).

**Status:** the "subexponential uniform supply `B`" supply statement is FALSE on `خ¼_{2^خ¼}`
at the deep band. Any positive supply/MCA result there must restrict the domain away from
dyadic structure or accept exponential `B`. Refutation is machine-checked (not a wall â€” a
proved negative). The campaign's recent linear claim should be re-scoped to random/generic
domains; the prize's smooth-domain case is exponentially worse and this is the honest state.

### O158 â€” THE PRODUCTION-RATE OBSTRUCTION: the multiplicative-subspace exponential supply is VACUOUS at fixed production دپ â€” it requires vanishing rate, exactly like DG25 (nubs incidence/census lane, 2026-06-13)

Boundary map of `EsymmFiber.smooth_dyadic_supply_lower_bound` (the just-landed "smooth
dyadic domains have exponential sub-Johnson supply") vs the prize's production parameters.
Claimed #389 comment 4697214100; exact integer arithmetic, `scripts/probes/incidence/boundary/`.

**The construction's firing condition (exact, from the Lean hypotheses):** a خ¼_d-coset
union is an explainable t-core iff `d = 2^j | t`, `d â‰¥ m+2`, `t = k+m+1`; supply
`C(n/d, t/d)`. Exponential supply requires `d = O(1)` (so `n/d â†’ âˆ‍`).

**The obstruction (verified mu=5..14, all production rates):** at fixed production rate
`دپ âˆˆ {1/2,1/4,1/8,1/16}` (so `k = دپآ·2^خ¼` is a large power of 2), the best coset-union
supply rate `logâ‚‚(supply)/n â†’ 0` as the domain grows â€” **POLYNOMIAL, not exponential**
(دپ=1/2: identically 0 at every خ¼; دپ=1/4,1/8,1/16: halving each خ¼-step). 2-adic mechanism:
exponential needs constant `d=2^j â‰¥ m+2` dividing `k+m+1`; for `k=2^a` and constant `m`,
`2^j | (2^a+m+1)` with `2^j â‰¥ m+2` forces `2^j | (m+1)` âˆ§ `2^j â‰¥ m+2` âں¹ `m+1 â‰¥ 2^j â‰¥ m+2`,
impossible. The only firings have `d âˆ‌ n` (giving `C(O(1),O(1))` = constant supply) or
sit at the s=1,2 single/few-coset tips (linear supply, rate â†’ 0).

**Where the exponential bite actually lives:** non-production rates `دپ â†’ 0` (fixed small
absolute `k`, rate shrinking with n) â€” e.g. k=5,9,13 show genuine positive supply rate.
**This is exactly the DG25 escape** (DISPROOF_LOG آ§3/Loop: DG25's super-poly capacity
disproof needs vanishing rate `دپ â‰ˆ eآ·n^{1/3}/n â†’ 0`, not fixed دپ). The multiplicative-
subspace construction is the explicit-smooth-domain shadow of DG25 and respects the SAME
rate barrier â€” it does not cross into fixed-rate territory.

**Consequence for #389 / the pin:** the EsymmFiber theorem is true and axiom-clean, but
its defeat of subexponential `ExplainableCoreSupply` (and hence any threat to
`CensusDomination` / the خ´* pin) is confined to vanishing-rate regimes. **At the
production rates the prize actually targets, this construction yields only polynomial
supply â€” it does NOT break the pin there.** HONEST SCOPE: this maps ONE construction's
support; it does NOT prove `CensusDomination` (other supply sources may exist, e.g. the
Frobenius-subfield blowup O-entry, which is additive-structure-coupled and a separate
question). It removes the multiplicative-subspace route as a production-rate obstruction
and pins it to the same vanishing-rate barrier as the known capacity disproofs.

## 2026-06-13 â€” THE SUPPLY-SIDE PACKING BOUND IS WORST-CASE VACUOUS; the line-partition is the algebraic fix (Fable)

Distinct from the failure-side packing entries above (those bound bad-scalar *counts* from
below): this is about the **supply** (`ExplainableCoreSupply`, the #389 positive direction),
which counts explainable `(k+m+1)`-cores of a word `w` from above.

**The exact identity** (`ExplainableCoreExactCount.lean`, axiom-clean):
`#cores(w) = خ£_c C(|agreeSet c w|, k+m+1)` â€” each core lies in exactly one codeword's
agreement set (`k+m+1 > kâˆ’1` âں¹ uniqueness via `rsCode_pairwise_agreeSet_card_le`). So the
supply IS the agreement-size profile `(a_c)`.

**The refutation.** Combined with the `k`-subset packing `خ£_c C(a_c,k) â‰¤ C(n,k)` and the fact
that `C(a,k+m+1)/C(a,k)` is increasing in `a`, the extremal profile is a SINGLE codeword with
`a_c â‰ˆ n`, giving `#cores â‰ˆ C(n,k+m+1)` = the trivial bound (`probe_supply_extremal.py`:
single/trivial = 1.0000 at every production scale n=16â€¦256). **So no purely combinatorial /
packing / Johnson-first-moment argument can give a sub-trivial per-word supply** â€” a word
near a single codeword saturates it. Any supply proof MUST use the algebraic structure of
which words arise as bad-scalar lines.

**The line-partition fix** (`LineCorePartition.lean`, axiom-clean): for the bad-scalar line
`w_خ³ = uâ‚€ + خ³آ·uâ‚پ` with `uâ‚پ = xلµڈ` far from the code (agreement `< k+m+1`),
`line_core_unique_scalar` shows each core is explainable for â‰¤ 1 scalar (secant-slope
codeword would agree with `uâ‚پ` on `k+m+1` points), so `line_total_cores_le`:
`خ£_خ³ #cores(w_خ³) â‰¤ C(n,k+m+1)`. This is the line-level (aggregate) supply â€” the algebraic
constraint the refutation proved is required. NOT a full closure: it bounds the line aggregate,
not the per-scalar worst case; the per-scalar gap remains the open wall, but it is now precisely
localized to "is the worst-case-خ³ core count `â‰ھ` the line average `C(n,a)/q`?"

## 2026-06-13 â€” smooth-domain خ´* tracks subgroup ARITHMETIC, not smoothness (QR-bad / 2-power-good)

A structural finding from the exact m=0 (cubic) supply on two smooth domains, both
machine-checked, that sharpens what "smooth-domain immunity" can mean:

* **2-power NTT domain `خ¼_16 âٹ‚ Fâ‚‚â‚…â‚‡`** (`cubicSupply_mu16_F257_eq_zero`): cubic word
  `xآ³` has **exactly 0** explainable 3-cores at radius `13/16` (one step below
  capacity) â€” the char-0 Mann rigidity (no cube root in `خ¼_{2^k}` âں¹ no three 2-power
  roots of unity sum to zero) **survives to `Fâ‚‚â‚…â‚‡`**.  خ´*-GOOD.
* **QR index-2 domain `QR* âٹ‚ F_q`** (`qr_zeroSum_ordered_card`): the cubic ordered
  zero-sum count is `8آ·#ord + 6q = qآ² + 5`, i.e. `(qâˆ’1)(qâˆ’5)/8 = خک(nآ²)` â€” the
  additively rich index-2 subgroup carries **quadratic** near-capacity cubic supply.
  خ´*-BAD.

So a domain being "smooth" (a multiplicative subgroup) is NOT enough for a good خ´*;
the relevant invariant is the **additive structure of the specific subgroup**.  The
production NTT domains (`خ¼_{2^k}`, FFT) are exactly the arithmetically-rigid good ones;
index-2 (QR) is bad.  This is consistent with the GV/HBK programme (the additive energy
`E(خ¼_n)` is the governing quantity) and with `representationCount_eq_gcd_degree`
(`GVRepBound G M âں؛ deg gcd(Xâپ؟âˆ’1, (Ccâˆ’X)âپ؟âˆ’1) â‰¤ M`, no slack): the gcd degree, hence
the energy, hence خ´*, is a subgroup-arithmetic quantity.  CAVEAT: `خ¼_16/Fâ‚‚â‚…â‚‡`'s zero is
PRIME-DEPENDENT (other primes give positive cubic supply for `n=16`); there is no
general `gcd(3,n)=1 âں¹ supply 0` transfer to `F_p` (the char-0 rigidity does not
transfer in general â€” that gap is exactly why the unconditional bound needs Stepanov).

### O160 â€” SidonModNeg(خ¼_n) holds far below âˆڑp but the threshold is NOT universal-in-âˆڑp (probe + refutation, 2026-06-13)

Direct attack on the energyExcess core (`EnergyExcessCore.lean`): the whole #389 cubic/energy
wall closes iff `energyExcess(خ¼_n) = Eâپ؛(خ¼_n) âˆ’ (3nآ²âˆ’3n) = 0`, i.e. iff `خ¼_n` is `SidonModNeg`
(`AdditiveEnergySidonModNeg.lean`; in-tree `E â‰¤ 3nآ²` is conditional on it).

**Probe (`scripts/probes/probe_sidonmodneg_threshold.py`, exact `Eâپ؛` in `F_p`):** small
multiplicative subgroups are EXACTLY Sidon-mod-negation (`excess = 0`, `E = 3nآ²âˆ’3n` exactly) far
below the random heuristic â€” e.g. `p=786433, n=256`: `nآ³/p â‰ˆ 21` exotic quadruples predicted,
**0 observed**. Holds for any order (not just 2-power): `n=6..192 | pâˆ’1` all zero-excess.

**Bold conjecture E'' (exact universal minimality, `n â‰¤ câˆڑp âں¹ excess 0`): REFUTED.** No universal
multiplicative-in-âˆڑp constant exists:
- `p=786433`: zero excess at `n=256` (`n/âˆڑp = 0.289`).
- `p=23068673`: ALREADY nonzero at `n=1024` (`n/âˆڑp = 0.213`).
Since `0.213 < 0.289`, no constant `c` with `n â‰¤ câˆڑp âں¹ excess 0` fits both. The
Sidon-mod-negation threshold is genuinely p-dependent (number-theoretic), not a clean power law.
(Flip points `n/âˆڑp`: 0.32, 0.58, 0.38, 0.21 across p = 40961, 786433, 7340033, 23068673.)

**What survives (honest):** the PRIZE regime `n â‰² 2آ³âپ° â‰ھ âˆڑq â‰ˆ 2آ¹آ²âپ¸` sits ~2âپ¹âپ¸ below every observed
flip, so `SidonModNeg(خ¼_n)` (hence `energyExcess = 0`, `E = 3nآ²âˆ’3n`) is empirically overwhelming
there â€” but proving it needs the p-dependent threshold, which Weil/character-sum bounds do NOT
force to exactly zero (the observed suppression is stronger than Weil predicts; an open
number-theoretic fact).

**Why this does NOT pin خ´* (the load-bearing caveat):** even with `energyExcess = 0`, خ´* is not
closed. Zero excess controls the additive-energy / cubic-supply front only. The **capacity-edge
supply is a SEPARATE mechanism** (`CS25*`: deep-hole + random-word combinations cover
small-distance balls, `خµ_mca â†’ 1` at capacity, NOT additive-energy-governed), which sets the upper
bracket `خ´* â‰¤ 1âˆ’دپâˆ’خک(1/log n)`. Residual-free خ´* therefore requires BOTH the subgroup sum-product
front (this entry) AND the covering / list-decoding-capacity front (CS25/BCHKS Conj 1.12) â€”
two independent recognized-hard problems. No fabricated closure.

### O161 â€” the coset/power-word construction is NOT the extremal max-list word (Conjecture J refuted, 2026-06-13)

Direct attack on خ´*: the in-tree `rootsOfUnity_dyadic_codeword_list_ge` gives list `â‰¥ C(n/d, t/d)`
via coset-union subsets; worked against the budget `list = qآ·خµ*` with `q = poly(n)` this reproduces
the BCHKS upper bracket `خ´* â‰¤ 1âˆ’دپâˆ’خک(H(دپ)/log n)` CONSTRUCTIVELY. **Conjecture J** asked whether this
construction is also the MAXIMUM list (which would pin خ´* residual-free).

**Probe (`scripts/probes/probe_extremal_word.py`, exact list enumeration, Fâ‚‰â‚‡, خ¼â‚پâ‚†, k=2):**
- `t=3` (sub-Johnson): `L(xآ³) = 0` (this خ¼â‚پâ‚† has no zero-sum triples) but `max over 40 random
  words = 11`. The power word is BELOW the random baseline; the coset construction (degenerate
  here, 3âˆ¤16) gives 0. The true max-list word is neither the power word nor coset-structured.
- `t=4`: `L(xâپ´)=4`, random max 3 (power word competitive here); `t=5`: all 0.

**Verdict: Conjecture J REFUTED.** The coset/power-word construction is a valid list LOWER bound
(hence a valid خ´* UPPER bracket) but is NOT extremal â€” the maximum sub-Johnson list is larger and
word-dependent. Pinning خ´* requires the extremal/worst-word characterization (H-MAX), which this
shows is not the obvious construction. No fabricated closure; H-MAX remains the open residual.

**Caveat:** small-scale, `max-over-40-random` is a weak lower bound on the true max (could be far
larger); this strengthens, not weakens, the refutation (even a weak search beats the construction).

### O162 â€” H-MAX (extremal middle-band list) â†” subset-sum fibres â†” additive energy: the loop closes (2026-06-13)

### O164 â€” H-MAX deep-band fiber is O(1) at production primes (the e-symm map is near-injective/Sidon); a small-prime SATURATION GUARDRAIL (nubs, 2026-06-13)

subsets into pآ² = 9409 targets âں¹ "max fiber" 51,160 â‰ˆ the pigeonhole average, fully
saturated (#targets hit = 9409 = pآ², all of them). This is a small-prime artifact, NOT
supply â€” the same char-0â†’mod-p failure mode O134 flagged, in its most extreme form.

**The faithful result (production-scale prime p = 2013265921):** at n=16, m=1, both
production rates, the e-symm targets are NOT saturated (C(16,10) = 8008 â‰ھ pآ²), and the
TRUE max fiber over ALL targets is **3** (brute-materialized: 7864 distinct targets for
8008 subsets â€” the map is near-injective). The structured zero-target (eâ‚پ=eâ‚‚=0) fiber is
**0**. So the deep-band supply is **O(1)**, not exponential: the p=97 numbers were pure
saturation.  (matching-trick fiber
counter, validated against brute force).

**Alignment + consequence:** max fiber O(1) âں؛ the e-symm map on t-subsets of خ¼_n is
near-injective â€” exactly the fleet's  (O160) and 
(E = 3nآ²âˆ’3n) framing; this is the empirical confirmation of that analytic attack. So
**H-MAX (no exponential deep-band supply at production) holds empirically**, and the
asymptotic closure is precisely the Stepanov/Sidon/additive-energy bound the fleet is

analytic (Stepanov) bound, not brute-measured (production-prime materialization is
memory-prohibitive). Combined with O158/O159 (both structured explosion mechanisms

constructions vacuous, the e-symm map near-injective âں¹ no known route to super-polynomial
supply â€” the CensusDomination premise, supported empirically; the proof is the fleet's

## 2026-06-13 â€” "E(خ¼_n) â‰¤ Cآ·nآ²آ·log n for ALL pâ‰،1 mod n" is FALSE (Fermat-prime degeneracy); restrict to n â‰¤ âˆڑp (Fable fleet)

field (n â‰ˆ p). The correct, surviving form restricts to the **small/boundary subgroup regime
n â‰¤ âˆڑp** (the production NTT range, n â‰¤ âˆڑp for Goldilocks): there the worst-case energy is
â‰ˆ 1.3آ·nآ²آ·ln n (probe_worstcase_energy, probe_boundary; ratio E/(nآ²ln n) BOUNDED and DECREASING
2.62â†’0.74 across n=8..256). So: the nآ²log n bound is FALSE universally, TRUE (empirically) for
n â‰¤ âˆڑp â€” exactly the deployed regime. The boundary nâ‰ˆâˆڑp bound remains the open core (= GV/HBK
square-root-cancellation, faces B2/B3 below n^{5/2}).

- The boundary `r â‰ˆ âˆڑ(2^خ¼) = âˆڑn` (at m=1) COINCIDES with `packing_covers_sqrt` (the
  elementary route reaches exactly ~âˆڑn) and `packing_exceeds_budget_deep_band` (fails at

### O168 â€” O163 "energy silent at prize scale" is now a CHECKED conditional theorem (nubs, 2026-06-13, goal clause 3)

O163 stated only in PROSE that the GV/Stepanov additive-energy bound makes the cubic/energy
supply silent at prize scale. Now a theorem (`CubicSupplySilentPrizeScale.lean`, axiom-clean
[propext,Classical.choice,Quot.sound], independently compiled + single-module build green):

* `cubicSupply_silent_at_prize_scale` â€” under `GVRepBound (image dom univ) M` (the in-tree
  integer-clean form of the cited GV/Stepanov `E(خ¼_n) â‰² n^{8/3}`, kept as a NAMED HYPOTHESIS,
  never asserted â€” the TZPrimeSupply pattern) and `n â‰¤ 2^40` (prize domain bound), the cubic
  word's explainable-3-core supply `S` satisfies `S < 2^128 = خµ*آ·q`. The energy/cubic
  mechanism CANNOT breach the prize threshold â€” it is silent.
* `gv_supply_envelope_lt` â€” the bare numeric kernel `260آ·(2^40)^11 < (2^128)^6` (norm_num).
* Proof = the landed capstone `cubicSupply_pow_le_of_gvRepBound` (`S^6 â‰¤ 260آ·n^11`) +
  monotonicity in nâ‰¤2^40 + the envelope + strict monotonicity of xâ†¦x^6 (`lt_of_pow_lt_pow_leftâ‚€`).
  Margin huge: proven supply â‰ˆ 2^74.7 â‰ھ 2^128 (O163's prose â‰ˆ2^85 was a loose over-estimate).

HONEST SCOPE (goal clause 3 compliant): the GV energy bound is a HYPOTHESIS, never asserted
â€” this does NOT prove the energy bound (that's the open Stepanov/additive-energy input). It
proves the CONDITIONAL: given the cited bound, the energy route is provably below breach at
prize scale. Strengthens the dossier â€” the "energy mechanism is silent" leg of the
خ´*-residual map is now checked, not prose. Found via Opus-4.8 recon+prove+verify; the verify
lane independently reconstructed + axiom-confirmed it; I re-verified from a third seat before landing.

### O169 â€” the cubic-supply silence is UNCONDITIONAL: O168's GV hypothesis is REMOVABLE for the threshold (nubs, 2026-06-13)

Strengthening O168. `cubicSupply_silent_unconditional` (same file, axiom-clean, build green):
the cubic explainable-3-core supply is `< 2^128 = خµ*آ·q` at `n â‰¤ 2^40` with **NO additive-energy
hypothesis** â€” it is `â‰¤ C(n,3) â‰¤ nآ³ â‰¤ (2^40)آ³ = 2^120 < 2^128` by pure subset counting
(`Finset.card_filter_le` + `Nat.choose_le_pow`). So O168's `GVRepBound` was UNNECESSARY for the
silence threshold; the GV/Stepanov energy bound buys only the tighter estimate (â‰ˆ2^74.7 vs 2^120),
not the silence. (The in-tree unconditional order-2 Stepanov bound `additiveEnergy_lt_cube_stepanov`,
E(خ¼_n) < nآ³, gives the same via the energy route; the subset count is the cleanest.) Net: the
cubic/energy supply route is provably silent at prize scale UNCONDITIONALLY â€” one fewer residual
on that leg of the خ´*-map. Honest scope: this is the cubic (k=2) word's supply only â€” it does NOT
touch the general deep-band CensusDomination (still the open core); it cleanly closes the cubic leg.

### O170 â€” SELF-CORRECTION: O168/O169 cubic-supply silence is CORRECT but VACUOUS for the prize (cubic supply â‰، 0 on 2-power خ¼_n); the framing overclaimed (nubs, 2026-06-13)

Honesty correction, triggered by the fleet's d7feabd7a guardrail + independent re-verification.
O168/O169 prove the cubic (3-term zero-sum) explainable supply is < 2^128 at n â‰¤ 2^40. The

= 0 (4-term count = 6/28/120 = C(n/2,2)). So O168/O169 bound a quantity that is already ZERO for
the prize 2-power domains â€” they are **vacuous-for-prize**, and my framing "closes the cubic/energy
supply leg of the خ´*-map" OVERCLAIMED. RETRACTED.

**What's actually operative:** the supply for 2-power خ¼_n runs through the 4-term/even zero-sum
route (= additive energy E(خ¼_n)), NOT the cubic route. At production SPLIT primes the relevant
energy is the OPEN GV/Stepanov quantity E(خ¼_n)=n^{2+o(1)} â€” its prize-scale silence is governed by
that open bound (in-tree as the GVRepBound hypothesis), NOT closed by O168/O169. The exact value
E=3n(nâˆ’1) is the INERT/char-0 case; production primes are split, where it is the open problem.

NET (honest): O168/O169 stand as correct lemmas but contribute nothing to the prize 2-power supply
question (they bound 0). The energy-supply leg for the prize is NOT closed â€” it remains the open
GV/additive-energy bound. This entry corrects the record; no fabricated closure.

### O173 â€” THE SECOND-MOMENT PAIR-SUM GATE IS INSUFFICIENT: خ£_d A_d I_âˆ©(d) = خک(E[N]آ²) (NOT o(Eآ²)) through the upper window, and خک(1) overdispersion is exponentially blind to the KKH26 worst line â€” so the upper-window خ´* wall is genuinely worst-case extremality, not any moment bound (nubs, demand/list-decoding seat, 2026-06-13)

Targets lalalune's localization comment (2026-06-13), which proved خ´* = H_qâپ»آ¹(1âˆ’دپâˆ’log_q(1/خµ*)/n)
in the lower window and reduced the upper window to ONE explicit gate: "is خ£_d A_dآ·I_âˆ©(d) =
o(E[#bad]آ²) at threshold?" (A_d = MDS weight enumerator, I_âˆ©(d) = radius-R two-center ball
intersection). I built and computed that object exactly â€” probe_pairsum_gate.py, the named
"machinery to build next" (Opus 4.8 inline; implementations validated: خ£_w A_w = q^k exact,
I_âˆ©(0) = V(R) exact; lower-window switch lands precisely at (1âˆ’دپ)/2).

* **(a) Lower window خ´ < (1âˆ’دپ)/2 â€” MACHINE-CONFIRMED unconditional.** pair_term = خ£آ·q^n/(q^kآ·Vآ²)
  = EXACT 0: the dâˆˆ[d_min,2R] band is empty (2R<d_min=nâˆ’k+1), so no codeword pair has
  overlapping radius-R balls âں¹ worst = average âں¹ the closed form holds with zero residual.
  Verified at دپâˆˆ{1/4,1/2}, nâˆˆ{16,32,48,64}, q from 257 up to the genuine prize q=nآ·2آ¹آ²âپ¸. The
  switch lands at exactly (1âˆ’دپ)/2 (دپ=1/2,n=32: pair_term 0 at خ´=0.25, turns on at 0.281).
* **(b) Upper window â€” the gate's "o(Eآ²)" hope is FALSE.** pair_term rises from â‰ˆ0 just above
  (1âˆ’دپ)/2, PLATEAUS just below 1 across the whole window, and crosses 1 EXACTLY at capacity
  خ´=1âˆ’دپ (coincident with E[N]â†’1). So خ£_d A_d I_âˆ©(d) = خک(E[N]آ²), NOT o(E[N]آ²): the pair sum is
  COMPARABLE to the meanآ², Var â‰ˆ E[N]آ², only O(1) typical overdispersion. lalalune's hoped-for
  o(Eآ²) certificate does not exist in the upper window â€” refuted by exact in-regime arithmetic.
* **(c) Even خک(1) is exponentially blind to the wall.** KKH26 (ePrint 2026/782, Krachunâ€“Kazaninâ€“
  Habأ¶ck) PROVES a worst line with 2^{خ©(1/خ·)} close points at خ´=1âˆ’دپâˆ’خک(1/log n). The 2nd-moment
  overdispersion خک(1) â‰ھ 2^{خ©(1/خ·)}: the average pair sum cannot feel the measure-zero structured
  worst line. A small (or any) second moment is a typical/whp certificate (Chebyshev), never a
  worst-case one â€” so proving the gate could NOT close the upper window even if it were o(Eآ²).

**Where it lands:** the second-moment/pair-sum route to the upper-window خ´* is now RULED OUT as
a certificate â€” both because the gate is خک(Eآ²) not o(Eآ²), and because خک(1) overdispersion is
exponentially below the proven KKH26 worst line. This converts lalalune's "open: is the gate
o(Eآ²)?" into "answered NO, and it wouldn't suffice regardless." The lower window (خ´<(1âˆ’دپ)/2) is

combinatorial extremality argument, not a character-sum/interpolation/moment bound." Probe:
scripts/probes/probe_pairsum_gate.py.
### O174 â€” the closed-form-FREE per-line structural route: d-ary split + 2^r sign-symmetry + single-line dominance all CONFIRMED, but the per-line injection into C(n/2,r)آ·2^r BREAKS for râ‰¥4 (axis-support mismatch) â€” same open core as O172 (nubs, 2026-06-13)

max_d â‰¤ K = 2^rآ·C(n/2,r)) WITHOUT the O172 closed form. **Verdict: PARTIAL.** The per-line

* **d-ary split generalizes in FORM [confirmed].** High-freq line uâ‚€=د‡_d(i)=x_i^{n/d}, uâ‚پ=uâ‚€/x_i:
  aligned-S(خ³) âں؛ the aâ‚€=r+1 points lie on one deg-<(râˆ’1) curve; clearing 1/x_i gives
  P(x_i)âˆ’د‰ت²(x_i+خ³)=0, P free degâ‰¤râˆ’1; د‡_d constant per residue class mod d so S splits into d
  classes, class j = roots of Q_j=Pâˆ’د‰ت²(x+خ³), degâ‰¤râˆ’1. The Q_j form a PENCIL
  Q_jâˆ’Q_{j'}=âˆ’(د‰ت²âˆ’د‰^{j'})(x+خ³) â€” the d-cyclic product condition generalizing the r=3 antipodal
  pair-product (d=2: x_a x_b + x_c x_d = 0). Aligned sets DO split into د‡-class distributions
  (r=3 order-2: {(2,2):96,(4,):140}; r=8 order-16: all-1s).
* **OBSTRUCTION #1 (per-class size).** Naive "class size â‰¤ deg Q_j = râˆ’1" FAILS on degenerate
  classes: r=3 (4,)-distribution (140 sets, size 4 > râˆ’1=2) = the B=0 collinear-degenerate family
  that ALL collapse to the single خ³=0 (the "+1" in nآ·C(n/4,2)+1). Harmless to the count, breaks

* **OBSTRUCTION #2 (the real one â€” axis-support mismatch).** A per-line injection
  {bad خ³}â†’{r-subset of n/2 antipodal axes}أ—{2^r signs}=K requires aligned sets to touch exactly
  r axes. Measured axis-support: r=3 {2:12,3:128,4:96} (up to r+1, too many to fit r-subsets);

  #bad=104=2أ—52, bad-خ³ set CLOSED under negation خ³â†”âˆ’خ³; 2^r=256=K (C(8,8)=1). 2^r = the
  free-negation sign symmetry of the antipodal-balance engine (matches RESULTS-GENERAL-LAW).
* **NON-OBSTRUCTION: single-line dominance HOLDS.** Obligation = worst witness PAIR = one line,

  No harmful cross-line interference at any tested band.

  r=3) but |{خ³}|=|{âˆ’eâ‚پ}|=96=nآ·C(n/4,2); CF.md's "خ³=âˆ’eâ‚پ" is the in-tree witness_pin convention,
  count-equivalent, so the proven r=3 closed form stands.

**Where it lands:** the per-line bad set is the JOINT (eâ‚پ..e_{râˆ’1})-level-set whose cardinality

  higher-order additive energy E_{1,2}(خ¼_n) over a thin 2-power subgroup in the prize regime â€” the

### O176 â€” THE DEMAND-SIDE WALL LOCATED: general-r (râ‰¥4) #bad â‰¤ K has NO clean closed form (refuted) and its tightest clean bound #bad â‰¤ K/2 REDUCES-TO-OPEN â€” the exact new-math target is an upper bound on the e1-support of the (e1,e2) joint level-set = joint additive energy E_{1,2}(خ¼_n) over a thin 2-power subgroup, ABSENT from the literature (nubs, demand seat, 2026-06-13)

  slip: n=32 r=4 maximizer is (xآ¹âپ¶,xâپ¹)=3105 (NOT (xâپ¸,xâپµ)=865).
* **The object, exactly [PROVEN reductions].** Via the Vieta pin خ³=âˆ’eâ‚پ(S) (in-tree
  `badscalar_eq_neg_subset_sum`) and Newton eâ‚پآ²=pâ‚‚+2eâ‚‚, the deep-band #bad-scalar count =
  the **eâ‚پ-axis support of the (eâ‚پ,eâ‚‚) joint level-set** over خ¼_n = #{distinct خ£x over (r+1)-subsets
  S of خ¼_n with خ£x and خ£xآ² both line-forced}. Equivalently the support side of the joint additive
  energy **E_{1,2}(خ¼_n)** = #{(a,b,c,d): a+b=c+d, aآ²+bآ²=cآ²+dآ²} (and its (r+1)-fold analogue).
* **The bound, conjectured [CONJECTURE C-half, calibrated, NOT a closure].** #bad(r) â‰¤
  K/2 = 2^{râˆ’1}آ·C(n/2,r): dominates every measured rung (n=16 r=3..8 = 97,145,89,113,225,104;
  n=32 r=3..5), stays â‰¤ K, dominates the proven r=3 form; K/3 and K/4 FAIL at the binding band
  (n=16 r=8). But the /2 is a calibration FIT (no proof-handle), and the proof
  **REDUCES-TO-OPEN**: an upper bound on the eâ‚پ-support / E_{1,2}(خ¼_n) â€” which is **absent from
  the literature**, confirmed by a ruthless four-fold gap:
  1. **Ground set:** moment-subset-sum bounds (LMRW 1910.05894, GPP 2401.06964) are over the
     full field F_q or polynomial images (~q points), NEVER a thin multiplicative subgroup خ¼_n;
     GPP explicitly does not use multiplicative structure.
  2. **Object/moment-dimension:** they bound the per-target count Nâ‚‚(câ‚پ,câ‚‚) (~O(1), main term
     â‰ھ1, vacuous for thin G); the prize needs the 1-D eâ‚پ-support projection (~خک(nآ³) at r=3).
  3. **Parameter window:** they need k large, mâ‰ھk, kâ‰¤q^{0.24}; the prize is m=2 FIXED, k=r+1 small,
     deep band â€” outside every stated window.
  4. **q-dependence:** their bounds are q-dependent (/q^m, q^{k/2}); the prize count is
     q-INDEPENDENT poly(n) (O172 envelope: faithful = worst case). The additive-energy route
     (Hansonâ€“Petridis, Shkredov) has the right q-free object but is prime-field-calibrated with
     documented F_q-transfer failure (in-tree O30), the joint E_{1,2} is itself OPEN (only the

**Where it lands [HONEST, per the CLOSED requirement]:** r=3 PROVEN (O172/DeepBandR3Bound), a=4
slice PROVEN (A4CensusValue), general-r â‰¤ K is CONJECTURE C-half whose proof needs genuinely new

### O177 â€” THE r=4 RUNG PROVEN (axiom-clean Lean, #badâ‚„ â‰¤ K AND â‰¤ K/2 all n=2^k) extends the demand frontier r=3â†’r=4; equivariance orbit-reduction proven; and the open core is now ANCHORED to the quadratic Vinogradov / PTE framework (Mansfieldâ€“Mudgal 2024) â€” correcting O176, with a concrete forward lead (nubs, demand seat, 2026-06-14)

Three results this cycle (new-math fan-out B1/B2 + a deep literature sweep), all adversarially

  descent to the proven r=3 form** (the ev1/ev4 parity classes biject, even-sublatticeâ†’خ¼_{n/2},
  onto the r=3 maximizer). `deepBandBadCount4_le_budget` (#badâ‚„ â‰¤ K, gâ‰¥2) AND

  Exactness #bad=formula is [COMPUTED] (descent bijection, kernel n=16=145/32=3105/64=57409);
  the â‰¤K and â‰¤K/2 BOUNDS are [PROVEN] â€” same standard as the landed r=3 brick. **The parity-split
  STOPS at r=4**: the r=5 maximizer is the full-order line (not order-2), so this lever cannot
  pin râ‰¥5. HONESTY CATCH: a prior-session DeepBandR4Bound.lean carried `sorryAx` + 3 failing
  tactic blocks â€” caught, all three fixed, re-verified genuinely axiom-clean before landing.
* **Equivariance orbit-reduction [PROVEN structural law, B2].** `#bad = dآ·orbits + [0âˆˆbad]`,
  `d = n/gcd(n,eâˆ’f)`, orbit invariant `خ³^d` (خ³ = âˆ’eâ‚پ, Vieta-pinned); the bad set is خ¼_d-invariant
  and negation-closed (0 violations, n=16 r=3..8 / n=32 / n=64). For n=2^k, negation g^{n/2} is
  already a power of the rotation (antipodal-subsumption), so Lamâ€“Leung gives no extra global
  collapse â€” it must act on the orbit-reps. REFUTED the clean uniform bounds orbits â‰¤ C(n/2,2)
  and C(n/2,râˆ’1) (both asymptotically false). So uniform #bad â‰¤ K for râ‰¥4 reduces to the
  **orbit-rep census** = the joint (eâ‚پ,eâ‚‚) deficit-2 level-set mod rotation.
* **The open core ANCHORED + O176 CORRECTED [literature sweep].** The deep-band (خ£x, خ£xآ²)
  joint constraint is *literally* a degree-2 Prouhetâ€“Tarryâ€“Escott / **k=2 quadratic Vinogradov
  mean value system**; the collision count خ£ Nâ‚‚آ² = the summed joint energy. **Mansfieldâ€“Mudgal,
  "A Quadratic Vinogradov MVT in Finite Fields" (arXiv:2310.02950, QJM 2024)** bounds it:
  `J_s(A) â‰ھ |A|^{2sâˆ’2âˆ’1/9}` for arbitrary `A âٹ† F_p`, `sâ‰¥3` â€” covering our whole range. This
  **CORRECTS O176's claim that "the joint higher-order energy is itself open"**: the *summed*
  energy IS bounded in the literature. BUT it still does not pay the prize â€” three verified gaps:
  (1) WRONG DIRECTION (decisive): it upper-bounds the *energy*; Cauchyâ€“Schwarz then gives a
  *lower* bound on the support, while #bad is a support *upper* bound; (2) WRONG FUNCTIONAL:
  J_s = خ£ Nâ‚‚آ² (full collision count), not its eâ‚پ-axis projection (#bad); (3) FIELD: stated over
  F_p (Rudnevâ€“Wheeler / Stevensâ€“de Zeeuw incidence engine), F_q transfer carries an unproven
  no-large-subfield condition. **FORWARD LEAD (concrete, literature-grounded):** extract a
  *per-fiber* `Nâ‚‚(c1,c2)` bound from Mansfieldâ€“Mudgal's incidence machinery (rather than the
  summed J_s) â†’ converts the energy bound into the support bound `#bad â‰¤ K`. No paper does this
  for خ¼_n; that is the precise new-math step.

**Where it lands:** demand-side proven frontier is now **r=3 AND r=4** (both #bad â‰¤ K/2,

## (#407, conjecture campaign) GV rep bound r(c)â‰¤2 WORST-CASE at prize scale â€” REFUTED
Bold conjecture: `max_{câ‰ 0} r(c) â‰¤ 2` for ALL primes p~n^خ² (خ²=4â€“5), dyadic خ¼_n â€” would close the GV face.
REFUTED: the rep-bound bad primes (where r(c)>2) are IDENTICAL to the energy P_max bad primes
(8â†’41, 16â†’337, 32â†’194977 in a truncated sweep; the EXACT P_max ~ n^{4.87} is EXPONENTIAL, per
`probe_energy_pmax_growth.py`), which lie INSIDE the prize regime p~n^{4â€“5}. So `r(c)â‰¤2` holds only
GENERICALLY (G2, 80 random primes/n) â€” worst-case `r(c)>2` at bad primes âں¹ the Garcأ­aâ€“Voloch rep-bound
face is OPEN at prize scale. Clean by-product (kept): **rep-bound P_max = energy P_max** (the triple-
intersection obstruction `p|Res(x^nâˆ’1,(câˆ’x)^nâˆ’1)` is the same cyclotomic-norm divisibility as the
4-term additive-energy coincidence). Probe: `scripts/probes/probe_gv_repbound_pmax.py`.
Lesson re-confirmed: sweep to the EXACT P_max (cyclotomic norm), not a truncated prime range.

## (#407) The clean 2-adic descent `M(n) â‰¤ âˆڑ2آ·M(n/2)` (fleet 00:16 reframing) â€” REFUTED as a uniform inequality
`M(n)=max_b|S_b(خ¼_n)|`, the L^âˆ‍ sup-norm. The fleet reframed the open core as the descent
`M(n)â‰¤âˆڑ2آ·M(n/2)` (telescopes to âˆڑn). Measured ratio `M(n)/M(n/2)` over a fixed prime, FFT-exact:
n=8,16,32 â†’ 1.99,1.88,1.51 (VIOLATE, boundary); n=64..1024 â†’ 1.36,1.44,1.45,1.31,1.31. The ratio
oscillates AROUND âˆڑ2â‰ˆ1.414 and EXCEEDS it at n=128 (1.4374) and n=256 (1.4496). So the CLEAN uniform
`â‰¤âˆڑ2` descent is FALSE â€” the per-step cancellation has upward fluctuations and does not telescope to a
clean âˆڑn bound. The descent holds only in GEOMETRIC-MEAN / asymptotic sense (consistent with M~âˆڑ(n log p),
ratioâ†’âˆڑ2). The open gap is exactly whether the descent-fluctuations (the transfer-cocycle Lyapunov
exponent, cf `probe_dyadic_cocycle_recursion.py`) average to â‰¤(1/2)log2 with no worst-case upward bias â€”
the same moment-vs-max / sqrt-cancellation core, NOT closed by the descent reframing.
Probe: `scripts/probes/probe_descent_inequality.py`.

## (#407) Sharpening: the descent open core = a phase-alignment large-deviation, NOT a uniform descent
Following the refutation of the clean `M(n)â‰¤âˆڑ2 M(n/2)` descent (above): at the worst b, `S_b(خ¼_n)=A+B`,
`A=خ·_b(خ¼_{n/2})`, `B=خ·_{bخ¶}(خ¼_{n/2})` (fleet `GaussPeriodTower.lean`). The per-level ratio
`r=|A+B|/max(|A|,|B|) âˆˆ [âˆڑ2, 2]`: `=âˆڑ2` iff `Aâٹ¥B`, `=2` iff phase-ALIGNED (`cos=1`). So
`M(n)=âˆڈ_{j=1}^{k} r_j` and (since measured `M(n)~âˆڑ(n log p)`) `خ£_j log(r_j/âˆڑ2) â‰ˆ آ½ log log p` â€” MOST
levels are â‰ˆâˆڑ2 with small upward fluctuations. **The open core is therefore a WORST-CASE-PATH
(large-deviation) bound on the phase-alignment cocycle**, NOT a uniform per-step inequality: خ´* closes
iff no frequency b has a persistently-aligned path down the 2-adic tower (which would give M(n)~n).
Empirically M~âˆڑ(n log p)â‰ھn âں¹ no fully-aligned path exists (prize morally true), but PROVING the
worst-path accumulation â‰¤ âˆڑ(log p) is the recognized-open sup-norm/Lyapunov large-deviation problem.
This bounds what `GaussPeriodTower` (exact recursion) can yield: the recursion is exact, the closure
needs the cocycle large-deviation, which is the open analytic input. Probe: `probe_descent_inequality.py`.

## 2026-06-13 #407 â€” DEEP-MOMENT VALIDITY refuted (provably false in the prize regime)
Refuted the آ§3 "single open input": that `E_r(خ¼_n)` stays near its char-0 Bessel value for `r â‰چ log q`,
which would let the moment arrow `B â‰¤ (qآ·E_r âˆ’ n^{2r})^{1/2r}` yield the floor `âˆڑ(n log q)`.

DISPROOF (rigorous + numeric, `/tmp/char0_floor.py`, `forced_anomaly.py`; Bessel law verified exact
r=2..7, computed for any n via `E_r^{char0}=خ£_k C(n/2,k)g_{r,k}`):
1. The char-0 moment bound `(q E_r^{char0} âˆ’ n^{2r})^{1/2r}/floor` EXPLODES with n: 1.15, 1.96, 3.73,
   10.9, 19.8, 68.7, 246, 1724, 6420 for n=2^4..2^30 (خ²=4). It does NOT â†’ 1. The "yields the floor at
   râ‰چlog q" claim holds ONLY at small n (n=16: optimal r=11â‰ˆlog q, 1.15أ—floor) â€” a small-n artifact.
2. WHY: `E_r^{char0} ~ (2r)!آ·C(n/2,r) ~ n^r`, but the trivial `b=0` term is `n^{2r}/q = n^{2râˆ’خ²}`. For
   `r>خ²`, `n^r < n^{2râˆ’خ²}`, so `qآ·E_r^{char0} < n^{2r}`. Crossover `r*` â†’ خ²+1 as nâ†’âˆ‍ (خ²=3â†’4, 4â†’5, 5â†’6,
   6â†’7). So char-0 energy is EXHAUSTED by the diagonal at depth â‰ˆخ²+1, far below floor depth ~log q.
3. FOURIER POSITIVITY forces the anomaly: `خ£_{bâ‰ 0}|خ·_b|^{2r} = q E_r^{Fp} âˆ’ n^{2r} â‰¥ 0` (خ·_0=n) and
   `E^{char0} â‰¤ E^{Fp}` (â„‚-collisions âٹ† ً‌”½_p-collisions), so for r>r*: `q(E^{Fp}âˆ’E^{char0}) â‰¥ n^{2r} âˆ’
   q E^{char0} > 0`. Deep-moment validity (E^{Fp}â‰ˆE^{char0}) is thus IMPOSSIBLE past râ‰ˆخ²+1 â€” it
   contradicts positivity. Not merely unproven: structurally false.
Prize regime (n=2^32, خ²=4): char-0 caps at r*â‰ˆ5 â‰ھ log qâ‰ˆ89; bound ~10^4أ—floor. So the moment route
(char-0 OR char-p) provably cannot reach the floor. The only provable moment-side fragment is the norm
bound `A_r=0 for (2r)^{n/2}<p` (formalized, `RootSumNormBound.lean`), securing depth râ‰ˆ2 only.
CONSEQUENCE: stop pursuing deep-moment validity; the prize is purely the L^âˆ‍/BGK Gauss-phase sup-norm
(= the phase-cocycle worst-path large-deviation in the entry above). Probes: char0_floor.py, forced_anomaly.py.

## 2026-06-14 (wakesync/#407): three poly-orbit-count CLOSURE ideas REFUTED
Context: orbit-count crossing law `خ´*=sup{خ´:âˆ€pencil N_pencil(خ´)â‰¤gcd(bâˆ’a,n)}`; need Nâ‰¤poly(n) closed.
N = #achievable agreement sets A (sizeâ‰¥(1âˆ’خ´)n) mod د‰-rotation. Each A â†” UNIQUE sparse pencil
P=x^b+خ±x^aâˆ’g (proven injective: Pâˆ’P' degâ‰¤a<|A|âں¹=0). Verified n=8 pencil(6,7),agrâ‰¥5: 40 bad خ±,
40 distinct A (bijective âœ“), 5 rotation-orbits (N=5 = #distinct cyclic gap-patterns of A).
- **(C1) Dyadic-coset closure** (A = union of خ¼_n-subgroup cosets âں¹ poly-many): **REFUTED** â€” the
  size-5 achievable A in خ¼_8 are NOT coset unions (e.g. {0,1,2,3,7}, {1,3,4,6,7}); odd size alone
  rules out unions of even-size dyadic cosets. The achievable A are generic-looking gap-patterns.
- **(C2) DFT-uncertainty closure** (sparse-DFT âں¹ sparse-time âں¹ bounded agreement): **REFUTED for
  the window** â€” v=e_b+خ±e_a has DFT-support {0..kâˆ’1,a,b} of size k+2â‰ˆدپn (a CONSTANT FRACTION, not
  sparse), so the uncertainty bound time-weightâ‰¥n/(k+2)=O(1) is trivial. Dyadic n (non-prime) has a
  WEAK uncertainty principle (subgroup sparse-sparse vectors), which is exactly why the window is hard.
- **(C3) Mann-direct closure** (sparse P âں¹ few roots-of-unity âں¹ few A): **REFUTED** â€” sparse polys
  CAN have many roots of unity (x^nâˆ’1 is 2-sparse with n roots); Mann limits the STRUCTURE of single
  vanishing sums, not |A|, and the per-point relations (one (k+2)-term vanishing sum per خ¶^iâˆˆA) are
  independent, so Mann does not bound the count of achievable A.
VERDICT: N = #achievable cyclic gap-patterns; bounded above by all patterns (exponential); whether
ACHIEVABLE is poly = the beyond-Johnson list size viewed through the orbit action = the recognized
open core (issue #407 contract). Genuine partial: orbit-count law + Aâ†”P injectivity are clean/proven.

## 2026-06-13 #407 â€” LocalAlignedChildSubmaximality (the SINGLE open Lean input of the live phase-chaining route) is REFUTED worst-case

**Target.** `Frontier/_DyadicPhaseChaining.lean:309` `LocalAlignedChildSubmaximality (M) (N) := âˆ€ i<N, âˆƒ x y, M(i+1)=x+y âˆ§ xآ²+yآ² â‰¤ M(i)آ²`. The conditional chain `squareDescentLaw_of_localAlignedChildSubmaximality â†’ level_le_of_squareDescentLaw` derives the prize floor `B â‰¤ âˆڑ(nآ·drift)` from it. Intended instantiation `M(i)=|S_{b*}(خ¼_{2^i})|`, split into the dyadic half-cosets `خ¼_{2^{i+1}}=خ¼_{2^i} âٹ” خ¶خ¼_{2^i}`, children `A=S_{b*}(خ¼_{2^i})`, `B=S_{b*خ¶}(خ¼_{2^i})`.

**Two independent refutations (FFT-exact, 4 large primes, proper subgroups, n up to 4096):**

1. **Half-coset interpretation** (the intended `x=|A|, y=|B|`): the binding inequality `|A|آ²+|B|آ² â‰¤ M(i)آ²` FAILS worst-case at EVERY level and EVERY tested prime. Worst ratio `R=(|A|آ²+|B|آ²)/M(i)آ²` reaches `â‰ˆ1.995` at the first level and stays `>1` throughout (n=2048â†’4096 at p=4005889: R=1.2456; submax@b*=1.2456). At the level-(i+1) maximizer b* the phase alignment is EXACT â€” `cos(A,B)=1.0000` to 4 dp across all rows/primes (the empirical anchor is REAL) â€” but that is precisely what breaks submaximality: aligned + comparable-magnitude children give `|A|آ²+|B|آ² â†’ 2آ·M(i)آ²`.

2. **Literal Lean def, ANY real split** (the def only needs *some* x,y): min of `xآ²+yآ²` s.t. `x+y=s` is `sآ²/2`, so a valid split EXISTS iff `M(i+1)آ²/2 â‰¤ M(i)آ²`, i.e. the def is **logically equivalent** to the uniform descent `M(i+1) â‰¤ âˆڑ2آ·M(i)`. That descent is FALSE worst-case (re-confirmed independently, sharper than the earlier M-ratio entry): violations persist at large n â€” p=2021377 n=1024â†’2048 ratio 1.4743; p=4005889 n=2048â†’4096 ratio 1.5618. Not a boundary artifact.

**Conclusion.** The live L^âˆ‍/phase-chaining route's single open input is FALSE as a per-level worst-case inequality. The phase alignment `cos=1.0000` is an exact algebraic identity (confirmed), but it is the OBSTRUCTION to submaximality, not the lever. Consistent with #7.2 (no per-step/Lآ² inequality reaches the floor) and the prior `M(n)â‰¤âˆڑ2آ·M(n/2)` refutation: the residual is genuinely a **worst-case-PATH (Lyapunov / large-deviation) bound on the phase-alignment cocycle** `âˆڈ r_j`, `r_jâˆˆ[âˆڑ2,2]` â€” NOT any single-level submaximality. The `_DyadicPhaseChaining.lean` consumer chain stays valid as stated (it is conditional); its hypothesis is just not instantiable at the real Gauss-period level.

Probe: `scripts/probes/probe_local_aligned_child_submaximality.py` (self-contained, no sympy). Machine-checked countermodel: `Frontier/_DyadicPhaseChainingSubmaxRefuted.lean` (the defâ†”âˆڑ2-descent equivalence `localAlignedChildSubmaximality_iff_sqrt2_descent` + the concrete violation `not_localAlignedChildSubmaximality_submaxCounterexample`; axiom-clean `[propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound]`).

## 2026-06-14 (wakesync/#407): N-scaling test â€” CONSISTENT with sharp-threshold conjecture (not refuted) + rigidity scope correction
N-scaling (orbit reps, worst far pencil, agr=k+1): n=8â†’N=5, n=12â†’N=435. NOT a refutation:
agr=k+1 puts خ´=1âˆ’(k+1)/n just ABOVE the conjectured خ´*=1âˆ’دپâˆ’H(دپ)/(خ² logâ‚‚n) (n=12: خ´*â‰ˆ0.407,
agr=7 gives خ´=0.417>خ´*). So N exploding there = the expected SHARP proximity-gap threshold
(N small below خ´*, خک(q) above). At the actual threshold I=budgetâ‰ˆn, N=I/S=gcd=O(1) automatically.
Numerics bracket خ´* between agr=8(خ´=0.333) and agr=7(خ´=0.417), consistent with conjectured 0.407.
1/n granularity too coarse to pin خ´* sharply at nâ‰¤12. So: conjectured خ´* SUPPORTED, threshold
LOCATION (= beyond-Johnson list onset) = the open core. NOT closed.
RIGIDITY SCOPE CORRECTION (honest): pencil_unique_of_large_agreement needs |A|>b, but the window
has |A|=(1âˆ’خ´)nâ‰¤b (since deg P=bâ‰¥|A| to have |A| roots). So the landed rigidity lemma applies to the
DEGENERATE near-full-agreement regime, NOT the prize window. The empirical n=8 bijection (40 bad خ± â†”
40 distinct A at |A|=5<b=7) is a generic-distinctness phenomenon, not covered by the lemma. The lemma
is correct math but its window-relevance was overstated; the window injectivity is separate/open.

## 2026-06-13 (#407): cocycle worst-case-PATH probe â€” route SURVIVES (no sustained near-2 path) but "GM = exactly âˆڑ2" sub-conjecture REFUTED

**Object.** The alignment cocycle `r_i = M(i+1)/M(i)`, `M(i)=max_{bâ‰ 0}|S_b(خ¼_{2^i})|`, telescoping
`M(K)=M(2)آ·âˆڈ_{i=2}^{K-1} r_i`. The refuted uniform-step input demanded `r_i â‰¤ âˆڑ2` at every `i`; the
re-localized open core (issue #407 آ§3) is a worst-case-PATH bound: does the *product* (geometric mean)
stay near âˆڑ2, or does some frequency sustain `r_i â‰ˆ 2` down the whole tower (âں¹ `M(K)â‰ˆ2^K=n`, floor refuted)?

**Probe** `scripts/probes/probe_cocycle_worst_path.py` (FFT-exact, 5 large primes pâˆˆ[1.5M,8M], proper
subgroups خ¼_{2^خ¼}âٹٹF_p*, n up to 4096). Measures the REALIZED sup-norm cocycle `r_i=M_max(i+1)/M_max(i)`:

| prime | p | GM(cocycle) | max single step | M/âˆڑ(nآ·log(q/n)) |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| A | 2021377 | 1.5154 | 1.9934 | 1.4188 |
| C | 1502209 | 1.5384 | 1.9935 | 1.4527 |
| D | 4005889 | 1.5045 | 1.9956 | 1.4151 |
| E | 8040449 | 1.5043 | 1.9975 | 1.3467 |

**Findings (all FFT-exact, no sampling):**
1. **Route SURVIVES.** No sustained near-2 path: the realized geometric mean is `â‰ˆ 1.50â€“1.54 < 2`.
   The naive per-frequency "fixed-b path GM" and "envelope-of-ratios GM" both blow up (6â€“10âپµ), but
   those are *near-zero-denominator artifacts* (`min_b|S_b(خ¼_4)|â‰ˆ0` at low levels), NOT the controlling
   cocycle. The controlling object is the realized sup-norm cocycle, whose GM is bounded below 2.
2. **Floor holds numerically.** `M/âˆڑ(nآ·log(q/n)) âˆˆ [1.34, 1.45]` â€” bounded, constant, the BGK/MRSS
   envelope. And `(GM/âˆڑ2)^{K-2} â‰ˆ 0.7آ·âˆڑ(log(q/n))` across all primes: the excess of GM over âˆڑ2 supplies
   *exactly* the polylog factor. This pins the right SHAPE: `G = âˆڑ2آ·(1+خک(1/خ¼))`, drift product = polylog.
3. **"GM = exactly âˆڑ2" REFUTED.** GM â‰ˆ 1.51 > âˆڑ2 = 1.414 at every prime â€” a uniform `r_iâ‰¤âˆڑ2` (and even a
   `âˆڑ2`-geometric-mean) bound is impossible; the correct statement carries the `(1+خک(1/خ¼))` drift.

**Conclusion.** The cocycle large-deviation route is the right open core and is NOT refuted by the data:
no persistently-aligned path exists, individual steps approach but never exceed 2, and the geometric mean
sits at `âˆڑ2آ·(1+o(1))` reproducing the floor. The genuine open input is therefore the **geometric-mean
(Lyapunov) bound** `(âˆڈr_i)^{1/L} â‰¤ âˆڑ2آ·(1+خک(1/خ¼))`, i.e. `CocycleGeometricMeanLaw` â€” formalized as a
closed deterministic consumer in `Frontier/_DyadicCocycleLargeDeviation.lean` (axiom-clean;
`floor_of_cocycleGeometricMeanLaw` chains it to the floor, `not_cocycleProductBudget_of_level_gt` is the
refutation hook should a future probe find a sustained near-2 path). This remains the BGK/MRSS
incomplete-character-sum 25-yr-open problem; the consumer is closed, the analytic input is not.

## REFUTED (2026-06-13, #407): the Nullstellensatz/PNT "good-prime dodge" of the char-p transfer

**The idea (bold, looked like it might CLOSE the prize).** The char-0 optimality is axiom-clean Lean:
every gap-vanishing config `Sâٹ†خ¼_{2^خ¼}` over a char-0 field is a coset-union (`LamLeungTwoPow.full_tower`),
so `e_m(S)` lies in the `r`-fold sumset `H^{(+r)}` = roots of a fixed integer polynomial `F` of
`deg F = |H^{(+r)}|`. Over â„ڑج„, `F(e_m)` vanishes on the gap-variety `V(I)`, so `F(e_m)âˆˆâˆڑI` (Hilbert
Nullstellensatz). Clearing denominators in the certificate gives `Dآ·F(e_m)^t âˆˆ I_â„¤` for some `Dâˆˆâ„¤`.
Then for ANY prime `q âˆ¤ D`: every gap-valid `S` over `F_q` has `F(e_m(S))â‰،0`, so `#bad â‰¤ deg F =
|H^{(+r)}|` â€” the optimality upper bound, UNCONDITIONALLY, with NO char-p Lamâ€“Leung. And a "good" prime
`q âˆ¤ D`, `q â‰، 1 (mod n)`, `q = خک(n^خ²)` should EXIST: `D` has `â‰¤ logâ‚‚ D` prime factors, and Thornerâ€“Zaman
PNT-in-AP gives `â‰« n^{خ²}/د†(n)` primes `â‰،1 (mod n)` in `[n^خ², 2n^خ²]`. Pick one coprime to `D`. Closed â€”
inputs are KNOWN proven math (effective Nullstellensatz + PNT-in-AP). خ´* pinned in the prize regime.

**Why it FAILS (quantitative kill).** `D` is DOUBLY exponential, vastly larger than the prize prime:
- The gap ideal `I` lives in `N` variables â€” either `Nâ‰ˆ|S|â‰ˆدپnâ‰ˆ2^28` (point coords) or `Nâ‰ˆ2mâˆ’1â‰ˆn`
  (elementary-symmetric coords); both are `خک(n)`, NOT `O(polylog n)`. The generators (power sums `p_j`,
  `j<2m`) have degree `dâ‰ˆn`.
- Effective arithmetic Nullstellensatz (Krickâ€“Pardoâ€“Sombra, sharp): `log|D| â‰² d^{N+1}آ·(h+â€¦)`. Here
  `d^N â‰ˆ (2^30)^{2^28} = 2^{30آ·2^28} â‰ˆ 2^{8أ—10^9}`. So `logâ‚‚ D â‰ˆ 2^{8أ—10^9}` and
  `#{prime factors of D} â‰¤ logâ‚‚ D â‰ˆ 2^{8أ—10^9}`.
- Candidate primes `â‰،1 (mod n)` in `[n^خ²,2n^خ²]` number only `â‰ˆ n^خ²/د†(n) â‰ˆ 2^{30خ²âˆ’29} â‰ˆ 2^{91}` (`خ²=4`).
- `2^{8أ—10^9} â‰« 2^{91}`: the bad primes (factors of `D`) GENERICALLY include every candidate â€” the prize
  prime cannot be dodged. The pigeonhole runs the WRONG way.
- Same kill for the single-power-sum NORM-bound lift: `خ£_{xâˆˆS}xâ‰،0 (mod q)` lifts to â„‚ only if
  `q âˆ¤ N(خ£x)`, and `|N(خ£x)| â‰¤ |S|^{د†(2^خ¼)} = |S|^{2^{29}}`, so the lift needs `q > |S|^{2^{29}} â‰ˆ
  2^{1.5أ—10^{10}}` â€” unreachable (prize `qâ‰ˆ2^{120}`).

**Conclusion (sharpens the open core).** NO generic-elimination / norm route yields a `poly(n)`-height
transfer; every one has a denominator `exp(2^{خک(خ¼)})` that the prize prime `q=n^خ²` cannot exceed. Closing
the prize regime requires a STRUCTURAL certificate â€” `poly(n)`-height, exploiting the 2-adic cyclotomic
structure and the SIMULTANEOUS gap window (not one power sum) â€” and no such certificate is known. That
missing certificate is exactly the Gauss-period sup-norm / char-p Lamâ€“Leung coset-saturation open core
(Paley Graph Conjecture territory; SOTA Di Benedetto `n^{1âˆ’31/2880}`). The dodge therefore DEFERS to the
open math; it is not a closed route. (Same wall, restated with a quantitative denominator bound.)

## Dual-assault refutations (2026-06-13, 11-expert adversarial workflow, #407)

These M(n)/floor proof routes are REFUTED with machine-checkable evidence (probes in scripts/probes/, /tmp):
- **Stepanov auxiliary polynomial**: heavy set K(c)=#{bâ‰ 0:|خ·_b|>câˆڑn}=خک(p) uniformly in خ² (خ·_b/âˆڑnâ†’N(0,1),
  measured K/pâ‰ˆ0.044 at c=2 matching Pr|Z|>2=0.0455, all خ²); auxiliary on heavy set needs degâ‰¥K=خک(p) âں¹
  Stepanov multiplicity m<p/K=O(1) âں¹ collapses to m=1 moment. + Frobenius effective-degree cap <p (in-tree).
- **Amplification / shifted moments**: D_r(h)=خ£_b خ·_b^r خ·ج„_{b+h}^r = pآ·خ£_t N_r(t)آ²e_q(âˆ’ht), N_râ‰¥0 âں¹
  max_h|D_r(h)|=D_r(0)=pآ·E_r (positive-definite, argmax h=0 verified nâˆˆ{8,16,32}). No amplifier beats flat energy.
- **2-adic dyadic descent** M(n)آ²â‰¤2M(n/2)آ²: self-referential â€” M(n)آ²â‰¤2max_b(|A|آ²+|B|آ²)=M(n)آ²+M_د‡(n)آ²,
  M_د‡=quadratic-twisted level-n sup-norm SAME size (M_د‡/M~1). cos(A,B)=1 is trivial realness, not alignment.
  Strict form FALSE at finite n (ratio 1.89,1.76,1.58 > âˆڑ2); true law M(n)آ²â‰¤(2+خک(1/log n))M(n/2)آ².
- **Average + concentration (floor)**: MDS average E_line[I]â‰ˆC(n,k+m)q^{1-m}=q^{-خک(n/log n)} at the WINDOW
  INTERIOR (â‰ˆ2^{-1e7} at خ¼=30, NOT â‰ˆn); floor is EXTREME-VALUE, worst/avg gap q^{خک(n/log n)} unbridgeable.
- **B4 interleaved LDâ‡’MCA**: circular â€” خ›(C^{â‰،m})â‰¤(b+r choose r)خ›(C)^r (GGR11 r=4-5) is a monotone
  amplification of the single-code list; forcing â‰¤n needs خ›(C)â‰¤O(1)=the prize. خ›(C)â‰¤خ›(C^{â‰،m}) exact.
- **K=O(1) sheaf conductor**: FALSE in the rank reading â€” conductor=dim Hآ¹_c~n^{2r-1}, Swan=0 (all tame);
  Weil-II lossy by âˆڑrank=n^{r-1/2}. The cancellation is in the WEIGHTS, not the conductor (= BGK, open).
- **Large-sieve / effective Deligne in-regime**: dimension-obstructed â€” effective only when fâ‰¤âˆڑq âں؛ nâ‰³âˆڑp;
  prize nâ‰ھâˆڑp is over-dimensioned by âˆڑp/n. (خ£_b|خ·_b|^{2r}=qآ·E_r exact, zero slack.)
- **2-power exact Sidon âں¹ BGK gain**: ZERO â€” خ¼_n jointly Sidon with every dilate (E^+(خ¼_n,خ¾خ¼_n)=nآ² diag-only),
  best possible additive input, yet BSG losses are seed-energy-independent (regime-driven خ؛=log n/log p).

RESULTS (n=8,16; دپâˆˆ{1/4,1/2}):

  n=16,دپ=1/2: count 3904(د„9)â†’40(د„10)â†’4(د„11). n=16,دپ=1/4: 2688(د„5)â†’88(د„6)â†’16(د„7)=n baseline.
â€¢ small-subgroup synthesis = RATE-SENSITIVE (refutation pressure on the UNIFORM claim):
  دپ=1/2: worst-stack s SHRINKS cleanly 16â†’8â†’4 as خ´â†’threshold âœ“ (supports synthesis).

## 2026-06-14 (#407): char-free antipodal even-product law REFUTED in characteristic 2
Attempted to use the forward product law

`S = -S`, `0 âˆ‰ S` âں¹ `âˆڈ_{xâˆˆS}(X-x)` has zero odd coefficients

as a char-free engine for the half-tower recursion.  The product-transform identity is sound, but
the advertised parity step is false without excluding self-antipodal nonzero elements.

Counterexample: in any field of characteristic `2`, every element satisfies `-x = x`.  Pick any
nonzero `a` and take `S = {a}`.  Then `S.image (fun x => -x) = S` and `0 âˆ‰ S`, but
`|S| = 1` and

`âˆڈ_{xâˆˆS}(X-x) = X-a`,

whose coefficient of `X^1` is `1`, not `0`.  Thus zero-free negation-closure alone does not make
negation a fixed-point-free involution in characteristic `2`.

Correct salvage: require either characteristic zero / `(2 : F) â‰  0`, or directly assume
`âˆ€ x âˆˆ S, -x â‰  x`.  This is already consistent with the existing tower-slice files that explicitly
carry `char â‰  2` or char-zero hypotheses.  Do not use the char-free forward even-product lemma in the
prime-field dyadic lane unless the fixed-point-free hypothesis is present.

## 2026-06-14 (wakesync/#407): VERIFIED closed form for |H^{(+r)}(mu_{2^mu})| + sharp s=64 clean/BGK boundary
GENUINE NEW (verified, provable):
  |H^{(+r)}(mu_{2^mu})| = #{d in Z^{s/2} : |d|_1 <= r, |d|_1 = r mod 2}   (integer pts in L1 ball, dim s/2).
Proof: {1,zeta,...,zeta^{s/2-1}} is a Z-basis of Z[zeta_{2^mu}] (only relations = negation pairs
zeta^j+zeta^{j+s/2}=0), so Sum m_j zeta^j collapses to d_j=m_j-m_{j+s/2}; distinct sums <-> distinct d
with |d|_1<=r, parity r. VERIFIED brute=formula for s=4,8,16 all r (probe_l1_closedform_count.py).
By Lam-Leung [LL96fin] the char-p count = this char-0 count for 2r<q (no char-p inflation at small weight)
=> CLOSED FORM evaluable at ANY scale incl prize n=2^30 (breaks the n>=32 enumeration wall).

PRIZE PARAMS (n=2^30, q=n*2^128, budget=q*eps*=n): smallest r* with L1count(s/2,r*)>=n, then
Lam-Leung norm bound (2r*)^{phi(s)}<q (phi(2^mu)=s/2) HOLDS for s<=64, FAILS for s>=128:
  s=32 r*=11 (22)^16 log2=71<158 YES | s=64 r*=8 16^32 log2=128<158 YES | s=128 r*=6 12^64 log2=229>158 NO.
=> subgroups up to s=64 are char-p CLEAN with NO BGK; only s>=128 needs the Paley wall.
STRIKING (unproven) alignment: heuristic worst-case s* ~ 2 log2 n = 60 sits right at the s=64 clean
boundary. IF worst active subgroup at delta* is <=64 => prize closes clean via Lam-Leung, no BGK.

HONEST BLOCKER (why this is NOT closure): the (s,r,delta) calibration linking |H^{(+r)}(mu_s)| to the
actual bad-SCALAR count at radius delta FAILS the probe monotonicity: probe count DECREASES with
agreement tau, but |H^{(+r)}| with r=tau-k INCREASES. So "bad-count = |H^{(+r)}|" is NOT a clean
equality; the worst-s<=64 claim rests on a 1-anchor heuristic (s*=8@n=16, 2^{s/2}=n), NOT established.
=> cannot prove worst active s<=64 => cannot conclude clean-vs-BGK for the prize. The calibration is the
concrete remaining gap. SOLID: closed form + s=64 boundary. OPEN: the calibration (which s is worst at
delta*). Genuine bricks, not closure. Probe committed.

## 2026-06-14 (wakesync/#407): REFUTED â€” bad-scalar count â‰  |H^{(+r)}(mu_s)| (calibration kills the closed-form route)
Calibration (probe_calibration_Hr_vs_badcount.py): for the canonical FAR mu_s Kambire stack

  â€¢ Spurious bad count is large at tau=k+1 (explosion), drops to small spurious at tau=k+2 (only s<=8),
    then per-subgroup trivial baseline = s (one orbit). |H^{(+r)}| has none of this د„-structure.

route is WITHDRAWN as a bad-count computation. The L1-ball closed form is a CORRECT theorem about

NOT the prize bad-scalar count â€” the Kambire identification bad-count=|H^{(+r)}| is FALSE against ground
truth. So the s=64 boundary does NOT bound the prize; that computation is moot for delta*.
WHAT SURVIVES (honest): (a) the L1-ball closed form as a standalone verified combinatorial identity;

baseline = s per subgroup; (c) delta* location ~1-rho-O(1/log n) still corroborated.
WHAT DIED: my hope that the closed form computes the prize bad count and lets s*(n)/clean-vs-BGK be read
