## R2 / GW kernel mis-wiring catch â€” `GWDirectionFinrankLe` is false for genuine codes (2026-06-14)

**Attempt audited.** The CZ25/Guruswamiâ€“Wang `|L|>1` capacity kernel reduction
(`GWKernelReduction.lean:cz25CoordFiberCap_of_interp_and_multiplicity`) discharges
`CZ25CoordFiberCap` from `{BRICK-I, BRICK-V}` plus two "orchestrator-wired" brick conclusions
`{BRICK-W = GWDirectionFinrankLe, BRICK-L = GWAffineFiberCharge}`. The headline docstring claims
`hW : GWDirectionFinrankLe s C` is "the conclusion of BRICK-W (proven in lane `GWBrickW`)".

**Disproof (machine-checked, `GWDirectionScopedWiring.lean`).**
`GWDirectionFinrankLe s C := âˆ€ A â‰¤ C, finrank A â‰¤ s âˆ’ 1` instantiated at `A := C` gives
`finrank C â‰¤ s âˆ’ 1` (`gwDirectionFinrankLe_forces_small`). On the whole code space `C := âٹ¤`,
`finrank âٹ¤ = |خ¹|آ·s`, so for `s â‰¥ 2` it exceeds `s âˆ’ 1` and the Prop is **false**
(`gwDirectionFinrankLe_refuted`, axiom-clean). Hence `hW` holds *only* for codes of dimension
`â‰¤ s âˆ’ 1` â€” never for a capacity-regime folded-RS code (`dim C = k â‰« s`). The proven BRICK-W lane
(`GWAffinePinning.gw_solutionSet_finrank_le`) bounds the finrank of the *single* GW solution
submodule `Wâ‚€ = gwHomogSolution A خ³ k`, **not** of every `A â‰¤ C`; the named Prop over-states the
scope and is not its conclusion. The conditional headline is `sorry`-free but consumable only with
an unsatisfiable premise.

**Repair landed (axiom-clean).** Drop the universal `hW`; carry the genuine BRICK-W bound
`finrank A â‰¤ s âˆ’ 1` *with* the BRICK-V output `A` via `GWAgreeForcesDirectionScoped` (the shape the
proven lane actually delivers). `cz25CoordFiberCap_of_interp_and_multiplicity_scoped` re-derives the
identical `CZ25CoordFiberCap` from `{BRICK-I, BRICK-V-scoped, BRICK-L}` + `0 â‰¤ د„(râ‚€)`, reusing the
arithmetic collapse verbatim. The scoped Prop is satisfiable
(`gwAgreeForcesDirectionScoped_holds_of_close_list_singleton`, take `A := âٹ¥` on the sub-Johnson
slice), unlike `GWDirectionFinrankLe`.

**Remaining open core (untouched).** The genuinely-deep `|L|>1` content is the affine-flat charge
`GWAffineFiberCharge` (BRICK-L), i.e. the per-coordinate `card(fiber) â‰¤ finrank(span of fiber diffs)`
cap â€” the `q^{dim}` vs `dim+1` obstruction documented at `CZ25SpanDimension.lean:292â€“302`. Not a
closure: a leg-statement was corrected, not the kernel solved.

## 2026-06-14 â€” Galois/house and p-adic/Stickelberger angles REFUTED (probe_galois_house_padic.py)

Two structural angles NOT in the prior 1149-conjecture sweep, both reduce to the open archimedean
sup-norm M(n)=max_b|eta_b|:
- **Galois/house:** the m periods are Galois conjugates; M = house (max conjugate). house/|norm|^{1/m}
  = 5.0, 6.4, 7.6 (n=8,16,32, growing); min|eta| ~ 0.002 (conjugates wildly unequal). The computable
  NORM (Habegger "Norm of Gaussian Periods") UNDER-estimates the house 5-8x and does NOT determine it.
  House = the open sup-norm. REFUTED.
- **p-adic/Stickelberger:** Gauss-sum p-adic valuation = digit-sum (Gross-Koblitz, exact/proven). But
  corr(|eta_i|, 2-adic-val(i)) = 0.019 ~ 0 â€” the p-adic structure gives ZERO handle on archimedean size.
  REFUTED.

## 2026-06-14 â€” HANDLE A3 (Stickelberger/Gauss-sum DFT-of-phases) REFUTED (probe_a3_*.py)

Sharper than the crude correlation entry above. The explicit-formula identity is VERIFIED to ~1e-13:
`eta_b = (1/m)[-1 + sqrt(q)*P(b)]`, `P(b) = sum_{chi!=1 in H^perp} u_chi * conj(chi)(b)` = the DFT (on
Z/m, m=(q-1)/n) of the UNIMODULAR Gauss-sum phases `u_chi = g(chi)/sqrt(q)`. So
`M(n) = (sqrt(q)/m)*max_b|P(b)|`, exactly the sup-norm of the DFT of the phases.

A3 hope: Stickelberger/Gross-Koblitz forces `max_b|P(b)| <= sqrt(2 m log m)` (=> M <= sqrt(2 n log q)).
MEASURED (n=8,16,32,64; p~n^4; |g|err~1e-13 confirms |g(chi)|=sqrt(q) exactly, phases truly unimodular):
  Psup/sqrt(2 m log m) = 0.756, 0.848, 0.891, 0.964  (below the GENEROUS target but RISING toward 1)
  Psup/random-unimodular-DFT-sup = 1.04, 1.16, 1.27, 1.26  (phases give NO cancellation vs random;
      slightly ABOVE random â€” mild positive tail correlation, sup/mean 4.3..6.0 vs Rayleigh 2.3..2.8)
  M/sqrt(2 n log p) = 0.655, 0.735, 0.772, 0.835  (the ACTUAL prize ratio, also creeping up)
Phase diagnostics: angle chi^2/df â‰ˆ 1 (equidistributed), adjacent-phase corr -> 0 (decorrelated); the
sup is mildly diffuse (2-4 freqs within 90%, ~20 within 80%, top1/top2 â‰ˆ 1.05-1.09), worst frequency j*
has NO fixed arithmetic label (j*=0,6986,238435; v2 varies).

EXACT OBSTRUCTION. Stickelberger/Gross-Koblitz pins only the **p-adic valuation** (prime-ideal
factorization = base-p digit sum) of g(chi); the **archimedean PHASE** is intrinsically ambiguous â€” the
sign/unit-root ambiguity is unresolved even for the index-2 and index-4 cases (Yang-Xia 0912.1414;
Aoki, "evaluation of Gauss sums of 2-power order", only the semiprimitive p^jâ‰،-1 case is clean). By
Katz/Deligne (Rojas-Leon) the phases EQUIDISTRIBUTE, i.e. behave as random unimodular. Therefore the DFT
sup-norm is governed by the random-phase model â€” which is EXACTLY what Katz already gives asymptotically
(KatzEffectiveGaussSum.lean). Stickelberger adds NOTHING to control the archimedean DFT sup-norm; the
p-adic structure and the archimedean size live on opposite sides. The route reaches the random/Wick scale
`M ~ sqrt(c n log q)` only HEURISTICALLY (same as the moment route), not as a proof for the fixed
deterministic prime â€” proving the random-phase upper bound for THIS specific deterministic phase family IS
the open BGK/Paley sup-norm problem (best PROVEN n^{1-o(1)} BGK; di Benedetto n^{0.989} only for n>q^{1/4}).
GAP TO sqrt(n): the exponent stays at di Benedetto's 0.989 (no improvement); A3 is an exact REFRAMING of
the open object, not a new bound. NOT dyadic-specific (the DFT-of-phases identity holds for any subgroup;
n=2^mu only forces eta_b real). Probes: /tmp/probe_a3_fast.py, /tmp/probe_a3_g3.py, /tmp/probe_a3_final.py.

## 2026-06-14 (#407 cumulant2power): REFUTED â€” cumulant explosion is NOT confined to the imprimitive 2-power tower
HYPOTHESIS (issue407-cumulant-dichotomy + poisson-concentration-tower): the cumulant
K_r = خ£_{bâ‰ 0}|خ·_b|^{2r} = pآ·E_r(خ¼_n) âˆ’ n^{2r} escapes the moment no-go generically but EXPLODES at
2-power primes (prize n=2^خ¼); the hope was that the explosion is CONFINED to the O(log n) IMPRIMITIVE
tower directions (the خ¼_n-sub-tower {b: خ·_b factors through خ¼_{n/2^j}}), with PRIMITIVE directions
staying sub-Gaussian â€” which would reduce the floor to a FINITE STRUCTURED set.
PROBE (probe_cumulant_primitive_split.py, multi-prime, PROPER subgroups خ¼_n, n up to 256, r up to 8,
r*~ln p): خ·_b is constant on cosets bآ·خ¼_n; split K_r by the 2-adic tower invariant v2(ord b) (imp set
= {b: v2(ord b) â‰¤ v2(n)}, the خ¼_n-sub-tower incl خ¼_n itself), measure K_tot/Wick vs K_imp/Wick, plus
min #cosets carrying 90/99% of K_{r*}.
RESULTS â€” the confinement is FALSE:
  â€¢ Explosion is REAL and ONLY at 2-power-structured primes (v2(pâˆ’1)â‰«v2(n), Fermat 65537=2^16+1):
    K_r/Wick â†’ {6.7 @ n=32, 302 @ n=64, 123 @ n=128} at r=8. Generic primes (577,1153,2113,40961,
    12289,786433,7340033): K_r/Wick â‰¤ ~1.6 (NO excess to control â€” Wick already holds).
  â€¢ DECISIVE COUNTEREXAMPLE (p=65537, n=128): K_tot/Wick = 123 (massive explosion) but the imprimitive
    tower carries K_imp/Wick = 0.016 â€” the entire excess lives in the COMPLEMENT of the خ¼_n-tower. The
    heaviest cosets there (bâˆˆ{129,33,63,3,225,7}) have v2(ord b)âˆˆ{15,11,16,16,13,16} = GENERIC
    multiplicative order, i.e. NOT imprimitive. Flagged "excess NOT in imp tower!" at every r.
  â€¢ The clean "concentrates on خ¼_n itself" picture is a COINCIDENCE of (65537, nâ‰¤64), where r* aligned
    with the subgroup (argmax_in_mu_n=True there); it BREAKS at nâ‰¥128 (argmax_in_mu_n=False) and at all
    other primes (heaviest cosets are large-height, large-order, prime-dependent).
  â€¢ Additive-height is ALSO not the label: corr(min-coset-|b|, |خ·_b|^2) âˆˆ [âˆ’0.14,+0.11] â‰ˆ 0; for the
    larger generic primes the heaviest cosets have LARGE height (6747, 177881).
WHAT SURVIVES (honest narrowing, NOT closure): WHEN the cumulant explodes it IS concentrated on O(1)
cosets (90% from <1 coset, 99% from ~1â€“4 cosets) â€” but those carrying cosets carry no uniform
multiplicative or additive structural label, so they do NOT form a fixed describable finite set across
primes. The explosion is exactly the 2-power-prime resonance, and it lives on the DIFFUSE
incidence/additive-energy face â€” consistent with the line-ball-incidence wall (face 4) and the BGK/Paley
floor, NOT a finite imprimitive set that could be controlled by enumeration.
CONSEQUENCE: the "reduce floor to a finite structured set" route via imprimitive/primitive splitting is
WITHDRAWN. The cumulant-2-power explosion is genuine but unstructured-at-the-coset-level; it relocates,
not narrows, the open core. NOT closure. Probe committed.

## #407 laneF â€” cross-parity (butterfly cross-term) first moment CLEAN; positive-part confinement REFUTED (2026-06-14)

CONTEXT. The dyadic FFT butterfly splits a subgroup Gauss sum into two children:
`خ·_b(H)=خ·_b(G)+خ·_{خ¶b}(G)`, `H=Gâٹ”خ¶â€¢G`. The CROSS-PARITY term
`X(b):=â€–خ·_b(H)â€–آ²âˆ’â€–خ·_b(G)â€–آ²âˆ’â€–خ·_{خ¶b}(G)â€–آ²=2Re(خ·_b(G)آ·conj خ·_{خ¶b}(G))`
is the alignment-excess / odd-part contribution to the far-line incidence. The lane asked:
is the odd-part amplification confined to O(log n) imprimitive directions, with a clean
cross-parity bound?

LANDED (axiom-clean, `CrossParityAggregate.lean`, Parseval-only, q-INDEPENDENT):
  â€¢ `crossTerm_sum_zero`: `âˆ‘_{bâˆˆF} X(b) = 0` (full first moment vanishes).
  â€¢ `crossTerm_sum_nonzero_eq`: `âˆ‘_{bâ‰ 0} X(b) = âˆ’2|G|آ²` (off-zero aggregate, exact, negative).
  â€¢ non-vacuous on real dyadic tower (`dyadic_dilate_split`, `crossTerm_sum_nonzero_eq_dyadic`).
  Interpretation: on AGGREGATE across frequencies the butterfly children are ANTI-aligned â€”
  cross-parity SUPPRESSES, never amplifies. The only positive cross-parity in aggregate is the
  trivial b=0 term (X(0)=2|G|آ²=nآ²/2). Verified p-indep across nâˆˆ{8,16,32,64}, â‰¥5 primes each
  (`probe_407_crossparity_identity.py`).

REFUTED (machine, `probe_407_crossparity_localize.py`, prize regime proper subgroups, multi-prime):
the conjecture that the POSITIVE (amplifying) cross-parity is confined to O(log n) imprimitive
FREQUENCIES is FALSE. The positive part `âˆ‘_{X(b)>0} X(b)` and #{b:X(b)>0} scale with خک(q) (a
constant fraction of all frequencies), NOT O(log n). The aggregate is held NEGATIVE by an even
larger negative mass, not by sparsity of the positive part. (Corroborates the earlier cumulant
2-power-explosion entry: the heavy part is diffuse/unstructured at the coset level, not a finite
imprimitive set.)

PRECISE REDUCTION TO NAMED CORE: only the Lآ¹/first-moment of X is q-independent. The worst single
frequency `max_b X(b)` (~âˆڑ(n log q)) and the energy `âˆ‘_b X(b)آ²` (~qآ·Eâ‚‚(G), `probe_407_crossparity_L2.py`)
are NOT q-independent = exactly the BGK / additive-energy sup-norm wall already named in
`SubgroupGaussSumDilationRecursion` (âˆڑ2-vs-2 gap) and `_DyadicDeviationDecayEnvelope`. So the
cross-parity decomposes cleanly into an elementary first moment (this brick) + the open BGK core
(Lâˆ‍/Lآ²). The Panâ€“Xu cross-parity split does NOT escape the floor; it relocates the open content to
the per-frequency worst case, with the aggregate now provably anti-aligned. NOT closure.

`âˆ‘â±¼ خ´â±¼`, and `GZPCondition` is satisfied **vacuously by `خ´â‰،0`** (no `خ؛â‰¤0` has positive total),
giving an EMPTY index, span `âٹ¥` â€” yet the RS dual is nonzero whenever `k < card خ¹`. So
`âٹ¥ = (RS dual)` is impossible. Instantiated at `t=0` (the `0أ—0` minor side condition is automatic).

REPAIR (faithful, non-vacuous, SUFFICIENT for the assembly), in
`ArkLib/Data/CodingTheory/AGL24DualZeroPatternPinned.lean`:
* `GMMDSDualZeroPatternTheoremPinned` â€” restrict to `خ´` carrying the genuine GM-MDS dimension
  count `âˆ‘â±¼ خ´â±¼ = card خ¹ âˆ’ k` (the count the unpinned target silently dropped).
* `gzp_of_orientation_delta_sum` (axiom-clean) â€” the `خ´` that `gzp_of_orientation` ACTUALLY
  produces (`خ´â±¼ = indeg j` off the root, `خ´لµ£ = indeg r âˆ’ k`) sums to `(âˆ‘â±¼ indeg j) âˆ’ k =
  card خ¹ âˆ’ k`, i.e. **satisfies the pin**. Since the assembly only ever feeds GM-MDS the
  orientation-derived `خ´`, the pinned form is all it needs.
* `symbolicFullRank_of_classical_imports_pinned` (axiom-clean) â€” the campaign capstone
  RE-ROUTED: Frank's orientation + the *pinned* target discharge the symbolic Theorem 2.11
  interface, identical conclusion, dimensionally faithful import.
* Non-vacuity certificates: `pinned_hypothesis_inhabited` (a GZP satisfying both `GZPCondition`
  and the pin exists for every orientation with `k â‰¤ indeg r`) and `pinned_dimension_consistent`
  (under the pin `card (GZPCopyIdx خ´) = finrank(RS dual)` exactly â€” a spanning family is
  feasible; re-exports the in-tree `gzpCopyIdx_card_eq_dual_finrank`).

NOTE: the pinned target itself is NOT proven here (and `DualRowsFromNonsingularEvalPinned`, the
repaired connector residual, remains the genuine open GM-MDS kernel construction: build the
`âˆ‘خ´ = card خ¹ âˆ’ k` edge-supported dual vectors from the evaluated RIM kernel and apply
`LinearIndependent.span_eq_top_of_card_eq_finrank`). What this catch delivers is: (i) the named
target as stated is unprovable (false), so any "proof" of it would be vacuous/laundered; (ii) the
precise faithful re-statement; (iii) the verification that the re-statement fully suffices for the
assembly, with the pin shown to hold at every consumed `خ´`. The Lovett algebraic core
(`lovettThm17_unconditional`) is proven; the remaining genuine GM-MDS content is exactly the
pinned dual-row span construction.

## 2026-06-14 (#407 Lane C): "bad-prime density SPARSE (~4%)" REFUTED â†’ off-BGK density route hits the SAME n=128 wall
The prior Half-Sum probe (`probe_halfsum_candidate_density.py`, commit a309cf75c) reported the
candidate-bad-prime density at n=32 as ~3.95% and proposed an OFF-BGK density bound as the open part.
That 3.95% is an ARTIFACT of capping the antipodal-free subset size at r<=6. New EXACT probe
(`scripts/probes/probe_halfsum_density_exact.py`), two independent methods cross-validated to agree
EXACTLY on n=16 (both give the same 11 candidate-bad primes {17,97,113,193,241,337,353,401,433,577,881},
max 881):
  (A) integer cyclotomic norm = Sylvester/Bareiss resultant Res(Phi_n, sum_{i in S} zeta^i) (no floats);
  (B) direct mod-p: p=1 mod n is candidate-bad <=> exists d in {0,+-1}^{n/2}, d!=0, with sum d_j g^j â‰، 0
      mod p (g a primitive n-th root; zeta^{j+n/2}=-zeta^j). Meet-in-the-middle subset-sum mod p.

WITH NO SUBSET CAP (the full antipodal-free family) the density is NOT sparse:
  n=32, window [n^3,n^4): density = 0.93 (747/800), NOT 0.04.  =>  the "sparse density" headline is FALSE.

CORRECT STRUCTURE (the genuine localization): the candidate-bad prime set is FINITE for every fixed n
â€” it is exactly the primes pâ‰،1 mod n dividing one of the finitely many norms |N(sum d_j zeta^j)|,
d in {0,+-1}^{n/2}, all bounded by  C(n) := max_d |N(sum d_j zeta^j)|.  ANY prime p > C(n) is CLEAN.
So density(window) -> 0 as the window height -> infinity, EXACTLY 0 above C(n). Measured cutoff at n=32:
density 0.997 (p~2^12) -> 0.77 (2^20) -> 0.057 (2^24) -> 0.000 (p>=2^32), tracking C(32) ~ 2^31.

C(n) SCALING (exact n=8,16; hill-climb LB n=32,64):
  log2 C(8)=3.17, log2 C(16)=11.23, log2 C(32)=31.1, log2 C(64)=79.1.
  Proven lower bound log2 C(n) >= n/2 - 1 (the all-ones half-sum has norm EXACTLY 2^{n/2-1},
  HalfSumNormClosedForm.lean); Hadamard upper bound (n/2)آ·log2(n/2).

THE PRIZE CROSSOVER (why density does NOT help): prize prime has log2 p ~ log2 n + 128.
  n <= 64 : log2 C(64)=79.1 < log2 p ~ 134  => p > C(n)  => EVERY prize prime CLEAN (density 0). [genuine
            unconditional off-BGK clean result for small subgroups n<=64]
  n >= 128: log2 C(n) extrapolates above log2 p ~ 135  => norms exceed p, density GENERICALLY positive.
This INDEPENDENTLY reproduces the s=64-clean / s>=128-BGK boundary of the Lam-Leung route (DISPROOF_LOG
entry "VERIFIED closed form for |H^{(+r)}|... s=64 boundary", same day): the off-BGK DENSITY argument
does NOT bypass the wall â€” it hits the SAME nâ‰ˆ128 crossover via a different (norm-SIZE vs char-p-
faithfulness) mechanism.

NET (honest outcome = machine-checked refutation + precise localization):
 â€¢ REFUTED: "candidate-bad density sparse (~4%) / floor holds for almost-all primes by sparsity." Density
   is ~1 at the prize window for n>=128. The 4% was a small-subset-cap artifact.
 â€¢ TRUE & USABLE: for fixed n, density->0 in p (finiteness, p>C(n) clean); for n<=64 every prize prime
   is UNCONDITIONALLY clean (p>C(n)). This is a real off-BGK partial, but only for small subgroups.
 â€¢ The open core is NOT a density bound â€” it is exactly the n>=128 crossover C(n) vs p, i.e. the same
   wall (BCHKS 1.12 / BGK / Paley). Probe committed; no Lean brick (the statement is a refutation +
   numeric localization, not a clean axiom-clean Prop beyond the already-landed n/2-1 norm LB).

## Cyclic-sieving / n-core list-growth route â€” NO super-poly boost, NO refutation (2026-06-14)

**Attempt.** The cyclic-sieving / Schur-at-roots-of-unity lever (memory
`issue389-schur-roots-of-unity-lever`): the smooth-domain `خ¼_n` GM-MDS / HOMDS list certificate for
a degree-pattern `خ»` is `det(خ¶^{خ²_j i})`, `خ²_j = خ»_j+(nâˆ’1âˆ’j)`; it VANISHES iff two `خ²_j` collide
mod `n` (abacus `n`-core NONEMPTY) â€” exactly the in-tree axiom-clean
`HOMDSSmoothObstruction.homds_det_ne_zero_iff_nCoreEmpty`. Each vanishing certificate is an extra
linear dependence = a candidate spurious list codeword. OPEN QUESTION the lever poses: does
cyclic-sieving / hook-content vanishing boost the smooth-`خ¼_n` coset list ABOVE the trivial
single-coset `O(1/دپ)` to SUPER-POLY (âں¹ floor FALSE for plain RS) or stay poly?

**Machine-checked finding (PRIZE regime: proper subgroup `خ¼_{2^خ¼}`, multi-prime; probe
`scripts/probes/probe_cyclicsieving_listgrowth.py`, full `C(n,k)` exact enumeration as ground
truth).** The route does **NOT** boost the list and does **NOT** refute the floor:

* **(A) The "super-poly" is a SUPPORT/dependence over-count, not codewords.** An intermediate
  enumeration that multiplied a per-coset kernel dimension by `C(#cosets, #needed)` (counting
  coset-UNION supports) grows super-poly â€” but a single coset support of `m=k+1` columns has NO
  `خ²`-collision mod `n` in the list window `a>k` (count `0`). The explosion lived entirely in the
  binomial coset-union factor, i.e. it is exactly the **proven** core-vs-list factor
  `#cores â‰¤ Lآ·C(A,k+m+1)` (`SubJohnsonListSupply.explainableCoreSupply_of_listBound`,
  `ExplainableCoreExactCount.explainable_cores_eq_sum_agreement`, both axiom-clean in-tree). The
  `n`-core enumeration counts the `C(A,آ·)` SUPPORTS, NOT the list `L`. (Same "cores exp, list poly"
  catch as `probe_prize_coreVSlist`.)
* **(B) EXACT worst-case CODEWORD list (full `C(n,k)`) in the strict interior window is
  `O(1/دپ)`, `p`-independent.** `دپ=1/2`, agreement `aâ‰¥k+2`: list `âˆˆ {0,1,3}` across `n=8,12,16` and
  multiple primes â€” a handful, no growth.
* **(C) DECISIVE: the only large list is the GENERIC `a=k+1` MDS boundary spike, identical for
  smooth and random domains.** At `a=k+1`, SMOOTH `خ¼_n` list vs a RANDOM (non-smooth) evaluation
  domain of the same size: `n=8` 3 vs 2, `n=12` 11 vs 12, `n=16` **70 vs 70**. The spike decays to
  `O(1/دپ)` within 1â€“2 steps as `a` rises off `k+1` (it is `outside` the prize's strict-interior
  window). The smooth/cyclic-sieving structure provides **no** advantage over a random domain.

**Status.** Neither closure nor refutation: a **precise reduction to the named open core**. The
cyclic-sieving lever does not independently bound or blow up the list; it reduces to the same
generic-MDS worst-case list bound `L` of `SubJohnsonListBound dom k m L A`
(`SubJohnsonListSupply.lean`, the recognized explicit-RS-beyond-Johnson open problem = BGK / Paley
/ BCHKS Conj 1.12), with smooth `خ¼_n` behaving like a random domain on the list axis. Do not
re-attempt "n-core / hook-content vanishing super-poly-boosts the smooth list": the boost is the
`C(A,آ·)` support factor, already proven worst-case vacuous, not the list `L`.

## 2026-06-14 (#407 laneB / Chai-Fan Q2): orbit-COMPRESSION ratio quantifies the sparsity-exclusivity â€” multi-prime CORROBORATION + 3-monomial algebraic root
**CONTEXT / overlap (honest).** The "Action-Orbit per-line bound is MONOMIAL-EXCLUSIVE" entry above
(`ActionOrbitGeneralF.lean`, same day) already PROVED the core: per-line `خ³`-orbit closure exists iff
the direction `f` is a monomial (the dilation-eigenvector pin), and probed (n=8, single prime) that
general `f` has no closure. THIS entry CORROBORATES and QUANTIFIES that result from a complementary
angle â€” the MCA correlated-agreement object itself, multi-prime â€” and lands the algebraic root in Lean.
It is a refinement, NOT an independent discovery; the monomial-exclusivity headline belongs to the
entry above.

**New angle: the affine line `u+خ±v` of TWO arbitrary words** (= the exact `V_خ´` / `خµ_mca` object of
`BridgeLoop43`, not a single-direction `gâ‚€+خ³f` line). On the proper subgroup `خ¼_16 âٹ‚ F_p*` for
p âˆˆ {40961, 65537, 786433} (multi-prime, far directions, interior خ´ at t=k+1), exact bad set
`{خ± : u+خ±v is t-close to RS_k}` via the linear-in-خ± divided-difference syndrome (`-A/B` per
(k+1)-subset, exact), and the COMPRESSION RATIO `r = |bad|/N` (N = orbit count under `خ±â†¦خ±آ·w`).
`scripts/probes/probe_407_laneB_q2_compression_ratio.py`:
* SPARSE far pencil `(a,b)`: `r = S = n/gcd(bâˆ’a,n)` **EXACTLY** ({16,8,4}), orbit-CLOSED, and the
  orbit count `N` is **p-INDEPENDENT** (â‰ˆ250 @ k=4 across all three primes) â€” `N` small + `q`-uniform.
* DENSE line (random / superposition of many far monomials): orbit-closed in **0/40 trials, every
  config**; `r â†’ 1` as p grows (`1.98â†’1.57â†’1.04` @ k=4) â€” NO compression, `N â‰ˆ |bad|` grows with `q`.
The p-INDEPENDENCE of sparse N vs the `q`-GROWTH of dense N is the quantitative statement of why
`خµ_mca = Nآ·S/qآ²` is `O(1)/|F|` only for sparse: the orbit compression is the entire lever and it is
sparsity-exclusive. (NB: the bad-COUNT |bad| â‰ˆ #subsets for any line at t=k+1 â€” a union artifact; only
the ORBIT count N is meaningful. Compare N, never |bad|.)

**Algebraic root in Lean (axiom-clean, real `lake build` 3297 jobs).**
`Frontier/LaneB_Q2_SparsityExclusive.lean`:
- `pencil_subst_two`: dilation rescales the pencil into ANOTHER pencil, single coeff shift `خ±â†¦خ± خ¼^{bâˆ’a}`.
- `triexp_subst` + `triexp_no_single_orbit`: a 3-monomial's two free coeffs rescale by DIFFERENT
  factors `خ¼^{bâˆ’a} â‰  خ¼^{câˆ’a}` (witnessed `2â‰ 4` over ZMod 17) âں¹ no single cyclic action closes the bad
  set â€” the exact algebraic reason dense bad sets are not orbit-closed.
- `badCount_eq_orbitSize_mul_orbitCount`: orbit-closure âں¹ `|B|=Sآ·N` (the compression); its hypotheses
  fail for dense lines.
Reduces to the SAME open core as the entry above: Lane B = across-line incidence = Q1/Q2 = BGK/Paley.
Outcome: precise-reduction-to-named-core (corroboration). NOT closure. Lean + probe committed.

## R1 assessment (2026-06-14): GG25 curve-decodability for EXPLICIT PLAIN RS â€” precise obstruction (honest-open)

**Question.** The in-tree GG25 chain (curve-decodability â‡’ MCA, Lemma 3.2) is built and
axiom-clean. Does any new angle give curve-decodability for *explicit plain* smooth-domain RS
in the prize window, or what is the precise obstruction?

**Verified in-tree structure (everything downstream of curve-decodability is PROVEN; the input is not).**
- `GG25CurveDecodability.CurveDecodable` â€” the Def 3.1 predicate (the HYPOTHESIS).
- `GG25SpreadBound.{disagree_spread_bound, all_seeds_close}` â€” Lemma 3.2 (proven).
- `GG25MCAFromCurveDecodability.all_seeds_close_of_curveDecodable` â€” Thm 3.3, MCA from
  curve-decodability (proven). All consume `CurveDecodable` as a hypothesis.
- The ONLY in-tree *producers* of `CurveDecodable` at the nontrivial regime `b > â„“+1`:
  * `CurveDecodability.{markedCurveDecodable_interleaved, curveDecodable_interleaved}` (Jo26 Thm 5.7):
    transfers curve-decodability of a BASE code to its `s`-fold INTERLEAVING under `a.choose b â‰¤ q`.
    **Takes base curve-decodability as hypothesis.**
  * `FarWordSupplyCounting.markedCurveDecodable_interleaved_of_curveDecodable_rs:280`:
    same, specialized to RS â€” line 280 takes `hC : CurveDecodable F (ReedSolomon.code domain k) â„“ خ´ a b`
    **as an explicit hypothesis.**
  * Small-witness `b â‰¤ â„“+1`: `Jo26CurveInterpolationRegime.curveDecodable_interpolation` /
    `GG25SmallWitness.markedCurveDecodable_of_small_witness` â€” FREE via Lagrange, ANY linear code,
    but **trivial regime** (Jo26 Remark 5.3: applications need `b > â„“+1`).
- In-tree subspace-design is established ONLY for FOLDED RS over a geometric domain
  (`ReedSolomon.Folded.frs_geomDomain_isSubspaceDesign_cz25Profile`, used by `CapacityBounds*`).
  **Plain RS is NEVER shown subspace-design anywhere in-tree.**

**The paper-level obstruction (GG25 = ECCC TR25-166 / ePrint 2025/2054), read from the PDF.**
The ONLY production engine for curve-decodability at `b > â„“+1` is **Theorem 4.7**: *every
`د„`-subspace-design code `C` is `(â„“, 1âˆ’د„(r)âˆ’خµ, a, خµ/(r+خµ)آ·a)`-curve-decodable for `خµ â‰¥ (â„“+1)/r`*.
Its proof (Theorem 4.5 [AHS25]-pruning + Lemma 4.1 dimension bound) uses the subspace-design
property (Def 2.17) at the decisive step. To reach the prize window radius `خ´ â‰ˆ 1âˆ’دپ` one needs
`د„(r)+خµ â‰¤ دپ + خک(1/log n)`, hence (since `خµ â‰¥ (â„“+1)/r`) `r = خ©(log n)` AND `د„(r) â‰ˆ دپ` up to
`r = خ©(log n)` â€” i.e. a **strong subspace design** (Def 2.19). GG25 gets this for FRS /
multiplicity (via [GK16]) and for RANDOM RS / RLC (via the [LMS25, BCDZ25b] LCL-threshold
transfer, which needs random evaluation points). The paper itself states curve/line-decodability
**does not fit the LCL framework directly** and gets random RS only through the V-decodability
detour (Prop 5.3), which itself needs **`|F| > aآ·2^{â„“+2}`** â€” exponential in â„“ â€” and random eval points.

**Why explicit plain RS at constant rate is excluded â€” quantified.**
`/tmp/probe_407_R1_plainRS_curvedecodability_obstruction.py` measures `د„(r)` for plain RS over
F_p (eval set = F_p) directly from Def 2.17. Result: `د„(1) â‰ˆ دپ` (single codeword = MDS, â‰¤kâˆ’1 roots),
but `د„(r)` **blows up toward 1 as r grows** (F_17, k=8, دپ=0.47: د„(1)=0.06, د„(2)=0.53, د„(7)=0.87;
same shape F_{11,13,19,23}). A low-dim subspace of plain RS can concentrate zeros (the MDS bound
controls one codeword, not a basis sharing roots), so plain RS is NOT a strong subspace design;
Theorem 4.7 gives a VACUOUS radius (`1âˆ’د„(r)âˆ’خµ â‰ھ 1âˆ’دپ`, below Johnson). The missing statement is
exactly **list-recovery of explicit plain RS into a low-dimensional (not small) subspace** (Lemma 4.1
for plain RS at growing r) â€” an OPEN list-decoding-theory problem, the same wall as the خ´* core.

**Conclusion.** No new angle bridges GG25 curve-decodability to explicit plain RS in the prize
regime via the in-tree chain. The precise obstruction is a single named gap: **plain RS is not a
(strong) subspace-design code and the dimension-bound input (Lemma 4.1 / Thm 4.7 with د„(r)â‰ˆدپ to
r=خ©(log n)) is unproven for it** â€” equivalently, low-dim list-recovery of explicit plain RS, the
list-decoding-theory open problem GG25 explicitly side-steps by restricting to FRS/multiplicity/
random RS (field linear in n / random eval points). Honest-open; obstruction pinned to one Prop.

## n-core-EMPTY certificate count is EXACTLY `C(S+n-1,n-1)` (super-poly) â€” codeword list stays poly; multi-n {16..256} + axiom-clean Lean brick (2026-06-14)

**Sharpens the "Cyclic-sieving / n-core list-growth route" entry above** (the single most
prize-decisive remaining cyclic-sieving experiment, run multi-n to the prize scale `n=256` and
backed by a landed Lean theorem). The earlier entry concluded "super-poly support, poly list" from
`n=8,12,16`; this pins the exact certificate count and proves the super-poly half in Lean.

**The exact certificate count (validated against brute force, ALL `n`).** The in-tree object
(`AbacusNCore.nCoreEmpty`, `n` beads; `HOMDSSmoothObstruction`; `RootsOfUnityVandermonde`):
`خ²_j = خ»_j+(nâˆ’1âˆ’j)`, `خ»` with `â‰¤n` parts; `nCoreEmpty(خ») âں؛ {خ²_j mod n}` pairwise distinct
`âں؛ det(خ¶^{خ²_j i})â‰ 0` on `خ¼_n` `âں؛ s_خ»(خ¼_n)â‰ 0` (RSW hook-content). Reparametrize: an `n`-core-EMPTY
config is `خ² = desc-sort{r + nآ·c_r : râˆˆFin n}` for a unique `c : Fin n â†’ â„•`, with `|خ»| = nآ·âˆ‘_r c_r`.
Hence **`#{n-core-EMPTY خ» of size nآ·S} = #{c : âˆ‘c_r = S} = C(S+nâˆ’1, nâˆ’1)`** (stars-and-bars).
Probe `scripts/probes/probe_ncore_empty_listgrowth_prize.py` validates this closed form against
brute enumeration for `nâˆˆ{4,6,8,10}` (exact match) and cross-validates hook-content(`d=n`) ==
abacus-`n`-core (0 mismatches, `nâˆˆ{8,12,16}`). NB: it is **NOT** the `n`-quotient
`n`-tuple-of-partitions count (a different statistic that over-counts here â€” caught and corrected).

**Multi-n at PRIZE parameters (`خ¼_{2^خ¼}`, `دپ=1/2`, Johnson agreement `a=âŒˆâˆڑ(kn)âŒ‰`, multi-prime).**
Size-capped `n`-core-EMPTY certificate count (`خ£_{S} C(S+nâˆ’1,nâˆ’1)`):
`n=16 â†’153`, `n=32 â†’6545`, `n=64 â†’1.33e9`, `n=128 â†’7.90e17`, `n=256 â†’4.03e37`
(log-log slope 28.2, lin-log slope 0.34 âں¹ **SUPER-POLY**, far exceeding `qآ·خµ*=n`). BUT the EXACT
`F_p` codeword list at Johnson `a` is `1` (n=16); the worst over coset-glued + planted + random
words: at Johnson **1**, at `k+2` **3**, at the `k+1` boundary **20** with SMOOTHâ‰ˆRANDOM (n=12:
20 vs 14), confirming the spike is generic MDS, not cyclic-sieving. The agreement sweep at `n=16`
is the smoking gun: certificate count `1â†’17â†’153â†’969` while the exact list **collapses to 1** for
all `a>k+1`.

**Landed Lean brick (axiom-clean, real `lake build`, 743 jobs).**
`ArkLib/Data/CodingTheory/ProximityGap/NCoreEmptyCParametrization.lean`:
* `cParam c r := r + nآ·c r`; `cParam_mod : cParam c r % n = r` (one bead per runner);
* `nCoreEmpty_cParam : âˆ€ c, nCoreEmpty (cParam c)` (every `c`-config is `n`-core-EMPTY);
* `cParam_injective` (distinct `c` âں¹ distinct configs);
* `exists_injection_nCoreEmpty`, `infinite_nCoreEmpty` (for `nâ‰¥1` the `n`-core-EMPTY set is
  infinite), `nCoreEmpty_card_unbounded` (exceeds any finite `N`).
All `#print axioms = [propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound]`.

**Status â€” precise reduction, NOT closure, NOT refutation.** The super-poly is the `C(A,k+m+1)`
SUPPORT/core factor of the proven `#cores â‰¤ Lآ·C(A,k+m+1)`
(`SubJohnsonListSupply.explainableCoreSupply_of_listBound`) â€” exactly what the certificate
enumeration counts â€” **not** the codeword list `L`. The cyclic-sieving lever gives no list boost
over a random domain; the route reduces to the same named open core `SubJohnsonListBound`'s `L`
(= BGK / Paley / BCHKS Conj 1.12). Do not re-attempt "n-core/hook-content vanishing super-poly-boosts
the smooth list": the super-poly is now CLOSED-FORM `C(S+nâˆ’1,nâˆ’1)` and PROVEN to be the support
factor, not the list.

## REFUTED 2026-06-14: the "Mann-mod-P minimum-weight" reduction (W(n,p) â‰¥ 2âŒˆlog mâŒ‰) â€” moment route is DEAD, floor survives

**The proposed reduction (now refuted).** The char-`p` sub-Gaussian moment bound `E_r(خ¼_n,F_p) â‰¤ (2râˆ’1)!!آ·n^r`
to depth `râ‰چlog m` (which via the moment method gives the prize floor `M â‰¤ âˆڑ(2n log m)`) was reframed as a
**minimum-distance** claim: every `آ±`-/integer-sum `D = خ£ a_c خ¶^c` of `n`-th roots of unity with `D âˆˆ P`
(`خ£ a_c h^c â‰، 0 mod p`) but `D â‰  0` over â„‚ has L1-weight `W(n,p) â‰¥ 2âŒˆlog mâŒ‰` ("Mann's theorem mod P").

**Refuted â€” rigorous theorem + machine-verified witnesses (20-agent army, all 19 angles concur).**
- **Pigeonhole theorem (rigorous, multiple independent proofs G1/G10/R2):** `W(n,p) â‰¤ 2آ·wâ‚پ`,
  `wâ‚پ = min{w : C(n/2, w) > p}`. Over the `n/2`-element fundamental-domain basis `{خ¶^0..خ¶^{n/2âˆ’1}}`
  (`خ¦_n = x^{n/2}+1`), `C(n/2,w) > p` forces a mod-`p` collision of two distinct basis subsets; their
  difference is in `P`, weight `â‰¤ 2w`, and `â‰  0` over â„‚ AUTOMATICALLY (distinct basis subsets âں¹ distinct
  integer coordinate vectors â€” â„‚-nonzero is free, NOT a generic lattice bound, uses the cyclotomic basis
  essentially so it escapes the vacuous `p^{2/n}`). In the prize regime `p ~ n^4`, `wâ‚پ â†’ 5` (CONSTANT), so
  `W(n,p) = O(1)`, vs the target `2âŒˆlog mâŒ‰ â‰چ 6 logâ‚‚ n â†’ âˆ‍`. **False by an unbounded factor** (4أ— at n=32 â€¦
  30أ— at n=2^50).
- **Machine-verified witnesses (independently re-verified, `probe_407_excess_witness_verify.py`):** weight-6
  excess relations, each `خ£ خµ_i h^{c_i} â‰، 0 (mod p)` AND `|D|_â„‚ > 0`, at â€” n=64 p=16778497 `D=z^0+z^1+z^7âˆ’z^9âˆ’z^10âˆ’z^61`
  (|D|=0.85); n=128 p=268440449 (all `+1`, |D|=3.65); n=256 p=4294968833 (|D|=3.69); n=4096 p=281474976768001.
- **Scaling (G3/G9/G11 LLL+MITM, exact minima):** median `W` = 9,7,8,9,9,10,11 for n=32â€¦2048 â€” essentially
  constant (drifts ~`log` slowly), gap to target `2âŒˆlog mâŒ‰` widens without bound.

**Consequence â€” the MOMENT/ENERGY route is DEAD, not merely hard.** Short excess relations of constant weight
exist, so `E_r(خ¼_n,F_p) > E_r(خ¼_n,â„‚)` from constant order `r â‰ˆ wâ‚پ/2`, and `E_r^{F_p}` crosses the Wick value
`(2râˆ’1)!!n^r` near the optimal moment depth `r â‰چ log m` at some structured primes (prime-dependent; e.g.
n=32 p=1048609 crosses at râ‰ˆ9 < depth, but n=16 Fermat p=65537 never crosses in-window). So the only
meta-theorem-permitted route (high moments) is **provably insufficient** to reach the floor.

**BUT خ´* = floor SURVIVES (not refuted).** The actual sup-norm stays strictly below the floor:
`max_{bâ‰ 0}|خ·_b| < âˆڑ(2n log m)` for every tested prize-regime prime (ratio 0.75â€“0.96), with
`c = M/âˆڑ(n log m) â†’ âˆڑ2 from below` (1.06, 1.15, 1.25, 1.36 at n=8,16,32,64). The minimum-weight bound was a
SUFFICIENT-not-necessary condition; refuting it does not move خ´*.

**Net:** the prize requires a **direct sup-norm (BGK/Paley) bound** on `max_b|خ·_b|`; the
moment/energy/min-distance/second-order routes are now all rigorously eliminated. The `[propext,â€¦]`-clean
in-tree `GaussianEnergyBound` hypothesis (`E_r â‰¤ (2râˆ’1)!!n^r` at `râ‰چlog m`) is **FALSE at some prize-regime
primes** â€” do not attempt to prove it; it is refuted. Witnesses + theorem: `probe_407_excess_witness_verify.py`,
`probe_407_excess_lll_*.py`, `probe_407_G1_galois_norm_power_spread.py`.

## 2026-06-14 (wakesync/#407): WORKFLOW confirms r-dependent threshold T(د„)=(2k)^{2k/(د„âˆ’k)} + window-top floppy
Verification workflow (wf_46a17807, 8 finders + adversarial) corroborated the crossover law:
- n=8 dyadic: I(خ´) STABLE above threshold both passes (د„=3â†’304, د„=4â†’64; 30 + 20 above-threshold primes). âœ“
- n=12 NON-DYADIC control: I wildly unstable (swings 3 â†” ~5000) âں¹ rigidity is dyadic-specific. âœ“
- **n=16 د„=5 (r=1)**: ADVERSARIAL pass (primes to 60000) found I FLOPPY = 12 DISTINCT values
  [125900,126192,126208,...,129088] â€” because the TRUE threshold is (2k)^{2k/r}=(2k)^8=16.7M â‰« 60000.
- n=16 د„=6 (r=2): true threshold (2k)^{2k/2}=(2k)^4=4096; Iâ†’1040 stable above (direct run). âœ“
DIRECT DEMONSTRATION: threshold = (2k)^{2k/(د„âˆ’k)} (NOT a flat (2k)^4 â€” the script's hard-coded label was
wrong; the raw per-prime I-values are what matter). Smaller r=د„âˆ’k (window TOP, larger خ´) âں¹ LARGER threshold
âں¹ floppy up to huge q. This is exactly why the prize's binding window-top radius (خ·*=خک(1/log n), r*=خ·*n)
is dirty: T*=(2k)^{2دپ/خ·*}=n^{خک(log n)} â‰« q. Confirms the crossover law cleanâں؛خ·*>2دپ/خ² and the WALL at prize
scale. The earlier د„=6 stabilization was a shallower-radius (larger-r, smaller-threshold) artifact. NOT closure.

vs-simultaneous DICHOTOMY and the proven pâ‰¤(2k)^{2k/r} bound for the ANTIPODAL config (genuine, unrefuted);

## 2026-06-14 (#407 c4700736246 review): all seven open directions attacked; same-radius LDâ‡’MCA shortcut Lean-refuted

Reviewed the linked #407 direction map plus later corrections through c4703070507, then split the open
directions across agents. Verdicts:

* constant-index Gauss periods: the prize-shaped surviving conjecture is **value distribution/MGF
  sub-Gaussianity** of the `m` coset periods; fixed-index DFT/energy bridges are useful consumers, but
  moment/min-distance routes are dead and Katz/Rojas-Leأ³n effectiveness is vacuous in thin prize rows.
* action-orbit/Chai-Fan: orbit closure is real for monomial/eigen directions, but dense/general inputs are
  blocked by `eigen_forces_monomial`; naive constant orbit count is probe-refuted.
* half-sum/lacunary: small fixed-`n` identities are real, but sparse-candidate-prime folklore is false and
  the asymptotic lane re-enters char-`p` transfer/BGK.
* GLT/Fermat fixed-`r`: r=2 is useful calibration; r=3 already looks surface-weight, and any fixed `r`
  leaves the `q^(1/r)` extraction factor.
* Katz/Rojas-Leأ³n: existing conductor/effective-equidistribution window needs `n â‰³ âˆڑq`, outside prize
  production rows such as `q â‰ˆ nآ·2^128`, `n=2^30`.
* cross-parity/ideal-lattice: the literal split leak is a tautology; the aggregate identity is first-moment
  only and local sup-norm remains the BGK/Paley wall.
* eâ‚‚=0/cyclotomic face: formal algebra and dilation reduction are solid, but #400's `O(n)` hope is refuted
  (`#bad = n*K` with super-linear `K` in probes), and the cyclotomic height threshold is exponential. This
  lane is خ´*-connected only conditionally through saturated incidence / `q`-stability; as a raw invariant it
  does not independently reach the prize regime.

**Lean brick landed.** `InterleavedListMCACollapse.SameRadiusCounterexample.same_radius_interleaved_collapse_refuted`
formalizes the documented `Fâ‚ƒ`, length-4 counterexample:
`#mcaBadSet = 3`, same-floor interleaved list `#خ›â‚‚(3)=0`, hence the tempting same-radius bound
`#bad â‰¤ 1+(n-t)#خ›â‚‚(t)` is false. The existing doubled-radius theorem is therefore not an artifact; the
loss to `2t-n` is forced by a concrete finite code. Axiom audit is standard `[propext, Classical.choice,
Quot.sound]`. This kills another LDâ‡’MCA shortcut, but does not touch the true prize wall.

---

## REGIME MAP 2026-06-14: direct per-frequency BGK exponent at the prize point خ²=4 (12-angle army)

After all aggregate routes were eliminated, a 12-angle army attacked the ONLY live route â€” the direct
per-frequency bound `M(n)=max_b|خ·_b| â‰¤ n^{1/2+o(1)}`. No exponent improvement (that gap IS the Paley Graph
Conjecture), but a precise, corrected regime map:

**RETRACTION + corrected map (`issue407-sota-exponent-localization`).** An earlier edit of THIS entry claimed
the best single-frequency bound at خ²=4 is `n^{0.968}` (di Benedetto JNT 2020 Thm 3.3). **That was a
MISATTRIBUTION, now retracted (deepened D1/D2, verbatim from the PDF arXiv:2003.06165):** Thm 3.3's
`N^{2âˆ’31/960}` bounds a DOUBLE intervalأ—subgroup sum `S_a(N,H)=خ£_x خ£_y e_p(axy)`, NOT the single-frequency
Gauss period `M(n)=max_b|خ·_b|`. The honest single-frequency map at the prize:
- **Single-polynomial Stepanov is VACUOUS at the prize:** `M ~ n^{(خ²+1)/4} = n^{5/4}` at خ²=4 (worse than
  trivial), nontrivial only for خ²<3 (the `x^nâˆ’1=0` budget caps the auxiliary at `s<n`, contradicted when
  H~p^{3/4}â‰«n). Confirms the آ§3 HBK "vacuous below q^{1/3}" face exactly.
- **Best RIGOROUS single-frequency exponent for `M(n)`:** di Benedetto Thm 3.1 `n^{1âˆ’31/2880}=n^{0.989}` holds
  only for **خ²<4 strictly** (range p^{1/2}>H>p^{1/4}). **At the exact prize point خ²=4 it is a boundary endpoint**
  where only the Bourgainâ€“Garaev limiting iteration survives: `M â‰¤ n^{1âˆ’175/9437184} = n^{0.99998}` (nontrivial
  but a saving of ~1.85eâˆ’5). For خ²>4: only ineffective BGK `n^{1âˆ’o(1)}`. So the prior pessimistic
  "di Benedetto power-saving essentially vanishes at p^{1/4}" was **closer to right** than the `n^{0.968}`
  overcorrection â€” at خ²=4 the rigorous saving is ~`10^{-5}`, not `0.03`. Vacuity cliff for the خ²<4 bound: خ²=4.775.

**The prize point خ²=1/4 (n=p^{1/4}) is EXACTLY the Burgess barrier (D4, four independent confirmations).**
Burgess 2r-th moment gives خ±(r)âˆ’1/4 = 1/(4rآ²)>0 for all finite r âں¹ trivial exactly at خ¸=1/4; nontrivial only
for خ¸>1/4 (e.g. n=p^{1/2} â†’ n^{0.8125} Konyagin). So the prize sits at the hardest possible Burgess point.

**NEW structural fact (D3): the dyadic tower is MAXIMALLY ANTI-CANCELLING at the worst frequency.** Since n is
even, âˆ’1 = h^{n/2} âˆˆ خ¼_n, so **خ·_b is REAL for every b** (verified |Im|~1e-15). In the tower split
`خ·_b(خ¼_{2n}) = خ·_b(خ¼_n) + خ·_{bخ¸}(خ¼_n)`, the worst-b maximizer ALWAYS selects same-sign children
(`cos(eE,eT)=+1.0000`, 0/100 top maximizers cancel), so `M(2n)=|eE|+|eT|` â€” the cross term is `+Mآ²/2`, the
OPPOSITE of cancellation. The per-level ratio `R=M(2n)/M(n) > âˆڑ2` in 100% of 40 primes (min 1.442). So the
tower-cancellation route to `M(2^خ¼) â‰¤ âˆڑnآ·poly` is REFUTED worst-case; the only rigorous per-level bound is the
trivial triangle `Râ‰¤2`. The âˆڑ-cancellation lives entirely in the sub-maximality of the larger child
(fracâ†’0.74â€“0.83), which IS the BGK sup-norm bound itself (circular).

**Numeric calibration (D10): `M(n) = n^{1/2+o(1)}` CONFIRMED â€” the target is correct.** Best model
`M = 0.69آ·âˆڑnآ·(ln m)^{0.75}`, Rآ²=0.994; local doubling exponent decreases 0.94â†’0.58 (خ¼=4..9), extrapolates to
خ±_âˆ‍ = 0.45آ±0.03 (consistent with 1/2). No worst-b counterexample exists (D11): all `M â‰ˆ 1.3آ·âˆڑ(n log m)`, well
below every rigorous bound. So `خ±>1/2` is ruled out; the conjecture/target is right, just unproven.

**NET: the prize point is the hardest spot of the hardest open problem** (thin-subgroup BGK at the Burgess
barrier). Best proven n^{0.968}; needs n^{0.5}; gap = the fixed sum-product constant â†’ 1/2 = Paley Graph
Conjecture. Aggregate routes (energy D2, slice-rank D7, decoupling D8) all trivial-n / reduce-to-wall.
Probes: probe_407_direct_bgk_*.py (army-landed). Do not re-run D1â€“D12; the map is complete.

### D13 (2026-06-14, independent push): larger-n calibration + exact rigor ledger CONFIRMED

Independent re-derivation (probes `d13_direct_bgk_push.py`, `d13b_largeN_push.py`, `d13c_exact_worstfreq.py`)
corroborates the D1â€“D12 map and EXTENDS the numerics to n=256 at the true prize point (p~n^4, m up to
14.2M cosets, never full group):

* **RIGOR LEDGER recomputed from first principles at خ²=4** (every step explicit, exact `Fraction`):
  di Benedetto Thm 3.1 = n^{0.989236} (= 1âˆ’31/2880, nontrivial since خ²=4 < 191/40=4.775);
  **di Benedetto Thm 3.3 = n^{0.967708} (= 1âˆ’31/960) â€” the PROVABLE MIN in-regime**, confirms n^{0.968};
  Kowalski 2024 = n^{0.999653}; Burgess best-r (incomplete-sum form 1âˆ’1/r+خ²(r+1)/(4rآ²)) = **n^{1.00016}>1
  TRIVIAL at خ²=4** (best r=79), while خ²=2 â†’ 0.875, خ²=3 â†’ 0.979 (both nontrivial). This pins the **Burgess
  barrier at EXACTLY خ²=4**: the moment method is vacuous precisely at the prize point and nontrivial just
  below it. Single-poly Stepanov n^{1.25}, energy â†’ n^{1.0}, both trivial (confirmed).
* **TRUE M-exponent keeps falling with n** (decreasing toward 1/2, far below every proven bound):
  measured mean M-exponent 0.968 (n=8) â†’ 0.942 (16) â†’ 0.891 (32) â†’ 0.855 (64) â†’ 0.833 (128) â†’
  **0.790 (n=256)**. Local/sampled doubling exponent logâ‚‚(M(2n)/M(n)) drops 0.86 â†’ 0.69 â†’ 0.68 â†’ 0.48
  (crosses below 1/2 at n=128â†’256); the sampled max is a lower bound so the trend is conservative,
  consistent with خ±_âˆ‍ = 1/2. **Target M=n^{1/2+o(1)} re-confirmed correct at larger n.**
* **NO REFUTATION, sharper floor constant.** Exact (full-coset-enumeration, no sampling) M(128) at the
  second prize prime gives **M/âˆڑ(2n log m) = 0.9887 < 1** â€” the closest-to-ceiling value yet, refining the
  campaign's "câ†’âˆڑ2 from below": the worst frequency sits at ~0.989آ·âˆڑ2آ·âˆڑ(n log m), strictly under the
  conjectured floor, with the gap to âˆڑ2 shrinking in n. No b with |خ·_b| > âˆڑ2آ·âˆڑ(n log m) exists across
  all (n,p) tested up to n=256. The conjecture/target stands; only the proof is open.

NET unchanged: best proven n^{0.968} (Burgess vacuous at خ²=4, only sum-product survives), needs n^{0.5},
the half-power gap IS the Paley Graph Conjecture for a thin n=p^{1/4} subgroup. The D13 push tightens the
calibration (n=256, exact M/floor=0.989) but finds no new lever and no refutation â€” the cap is structural.

### D14 (2026-06-14, PDF-verbatim correction): the "n^{0.968} provable-min" is a MISATTRIBUTION

The D13 ledger line above ("di Benedetto Thm 3.3 = n^{0.967708} â€” the PROVABLE MIN in-regime, confirms
n^{0.968}") is **WRONG for the single-frequency Gauss period M(n)** and is corrected here against the
**verbatim** theorem statements of di Benedettoâ€“Garaevâ€“Garcأ­aâ€“Gonzأ،lez-Sأ،nchezâ€“Shparlinskiâ€“Trujillo, JNT
2020 (arXiv:2003.06165), extracted directly from the PDF (`~/papers/arklib/.../arxiv-2003.06165-â€¦pdf`,
via `pdftotext`; matches probe `d13_perfreq_pushbig_and_boundary.py` part E2):

* **Theorem 3.1 (verbatim):** "Let H be a multiplicative subgroup of ً‌”½*_p of order H with **p^{1/2} > H >
  p^{1/4}**. Then max_{(a,p)=1}|S_a(H)| â‰² H^{2689/2880} p^{1/72}. In particular, when **H > p^{1/4}**,
  Theorem 3.1 gives max|S_a(H)| â‰² H^{1âˆ’31/2880}." â†’ the n^{0.989236} headline is for the SINGLE-frequency
  subgroup sum and holds for **H > p^{1/4} (خ² < 4)**; both range inequalities are strict.
* **Theorem 3.3 (verbatim):** bounds **S_a(N,H) = خ£_{xâˆˆN} خ£_{yâˆˆH} e_p(aآ·xآ·y)** â€” a DOUBLE
  intervalأ—subgroup sum (N = an interval of N consecutive integers), NOT M(n). Its "N^{2âˆ’31/960}" headline
  is for N,H = p^{1/4+o(1)}. **31/960 is the saving for the interval-subgroup PRODUCT sum, not for
  max_a|S_a(H)| (= our Gauss period).** Attributing n^{0.968} to M(n) conflates two different objects.
* **Boundary خ² = 4 exactly (H âˆ¼ p^{1/4}):** outside Thm 3.1's strict range. The paper's own intro (eq 1.2)
  cites Bourgainâ€“Garaev for the limiting case: max|S_a(H)| â‰¤ H^{1âˆ’175/9437184+o(1)} (saving 1.85أ—10âپ»âپµ â‡’
  **n^{0.99998}**). The text states di Benedetto "replaces 175/9437184 with 31/2880" in (1.2), so under the
  `â‰²` convention (which absorbs p^{o(1)} = H^{o(1)}) the n^{0.989} survives at the boundary; the strictly-
  in-range proven value needs خ² < 4 and the citable endpoint value is Bourgainâ€“Garaev's n^{0.99998}.
* **Strictly thin prize خ² > 4 (n < p^{1/4}):** BELOW Theorem 3.1's range entirely; no di Benedetto bound,
  only ineffective BGK n^{1âˆ’o(1)} (o(1) unquantified, â†’ 0 as the subgroup thins).

**CORRECTED single-frequency cap (M(n), prize regime n â‰¤ p^{1/4}):**
  خ² slightly < 4 (n = p^{1/4+خµ}): **n^{0.989236}** (di Benedetto Thm 3.1, genuinely in-range);
  خ² = 4 exactly (n = p^{1/4}):    **n^{0.989}** via `â‰²`, or **n^{0.99998}** Bourgainâ€“Garaev strictly;
  خ² > 4 (n < p^{1/4}):            **n^{1âˆ’o(1)}** BGK, ineffective.
The honest best PROVEN single-frequency exponent in-regime is **n^{0.989}** (NOT n^{0.968}); the lower
n^{0.968} number is a different (double-sum) theorem. Single-poly Stepanov n^{(خ²+1)/4}=n^{1.25} and energy
n^{1.0} remain trivial (independently re-confirmed). Everything is still a tiny fixed power-saving constant,
nowhere near n^{1/2}; the gap = the Paley Graph Conjecture for a thin خ²=4 subgroup at the Burgess barrier.
Result type: **maps-the-cap** (corrects the constant in the cap; no exponent improvement, no refutation).

### D14 (2026-06-14, independent push): BOUNDARY-ENDPOINT correction + structured-prime constant

Probe `d5_regime_push.py`. Two genuinely new, honestly-labelled results; rest re-confirms D1â€“D13.

* **RIGOR CORRECTION â€” di Benedetto Thm 3.1 does NOT cover n=p^{1/4} exactly; خ²=4 is the EXCLUDED
  endpoint.** Resolves the internal inconsistency between `d1b_dibenedetto_regime_map.py` /D13
  ("خ²=4 comfortably INSIDE, n^{0.989}") and `SubgroupGaussSumDilationRecursion.lean:43` ("خ²>4 sits
  outside every explicit theorem"). The verbatim range is the OPEN interval `p^{1/2} > H > p^{1/4}`;
  with H=n and خ²=log_n p this is `2 < خ² < 4` (n STRICTLY thicker than p^{1/4}). So **خ²=4 (n=p^{1/4})
  is the closed endpoint excluded by the strict `H > p^{1/4}`**; n^{0.989236}=n^{1-31/2880} is only the
  LIMIT as خ²â†‘4 (rigorous for every خ²<4, i.e. n above p^{1/4}). At n=p^{1/4} exactly the only covering
  bound is Bourgainâ€“Garaev n^{1-175/9437184}=n^{0.99998} (saving 1.85e-5). For خ²>4 (n strictly thinner
  than p^{1/4}) NO explicit theorem is nontrivial (Thm 3.1 dies at خ²=4.775 but is uncovered already at
  خ²>4). **Honest in-regime rigorous bracket: M(p^{1/4}) â‰¤ n^{0.989} (open side, any n>p^{1/4}) â†”
  n^{0.99998} (endpoint n=p^{1/4}); both â‰« n^{0.5}, the 0.011-vs-2e-5 distinction is immaterial to
  the 0.489 gap.** Net SOTA at the prize is the di Benedetto LIMIT n^{0.989}, approached from خ²<4,
  never attained AT خ²=4 by an explicit theorem â€” confirms `â€¦DilationRecursion.lean:43`, refines D13.
* **STRUCTURED-PRIME constant overshoot (exact, 12 primes at n=64).** Exact full-coset M for 12
  consecutive prize primes p~64^4: M/âˆڑ(2n ln m) âˆˆ [0.86, 1.05]; the TWO primes with c>1 (i.e. M
  slightly above the conjectured âˆڑ2 floor) are exactly the two with the highest 2-adic valuation
  vâ‚‚(pâˆ’1)âˆˆ{8,9,10} of the 12. So the âˆڑ2 constant ceiling is **regime-gated FALSE at 2-power-structured
  primes** (matching `issue407-thinness-essential-regime-gated`), overshoot â‰¤5%, while the EXPONENT
  خ±=log_n M âˆˆ [0.851,0.899] stays < 1 at every prime â€” M=n^{1/2+o(1)} intact, only the clean c=âˆڑ2 is
  not a uniform ceiling. NO refutation (no خ±>1, no |خ·_b|>1.05آ·âˆڑ(2n ln m)).
* **EXACT calibration extended (n=16..128 full enumeration, no sampling):** خ± = 0.941, 0.902, 0.866,
  0.830 (matches D13 0.942/0.891/0.855/0.833); exact c=M/âˆڑ(n ln m) âˆˆ [1.18,1.30] bounded. n=256,512
  rows SAMPLED = conservative lower bounds (sampling deflates the max; their خ±â‰ˆ0.72â€“0.75 and the
  spurious n=128â†’256 doubling dip 0.16 are sampling artifacts, NOT real drops â€” do not over-read).

NET: SOTA at the prize point is the di Benedetto n^{0.989} LIMIT (خ²â†‘4), with the endpoint n=p^{1/4}
itself covered only by Bourgainâ€“Garaev n^{0.99998}; literature re-checked 2026-06 (arXiv:2003.06165
still SOTA, NO 2024â€“26 improvement indexed). Gap to n^{0.5} = the open Paley Graph Conjecture. Cap
structural; no new lever, no refutation.

### D9 (2026-06-14): KARATSUBA short-character-sum method â€” maps the cap with the EXACT obstruction

Probe `d9_karatsuba_short_charsum.py`. Karatsuba's method (raise `|S|` to the 2k-th power via
coset-stability `|S|â‰¤(1/n)خ£_{tâˆˆخ¼_n}|خ£_x e_p(btx)|`, expand into a 2k-fold additive-energy count,
then BSG/sum-product expand) belongs to and is capped by the SAME Bourgainâ€“Garaevâ€“di Benedetto
lineage as D1/D13. No exponent improvement. Two clean, decision-grade additions:

* **RECONCILES the di Benedetto applicability dispute precisely (closed vs open regime).** Both
  prior readings are correct for what they describe. di Benedetto Thm 3.1's exponent
  `E(خ²)=2689/2880+خ²/72` is on the OPEN interval `p^{1/4}<H<p^{1/2}`. For ANY fixed `خ²<4` (H a
  hair ABOVE `p^{1/4}`) it gives a fixed saving up to `31/2880` (`Eâ†’0.98924` as `خ²â†’4âپ»`); that
  `0.98924` is the SUPREMUM as `Hâ†“p^{1/4}`, **not attained at the boundary**. AT the exact prize
  equality `n=p^{1/4}` (`خ²=4` on the nose â€” which the prize must cover UNIFORMLY for all such
  configs) the sum-product headroom is exhausted and one falls back to Bourgainâ€“Garaev's
  LIMITING saving `175/9437184 = 1.854eâˆ’5`, i.e. **certified `M â‰¤ n^{0.9999815}` uniformly over
  the CLOSED prize regime** (`9437184 = 9آ·2^20`; Karatsuba's خ´آ²-saving law gives the same value
  at خ´=1/4). So: open-interior guarantee `n^{0.98924}` (خ² strictly <4), closed-regime uniform
  guarantee `n^{0.99998}` (خ²=4 included). The prize needs the closed regime âں¹ the honest
  rigorous in-regime number at the exact boundary is `1.85eâˆ’5`, not `31/2880`.
* **MEASURES the exact obstruction: the BSG saving's trilinear input is p-INDEPENDENT and has
  ZERO equidistribution headroom at خ´=1/4.** The power saving needs the 3-fold input of `خ¼_n` to
  spread (max_c râ‚ƒ(c) near the equidistribution value `nآ³/p`). Measured exactly across multiple
  large structured primes per n (prize regime): `Eâ‚‚(خ¼_n)/nآ²` and **`max_c râ‚ƒ(c)/n` are EXACTLY
  p-independent** (identical to â‰¥3 decimals across all primes at fixed n, both â†’3 as n grows; so
  `max_c râ‚ƒ(c) â‰ˆ 3n`). Equidistribution at خ´=1/4 would require `max_c râ‚ƒ ~ nآ³/p = O(1)`; the
  measured `3n` sits a factor `nآ²=p^{1/2}` ABOVE it â€” **maximally far**. The `+خµ` in the required
  range `t>p^{1/4+خµ}` IS exactly this trilinear headroom, and it vanishes at the boundary, forcing
  the saving from `31/2880` down to the limiting `175/9437184`. The خ´آ² (not خ´) scaling is the two
  Cauchyâ€“Schwarz steps in the BSG amplification, each spreading only âˆڑ-much.
* **Ground-truth local M-exponent (worst over a few large primes/n): 0.95â†’0.91â†’0.90â†’0.84 for
  خ¼=4..7**, local doubling slope dropping toward آ½ (consistent with D13's خ±_âˆ‍=آ½ and the truth
  `M=خک(âˆڑ(n log m))`); the method cannot certify it.

NET (D9): adds the precise closed-vs-open reconciliation (uniform certified `n^{0.99998}` at the
exact boundary the prize must cover) and the exact p-independent trilinear obstruction
(`max_c râ‚ƒ/n = 3`, headroom 0 at خ´=1/4). No exponent gain, no refutation â€” confirms the cap is
structural and pins WHY with the explicit constant. `result_type = maps-the-cap`.

## D14 â€” ROUTE B (tail-restricted moment): the anomaly IS the spike, tail-restriction is a no-op (REFUTED)

**Claim tested:** the deep-moment anomaly (خ£_t|خ·_t|^{2r} explodes vs Gaussian at high-2-adicity
primes) is a BULK effect, so a TAIL-restricted moment خ£_{|خ·_t|â‰¥rms}|خ·_t|^{2r} would stay random-like
to depth r~log d and yield M â‰¤ âˆڑ(2n log d) directly (reaching n^{1/2}).

**Verdict: REFUTED. Tail-restriction removes nothing because there is no bulk to remove.**

1. **Full vs tail moment are numerically IDENTICAL.** For every r up to and past r~log d, the ratio
   `(خ£_t|خ·_t|^{2r})/Gauss` equals `(خ£_{|خ·|â‰¥rms}|خ·_t|^{2r})/GaussTail` to `|diff| â‰¤ 6eâˆ’4` at all
   tested large-d prize-geometry primes (p=268369921 n=64 d=4.19M; p=7340033 n=256 d=28672;
   p=5767169 n=256 d=22528). When the full moment is anomalous the tail moment is anomalous by the
   SAME factor.

2. **The anomaly lives in the EXTREME tail, not the bulk** (this corrects the earlier "BULK effect"
   framing). Band-decomposition of the r=8 moment at Fermat F4 (p=65537,n=64): 99.4% of the
   anomalous mass comes from the 3 periods in the [4,6)آ·rms band â€” i.e. the periods that produce M
   itself. At high r, `M^{2r}/خ£|خ·|^{2r} â†’ 1` (0.99+). The deep moment and the M-spike are the SAME
   object; "restricting to the tail" keeps exactly the terms causing the anomaly.

3. **The apparent sub-Gaussian violation at Fermat F4 n=64 is a finite-size (small-d) artifact, NOT
   structure.** At p=65537,n=64 (d=1024): M/âˆڑ(2n log d)=1.465, tail count ratio N(خ»rms)/[d e^{âˆ’خ»آ²/2}]
   blows up (8.7 at خ»=4, single-outlier 262 at خ»=5). But growing d at the SAME 2-adic structure
   (p=268369921=4095آ·2^16+1, n=64, d=4.19M): M/âˆڑ(2n log d) drops to **0.881<1** and the خ»=5 tail
   ratio drops to **0.00**. The session's reported "sub-Gaussian STABLE â‰ˆ0.5أ—Gaussian at d=28672,
   v2=20" is CORRECT; the n=64 Fermat violation was a d=1024 small-sample spike (N=1 vs pred 0.004).

4. **Consequence for the three sub-routes proposed:** (i) tail-restricted moment â€” refuted, identical
   to full (item 1). (ii) bulk-suppressing weight w(خ·) â€” any w that keeps the tail keeps the anomaly,
   any w that kills the tail kills M itself; no separation exists. (iii) Lآ¹-norm route
   خ£|خ·_t|=O(âˆڑ(d n log d)) â€” would need exactly the same sub-Gaussian tail control that the moment
   provides, with no extra leverage (it is a lower-r aggregate of the same tail).

**NET (D14):** Route B does not bypass the anomaly and does not reach n^{1/2}. The sub-Gaussian
upper tail (and hence M â‰¤ âˆڑ(2n log d) by EVT) holds numerically at large d uniformly across high-
2-adicity primes â€” but proving it is the SAME open problem as the deep-moment/cumulant bound at
structured primes (the tail and the deep moment are one object). No new exponent; the established
4th-moment bound M â‰¤ n^{5/8}=p^{5/12} (conditional on the open r=2 energy bound) remains the best
unconditional-route target. `result_type = obstruction-located` (tail = spike, no bulk to excise).

## D15 (2026-06-14, independent verbatim re-audit + SOTA-framing correction)

Re-attacked the ONLY live route (direct per-frequency `M(n)=max_{bâ‰ 0}|خ·_b| â‰¤ n^{1/2+o(1)}`) with a
fresh verbatim PDF audit of the SOTA literature and extended numerics. Confirms D9/D13/D14; adds one
genuinely new correction to the *prompt's own SOTA framing* and one sharper numeric.

* **CONFIRMED (independent verbatim, `pdftotext` of arXiv:2003.06165):** Thm 3.1 range is the OPEN
  interval `p^{1/2} > H > p^{1/4}` (both strict); its `H^{1-31/2880}` is the single-frequency Gauss
  period `S_a(H)=خ·_b`. Thm 3.3's `N^{2-31/960}` is the DOUBLE intervalأ—subgroup sum `S_a(N,H)=خ£_{xâˆˆN}خ£_{yâˆˆH}`,
  a different object â€” the `n^{0.968}` attribution to `M(n)` is a misattribution (matches D14). Best
  honest in-regime single-frequency cap: `n^{0.989236}` (خ²â†‘4 limit, Thm 3.1), `n^{0.99998}` Bourgainâ€“
  Garaev strictly at `n=p^{1/4}`, ineffective `n^{1-o(1)}` (BGK) for `n<p^{1/4}` (خ²>4).

* **NEW correction â€” the prompt's "Kowalski 2024 n^{1-1/2880}" is a CONFLATION.** Kowalski arXiv:2401.04756
  ("Exponential sums over small subgroups, revisited", Jan 2024) is **EXPOSITORY** â€” an account of the
  Bourgainâ€“Glibichukâ€“Konyagin theorem (`|S_a(H)| â‰¤ Hآ·p^{-خ´}` for `H>p^خµ`, with `خ´=خ´(خµ)>0` **ineffective**
  and `â†’0` as the subgroup thins). It is **NOT** a new explicit `n^{1-1/2880}` bound; the `1/2880`
  (and `31/2880`) belong to di Benedetto et al. (JNT 2020). So there is NO 2024 explicit improvement over
  di Benedetto at the prize point; the SOTA single-frequency exponent in-regime remains di Benedetto's
  `n^{0.989}` (open side) / Bourgainâ€“Garaev `n^{0.99998}` (boundary). **No method rigorously beats
  `n^{0.989}` for `M(n)` in the prize regime** (and at the exact boundary `n=p^{1/4}` nothing beats
  `n^{0.99998}`); `n^{0.989}` does not even strictly hold AT `خ²=4`.

* **NUMERIC (collinearity-break, `d14_break_collinearity_fixedn.py`).** Fixed-n vary-p fits
  `M = c(n)آ·(ln m)^{t(n)}` isolate the polylog exponent at fixed n (breaking the prize-regime `ln mâ‰ˆ3 ln n`
  collinearity): `t(n)` RISES `0.09â†’0.36â†’0.46â†’0.42` (n=8,16,32,64) toward ~آ½, NOT the global-fit 0.75.
  The cross-n intercept extraction `c(n)~n^خ±` is UNSTABLE (خ±=0.32, Rآ²=0.78) precisely because `t(n)` is
  itself n-dependent â€” this instability IS the identifiability obstruction made concrete (the 3-param
  joint fit is degenerate along the `(خ±,t)` ridge in prize-regime data). Robust signals unchanged:
  `M/âˆڑ(2n ln m) = c` rising `0.753â†’0.819â†’0.881â†’0.924` (n=8..64, toward the BGK constant from below),
  `M â‰ˆ 1.3آ·âˆڑ(n log m)`. Both consistent with `M=n^{1/2+o(1)}`; neither pins the exponent below the BGK
  target by finite numerics â€” that residual super-polylog `o(1)` IS the open Paley-graph gap.

**NET (D15):** no exponent improvement (the gap to آ½ is the Paley Graph Conjecture for a thin `خ²=4`
subgroup at the Burgess barrier). One framing correction landed: Kowalski 2024 is expository, not an
explicit improvement; SOTA single-frequency cap is di Benedetto `n^{0.989}` (open) / Bourgainâ€“Garaev
`n^{0.99998}` (the exact prize boundary). `result_type = maps-the-cap`.

## 2026-06-14 (#407 method-sweep): Bloom-Sisask quantitative Roth / Sanders Bogolyubov-Ruzsa â€” DEAD, exactly why

METHOD. The SOTA additive-combinatorics quantitative Roth/Bogolyubov technology (Bloom-Sisask
`n/exp(c(log n)^{1/?})` Roth; Sanders Bogolyubov-Ruzsa; Chang's lemma; Plأ¼nnecke-Ruzsa; BSG) applied to
the additive structure of `خ¼_n âٹ‚ F_p` (additive group), against the three live faces R1 (`|خ¼_n^{(+r)}|`),
R3 (DFT sup-norm `max_b|خ·_b|`), and the additive energy `E(خ¼_n)`. 0 in-tree files prior; the cone's
"spectrum" probes are about the Paley/Gauss-sum spectrum, a different object.

PROBES: /tmp/bsr_regime.py, /tmp/bsr_subsetsum.py, /tmp/bsr_spectrum.py, /tmp/spectrum_empirical.py,
/tmp/bsr_constraints.py (exact subgroup spectra computed for (p,n) up to (65537,128)).

FOUR INDEPENDENT KILLS, all in-regime (rechecked, not dismissed out-of-regime):
- **(1) WRONG DIRECTION.** The prize wants UPPER bounds on SMALL objects (sumset small â‰¤ n, max|خ·_b|
  small â‰¤ Câˆڑ(n log m), energy small ~ nآ²). Roth/Bogolyubov machinery is *triggered by LARGE energy /
  SMALL doubling* and *produces* structure from it (BSG: large energy âں¹ structured subset; Bogolyubov:
  2Aâˆ’2A âٹ‡ large Bohr set). `خ¼_n` thin is the SMALL-energy (`E~3nآ²âˆ’3n` Sidon, proven for n<log p),
  LARGE-doubling (`|A+A|~nآ²`, `K~n`) regime â€” the machinery's hypotheses are on the opposite side. The
  Cauchy-Schwarz LOWER bound `Eâ‰¥|A|âپ´/|A+A|~nآ²` is the floor and is already known; BSG adds nothing below
  it.
- **(2) DENSITY VACUITY.** Every Roth/B-S/BR bound enters the ambient `F_p` through the additive density
  `خ±=n/p=2^-128`. The Bloom-Sisask no-3AP density ceiling is `~1/(ln p)^{1.04}â‰ˆ2^-7.5`; `خ±=2^-128` is
  `~2^120` BELOW it. `خ¼_n` is far too sparse to trigger any Roth conclusion. Plأ¼nnecke-Ruzsa `|rA|â‰¤K^r|A|`
  with `K~n` gives `|خ¼_n^{(+r)}|â‰¤2^3191 â‰« p` = worse than the trivial `â‰¤p`. Sanders BR with doubling
  `K~n=2^30` yields a Bohr set of rank `O(K polylog)=exp-large`, density `exp(âˆ’logâپ´K)=0` = vacuous.
- **(3) CHANG GAP = âˆڑn (the sharp obstruction on R3).** Chang's lemma / Bloom-Sisask spectral boosting
  bound the SIZE and DIMENSION of the large spectrum `خ”_دپ={b:|خ·_b|â‰¥دپآ·|A|}`, NOT the single MAX. Parseval
  `âˆ‘_b|خ·_b|آ²=pآ·n` âں¹ RMS`=âˆڑn`; the conjecture needs `supâ‰¤âˆڑnآ·âˆڑ(log m)` (only `âˆڑ(log m)` above RMS). To
  bound the max Chang must push `دپâ†’1` (`|خ·_b|~n=|A|`, the Lآ¹-trivial value); there it gives `|خ”_1|â‰¤ln(1/خ±)
  â‰ˆ89` â€” a FINITE set of â‰¤89 frequencies is *allowed* to have `|خ·_b|~n`. Chang/B-S NEVER rule out one
  `âˆڑn`-larger spike. Off by a full `âˆڑn` factor â€” the same `Lâˆ‍/Lآ²` wall already named at faces 3â†”4
  (BGK/Paley sub-Gaussianity). EMPIRICALLY the rho~1 spectrum (`|خ·_b|â‰¥0.9n`) is EMPTY in every case
  (p,n)â‰¤(65537,128) and `max|خ·_b|` tracks `âˆڑ(n log p)` (conjecture holds) â€” but that is a *char-sum/BGK*
  fact, not a Chang fact; Chang's provable bound stays at `n`.
- **(4) EXCLUDED BY PROVEN CONSTRAINT C1 (thickness-monotone).** Density-increment / doubling methods
  improve monotonically as the set gets denser or has smaller doubling â€” thickness-monotone by
  construction. The program PROVED the conjecture is thinness-ESSENTIAL (FALSE for thick خ²âˆˆ[2.3,3.2] at
  Fermat, TRUE for thin خ²â‰¥4), so ANY thickness-monotone method is excluded â€” exactly as di Benedetto /
  generic sum-product were. Additionally Chang's proof is an `L^{2k}`-moment (Rudin) argument and
  Bloom-Sisask uses Croot-Sisask almost-periodicity (high moments), so its core inherits the proven
  Fermat moment-explosion failure (`E_r^p` crosses Wick at r=5) â€” also barred by C2/C3.

NEEDS-EXTERNAL-INPUT (the single fact that would resurrect it): a Bloom-Sisask-style result that bounds
the Lâˆ‍ / single-max of the spectrum (not its count/dimension) for a thin MULTIPLICATIVELY-structured set,
i.e. a `âˆڑn`-improvement of Chang at `دپ~1` exploiting that `خ”_دپ` is a union of `bآ·خ¼_n` cosets. No such
result exists; it would BE the Paley Graph / BGK sub-Gaussianity bound â€” the open core itself.

VERDICT: dead-precise-why. NOT thinness-applicable (thickness-monotone, density-vacuous, wrong direction,
Chang off by âˆڑn). Records the method as swept. `result_type = dead-direction+density+chang-sqrtn-gap`.

## SWEEP (#407, 2026-06-14): Shkredov higher-energy SOTA â†’ exact crossCell residual â€” DEAD, exact gap

CONTEXT: complements prior lane wf-LF (commit eef47ce41, CrossCellShkredovBound.lean) which refuted the
DIAGONAL form `crossCell â‰¤ خµآ·(2N0(H,r))` and noted H=خ¼_{n/2} is Sidon-like (E_3(H)/|H|^3â†’1, no additive
structure for BSG to feed on). THIS entry adds the quantitative EXPONENT analysis of the MOMENT-method
face (M-bound), which that file did not have.

METHOD: feed best-known Shkredov-school higher additive-energy bounds E_k(G) for multiplicative
subgroups G âٹ‚ F_p through the EXACT dyadic split `N0(G,r)=2آ·N0(H,r)+crossCell` (H=خ¼_{n/2}), where
`crossCell(G,2k)=E_k(G)âˆ’2آ·E_k(H)` (CumulantDyadicDescent.lean; cumulant `خ£_{bâ‰ 0}|خ·_b|^{2r}=qآ·E_râˆ’n^{2r}`).
RECHECKED IN-REGIME (thin prize point n=2^30 < p^{1/4}, qâ‰ˆ2^158, خµ*=2^-128).

SOTA confirmed from literature (MRSS 2019 arXiv:1712.00410; Shkredov surveys):
- E_2(G) â‰¤ |G|^{49/20}=|G|^{2.45} (best PROVEN; 7/3â‰ˆ2.333 = conjectured barrier; sum-product conj = |G|^2).
- E_3(G) â‰¤ |G|^3 log|G|.
- E_k(G) â‰¤ |G|^{2k-2} log|G| (general kâ‰¥2; trivial+log, saves one factor of n over trivial n^{2k-1}).
- ALL valid in the regime |G| â‰¤ p^{2/3}, SATISFIED in the thin prize regime â€” so NOT a regime-vacuity kill.

THREE INDEPENDENT KILLS (all rechecked in-regime, probes /tmp/shkredov_*.py, /tmp/*crosscell*.py,
/tmp/which_regime.py, /tmp/faceA_exact.py, /tmp/exact_gap.py):

- **(1) EXACT GAP = (râˆ’2) factors of n; Shkredov saves only 1 of the (râˆ’1) needed.** In-tree trivial
  bound (MaximalEnergyUniformBound.N0_le_card_pow) is E_r â‰¤ n^{2r-1}; the Wick target the prize needs is
  E_r ~ (2r-1)!!آ·n^r (saves râˆ’1 factors of n); Shkredov gives E_r â‰¤ n^{2r-2}log n (saves EXACTLY 1 factor
  of n). At the moment optimum r* â‰ˆ log_n q â‰ˆ 79 ("r ~ log q"), Shkredov deficit is (r*âˆ’2)=77 factors of
  n. M-bound M=(qآ·E_r)^{1/2r} â†’ n (TRIVIAL full-period), NOT âˆڑn: logâ‚‚M = 30 + 49/r â†’ 30 = logâ‚‚n. Best
  Shkredov M over all r is 2^30.65 (11.9 bits above target 2^18.74); best Wick M is 2^18.93 (0.20 bits, at
  r=79). Full âˆڑq-to-Wick chasm plus (râˆ’2) extra factors of n.

- **(2) WRONG REGIME OF DESIGN â€” bounds LOOSE (above the TRUE value) precisely in the thin prize regime.**
  Empirically (exact N0(G,2k), thin qâ‰«n^4, /tmp/which_regime.py): E_k(G)/Wick â†’ 1 from below (E_2:
  0.75â†’0.94, E_3: 0.42â†’0.82, n:4â†’16), E_k/trivial â†’ 0. Thin-regime subgroup energy IS Gaussian/Wick
  (~n^k), BELOW every Shkredov bound. Shkredov's improving exponents (49/20, 5/8-regime) are SHARP at
  |G|~p^{2/3} (THICK), incidence/sum-product based âں¹ THICKNESS-MONOTONE = the EXCLUDED class (conjecture
  proven thinness-ESSENTIAL). The thin-uniform Shkredov bound is the trivial n^{2k-2}. Either way the
  bound lies strictly ABOVE the true Wick energy â€” can only PROVE a weaker statement than the truth.

- **(3) IS A MOMENT, STRUCTURE-BLIND âں¹ DEAD at Fermat by the proven non-moment requirement.** E_k(G) IS a
  2k-th moment; feeding through M^{2r}â‰¤qآ·E_r IS the moment method, already proven to CAP (CumulantFermat-
  Obstruction: Fermat p=65537,n=64, certificate 38.27 < true M=43.63). Shkredov subgroup-energy bounds are
  char-uniform (identical at Fermat 2^16+1 and generic p of same size) and â‰¥ Wick âں¹ NO extra info where
  the residual bites (structured primes where E_r^p EXCEEDS Wick). Fails constraints (3) NON-MOMENT and
  (2) Fermat-uniformity.

NOTE (the one competitive sub-case, still dies): E_3 Shkredov (n^3 log n) is within O(log n) of Wick
(15 n^3) â€” the only order where SOTA â‰ˆ Wick. But a single LOW order r=3 cannot reach M~âˆڑn:
Mâ‰¤(qآ·n^3 log n)^{1/6}=2^42.1 (23 bits over target). Moment method needs SMALL E_r at LARGE râ‰ˆlog q;
Shkredov DEGRADES to trivial n^{2r-2} exactly as r grows â€” useful only where the method does not need it.

NEEDS-EXTERNAL-INPUT (single fact that would resurrect): a higher-energy bound E_r(خ¼_n) â‰¤ C^rآ·n^r (Wick
exponent, saving ALL râˆ’1 factors) UNIFORMLY up to râ‰ˆlog q AND over Fermat primes = the char-p Lamâ€“Leung
transfer / BCHKS Conj 1.12 / Paley sub-Gaussianity = the open core itself. No Shkredov-school bound
approaches the Wick exponent for râ‰¥3; the school's ceiling is the 7/3 barrier at r=2 only.

VERDICT: dead-precise-why. Best achievable exponent: E_r â‰¤ n^{2r-2}log n âں¹ M â‰¤ ~n (trivial). Exact gap:
(râˆ’2) factors of n at the optimal order râ‰ˆlog_n qâ‰ˆ79 (M-bound overshoots target by 11.9 logâ‚‚ bits at best,
vs Wick's 0.20). `result_type = dead-wrong-exponent+thickness-monotone+is-a-moment`.

MAXIMAL). خ¼_n is additively as UN-structured as possible.

**OBSTRUCTION 2 â€” wrong tensor order + the proven âˆڑ-cap.** crossCell/R2 live at râ‰ˆlog qâ‰ˆ128; PFR/energy

over structured primes. That IS the Paley Graph Conjecture / BCHKS 1.12 (the open core). PFR is the

VERDICT: dead-precise-why. Premise (small additive doubling) measured FALSE (Kâ‰ˆn/2); conclusion vacuous

CONTEXT. The refutation hunt flagged a structured-prime spike: at Fermat/2-power primes the Wick âˆڑ2-floor

object) refutations.

    to EXACT-at-b*.) The exact single-level identity خ·_b(خ¼_n)=خ·_b(خ¼_{n/2})+خ·_{خ¶b}(خ¼_{n/2}) confirmed at
    machine precision (already substrate `eta_union_dilate`).
  â€¢ **The blow-up is خ²-GATED, not v2-gated** (`probe_tower_ratio_beta_vs_v2.py`, 34 curated primes â‰،1 mod
    16, n=16, خ² & v2(pâˆ’1) varied INDEPENDENTLY): corr(خ², M(n)/M(n/2)) = **+0.62** (thinness drives the
    ratio toward 2) but corr(v2, ratio) = **âˆ’0.04** (2-adic structure does NOT, once خ² is freed). The

  â€¢ **Prize regime is SAFE.** Holding n=16 fixed at thin خ²â‰¥4 on the WORST structured primes:
