## C31 (issue #444) â€” Katz Vertical/Horizontal Sato-Tate "support edge bounds uniform sup" â€” SECRETLY-OPEN

**Date:** 2026-06-15. **Probe:** `scripts/probes/probe_c31_satotate_support_edge.py` (commit f86e902f7).

**Claim:** the period-family geometric monodromy yields a limiting Sato-Tate measure whose SUPPORT
EDGE bounds `max_c|خ·_c| â‰¤ âˆڑ(2n log p)` past Johnson (reduces-to Katz vert/horiz Sato-Tate +
Rojas-Leأ³n classification + Deligne Weil II).

**Verdict: SECRETLY-OPEN.** Two independent horns, both confirmed prize-faithfully (proper خ¼_n,
n=2^خ¼, p prime, pâ‰«nآ³, m=(p-1)/n>1, never n=p-1):

- **HORN-EDGE (category error, load-bearing).** A Sato-Tate measure governs the LIMITING
  DISTRIBUTION of the normalized value `z_c = خ·_c/âˆڑn`; its support is the closure of attainable
  normalized values. By Rojas-Leأ³n (monodromy = GL(1)^f, only Hasseâ€“Davenport relations), `z_c` is a
  normalized sum of f=(p-1)/n independent unit Gauss phases âں¹ COMPLEX GAUSSIAN by CLT. Measured:
  `E|z|^{2r}/r! â†’ 1` in low orders (0.999, 1.31, â€¦), the Gaussian signature; the limiting measure has
  support = **all of C, NO compact edge**. So there is no "support edge" object to bound the sup. The
  factor âˆڑ(2 log p) is the EXTREME VALUE of this unbounded Gaussian over the p/n cosets:
  `max|z| / âˆڑ(2 ln(#cosets)) = 0.71 â†’ 0.81 â†’ 0.89 â†’ 0.96` (climbs to 1 as coset count grows) â€” i.e.
  the union-bound tail max ~ âˆڑ(n log(p/n)), which IS the OPEN BGK/Paley sup-norm, not an edge.
- **HORN-DIM (inherited from C13).** Monodromy torus dim = f=(p-1)/n ~ 2^128, not خ¼/n. Deligne/Weil II
  effective discrepancy = f/âˆڑq ~ âˆڑp/n ~ 2^46â€¦2^56 â‰« 1 â€” catastrophically vacuous for n â‰ھ p^{1/4}.
- **HORN-VERT/HORIZ.** Both vertical (vary sheaf/د‡) and horizontal (vary field, qâ†’âˆ‍) Sato-Tate are
  DISTRIBUTIONAL limiting statements; neither controls the DETERMINISTIC MAX over a thin designed
  subgroup at one FIXED prize prime.

Net: the "support edge" is precisely the open BGK extreme value (the conjecture asserts its own open
conclusion). Consistent with the route-elimination meta-theorem and the prior C13 SECRETLY-OPEN
finding. No new non-BGK lever; the open core is not discharged.

## [C37] (issue #444) â€” Burgess-Type Amplification for the Thin Dyadic Subgroup Sum â€” REFUTED-FALSE (2026-06-15)

**Date:** 2026-06-15. **Probe:** `scripts/probes/probe_C37_burgess_amplification.py` (commit e9697347a).

**Claim:** a Burgess-style shifted-multiplicative amplification of the period sum
`S_a(خ¼_n)=خ£_{xâˆˆخ¼_n} e_p(ax)` "recovers cancellation in the prize band by exploiting many small shifts
within خ¼_n," giving `M(خ¼_n) â‰¤ âˆڑ(n log p)` past Johnson (reduces-to Burgess machinery + in-tree antipodal
+ char transfer).

**Verdict: REFUTED-FALSE.** The load-bearing Burgess step is structurally inapplicable to a thin
multiplicative subgroup, demonstrated by direct probe over proper خ¼_n (n=2^خ¼, p prime, pâ‰«nâپ´, خ²=5,
n<p^{1/4}, never n=p-1).

- **What Burgess actually does (and what the claim mis-states).** The Burgess amplifier for a SHORT
  character sum `T=خ£_{xâˆˆI}د‡(x)`, `|I|=N`, works because `I` is an **interval**: the load-bearing first
  step replaces `T` by an average over translations `T â‰ˆ (1/|Sh|)خ£_{tâˆˆSh}خ£_{xâˆˆI}د‡(x+t)`, which is nearly
  lossless precisely because `|(I+t)âˆ©I| = Nâˆ’|t| â‰ˆ N` for the relevant small shifts. The 2r-th moment
  over `t` is then a COMPLETE multiplicative correlation sum bounded by Weil/âˆڑp. The whole gain
  `N^{1âˆ’1/r}p^{(r+1)/(4rآ²)}` is nontrivial only for `N â‰« p^{1/4}`. The conjecture's phrase "many small
  shifts WITHIN خ¼_n" conflates this with multiplicative shifts: `xâ†¦xh` for `hâˆˆخ¼_n` merely PERMUTES خ¼_n
  (zero amplification, `S_a` is fixed up to the `aâ†¦a h` relabel), while the only structurally-real shift
  is the ADDITIVE translation Burgess uses â€” and that needs the set to be an interval.
- **The amplification is identically zero for a subgroup (probe, decisive).** Measured the load-bearing
  overlap `|(خ¼_n+t)âˆ©خ¼_n|` over 600 random nonzero `t`, strictly in the prize band pâ‰«nâپ´ (خ²=5):

  | خ¼ | n | p (prime) | خ²=log_n p | p/nآ³ | mean `|(خ¼_n+t)âˆ©خ¼_n|` | nآ²/p (random-set value) | max |
  |---|---|-----------|-----------|------|----------------------|--------------------------|-----|
  | 4 | 16 | 1048609 | 5.00 | 256 | **0.0000** | 0.000229 | **0** |
  | 5 | 32 | 33554593 | 5.00 | 1024 | **0.0000** | 0.000030 | **0** |
  | 6 | 64 | 1073741953 | 5.00 | 4096 | **0.0000** | 0.000004 | **0** |
  | 7 | 128 | 34359740801 | 5.00 | 16384 | **0.0000** | 0.0000004 | **0** |
  | 8 | 256 | 1099511630593 | 5.00 | 65536 | **0.0000** | ~0 | **0** |
  | 10 | 1024 | 1125899906856961 | 5.00 | 10âپ¶ | **0.0000** | ~0 | **0** |

  (At the خ²=3 boundary `pâ‰ˆnآ³`, the in-tree run gives mean `nآ²/p â‰ˆ 0.005â€“0.11`, max 2 â€” still negligible.)
  The subgroup behaves like a RANDOM set under additive translation: `E|(خ¼_n+t)âˆ©خ¼_n| = n(nâˆ’1)/(pâˆ’1) â‰ˆ
  nآ²/p â†’ 0` for `n<p^{1/4}` (since `nآ²/p < p^{1/2}/p = p^{âˆ’1/2}`). Translating the summation set by any
  small `t` annihilates the overlap, so the Burgess average `â‰ˆ(1/|Sh|)خ£_t خ£_{xâˆˆخ¼_n}د‡(x+t)` carries NO
  information about `خ£_{xâˆˆخ¼_n}د‡(x)`. **Amplification efficiency = mean_overlap/n = 0.** There is nothing
  to amplify.
- **The threshold is exactly the prize-excluded side.** Burgess (and every short-interval/GRH variant)
  is non-trivial only for `Nâ‰«p^{1/4}`; the whole prize band is `n=p^خ´` with `خ´âˆˆ[0.135,0.215] < 1/4`
  (خ²>4). This is the same `p^{1/4}` wall that excludes di Benedetto et al. (the in-tree gaussperiods
  reading list, line 79-81: "Requires n>p^{1/4}; the prize has n<p^{1/4} strictly") and is the exact
  twin of the **C36 REFUTED-FALSE** Heath-Brownâ€“Konyagin Stepanov entry: both are `q^{1/3}`/`p^{1/4}`-blind
  to the thin prize subgroup. "Many small shifts" cannot move the threshold because the gain is
  exponent-fixed by the completion-to-Weil step, not by a constant.
- **No char-transfer / antipodal rescue.** The antipodal structure of `خ¼_{2^خ¼}` (Lamâ€“Leung: only the
  pair relation `z+(âˆ’z)=0`) governs the CHAR-0 additive-energy bound; it says nothing about additive
  translates of خ¼_n in F_p, and the probe's overlap-collapse holds AT the prize prime regardless. The
  char transfer brick (L5) transfers incidence, not a Burgess interval-gain that does not exist.

**Mechanism class.** Same horn as the entire interval/short-sum family (C36 Stepanov, all Weil/âˆڑq): the
method's nontriviality threshold (`N>p^{1/4}`) is precisely the prize-excluded boundary, and its
load-bearing translation-amplification step is identically lossless only for an INTERVAL â€” for a thin
multiplicative subgroup the additive shift-overlap is a measured 0 (random-set `nآ²/p`), so the amplifier
produces no cancellation. Matches the DEAD-ROUTES note ("Weil/âˆڑq: vacuous for n<âˆڑq") and the
gaussperiods reading-list headline ("even GRH/Burgess control intervals not subgroups, and need
n>p^{1/4}"). No new non-BGK lever; the open core is not discharged â€” the bound it claims to deliver
(`âˆڑ(n log p)` for n<p^{1/4}) IS the open BGK/Paley sup-norm.

**Probe committed:** `scripts/probes/probe_C37_burgess_amplification.py`.

## C38 (#444) â€” Multiplicative Energy Concentration via BSG on Bad Scalars â€” **SECRETLY-OPEN** (premise REFUTED) â€” 2026-06-15

**Claim.** The set of bad scalars `خ³` (for a far monomial pencil) has *small multiplicative
energy* by Balogâ€“Szemerأ©diâ€“Gowers ("since each contributes a structured agreement set"), so BSG
extracts an approximate multiplicative subgroup, Plأ¼nneckeâ€“Ruzsa controls its product set, and the
proven antipodal coset closure forces `|bad خ³| â‰¤ budget ~ n` PAST Johnson. REDUCES-TO: BSG +
Plأ¼nneckeâ€“Ruzsa + in-tree `FactorizationRigidity` coset closure + orbit-count law L3.

**Two independent kills, both machine-witnessed (exact over proper `خ¼_n`, `p` prime, `p â‰« nآ³`,
NEVER `n=pâˆ’1`; probes `probe_c38_bsg_scalar_energy.py`, `probe_c38_bsg_scalar_coset_followup.py`).**

**(1) The "small multiplicative energy" premise is FALSE â€” the bad set is a multiplicative COSET
UNION (maximal energy).** Computed the exact bad-scalar set `B(a,b,خ´) = {خ± : x^a+خ±x^b is خ´-close to
RS[خ¼_n,k]}` for far pencils, then its multiplicative energy `E_mult(B)`, product set `|Bآ·B|`, and
coset structure. Across all non-degenerate rows `B` is **exactly a union of cosets of `خ¼_n`**
(`coset|H|=8` at n=8, `coset|H|=16` at n=16 â€” verified by `bآ·خ¼_n âٹ† B âˆ€bâˆˆB`):

| n | k | دپ | p | (a,b) | خ´ | J | cap | \|B\| | budget n | E_mult | \|Bآ·B\| | E/\|B\|آ² | structure |
|---|---|---|---|-------|---|---|-----|-----|--------|--------|-------|--------|-----------|
| 8 | 2 | 1/4 | 4129 | (3,4) | 0.637 | 0.500 | 0.750 | 24 | 8 | 7680 | 48 | 13.3 | coset \|H\|=8 |
| 16 | 2 | 1/8 | 65537 | (3,4) | 0.841 | 0.646 | 0.875 | 400 | 16 | 5754880 | 4928 | 36.0 | coset \|H\|=16 |
| 16 | 2 | 1/8 | 1048609 | (3,4) | 0.772 | 0.646 | 0.875 | 48 | 16 | 61440 | 96 | 3.8 | coset \|H\|=16 |

A coset union has the *maximal* multiplicative energy for its size (`E_mult â‰ˆ 56â€“100% of |B|آ³`,
i.e. `13â€“36أ—` above the Sidon/random floor `|B|آ²`), with a *tiny* product set `|Bآ·B| â‰ˆ 2n`
(products stay in a bounded number of cosets). This is the **polar opposite** of the small-energy /
multiplicatively-Sidon set BSG needs. C38's premise is not merely unverified â€” it is *false on the
actual object*. (Mirror of the C32 vacuity: BSG is a HIGH-energy theorem; its hypothesis is
vacuous/inverted on these structured sets. Here, worse: `B` is the extremal coset, so BSG's
*conclusion* â€” "approximate multiplicative subgroup" â€” is **given for free** by `FactorizationRigidity`
and supplies nothing new.)

**(2) BSG/PR run BACKWARDS â€” they never bound `|B|`; the cardinality is the open object.** Even
granting structure: BSG (`E_mult â‰¥ |B|آ³/K âں¹ approx subgroup`) and Plأ¼nneckeâ€“Ruzsa
(`|Bآ·B| â‰¤ K^{O(1)}|B|`) take a *cardinality* and *produce* structure; neither yields an *upper bound
on `|B|`*. The only Cauchyâ€“Schwarz relation runs the wrong way: `E_mult(B) â‰¥ |B|âپ´/|Bآ·B|` (verified:
`7680 â‰¥ 24âپ´/48 = 6912`), i.e. `|B| â‰¤ (E_multآ·|Bآ·B|)^{1/4}`. To force `|B| â‰¤ n` you must first bound
`|Bآ·B| â‰¤ n` (and `E â‰¤ nآ³`). But `|Bآ·B|` = the number of cosets the product spans = the **orbit-count
law L3 input** (`I_pencil = N_pencilآ·S`, `S = n/gcd(bâˆ’a,n)`), whose unconditional bound is `â‰¤ n` via
`gcd(bâˆ’a,n) â‰¤ n` â€” exactly the **Johnson-capped** object (meta-theorem: every closed/coset/
orbit-count object caps at `1âˆ’âˆڑدپ`). Measured `|B| = 25n` and `|Bآ·B| = 308n` past Johnson
(n=16, خ´=0.841): the bad set is genuinely large past Johnson, and bounding its coset count below
budget is precisely the p-DEPENDENT BGK sup-norm `M(خ¼_n) â‰¤ Câˆڑ(n log(p/n))` (open; SOTA `n^{1âˆ’o(1)}`,
di Benedetto `n^{0.989}`). BSG/PR are p-INDEPENDENT low-order tools (their trilinear input is exactly
p-independent, see D9); they cannot supply the p-dependent moment.

**Verdict: secretly-open**, with the stated mechanism **refuted**. The "small multiplicative energy"
premise is false (the bad set is a maximal-energy coset union); BSG's structure conclusion is free
and inert (`FactorizationRigidity` already gives it); and the actual cardinality step â€” counting the
cosets below budget past Johnson â€” is the open BGK/Paley sup-norm. C38 collapses to **the same wall
as C32** (BSG vacuous), and to **reduces-to-johnson** on the only reading where the coset-closure /
orbit-count L3 bound is the operative lever (it caps at `1âˆ’âˆڑدپ`). Consistent with O11 (bad count =
subset-sumset size of the free set), the energy-method cap L8, and the p-independent-trilinear
obstruction D9.

**Probes committed:** `scripts/probes/probe_c38_bsg_scalar_energy.py`,
`scripts/probes/probe_c38_bsg_scalar_coset_followup.py` (self-contained, exact GF(p), proper `خ¼_n`,
`p` prime `pâ‰«nآ³`, NEVER `n=pâˆ’1`). Probe commit `be92452b1`.

## [C45] Lam-Leung Antipodal Saturation Caps List Exactly at Johnson â€” REDUCES-TO-JOHNSON (the honest boundary statement, by its own construction) â€” 2026-06-15

**Conjecture C45 (additive-combinatorics, feas 1).** Claim the iterated Lam-Leung coset
saturation proves the worst-case far-line list size equals exactly `|H^{(+r)}(خ¼_n)|` at the
Johnson radius and NO further, i.e. the closed antipodal object caps **precisely at Johnson
`1âˆ’âˆڑدپ`**. Explicitly self-described as "the honest boundary pin (closed and true but NOT past
Johnson)." Reduces-to: in-tree iterated Lam-Leung coset saturation + Mann/Conway-Jones + L8
meta-theorem (2nd-order capped at `âˆڑS`).

**Verdict: reduces-to-johnson** (the single most-common horn â€” and here it is admitted in the
statement itself). C45 is true and closed: it reduces only to named axiom-clean in-tree bricks
(`EvenOddAntipodalCharFree.eval_neg_eq_eval_of_oddCoeffZero` / `LamLeungMultisetAntipodal.
count_antipodal_of_sum_eq_zero`; `LamLeungUnconditionalQ` unconditional t=1 antipodal tightness;
`_wf3D5_lamleung_orbit_backbone.card_dvd_of_free_orbits`; `_MetaTheoremSecondOrderFloor.
secondMoment_method_floor`/`momentDepth_method_floor`). But its OWN conclusion is the Johnson
value â€” it pins the cap **at** `1âˆ’âˆڑدپ`, NOT in the window interior `(1âˆ’âˆڑدپ, 1âˆ’دپâˆ’خک(1/log n))`. So
it does not pin خ´* past Johnson; it is exactly the route-elimination meta-theorem restated as a
positive boundary claim.

**Why it cannot reach past Johnson (the mechanism, not just the self-admission).** The Lam-Leung
antipodal object is **char-0 / p-INDEPENDENT**: Mann/Conway-Jones gives the ONLY primitive
vanishing relation among `2^خ¼`-th roots of unity is the antipodal pair `z+(âˆ’z)=0`, so the
saturated object `H^{(+r)}(خ¼_n)` is a fixed combinatorial set independent of the prime `p`. Its
list count is governed purely by the second-moment / L2 geometric-mean = `âˆڑS = âˆڑ(n/gcd)` scale
(`_MetaTheoremSecondOrderFloor`: no second-moment-only method, at any depth `r`, beats `âˆڑS`).
That scale **is** the Johnson radius `1âˆ’âˆڑدپ`. The extra list elements that appear strictly inside
the window are exactly the p-DEPENDENT fibre points the antipodal skeleton **cannot see**
(`LamLeungUnconditionalQ` docstring: char-p independence FAILS in `F_q`, and those failures = the
open BGK problem). The cap C45 produces is therefore not just admittedly-at-Johnson â€” it is
**provably-at-Johnson**, because a p-independent antipodal/2nd-order object is L8-capped at `âˆڑS`.

**Probe (honesty contract satisfied).** `scripts/probes/probe_c45_lamleung_antipodal_johnson_cap.py`
(commit `7a8422f6d`): proper subgroup `خ¼_n < F_p*`, `p` PRIME, `p â‰« nآ³`, NEVER `n=pâˆ’1`.
- n=16, k=2, دپ=1/8: `p=65537` (p/nآ³=16.0, prime, n proper subgroup), Johnson خ´*=0.6464, `w_J=5`;
  Johnson list proxy 256, `âˆڑn=4` (antipodal/L2 scale).
- n=32, k=4, دپ=1/8: `p=1048609` (p/nآ³=32.0, prime), Johnson خ´*=0.6464, `w_J=11`;
  list proxy 1024, `âˆڑn=5.66` (L2 scale).
The antipodal count tracks the `âˆڑn` (L2 = Johnson) scale exactly; it has no p-dependent term and
therefore cannot grow into the window interior.

**Distinction from C01 (refuted-false).** C01 claimed an antipodal-sumset mechanism pins خ´*
= `(1âˆ’دپ)âˆ’logâ‚‚(n)/n` PAST Johnson and was refuted (true offset is `n/4`, linear, not `logâ‚‚ n`).
C45 makes the OPPOSITE, honest claim: the same antipodal object caps AT Johnson and no further.
C45 is the correct (true) reading of what the closed antipodal saturation actually delivers; the
gap to the prize is precisely the p-dependent BGK sup-norm `M(خ¼_n) â‰¤ Câˆڑ(n log(p/n))` that no
closed/antipodal/2nd-order lever can supply (route-elimination meta-theorem, L8).

2026-06-15 (push e69afd587) â€” t=3 TRINOMIAL n/2 floor PROVEN (axiom-clean) + t=4/k>=2 single-line count SATURATES (boundary note)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
POSITIVE (UncertaintyTwoPowerTrinomialFloor.lean, axiom-clean [propext,Classical.choice,Quot.sound]):
the GENUINE 3-term trinomial g(X)=(X^{n/4}-1)(X^{n/4}-i)=X^{n/2}-(1+i)X^{n/4}+i (i:=zeta^{n/4}, prim 4th
root, support {0,n/4,n/2}, all 3 coeffs nonzero) has EXACTLY n/2 roots in mu_n: (zeta^j)^{n/4}=i^j in
{1,i} iff j%4 in {0,1} (half of [0,n)). So s* >= n/2 already at t=3 -> the n/2 sparse-zero floor is
STRUCTURAL, not an artifact of degenerate t=2 single-coset sparsity. Sharpens UncertaintyTwoPowerJohnson
Refuted (binomial). 2-power-specific (needs order-4 zeta^{n/4}; Tao forbids it for prime n). p-indep
n=8..64 (probe_407_trinomial_n2_floor.py). NOT CORE (s* != prize delta*; the prize is the LIST, O(1) here).

BOUNDARY NEGATIVE (probe_t4_k2_floor.py, exact): the analogous k>=2 single-line sparse-zero count does
NOT give a clean n/2+(k-1) extension -- it SATURATES at the FULL group n (k=2: 8@n=8, 16@n=16). When the
codeword span {1,x,...,x^{k-1}} (k>=2) is allowed to vary jointly with 2 far monomials, the agreement
system can fit ALL n points at degenerate (non-far) directions -- the known "I=p, every gamma, at the
degenerate radius" saturation. => the meaningful single-line s* object is the FIXED-far-direction count,
and the clean non-degenerate trinomial brick is the t=3/k=1 boundary; the k>=2 naive count is vacuous.
Do NOT pursue a "t=4/k=2 sparse floor" lane -- it saturates trivially. CORE unaffected, OPEN.

2026-06-15 (opus-4-8 subagent) â€” crossCell UPPER-BOUND FORM AUDIT: the in-tree
`CrossCellAbsoluteBound` POINTWISE form is REFUTED, and so is the DC-subtracted form
(at finite n). Constraint lemma: the only true crossCell bound is ASYMPTOTIC (prize
budget q >> 2^r|H|^r), NEVER a finite-instance pointwise inequality.
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
CONTEXT. `CrossCellShkredovBound.lean` defines (and its docstring labels "the genuine open
core (NOT refuted; remains the wall)"):
    CrossCellAbsoluteBound :  crossCell(H,zeta,r) * q  <=  2^r * |H|^r   (all r>=2).
where crossCell(H,zeta,r) = N0(G,r) - 2*N0(H,r), G=mu_n, H=mu_{n/2}, q=p.
Round-1 L1 (#444 sec.11) flagged the single point p=97,n=8,r=4 as a violation but never
swept it. I settled it EXACTLY, multi-prime, PROPER mu_n (m=(p-1)/n>1), p==1 mod n,
NEVER n=q-1, via exact N0 counts (poly powering mod x^p-1, big-int).
  probe_407_crosscell_absolute_pointwise.py  +  probe_407_crosscell_dcsub_form.py.

RESULT 1 â€” the POINTWISE absolute bound A: crossCell*q <= 2^r|H|^r is REFUTED everywhere r>=3.
  - r=2: cross=0 EXACTLY at every prime (matches `crossCell_eq_zero_of_r_eq_one` / E_1 halving;
    the descent is exact at the 2nd moment). So the bound is non-trivial only for r>=3.
  - r>=3: VIOLATED at EVERY tested (n,p). The violation ratio (cross*q)/(2^r|H|^r) GROWS with p
    (= thinner subgroup at fixed n): e.g. n=8,r=4: ratio 2.27 (p=97) -> 13.5 (p=577) -> 60.8 (p=2593);
    n=32,r=4: 2.32 (p=577) -> ... . It is WORST at the thinnest (most prize-like) primes.
  - p-pattern: cross itself stabilizes to a p-independent count for p>>n^3 (n=8,r=8 cross=180320 for
    all p>=577), so cross*q ~ q grows linearly while 2^r|H|^r is fixed => unbounded violation.
  => the per-r absolute bound CANNOT hold pointwise; it is the wrong shape. The docstring's
     "NOT refuted; remains the wall" is FALSE for this pointwise statement.

RESULT 2 â€” even the DC-SUBTRACTED forms are pointwise-refuted at finite n (sharper than L1):
    C: crossCell*q <= (2^r - 2)|H|^r                 [random main term only]
    B: crossCell*q <= (2^r - 2)|H|^r + (2r-1)!!|H|^r  [random + Wick, the #444 sec.2 mandatory shape]
  - C: REFUTED for all r>=4 (and r>=3 at n=16,32) at every prime.
  - B (the +Wick MANDATORY-form analogue): REFUTED at n=8 p>=577 r=4 (ratio>1), and n=16 p=257 r=3,
    and similar â€” i.e. B FAILS pointwise exactly when q is too SMALL relative to 2^r|H|^r. B HOLDS
    whenever q is large enough (n=8 p<=193 all r tested), confirming it is an ASYMPTOTIC bound.

CONSTRAINT LEMMA (the precise wall shape):
  crossCell*q is an exact additive-resonance count whose leading behaviour is the RANDOM term
  (2^r - 2)|H|^r PLUS a positive arithmetic excess from spurious mod-p collisions. At FINITE n the
  excess can exceed BOTH the random term and the random+Wick budget when q = p is not yet >> 2^r|H|^r
  (i.e. when the subgroup is not yet "thin enough" for the budget). The bound becomes true ONLY in the
  asymptotic prize regime q ~ n*2^128 >> 2^r|H|^r at depth r~89. Therefore NO pointwise/per-instance
  crossCell upper bound (A, B, or C) is provable as stated; the genuine open core is the ASYMPTOTIC
  inequality crossCell*q <= (2^r-2)|H|^r + (2r-1)!!|H|^r in the limit q/(2^r|H|^r) -> infinity, i.e.
  exactly the char-p validity of A_r <= Wick at depth r~ln q (#444 sec.2). This is the SAME BGK wall;
  it just cannot be phrased as the finite pointwise `CrossCellAbsoluteBound` currently in the tree.

SCOPE (rule-6, honest): NOT a CORE closure. This is a FORM CORRECTION + refutation: it removes a
mislabeled "open core" (the pointwise absolute bound), shows the DC-subtracted pointwise form is also
finite-n-false, and re-localizes the genuine open statement to the asymptotic budget regime (consistent
with the mandatory A_r form, sec.2). rule-3: the violation is thickness-MONOTONE (worse at small m =
thicker-relative subgroup, better as p grows) and p-budget-driven, NOT a thin-vs-thick CORE signal -- so
this is purely a statement-shape correction, not a CORE lever. Python+numpy EXACT, multi-prime,
proper subgroup => axiom-clean trivially (no Lean proof changed; docstring corrected separately).

RESULT 1 â€” baseline E_3/Wick is p-INDEPENDENT and DROPS toward 1 as n grows (advantage shrinks):

     compound into a power saving. Consistent with the آ§7-lane-2 finding that depth-growth is
     necessary-not-sufficient and the thin/random sup-norm ratio is a FLAT ~0.92 constant.

RESULT 2 â€” the route has STRUCTURED-PRIME SPIKES that break any pointwise A_r â‰¤ Wick at finite n:
  at n=32, p=32993 (=2âپµآ·1031+1, m=1031), E_3/Wick = 1.706 > 1 while every neighbouring thin prime
  is ~0.91. DC-subtraction does NOT explain it (E_3âˆ’nâپ¶/p still 1.640آ·Wick; DCâ‰ˆ32545 is negligible
  at qâ‰ˆnآ³). So the per-instance A_3 = E_3âˆ’DC â‰¤ Wick is POINTWISE-FALSE at finite n â€” exactly
  mirroring the crossCell pointwise refutation (this log, earlier 2026-06-15 entry). Same wall shape:
  finite-n excess > Wick when q is not yet â‰« the random budget.

RESULT 3 (DECISIVE, rule-3) â€” the E_3 spike is DRIVEN ENTIRELY by the E_2 (2nd-order) anomaly:
  at the spike prime, E_2/2nآ² = 1.828 vs the FLAT baseline 1.453 at EVERY non-spike prime

CONSTRAINT LEMMA (extends آ§3 meta-theorem to r=3, explicitly measured):
  E_3(خ¼_n) carries NO independent thin-CORE information beyond E_2: its sub-Wick baseline shrinks
  toward random with n (no compounding), and its only deviations (finite-n spikes that violate

SCOPE (rule-6, honest): NOT a CORE closure. A reproducible probe + constraint lemma closing the

pair is 'NOROOT' (e1=0, e0!=0) -- a NOROOT pair ABORTS the whole subset. But 3 points

a SUBSET of the 17; 12 valid gamma dropped). So the published #bad (5,25,113,481 =

    s*:  9   10   11   12   13

    d*: .55  .50  .45  .40  .35
  n=16, p=65537 AND p=1048609 (p-independent, PROPER):
    k :  4    5    6    7
    s*:  7    9    9   11
    c*:  3    4    3    4          <-- O(1), {3,4} parity wobble (NOT shrinking with rho)

CONSTRAINT LEMMA (the corrected law):
  c*(n,k) = c*(n) is CONSTANT IN THE RATE k.  Equivalently s*(n,k) = k + c*(n) (the binding
  crossing TRACKS k, it is NOT pinned at n/2-1).  Hence delta* = (1-rho) - c*(n)/n = a fixed
  Theta(1/n) below CAPACITY (KKH26 ceiling shape), at every rate.  The prior Theta(n) is ONLY
  the value of the constant c*(n) at the k=n/4 SLICE (c*(n)=n/4-1 there), NOT a function of rho.

WHY THE PRIOR ERRED: at k=n/4, s*=n/2-1 so c* = (n/2-1)-n/4 = n/4-1, which is Theta(n) only
  because k=n/4 is itself Theta(n).  The prior read off "c*=n/4-1 at the data point" and wrote
  it as the rho=1/4 instance of "n(1/2-rho)-1", inferring a falling-with-rho law.  But the true
  s* GROWS with k (s*=k+const), so c* is rate-FLAT.  The prior's predicted c*=0 at k->n/2-3
  (BGK re-coupling) is FALSE: at n=20,k=9 the prior predicts c*=(N-1)-k=0 but the data gives c*=4.

VERDICT (rule-3, rule-4, rule-6): a WIN that MAPS the آ§6 dichotomy more precisely. The far-line
  crossing depth NEVER collapses to 0 by varying the rate => NO second horn opens within the
  accessible window-interior rate range; the far-line/numeric-enumeration face stays a bounded-depth
  (c*>=3) over-determined cyclotomic object OFF the BGK wall at EVERY rate rho in [1/4,1/2)
  (strengthens c.348 from the rate direction: off-floor at all rho, not just rho=1/4).  The آ§6
  "under-determined / re-coupling to BGK" direction is NOT reachable by the rate parameter.  CORE
  (BGK M(n)<=C sqrt(n log m)) stays OPEN.  Lean: DecouplingCrossingDepthRateConstant.lean
  (axiom-clean, [propext,Quot.sound]): crossingDepth_rate_constant, crossingDepth_rate_flat,
  crossingDepth_no_collapse, crossingDepth_pos_all_rates, capacity_defect_eq,
  prior_quarter_axis_slice, prior_rate_law_refuted_at_n20_k9.
  OPEN refinement (sub-goal b): exact closed-form constants of the per-direction decay I(c) and the
  exact c*(n) law (n=16->3/4 wobble, n=20->4; n>=24 deferred, engine too slow under load).

  generically but the FIXED prize domain mu_n does not relax => pinning delta* still needs the open

- **C25 (GM-MDS resultant Galois, g(mu_n)=0 char-0 via n-core) = refuted-false.**

  = Johnson regime). So the Galois resultant is DEGENERATE on the relevant shapes -> it cannot cap

## A3-newton-polygon-2adic (#444) = REFUTED (vacuous relocation to the 2-adic place)

**Angle.** Manifesto relocation (B)/angle 3 (truly novel, never-attacked): the pencil
`f_gamma(X)=X^a+gamma X^b-h(X)` (deg h<k) vanishing on a w-subset of `mu_n` (n=2^mu). Claim: the
**2-adic Newton polygon** of `f_gamma` counts `mu_n` roots-of-unity by 2-adic valuation, giving a
count SHARPER than Mann/antipodal PAST the Johnson boundary, exploiting that `mu_n` is 2-power so its
hardest structure is 2-adic not archimedean.

**Verdict = REFUTED.** Probe `scripts/probes/probe_a3_newton_polygon_2adic.py` runs the angle to
ground in its three inequivalent formulations; each falls on a different horn, all from the same
structural fact:

1. **NP of `f` in `X` (roots = the agreement points): VACUOUS.** Over `Q(zeta_n)`, `n=2^mu`, the
   prime 2 is totally ramified `(2)=(lambda)^{phi(n)}`, `lambda=1-zeta_n`, `v(lambda)=1`, `v(2)=n/2`.
   **Every `mu_n` root is a 2-adic UNIT** (`v_lambda(zeta)=0`). So the NP segment carrying the unit
   roots is FLAT (slope 0), length = total root count = the Mann count itself. The NP carries NO
   information beyond Mann â€” it does not even reach Johnson, it is vacuous one step earlier.
2. **`v_lambda` of the lacunary generalized Vandermonde minor: NON-DISCRIMINATING.** `v_lambda(det)`
   is a sum of POSITIVE pairwise-difference valuations for ANY distinct rows (extremal-antipodal=50,
   random=25 at n=16; 178 vs 125 at n=32), growing `~w^2*O(1)` UNIFORMLY. It cannot single out the
   Johnson-extremal config, so it cannot forbid extra roots past Johnson.
3. **2-adic valuation of the Gaussian period `eta_b` vs the archimedean wall `M`: DECOUPLED.** The
   wall `M=max_b|eta_b|` is an ARCHIMEDEAN sup. 2 is UNRAMIFIED in `Q(zeta_p)` (p odd); the 2-adic
   size of a period is a Frobenius-at-2 datum, decoupled from `|.|_inf`. The period polynomial has
   degree `m=(p-1)/n`, so the 2-adic place is 1 of `~m` places â€” a product-formula/height bound
   through it contributes `O(1)`, NOT `sqrt(n log)`. Prize-faithful checks (p prime, p=1 mod n,
   p>>n^3, `mu_n` PROPER): M/sqrt(n log(p/n)) = 1.07 (n=8), 1.16 (n=16), 1.18 (n=32) â€” bounded,
   archimedean, untouched by 2-adic data.

**Why it falls (the manifesto's own conservation law, sharpened).** The relocation (B) hoped the
2-power structure makes the hardest part 2-adic. But the wall is an ARCHIMEDEAN magnitude of a sum
whose summands are 2-adic UNITS. The 2-adic completion sees no size separation among units; it is
*blind* to the archimedean sup-norm. Mann (the archimedean shadow) at least pins the Johnson
boundary; the 2-adic NP carries strictly LESS â€” it is vacuous. NOT a reduction to the BGK wall and
NOT a reduction to Johnson: it is a vacuous relocation that never reaches either. Probe:
`scripts/probes/probe_a3_newton_polygon_2adic.py` (committed).

## A4-amice-iwasawa (#444) = REFUTED (the measure is REAL but the period is a p-adic UNIT, b-independent)

**Angle.** Manifesto relocation (B)/angle 4 (truly novel, `Amice/Iwasawa: 0` in-tree): the period
`f(b)=Sum_{x in mu_n} e_p(bx)` (`e_p(z)=exp(2 pi i z / p)`), viewed along the dilation tower
`b -> zeta b` (in-tree recursion `f_{i+1}(b)=f_i(b)+f_i(zeta b)`), interpolates to a **p-adic measure**
`mu` on `Z_2` whose Amice transform `A_mu(T)` has bounded Iwasawa `mu/lambda`-invariants, giving
`|f(b)| <= sqrt(2 n log(p/n))` via the p-adic-analytic norm. The relocation hoped the p-adic universe
(where `n < sqrt(q)` is no obstruction) makes the sup computable.

**The NEW LEMMA invented & PROVEN exact (the load-bearing math, even though it kills the angle).**
Work in `Q_p(zeta_p)`, totally ramified of degree `p-1` over `Q_p`, uniformizer `lambda = zeta_p - 1`,
`v(lambda)=1/(p-1)` (normalize `v(p)=1`). Write `zeta_p = 1 + lambda`. Then the period has the EXACT
`lambda`-adic (genuine p-adic, at the prime `p`) expansion
> `f(b) = Sum_{x in mu_n} (1+lambda)^{bx} = Sum_{j>=0} ( Sum_{x in mu_n} C(bx, j) ) lambda^j`
> `     = n + 0*lambda + ... + 0*lambda^{n-1} + C_n(b)*lambda^n + ...`
because `Sum_{x in mu_n} C(bx,j)` is a Z-combination of the **power sums** `S_t = Sum_{x in mu_n} x^t`,
and for a **proper 2-power subgroup** (`n | p-1`, `n<p-1`) subgroup orthogonality gives `S_t = n` if
`n | t` and `S_t = 0` otherwise. Hence **the first non-constant `lambda`-coefficient appears at `lambda^n`**,
and the constant term is exactly `n` â€” a `p`-adic **UNIT** (coprime to `p`). Therefore:
> `v_lambda(f(b)) = 0` and `f(b)` is a p-adic UNIT, for EVERY `b != 0`.

This is exact and new (the power-sum gap forcing the unit + the `lambda^n` onset). Probe-verified at
`(n,beta) in {(8,4),(8,5),(16,4),(16,5),(32,4)}`, prize-faithful (`p` PRIME, `p=1 mod n`, `p>=n^4`,
`mu_n` PROPER, `(p-1)/n > 1`, never `n=p-1`): nonzero `lambda`-coeffs (mod p) at exactly `j in {0, n, n+1, ...}`,
`c_0 mod p = n`, for the argmax-`b`, argmin-`b`, and arbitrary `b` ALIKE.

**Verdict = REFUTED (reduces-to-wall / magnitude).** The Amice/Iwasawa machine reads precisely this
p-adic valuation data. The measure on the dilation tower genuinely EXISTS (the period is an algebraic
integer, so its finite-difference/Mahler coefficients are p-adically bounded â€” the Amice criterion holds)
and its `mu`-invariant `= min valuation = 0` (unit), `lambda`-invariant finite. But the entire
invariant package is **`b`-INDEPENDENT**: every period is a unit with constant term `n`, regardless of
`b`. The archimedean ratio `M_arch/sqrt(n log(p/n))` ranges `0.96 .. 1.26` across these configs and the
worst-`b` vs typical-`b` separation is purely **archimedean phase** information â€” provably invisible to
the p-adic absolute value, which equals `1` (unit) uniformly. The p-adic-analytic norm cannot produce
`sqrt(2 n log)` because it cannot even distinguish the worst `b` from the best.

**Why it falls (the conservation law, third confirmation).** Relocation (B) hoped `mu_n` being 2-power
makes the hardest structure non-archimedean. At the prime `p` (this angle, distinct from A3's 2-adic
place): the summands `zeta_p^{bx}` are `1 + (b x) lambda + ...`, all units, and the leading p-adic term
of their sum is the COUNT `n` â€” the trivial/Johnson-side datum. The `sqrt(log)` excess separating the
floor from Johnson is a rare-event archimedean tail (Manifesto Sec. I), and tail phenomena are invisible
to a valuation that only sees the leading unit. The relocation is REAL (a genuine Iwasawa measure, not
vacuous like A3's flat Newton polygon) but **STERILE**: it transports the object to a place where it is a
b-independent unit. Distinct horn from A3 (vacuous at the 2-adic place) â€” here the measure is well-defined
yet carries no `b`-dependent magnitude information. Reduces ONLY to the magnitude/sup question = the BGK
wall; NOT to Johnson. Probes (committed): `scripts/probes/probe_a4_amice_iwasawa.py`,
`scripts/probes/probe_a4_padic_valuation.py`, `scripts/probes/probe_a4_iwasawa_invariants.py`.

## A2-chaining-entropy (route 54, âک…, #444): generic chaining with RS-structured entropy â€” REDUCES-TO-WALL

**Date:** 2026-06-15. **Angle:** relocate the cancellation locus from the n-point domain to the
b-index process viewed as an entropy/chaining FUNCTIONAL. `M(mu_n)=max_{bâ‰ 0}|X_b|`,
`X_b=خ£_{xâˆˆخ¼_n}e_p(bx)`, is the supremum of a sub-Gaussian process indexed by `bâˆˆF_p^*`. Generic
chaining (Talagrand/Dudley) bounds `sup â‰¤ âˆڑnآ·خ³â‚‚`, `خ³â‚‚=âˆ«âˆڑ(log N(T,d,خµ))dخµ`, where `N` is the
covering number of the index under the **canonical increment metric**
`d(b,b')آ² = (2/n)خ£_{xâˆˆخ¼_n}(1âˆ’cos(2د€(bâˆ’b')x/p))` (the metric making `X_b` sub-Gaussian). The
route-54 NEW LEMMA: the 2-adic dilation self-similarity (`X_b(G_{i+1})=X_b(G_i)+X_{خ¶b}(G_i)`,
in-tree `SubgroupGaussSumDilationRecursion`) collapses the index entropy to `o(log q)`, absorbing
the `âˆڑlog` excess and yielding the floor.

**Verdict = REDUCES-TO-WALL (W4).** Probes (p PRIME, n=2^خ¼, n|pâˆ’1, pâ‰ˆn^{3.2}, خ¼_n PROPER):
`probe_a2_chaining_entropy_v2.py` and `probe_a2_dilation_collapse.py` (both committed).

1. **The index metric entropy is `خک(log q)`, NOT `o(log q)`.** `probe_a2_chaining_entropy_v2`:
   at the finest scale `maxlogN/logq = 1.00` EXACTLY for every `(n,p)` tested
   (خ¼=2..7, q from 89 to 5.5M). The metric `d` separates almost all of `F_p`, so `N(خµ)â‰ˆq` at fine
   scale â€” the entropy is exactly `log q`. Plain chaining therefore gives `âˆڑ(log q)` = the W4 wall.
   The chaining is also TIGHT up to a constant (`خ³â‚‚/âˆڑ(log(q/n))â‰ˆ1.8` const, `M/(âˆڑnآ·خ³â‚‚)â‰ˆ0.6` const),
   so the entropy integral genuinely reproduces the floor SCALE â€” but with `log N=log q`, that scale
   is the wall, not the floor.
2. **The crux: the dilation orbit does NOT collapse the metric.** `probe_a2_dilation_collapse`:
   `d(b, خ¶b) â‰ˆ avg_d` (1.40 vs 1.38 at n=8; 1.41 vs 1.41 at n=128) â€” dilation neighbors are
   metrically GENERIC, never close. So the multiplicative orbit `b~خ¶b~â€¦~خ¶^{n-1}b` does not quotient
   the index onto few classes; there is no entropy collapse and no relocation.

**Why it falls (the conservation law, sharpened â€” the SAME shape as the refuted 2-adic NP angle).**
The chaining metric `d` is **archimedean and ADDITIVE** (translation-invariant on `F_p`: `d(b,b')`
depends only on `bâˆ’b'`). The self-similarity the Lآ²-doubling exploits is **MULTIPLICATIVE** (the
dilation `bâ†¦خ¶b`). For `c=(خ¶âˆ’1)b`, `{cx mod p : xâˆˆخ¼_n}=(خ¶âˆ’1)bآ·خ¼_n` is just another خ¼_n-coset, so as
`b` ranges `d(b,خ¶b)` has the SAME distribution as `d(0,c)` for generic `c`. The multiplicative orbit
is "diagonal" to the additive metric â€” they do not align. This is exactly the manifesto's
"Cayley-gap = the wall" reappearing: the increment metric is the additive Paley-graph metric whose
spectral radius IS `M(خ¼_n)`, so chaining over it cannot beat `M` â€” it is W4 wearing entropy clothes.
The Lآ²-doubling (which IS multiplicative-orbit-aware) only controls `خ³â‚‚` in the `âˆڑ2`-per-level
average direction; the L^âˆ‍ sup over the high-entropy additive index is untouched. NOT a reduction to
Johnson; a faithful reduction to the BGK/Paley wall W4. Probes committed:
`scripts/probes/probe_a2_chaining_entropy_v2.py`, `scripts/probes/probe_a2_dilation_collapse.py`
(and the v1 `probe_a2_chaining_entropy.py`, kept as the documented FALSE-floor pitfall: it measured
the covering number of the 2D VALUE cloud, trivially `O(log)`, which is NOT the index entropy).

## A8 [Bourgainâ€“Gamburd multiplicative dilation spectral gap] â€” REDUCES-TO-WALL (2026-06-15)

**Angle (#444 manifesto angle 8, never-attacked relocation):** relocate the cancellation off the
n-point domain into the *multiplicative* spectral gap of the dilation action `b â†¦ خ¶b` (خ¶ a 2^خ¼-th
root). New lemma attempted: a Bourgainâ€“Gamburd / superstrong-approximation uniform spectral gap for
the 2-power dilation Cayley/Schreier structure that bounds the period `M(خ¼_n)=max_{bâ‰ 0}|خ£_{xâˆˆخ¼_n}e_p(bx)| â‰¤ Câˆڑ(n log)`.

**Verdict: REDUCES-TO-WALL (falls on the amenability horn).** Two independent constraint lemmas:

1. **The dilation group is abelian/amenable â€” the EXACT excluded case in every BG theorem.**
   The dilation `xâ†¦خ¶x` is generated by the single cyclic group `âں¨خ¶âں©â‰…خ¼_n`. Its Schreier action on
   `F_p^*` decomposes into `(p-1)/n` disjoint orbits, each a single `n`-cycle. The pure dilation
   Schreier graph is therefore a disjoint union of `n`-cycles: 2nd eigenvalue = `2cos(2د€/n)â†’2`,
   "gap" = `2âˆ’2cos(2د€/n)~(2د€/n)آ²â†’0`. No expansion. BG/superstrong approximation requires a thick
   non-abelian (Zariski-dense in semisimple, property-(د„)) group; a single cyclic generator is the
   excluded affine/abelian obstruction. PROBE `probe_a8_bourgain_gamburd_dilation.py` (n=4..128,
   p>>nآ³, PRIME, proper subgroup): `dilSchr2 = 2cos(2د€/n)` exactly, decoupled from the wall
   `M â‰ˆ addCay2eig` (M/âˆڑn: 1.87â†’4.17, the same âˆڑlog wall as W4).

2. **The only non-abelian lift (affine group خ¼_nâ‹‰F_p) is SOLVABLE â†’ still amenable, AND its
   spectrum CONTAINS the wall as a sub-block (no relocation).** PROBE
   `probe_a8_affine_group_transport.py` + `probe_a8_affine_p_collapse.py`: the affine Cayley gap
   COLLAPSES with n (gap/4: 0.144â†’0.146â†’0.038â†’0.0096 for n=4,8,16,32) â€” not bounded below, so NOT
   an expander family. Moreover the affine operator's `دˆ_b`-isotypic block has top eigenvalue tied
   to `خ·_b=خ£_{xâˆˆخ¼_n}دˆ_b(x)`, so `M` is literally a sub-block eigenvalue of the affine Cayley
   operator: bounding the affine gap â‰، bounding `M` = the SAME wall, not a relocation off the domain.

**Constraint lemma (why the locus does not move):** the dilation action is abelian (cyclic خ¼_n)
and its semidirect lift is solvable; both are amenable, so no superstrong-approximation gap exists,
and the only operator whose spectrum touches `M` is the additive Cayley graph (=W4) or the affine
block that re-contains it. The "multiplicative" relocation collapses back onto the additive wall.
This is DISTINCT from W4=#49 (additive Cayley) only in framing; mathematically it reduces to it.

Probes: `scripts/probes/probe_a8_bourgain_gamburd_dilation.py`,
`scripts/probes/probe_a8_affine_group_transport.py`, `scripts/probes/probe_a8_affine_p_collapse.py`
(commit on branch main).

## A10 entropy-compression (#444, open-avenue C7) â€” REFUTED + reduces-to-Johnson (2026-06-15)

**Angle.** Relocate the cancellation locus to INFORMATION: new lemma "worst-case far-line
list L â‰¤ Lambda_r := 2^{H(P_r)}, the Shannon-entropy envelope of the (r+1)-subset-sum
distribution P_r over mu_n, which 2-power rigidity caps below budget past Johnson."

**Two independent kill-shots (exact probes, proper mu_n, p PRIME, p>>n^3):**

1. **The inequality is BACKWARDS (refuted).** In-tree (probe_jump_subsetsum) the worst-case
   list at the jump radius delta=1-(k+1)/n EQUALS N_dist = #distinct (k+1)-subset sums. Since
   Shannon entropy H â‰¤ log2(support), Lambda_r = 2^H â‰¤ N_dist = L always. The entropy envelope
   is a LOWER bound on the list, not an upper bound. `probe_a10_list_vs_entropy.py`: Lambda_r
   < N_dist in every row (e.g. n=32,t=8: Lambda=580227 < N_dist=1018113).

2. **The residual gain = the antipodal/Mann count = reduces-to-Johnson.** Even setting (1)
   aside and asking whether N_dist itself beats volume: the deficit log2(C/N_dist) (a)
   VANISHES relative to log2 C at the floor-end (small t) as n grows (def/log2C: 0.126â†’0.052
   â†’0.020â†’0.0085 for t=4, n=8â†’16â†’32â†’64 â€” `probe_a10_ndist_scaling.py`), and (b) at t=n/2 is a
   stable constant fraction ~0.144 that DISAPPEARS for antipodal-free random Sidon sets
   (mu_n def/l2C=0.144 vs Sidon=0.0019 at n=16 â€” `probe_a10_mann_identification.py`). The
   concentration is entirely the {z,-z} pairing (mu_{2^mu} âˆ‹ -1) = the vanishing-sum / Mann
   structure = the PROVEN-DEAD Johnson-boundary object.

**Verdict:** A10 falls on BOTH horns â€” the literal lemma is refuted (entropy bounds the list
from below), and the only real concentration it detects is the Mann antipodal count
(reduces-to-Johnson). The p-scaling DID show the concentration is algebraic (p-stable, not a
mod-p wraparound artifact) â€” that part is a genuine clean sub-finding â€” but algebraic = Mann.

Probes: `scripts/probes/probe_a10_entropy_compression.py`, `probe_a10_entropy_pscaling.py`,
`probe_a10_list_vs_entropy.py`, `probe_a10_ndist_scaling.py`, `probe_a10_mann_identification.py`

## A9 [Kelley-Meka 2023 / PFR] on the bad-خ³ + vanishing-subset locus â€” REDUCES-TO-WALL (energy/moment), two named obstructions (2026-06-15)

ANGLE (manifesto آ§IV.9, #444): relocate the cancellation locus to *post-2023 structure theory* â€”
the now-PROVEN Polynomial-Freiman-Ruzsa theorem (GGMT 2023) + Kelley-Meka exp-improved Roth (2023).
NEW LEMMA attempted: the bad-خ³ locus (a "3-term-progression-like / vanishing-subset" additive
object) is forced by KM/PFR into â‰¤ n cosets of a bounded-index subgroup PAST Johnson, giving
M(خ¼_n) â‰¤ Câˆڑ(n log). Distinct from the prior PFR-contrapositive-on-crossCell entry (that fed PFR
the *energy* of خ¼_n; this feeds it the *bad-خ³ set* and the *vanishing-subset locus* directly, which
is what the open-avenue inventory B2 actually asks).

PROBES (proper خ¼_n: p prime, n=2^خ¼, n|p-1, pâ‰«n^3, never n=p-1; commit eead0a0b3 on main):
`scripts/probes/probe_a9_vanishing_subset_km.py`, `probe_a9_badgamma_pfr_fast.py`,
`probe_a9_kelleymeka_pfr_badgamma.py`.

TWO INDEPENDENT KILLS:

- **(A) Kelley-Meka is the WRONG theorem (3AP-free + density-vacuity).** KM is one-directional: a
  3AP-FREE set of density خ± has خ± â‰¤ exp(âˆ’c(log p)^{1/12}) â‰ˆ 1/(log p)^{1.04}. It constrains
  3AP-free sets only *from being large*. Exact facts: خ¼_n IS additively 3AP-free (#3AP=0 for
  n=8,16,32,64) but its density خ±=n/p is catastrophically BELOW the KM ceiling â€” at the prize scale
  (n=2^30, pâ‰ˆ2^158) the KM ceiling is 2^-7.05 while خ±=2^-128, **121 bits below**. So KM's implication
  is vacuously satisfied; its contrapositive (density>ceiling â†’ âˆƒ3AP, the only form that lower-bounds
  a relation count) never fires. This is the post-2023 upgrade of the already-logged Bloom-Sisask /
  Chang density-vacuity kill: KM improves the *exponent of the ceiling*, but at خ±=2^-128 the route
  never reached the ceiling to begin with. (And for the bad-خ³ SET specifically: it is 3AP-RICH for
  nâ‰¥16, #3AP=32 at n=16, 81344 at n=32 â€” KM does not even apply there.)

- **(B) the locus is already at the Wick FLOOR (no sub-Wick room for ANY structure theorem).** The
  only quantity a structure theorem could improve is the additive energy E_r(خ¼_n), through the moment
  bound M â‰¤ (pآ·E_r)^{1/2r}. Exact energy is at the Wick floor: E_r/((2r-1)â€¼آ·n^r) â†’ 1 from BELOW
  (E_2/Wick = 0.875, 0.938, 0.969, 0.984 for n=8,16,32,64; monotone-to-1 at r=3,4 too). The char-0
  Wick value IS the true energy; PFR/KM can only *upper*-bound E_r, and no upper bound below the
  truth exists. So the moment route delivers the same M â‰چ âˆڑ(n log q) Paley/BGK wall, never past it.

- **(C) PFR is structurally vacuous + wrong coset type on the bad-خ³ set.** Measured doubling of the
  band-1 bad-خ³ set: K=|B+B|/|B| = 2.25, 7.04, 24.01, 173.75 for n=4,8,16,32 (â‰ˆ n/2-and-growing,
  maximal). PFR's cover needs K^C â‰¥ |B| cosets at every row = VACUOUS (no compression). Moreover the
  bad-خ³ set is ALREADY a union of *multiplicative* خ¼_d-cosets (orbits = 1,3,7,141 for n=4,8,16,32;
  proven group-like by the dilation/scaling identity, deltastar-407-elekes-szabo-grouplike) â€” the
  WRONG coset type for PFR (which produces additive subgroup cosets). PFR's conclusion is both
  redundant (the coset structure is a theorem) and of the wrong kind.

NET: A9 falls on the **energy/moment horn** (manifesto "PROVEN DEAD: all moment/energy"). It does
NOT reduce to Johnson per se â€” the bad-خ³ *count* question is the open Dvornicich-Zannier / N0-poly
statement â€” but the SUP-NORM route via energy lands squarely on the energy wall, and the additive
structure theory is in the wrong universe (additive vs multiplicative; density 2^121 below the KM
window; energy already minimal). The KM/Chang spectral-boosting lineage bounds the *count/dimension*
of large frequencies but never the single max (the same Lâˆ‍/Lآ² âˆڑn gap already named at faces 3â†”4).

IN-TREE: `ArkLib/Data/CodingTheory/ProximityGap/Frontier/_A9KelleyMekaPFRNoGo.lean` (axiom-clean:
propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound) â€” `km_vacuous_below_ceiling`, `km_trigger_dead_prizescale`,
`energy_floor`, `moment_route_ge_card`. `result_type = reduces-to-wall(energy/moment)+km-density-vacuous+pfr-wrong-coset-type`.

## A5-TERWILLIGER (route 85): operator norm of the Krawtchouk-weighted subcode sum (#444) â€” REDUCES-TO-WALL

**Date:** 2026-06-15. **Driver:** Opus 4.8. **Angle:** relocate the خ´* cancellation locus to the
*Terwilliger (subconstituent) algebra* `T(0)` of the Hamming scheme `H(N,p)`. The reduced far-line
sum `ً‌’®(uâ‚€) = خ£_{خ¾âˆˆD} K_w(wt خ¾) e_p(خ¾آ·uâ‚€)`, `D = Câٹ¥âˆ©uâ‚پâٹ¥`, is a `T`-module object. **New lemma sought:**
`D` sits in a *low-dimensional* `T`-module, so the operator norm of the `K_w`-weighted action bounds
`|ً‌’®| â‰¤ Câˆڑ(n log)` â€” a bound that lives in the operator (not on the domain `خ¼_n`).

**TWO structural facts kill it (both probed, proper `خ¼_n`, `n=2^خ¼`, `n|pâˆ’1`, `p` PRIME, `p>n^3`, never `n=pâˆ’1`):**

1. **The weight statistic is degenerate (MDS concentration), `probe_a5_terwilliger_collapse.py`.** For
   smooth-domain RS the dual `Câٹ¥` is MDS: a degree-`<dâٹ¥` polynomial almost never vanishes on a domain
   point, so the weight distribution of `D` is concentrated at `wt = N` (measured frac `0.994 â†’ 0.999 â†’ 1.0`
   for `n=4,8,16,32`; weight-distribution entropy `â†’ 0`). So `K_w(wt خ¾)` is the **constant** `K_w(N)` on
   `(1âˆ’o(1))` of `D`. The grading is "low-dim" only in the trivial MDS sense already exploited by MacWilliams;
   the Terwilliger module gives no NEW low-dimensionality.

2. **The `K_w`-weighting is diagonal in the character basis with KRAWTCHOUK eigenvalues (Delsarte/MacWilliams),
   `probe_a5_terwilliger_exact` / `/tmp/eig.py`.** The Boseâ€“Mesner element `W = خ£_i K_w(i) Aلµ¢` has
   transform-domain eigenvalues `خ»_j = خ£_i K_w(i) Kلµ¢(j) = p^Nآ·[j=w]` (verified exactly at `n=4,p=73`:
   `خ»_j = 0` for `jâ‰ 2`, `خ»_2 = 28398241 = 73^4آ·â€¦`, the Krawtchouk **orthogonality** relation). Restricted to
   the subconstituent module supported on `خ¼_n`, the per-frequency value of `W` is the **incomplete Gauss
   period** `خ·_b = خ£_{yâˆˆخ¼_n} e_p(by)`. Hence the Terwilliger operator norm is `max_{bâ‰ 0}|خ·_b| = M(خ¼_n)` â€” the
   BGK/Paley wall. The wall scaling itself reconfirmed: `M/âˆڑ(n log(p/n)) âˆˆ [0.90,1.18]`, `M/âˆڑn` grows `1.98â†’3.53`.

**NET:** the "low-dimensional Terwilliger module" controls only the *number of irreducible summands*, never the
*per-summand operator norm*, which after the (verified-exact) Delsarte diagonalization is the incomplete Gauss
period over `خ¼_n`. Route 85 **REDUCES TO THE WALL** â€” it does NOT relocate the cancellation locus. This is exactly
the catalogue's route-88 verdict ("association-scheme eigenvalue = Krawtchouk = the same") made explicit for the
*subcode* sum. Does NOT touch Johnson; falls on the **Cayley-gap/Paley wall horn** (`= W4`).

**IN-TREE (axiom-clean: propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound):**
`ArkLib/Data/CodingTheory/ProximityGap/Frontier/_a5_terwilliger_collapse.lean` â€” `terwilliger_reduces_to_wall`
(`TerwilligerDiagonalizes âں¹ (operator bound âں؛ wall bound)`), `terwilliger_no_independent_gain`.
Probes: `scripts/probes/probe_a5_terwilliger{,_collapse,_norm,_decomp,_exact}.py`.
`result_type = reduces-to-wall(Delsarte-diagonalization = Krawtchouk eigenvalue = incomplete-Gauss-period = W4)`.

## wf-OFG (#444): `OverDetFloorGood` at `خ´bind=(nâˆ’s*)/n` is REFUTED â€” over-det DISTINCT-خ³ count exceeds budget at the binding radius (2026-06-15)

**Lane mission:** discharge the D3 bracket's lower-side residual `OverDetFloorGood C خµ* خ´bind`
(`خ´bind â‰¤ 1 âˆ§ epsMCA C خ´bind â‰¤ خµ*`) from the over-det incidence bricks, on the premise it is
"reachable without the BGK wall".

**Refutation (exact char-0, `probe_wf4OFG_true_mcaevent_floor.py`, pâ‰،1 mod n, p>nâپ´, distinct-خ³
count is p-independent):** at `خ´bind=(nâˆ’s*)/n`, `s*=n/2âˆ’1` (دپ=1/4), budget `=n`, the TRUE
mcaEvent **distinct-خ³** count per stack EXCEEDS budget:
- n=8: 2-term max #{خ³}=56 > 8.
- n=12 (k=3, non-degenerate): MONOMIAL max #{خ³}=17 > 12; 2-term max=61 > 12.

Even the pure over-det MONOMIAL distinct-خ³ count is over budget at `خ´bind`. So `خ´bind` is a BAD
radius (`خµ* < epsMCA C خ´bind`), hence `خ´* < خ´bind` and the floor `خ´* â‰¥ (nâˆ’s*)/n` is FALSE there.

**Root cause / reconciliation:** the campaign's crossing depth `s*` is where the `(subset, خ³)`
INCIDENCE `I(n)` crosses budget, but `epsMCA` is governed by the DISTINCT-خ³ count (each خ³ once,
however many witness subsets). These functionals differ; the distinct-خ³ count is above budget at
`خ´bind`. The honest D3 lower side must use a strictly smaller radius where distinct-خ³ â‰¤ budget â€”
pinning which IS the open under-det/BGK wall. No free Johnson-side lower bound exists at `خ´bind`.

**IN-TREE (axiom-clean: propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound; fast-path audit green):**
`ArkLib/Data/CodingTheory/ProximityGap/Frontier/_wf4OFG_overdet_floor_bridge.lean`:
- `epsMCA_le_of_perStackBudget` / `OverDetFloorGood_of_perStackBudget` â€” the EXACT reduction of
  the D3 lower side to ONE counting Prop `PerStackBadScalarBudget C خ´ B` (uniform per-stack
  distinct-خ³ count â‰¤ B with B/q â‰¤ خµ*). UNCONDITIONAL bridge.
- `epsMCA_gt_of_stack_over_budget` â€” a single over-budget stack âں¹ `خµ* < epsMCA` (the machine
  shape of the probe refutation).
- `OverDetIncidenceSuppliesBudgetAtBinding` â€” documented refuted `Prop` (probe-refuted at nâˆˆ{8,12}).
`result_type = refuted(OverDetFloorGood@خ´bind) + reduced(D3-lower-side âں¶ PerStackBadScalarBudget,
which is the under-det/BGK wall, NOT the over-det count)`.

## RECONCILIATION (2026-06-15) â€” the far-line crossing depth `c*(n)` is rate-FLAT but GROWS in `n`; "bounded `c*`" was imprecise â€” off-BGK verdict UNCHANGED (strengthened)

**What:** three campaign statements about the max-over-directions over-det crossing offset
`c*(n,k) = s*(n,k) âˆ’ k` (binding `s* = min{s : maxI(s) â‰¤ budget = n}`) looked to conflict:
(1) `DecouplingCrossingDepthRateConstant.lean`: `c*` rate-FLAT, "bounded âں¹ off-BGK at every rate";
(2) DISPROOF "C01": `k=2` single-antipodal-direction offset is `n/4` LINEAR (4,8,16 @ n=16,32,64);
(3) GPU `e91c34348` (validated vs rust-pg n=8,12,16,20): `دپ=1/4` axis MAX offset `m*` = 3,3,5 @
n=8,16,32 ("grows ~logâ‚‚n").

**Reconciliation (object-precise, re-measured here with `secondhorn.rs`, PROPER خ¼_n, pâ‰«nآ³, pâ‰،1 mod n,
never n=qâˆ’1):**
- RATE-FLAT (in k, fixed n=16), FULL sweep k=2..6: c*(16,k) = 3,4,3,4,3 â€” stays in {3,4}, never
  collapses to 0, never grows with k. CONFIRMS (1) on the whole rate range (prior only swept kâˆˆ[4,7]).
- n-GROWTH (دپ=1/4 axis, k=n/4): c*(n,n/4) = 3,4,3,4,5 @ n=8,12,16,20,32. The n=32 value 5 STRICTLY
  exceeds every nâ‰¤20 value {3,4} âں¹ c* GROWS in n. (nâ‰¤20 mine via secondhorn matching the GPU
  cross-val set; n=32 from the GPU run.)
- The `n/4`-linear C01 datum is a DIFFERENT object (single antipodal direction, not the max), not in
  conflict with the max-over-dir {3,4,5} growth.

**Verdict:** the prior file's "bounded `c*`" conflated bounded-in-k (TRUE) with bounded-in-n (FALSE).
The off-BGK / no-second-horn conclusion does NOT need n-boundedness â€” it needs only c*â‰¥1 (positivity,
preserved a fortiori by growth) and c*/nâ†’0 (so خ´* = (1âˆ’دپ) âˆ’ c*/n APPROACHES capacity from below). The
n-growth thus STRENGTHENS off-BGK (far-line crossing rides ever-closer to capacity, ever-further from
the BGK/Johnson floor). NOT a CORE closure (BGK M(n)â‰¤Câˆڑ(n log m) stays OPEN); a precision-correction +
extension of the آ§6 decoupling sub-question.

Lean: `Frontier/DecouplingCrossingDepthGrowsInN.lean` (axiom-clean [propext,Quot.sound]):
cStar_n16_rate_bounded, cStar_n16_rate_pos, cStarQuarter_values, cStar_grows_in_n,
cStar_n32_exceeds_prior_range, deltaStarNumerQuarter_values, capacity_defect_eq_cStar,
deltaStar_lt_capacity, offBGK_survives_growth.
`result_type = reconciled(rate-flat âˆ§ n-growing) + corrected("bounded c*" âں¶ "c*/nâ†’0, off-BGK strengthened")`.

## pin-coset (#444): the supplyâ†’prize "coset-structured pinning subsets" residual is REFUTED â€” pinning subsets are GENERIC, the only collision is the antipodal خ³=0 (2026-06-15)

**Lane mission:** `SinglePencilQIndependence.mca_badscalar_packing` (axiom-clean) gives the packing
bound `#bad آ· C(a,k+1) â‰¤ C(|خ¼|,k+1)`, landing in the W5 SUPPLY STRIP. Its `mca_badscalar_sharp`
docstring flags the honest residual to the exact خ´* prize pin as: *"the pinning (k+1)-subsets are
COSET-STRUCTURED, not arbitrary"* â€” i.e. that the witness subsets are forced into the KKH26
fibre/coset shape, collapsing the supply-strip count `C(n,k+1)` to the prize budget
`2^rآ·C(2^{خ¼-1},r)`. This entry MAPS that residual.

**Refutation (exact char-0, `probe_pinning_subset_coset_rigidity.py` + `probe_antipodal_gamma0_check.py`,
PROPER خ¼_n, pâ‰،1 mod n, pâ‰«nآ³, multi-prime, thick control, never n=q-1):** on the r=2..3 worst-case
far line (Q0=x^{rm}, Q1=x^{(r-1)m}, k=(r-2)m+1, m=1 â†’ pure 2-power core), the pinning (k+1)-subsets
are OVERWHELMINGLY GENERIC, not coset-structured:
- n=8  r=2: coset-structured pinning 4/28,  GENERIC 24/28;  realized-خ³=25 â‰ˆ C(n,2)=28 (NOT prize 24).
- n=8  r=3: coset-structured 0/56,  GENERIC 56/56;  realized-خ³=40, C(n,3)=56 (NOT prize 32).
- n=16 r=2: coset-structured 8/120, GENERIC 112/120; realized-خ³=113 â‰ˆ C(n,2)=120 (NOT prize 112).
- n=16 r=3: coset-structured 0/560, GENERIC 560/560; realized-خ³=464, C(n,3)=560 (NOT prize 448).
THICK control (n=6,12): SAME genericity â‡’ the (non-)rigidity is NOT 2-power-specific â‡’ thickness-
monotone = wrong mechanism (rule 3).

**Collision structure (the real packing slack â€” `probe_collision_structure.py`):** the realized #bad =
C(n,a) minus a SPARSE, O(1) set of collisions. At r=2 there is EXACTLY ONE collided scalar, خ³=0,
of multiplicity n/2, realized by the n/2 antipodal pairs {x,âˆ’x} (the only coset-structured collision;
âˆ’x = xآ·g^{n/2}). At r=3 the collisions are small mult-(r-1) clusters that are NOT coset-structured
(0/8 at n=8, 0/16 at n=16). So realized #bad = خک(C(n,a)) = the SUPPLY STRIP, never the prize budget.

**Verdict:** the coset-rigidity of pinning subsets does NOT collapse the supply-strip packing bound to
the prize window (the single antipodal collision saves only n/2âˆ’1 of خک(C(n,2))). The supplyâ†’prize
collapse must be the W4 sub-exponential cancellation, NOT a coset-structure restriction on which
subsets occur. CORE stays OPEN.

**IN-TREE (axiom-clean: propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound; 0 sorryAx):**
`ArkLib/Data/CodingTheory/ProximityGap/PairPinnedScalarAntipodal.lean`:
- `pairPinnedScalar_eq` â€” the r=2 line xآ²+خ³x agrees with a constant on {x,y} (xâ‰ y) iff خ³ = âˆ’(x+y).
- `pairPinned_zero_iff_antipodal` â€” that scalar = 0 iff y = âˆ’x (the خ³=0 fibre = antipodal pairs).
- `antipodal_mem_nthRoots` â€” even n â‡’ (âˆ’x)^n=1 from x^n=1 (antipodal partner stays in خ¼_n).
`result_type = refuted(coset-structured-pinning-subsets supplyâ†’prize residual) + proven(r=2 pinned
scalar = âˆ’(x+y), خ³=0 fibre = antipodal pairing, the unique coset-structured collision)`.

**EXTENSION (n=24 sharp datum, decoupling crossing-growth lane):** secondhorn n=24 k=6 (rho=1/4,
p=331777, PROPER mu_n, p>>n^3): s=10 maxI=25 (>budget=24, bad), s=11 maxI=24 (=budget exactly, GOOD)
=> s*=11, c*=5. The n=24 value 5 BREAKS the {3,4} parity wobble of the n<=20 tail (3,4,3,4) => the
c* growth is REAL, not a parity artifact. Full rho=1/4 axis: c*=3,4,3,4,5,5 @ n=8,12,16,20,24,32.
Sub-linear (c*/n->0), log-like but NO clean closed form (c*(16)=3<log2 16=4; c*(20)=4<ceil log2 20=5).

## 2026-06-15 â€” IDEA [I025] "Levenshtein weighted higher-moment escape from the Welch=âˆڑn trap" â€” NO-GAIN (refuted as a route to an UPPER bound on M(خ¼_n))

IDEA: M(خ¼_n) is the max cross-correlation of the Sidelnikov family S_b(t)=e_p(bخ¶^t). Welch
(unweighted k=1) gives only âˆڑn (the Parseval floor). Levenshtein 1999 / Boztaإں introduce a free
weight w and higher even moments; the proposed NEW LEMMA: âˆƒ probability weight w (a positive
combination of additive characters OFF خ¼_n) and C with
    M^{2k} â‰¤ (1/w_min)آ·[ k-th weighted moment âˆ’ Welch floor ],
the optimal w over-weighting the worst frequencies and making the bracket O((n log m)^k) â€” the
L^âˆ‍-from-moments step the conservation law forbids unweighted.

PROBES (prize-faithful: خ¼_n = 2-power subgroup, n|p-1, p PRIME, pâ‰«nآ³ (خ²â‰ˆ3.0), proper m=(p-1)/n>1,
NEVER n=p-1), nâˆˆ{8,16,32}, scripts/probes/probe_levenshtein_weighted_moment_I025.py and
probe_levenshtein_kernel_I025b.py:

(Q1 â€” DIRECTION, the fatal one.) Welch/Levenshtein/Delsarte-LP bounds are LOWER bounds on the
max-coherence of a sequence FAMILY given its size (equivalently upper bounds on packing/code size),
proved by LP duality on the Gram measure against a positive-Gegenbauer weight. The probe confirms the
Welch value is a genuine LOWER bound: family-Welch |خ·| = 2.68 / 3.88 / 5.57 â‰¤ true M = 6.86 / 10.94 /
17.25 at n=8/16/32. The prize needs an UPPER bound on M for the FIXED family خ¼_n. These are OPPOSITE
directions. The weighted higher moment (a degree-k positive Gegenbauer combination) sharpens the
LOWER/packing bound by a moment-depth factor (the literature result that is real) but provides no
upper bound on the coherence of a given family.

(Q2/Q3 â€” the over-weight, granting the only valid upper-bound form.) The ONLY valid weighted-moment
upper bound is the trivial Markov inequality: for ANY nonneg weight خ½_b â‰¥ 0 with argmax b*,
    خ½_{b*}آ·M^{2k} â‰¤ خ£_b خ½_b|خ·_b|^{2k} =: WM_k(خ½),  so  M^{2k} â‰¤ WM_k(خ½)/خ½_{b*}.
This beats the unweighted power-mean only if خ½ over-weights b*. The probe measures the best bound from
weights the lemma ACTUALLY permits â€” a positive-definite (Bochner) function of the coset index, i.e. a
positive additive-character combination off خ¼_n (tested as clipped shifted Fejأ©r kernels on â„¤/m,
scanning shifts but NOT allowed to lock onto argmax). RESULT: the best legitimate over-weight upper
bound is ALWAYS â‰¥ M, converging to M strictly FROM ABOVE as kâ†’âˆ‍:
  n=8 : k=1â†’9.17  k=2â†’7.50  k=4â†’6.97  k=8â†’6.87  (M=6.86)   [ratio /M: 1.34â†’1.09â†’1.02â†’1.001]
  n=16: k=1â†’538   k=2â†’44.4  k=4â†’17.0  k=8â†’12.2  (M=10.94)  [ratio /M: 49â†’4.06â†’1.55â†’1.11]
  n=32: k=1â†’168   k=2â†’33.6  k=4â†’20.1  k=8â†’17.8  (M=17.25)  [ratio /M: 9.8â†’1.95â†’1.16â†’1.03]
The over-weight reaches M only once it has effectively concentrated ALL mass on the worst coset (kâ†’âˆ‍),
which (a) requires KNOWING the worst frequency (the whole open problem) and (b) even in that limit
recovers M, never âˆڑn. The unweighted power-mean (E|خ·|^{2k})^{1/2k} crawls toward M from BELOW (matching
the in-tree probe_407_moment_order_ceiling.py); the weighted version crawls from ABOVE. Neither
crosses âˆڑnآ·polylog from the correct side without locking onto argmax.

WHY (the crisp reason, restating the conservation law): a weight خ½ you can write down without peeking
at argmax has خ½_{b*} â‰¤ 1/|supp خ½|, so WM_k(خ½)/خ½_{b*} â‰¥ |supp خ½|آ·mean_supp(|خ·|^{2k}) â‰¥ M^{2k} only
captures the sup when خ½ is already a point mass at b*. The "over-weight the worst frequency" step IS
the L^âˆ‍-from-moments step, and the free weight does not break the phase-blindness â€” it relocates it
into the requirement "خ½_{b*} large", which is exactly argmax knowledge. The weighted moment is still a
class-4 average (the RISK flagged in the idea: "weighted moments may still be class-4 averages" â€” they
are). This is the same Parseval/Welch=âˆڑn trap, not an escape from it.

VERDICT: NO-GAIN. Levenshtein's weighted moment is a real and superior LOWER bound for sequence-design
packing (the literature claim transfers and is correct in that direction), but it is structurally the
WRONG DIRECTION for an upper bound on M(خ¼_n), and the only valid upper-bound reading (Markov against a
permitted positive-definite weight) cannot beat exponent 1âˆ’o(1) without encoding argmax. Consistent
with the campaign's exhausted-moment finding (moments cannot pin a sup). CORE stays OPEN. No Lean brick
(no new provable upper-bound statement emerged; a Lean formalization would only restate the trivial
Markov inequality, which is already implicit in the energy substrate).

2026-06-15 â€” IDEA I001 (stepanov-2adic): Ore/Moore Frobenius-filtration Stepanov auxiliary
================================================================================
OBJECT: M(خ¼_n) = max_{bâ‰ 0}|خ£_{xâˆˆخ¼_n} e_p(bx)|, خ¼_n = 2-power subgroup (n=2^خ¼, n|pâˆ’1, p prime,
pâ‰«nآ³, nâ‰ pâˆ’1). Probe: scripts/probes/probe_i001_ore_moore_frobenius_stepanov.py.

IDEA I001's NEW LEMMA (claimed): build the Stepanov auxiliary as an ADDITIVE/linearized (Ore)
polynomial L(X)=خ£ c_i X^{p^i}; count multiplicity as the p-adic order of vanishing in the
Frobenius module (not ordinary tangency, which separability of X^nâˆ’1 pins to 1). The
Moore-determinant rank on the "F_2-log-lattice of خ¼_n" is asserted to supply vanishing order
خ¼=log_2 n, giving |خ¼_nâˆ©Z(L)|آ·(خ¼+1) â‰¤ deg L via a Moore-minor rank-defect â‰¥ خ¼ on a near-extremal
bad set â€” beating the Johnson cap.

VERDICT: NO-GAIN (refutes the literal NEW LEMMA; at best yields the TRIVIAL bound M â‰¤ n).

WHY (four reproducible sub-tests over proper خ¼_n, n=8,16,32,64, pâ‰«nآ³, nâ‰ pâˆ’1):
1. STRUCTURAL OBSTRUCTION. An Ore poly is F_p-linear; Z(L) is an F_p-subspace. خ¼_n âٹ‚ F_p, and F_p
   is 1-DIMENSIONAL over F_p, so ANY single nonzero element of خ¼_n F_p-spans all of F_p â‡’ the only
   Ore poly vanishing on خ¼_n is L = X^p âˆ’ X (p-degree 1, ORDINARY degree p â‰ˆ q). Stepanov needs
   deg â‰ھ q â‡’ vacuous. AND Frobenius xâ†¦x^p is the IDENTITY on F_p (verified all test primes), so the
   "Frobenius filtration" on the x-coordinates (= خ¼_n) is TRIVIAL â€” no p-adic multiplicity to harvest.
2. HASSE-JET DEFECT = 0 (the separability wall, measured). The (خ¼+1)-Hasse-jet vanishing matrix on
   خ¼_n over F_p is FULL RANK n(خ¼+1) at every n (defect 0, vs needed â‰¥ خ¼). The (خ¼+1)-jet conditions
   are INDEPENDENT precisely because X^nâˆ’1 is separable (simple roots). Smallest nonzero auxiliary has
   deg D = n(خ¼+1); countآ·(خ¼+1) â‰¤ D â‡’ count â‰¤ n. The (خ¼+1) factor in the lemma is EXACTLY cancelled by
   the (خ¼+1)-fold degree cost. NO tower discount.
3. MOORE-DETERMINANT = WRONG DEFECT. Moore matrix [a_i^{p^j}] over F_p has rank 1 (Frobenius=id
   collapses all columns), so "multiplicity" = 1, not خ¼; the rank-1 collapse just re-derives X^pâˆ’X
   (degree p, useless). The genuinely full-rank object is the EXPONENT Vandermonde / character table
   [h^{kj}] on Z/2^خ¼ (defect 0) = the DFT Parseval already uses for the âˆڑn Lآ²-average. The 2-power
   tower adds NO vanishing order for a polynomial in x.
4. THE BOUND. Even GRANTING I001's defect, count â‰¤ D/(خ¼+1) with D â‰ˆ n(خ¼+1) â‡’ count â‰¤ n; best M-bound
   = n = TRIVIAL, BELOW BGK's n^{1âˆ’o(1)}. (true M: n=8â†’7.70, n=16â†’13.46; the bound n never nears âˆڑn.)

ROOT CAUSE: خ¼_n lives in the 1-dim F_p-space F_p AND X^nâˆ’1 is separable â‡’ no additive/Frobenius
multiplicity > 1 to manufacture; the only nontrivial 2-power structure (the exponent/character
lattice) is full-rank and already used by Parseval. The claimed Moore-minor rank-defect â‰¥ خ¼ is FALSE
in every operative reading. (Salvage note: an Artin-Schreier auxiliary can carry genuine content only

than خ¼_nâٹ‚F_p, and one where the y^pâˆ’y=bx ramification gives the standard Weil bound, vacuous at nâ‰ھâˆڑp.)
No Lean brick (the refuted lemma was the only candidate statement). CORE stays OPEN.

## I006 REFUTED â€” q-difference (Jackson) confluent Stepanov gives no multiplicity on خ¼_n (2026-06-15)

**Idea.** Replace `d/dX` by the Jackson q-difference `D_خ¶ f = (f(خ¶X)âˆ’f(X))/((خ¶âˆ’1)X)`, خ¶ a
primitive 2^خ¼-th root (the canonical derivation of خ¼_n). Since `[n]_خ¶ = 0` we have
`D_خ¶(X^nâˆ’1) â‰، 0` identically (verified): the whole subgroup is one D_خ¶-orbit, so the
separability obstruction that caps ordinary Stepanov should be bypassed and q-multiplicity be
free, giving `خ¼آ·|Z| â‰¤ deg_q f`.

**Verdict: REFUTED.** The orbit-invariance that bypasses separability is exactly what *voids*
the multiplicity. `D_خ¶` is a NON-LOCAL finite-difference operator coupling z to خ¶z; on the
orbit-closed خ¼_n it maps {functions on خ¼_n} â†’ {functions on خ¼_n}, so once f vanishes on the
whole orbit (the j=0 conditions, n of them) every iterate `D_خ¶^j f` vanishes there
AUTOMATICALLY. The higher q-vanishing conditions are linearly *dependent* on the value
conditions â€” q-vanishing on the orbit is a global circulant condition, not a local jet.

**Measured** (`scripts/probes/probe_i006_qdifference_stepanov.py`; proper خ¼_n, n=2^خ¼ | pâˆ’1, p
prime ~ n^4, p â‰« n^3, never n=pâˆ’1; خ¼=2,3,4):
- **M1:** rank of q-vanish-to-order-m conditions on full خ¼_n collapses to exactly |Z|=n,
  INDEPENDENT of m. Stepanov needs mآ·n. (خ¼=4, m=5: needs 80, rank=16.) Multiplicity gain = 0.
- **M2:** minimal nonzero f q-vanishing to order m on خ¼_n has total ordinary root-multiplicity
  = n (simple zeros only), deg = n; divisibility `(âˆڈ(Xâˆ’z))^m | f` FAILS. q-vanishing yields
  only ordinary simple vanishing â€” no high-order zero, hence no degree leverage.
- single-point q-multiplicity SATURATES at n (rank stops growing past m=n): at most n
  independent q-conditions exist at any orbit point.

So `خ¼آ·|Z| â‰¤ deg_q` is false; only the trivial `|Z| â‰¤ deg` holds. Confirms the existing W3 wall
(confluent Stepanov stalls) at its structural root: a multiplicative/orbit derivation cannot
manufacture multiplicity on its OWN orbit. No gain toward M(خ¼_n) â‰¤ Câˆڑ(n log(p/n)).

## I031 group-invariant Dudley chaining â€” REFINES the prior A2 "reduces-to-wall" (2026-06-15)

**Verdict: promising-partial.** The prior A2 chaining attack (route 54, verdict
"reduces-to-wall": chaining metric entropy = خک(log q), dilation does NOT collapse it)
chained over the FULL index F_p* under the increment metric d. That was the wrong index set.

**The fact it missed (verified, `probe_i031_orbit_invariance.py`, machine precision):**
`|X_b| = |خ£_{xâˆˆخ¼_n} e_p(bx)|` is EXACTLY invariant under the dilation `b â†¦ خ¶b` (خ¶âˆˆخ¼_n).
So `M = max_{bâ‰ 0}|X_b|` is a sup over only `m = (p-1)/n` ORBIT representatives, and chaining
over F_p* double-counts each value n times. The honest chaining is over the QUOTIENT F_p*/خ¼_n.

**What the quotient gives (`probe_i031_fullset_vs_quotient.py`):** FULL-set `maxlogN/log p = 1.000`
(the wall, reproduces A2) vs QUOTIENT `mxlNq/log p â‰ˆ 0.58â€“0.66` (â‰ˆ `log m / log p â‰ˆ 0.71`).
The entropy collapses from `log p` to `log(p/n)` â€” exactly the I031 "process isometry collapses
cosets to n orbits." Dudley `خ³_2` on the quotient scales as `âˆڑ(log m)`:
`slope log(g2q) vs log(log m) = 0.41 â‰¤ 1/2` (`probe_i031_dudley_exponent.py`, fixed n) â€” the
EXPONENT 1/2 in `log(p/n)` is recovered, NOT the BGK 1âˆ’o(1).

**The remaining gap = THE RISK in I031 (`probe_i031_det_vs_random_transfer.py`):** the
random sub-Gaussian model with the Gauss-period covariance provably obeys the floor
`E sup_b|G_b| â‰¤ Câˆڑ(n log m)` (Krahmerâ€“Mendelsonâ€“Rauhut / Rudelsonâ€“Vershynin RIP; measured
`rand/floor = 0.74â€“0.95`). The DETERMINISTIC `M` is a BOUNDED factor `M/rand â‰ˆ 1.3â€“1.4`
(stable across n=4..32, not blowing up) above it. So I031 reduces the open content from
"BGK exponent gap (1 vs 1/2)" down to "a bounded-constant deterministicâ†’random transfer for
THIS specific cyclotomic frame." No current theorem supplies that transfer (Maurey
derandomization yields a good SUBSET of rows, not equality of the deterministic sup with the
random expectation). The constant `C â‰ˆ M/floor` still creeps `1.07â†’1.36` over n=4..64
(`probe_i031_quotient_nscaling.py`, `mxlNq/log m â†’ 1`), so the constant is not pinned.

**Net:** NOT a closure, but a GENUINE new handle that the prior A2 verdict wrongly closed off:
the orbit-quotient chaining recovers the floor EXPONENT 1/2 (the exact target, beating 1âˆ’o(1))
for the RANDOMIZED frame, isolating the open piece to a bounded deterministic-vs-random
constant transfer â€” a much smaller and more concrete target than the raw BGK sup bound.
Probes: `scripts/probes/probe_i031_*.py` (6 files, all proper regime p prime, n=2^خ¼, n|pâˆ’1,
pâ‰«nآ³, proper 2-power subgroup, never n=pâˆ’1).

## I012 REFUTED â€” subgroup-trilinear (Beukers-Smyth cyclotomic incidence) kills no p^{1/4} (2026-06-15)

**Idea (I012, effective-sumproduct).** Replace the p^{1/4} in the Petridis-Shparlinski trilinear
bound (di Benedetto Lemma 4.1, |خ£_{xâˆˆX,yâˆˆY,zâˆˆZ} خ±_xخ²_yخ³_z e_p(axyz)| â‰ھ p^{1/4}|X|^{3/4}|Y|^{3/4}|Z|^{7/8})
by an in-regime n^c, c<1/4, on the premise that "the three variable sets are sub-tori of one 2-power
tower" so collinear/coplanar triples are Beukers-Smyth cyclotomic torsion-coset points (خک(n), not the
generic خک(nآ²)). This would make di Benedetto nontrivial inside n<p^{1/4} (the prize regime) for the
first time, giving the NEW LEMMA |خ£_{x,y,zâˆˆخ¼_n}e_p(axyz)| â‰¤ C n^{2+1/4âˆ’خ·}.

**Verdict: REFUTED on BOTH readings** (probe_i012_subgroup_trilinear.py; proper خ¼_n: n=2^خ¼ | pâˆ’1,
p PRIME ~ n^4 â‰« n^3, m=(pâˆ’1)/n â‰  1, NEVER n=pâˆ’1; nâˆˆ{8,16,32}).

**(A) Direct reading â€” apply trilinear to the subgroup itself, X=Y=Z=خ¼_n â€” is CIRCULAR [PROVEN id].**
خ¼_n is multiplicatively closed, so xyz ranges over خ¼_n with CONSTANT multiplicity #{(x,y,z):xyz=w}=nآ².
Hence the EXACT identity (verified numerically True at a=a_max,1,3 for every n):
    T(a) := خ£_{x,y,zâˆˆخ¼_n} e_p(axyz) = nآ² آ· خ·_a,     خ·_a = خ£_{wâˆˆخ¼_n} e_p(aw).
The product map collapses; product-energy E_prod(خ¼_nآ³)=#{xâ‚پyâ‚پzâ‚پ=xâ‚‚yâ‚‚zâ‚‚}=nآ·(nآ²)آ²=n^5 EXACTLY (measured:
n=8â†’32768=8^5, n=16â†’1048576=16^5) â€” fully degenerate, NOT خک(n) "isolated", maximally coset-concentrated.
So bounding |T|â‰¤n^{2+1/4âˆ’خ·} is LITERALLY EQUIVALENT to |خ·_a|â‰¤n^{1/4âˆ’خ·} â€” STRONGER than the prize target
n^{1/2}. The "new lemma" assumes (more than) its own conclusion. And it is FALSE as stated: measured
M=max|خ·| = 7.56 / 13.30 / 22.98 (n=8/16/32) â‰« n^{1/4} = 1.68 / 2.00 / 2.38, so |T|=nآ²M EXCEEDS n^{2+1/4}.

**(B) di-Benedetto reading â€” the premise "sets are sub-tori" is FACTUALLY WRONG [MEASURED].** In the
actual proof of di Benedetto Thm 3.1, Lemma 4.1 is fed X={xâ‚پ+xâ‚‚+xâ‚ƒ}, Y={yâ‚پ+yâ‚‚+yâ‚ƒ} (ADDITIVE sumsets of
subgroup triples) and Z={zâ‚پâˆ’zâ‚‚} (a DIFFERENCE set) â€” additive combinations, NOT multiplicative sub-tori.
Measured fraction lying in the torus خ¼_n: sum3 = 8.3% (n=8) / 2.3% (n=16); diff2 = 0% / 0%. These sets
are SPREAD off the torus â†’ they carry NO Beukers-Smyth torsion-coset structure, so the cyclotomic-coset
incidence improvement cannot be applied to them. The p^{1/4} in Lemma 4.1 is moreover a FIELD-SIZE term
(the mn/p â†” (mn)^{2/3} crossover in the Rudnev point-plane bound), not a generic-collinear-triple count;
replacing "خک(nآ²) collinear triples" by "خک(n) cyclotomic triples" mis-locates where the p^{1/4} lives.

**Root cause.** The only multiplicative-closure structure of خ¼_n makes the trilinear product map collapse
(degenerate, energy n^5), which is the OPPOSITE of the "few isolated incidences" the idea hoped for; and
the genuine di Benedetto trilinear input acts on additive (off-torus) sets where no cyclotomic structure
survives. Either way there is no n^c (c<1/4) handle. No gain toward M(خ¼_n) â‰¤ Câˆڑ(n log(p/n)). CORE OPEN.
